engagements.title: Call with Robert Doto
engagements.body:
Robert called to discuss the status of the Dynamic Attribute timeline that's holding up their decision on Allmoxy overall. He asked directly if this is a bandwidth issue for us or if we're not interested in the initiative from a product roadmap perspective. I told him it was the first but we're working through those constraints and left it at that. Adding to heads meeting to give team context as Robert expressed the feeling at Peacock is Allmoxy is not interested in making the partnership work(though he appreciated the transparence on the high level hold up for Dynamic Attributes.)


@Brady Lewis @Ashley Schuurman @Sarah Pierce @Cheneil Garrett for vis

Title: Peacock API Integration Date: Wed Aug 21 2024 10:00:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Participants: sarah@allmoxy.com, madison@allmoxy.com, garywilliams@ptxpartners.com, donaldvarona@ptxpartners.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 30 Overview During the Project Status Update and Strategy Discussion meeting focused on the Peacock API Integration, key stakeholders provided updates on progress and outlined strategic next steps. Gary reported reconnecting with the Peacock team after a hiatus, emphasizing readiness for front-end integration and discussing upcoming features such as dynamic attributes planned for the next release. Sarah added that she had met with Peacock representatives to clarify required terminology for the Almoxy interface, highlighting a need for translation of certain hard-coded terms. The meeting also addressed challenges related to organizing data for Cabinet Vision import, with potential solutions including using Netsuite for data reformatting. Action items were assigned, with Gary tasked to summarize the current status and contact Robert for further guidance, while Sarah would follow up on necessary tweaks related to the integration. Overall, the meeting fostered a collaborative environment aimed at resolving existing issues and aligning next steps for a successful integration. Action Items **Gary Williams** Send a note to Sarah summarizing understanding of current status (12:16) Contact Robert to discuss re-engagement and next steps (15:45) Send an update before the end of the week (16:15) **Sarah Pierce** [+ 2 More] Shorthand Bullet 🔄 **Project Status Update** (00:02 - 04:20) Gary reconnecting with Peacock after hiatus Discussing front-end readiness and integration expectations Sarah mentions meeting with Peacock team about features and launch options Dynamic attributes feature coming in next release [+ 14 More] Gist Key stakeholders discussed the Peacock API Integration, providing updates on progress and outlining strategic next steps for successful collaboration. Bullet Gist 🔗 Peacock Team Reconnection: Gary highlighted his reconnection with the Peacock team, focusing on front-end integration readiness. 📋 Terminology Clarification: Sarah discussed the need for clear terminology for the Almoxy interface, addressing translation challenges. 🔄 Data Organization Challenges: The team tackled issues related to organizing data for Cabinet Vision import, proposing Netsuite for reformatting. ✅ Defined Action Items: Action items were assigned, with Gary summarizing status and Sarah following up on integration tweaks. 🤝 Collaborative Environment: The meeting promoted collaboration to resolve challenges and align next steps for integration success. Short Summary During the Project Status Update and Strategy Discussion meeting, stakeholders focused on the Peacock API Integration, highlighting critical updates and strategic actions. Gary noted reconnection with the Peacock team and readiness for front-end integration, mentioning new features like dynamic attributes in the next release. Sarah emphasized the importance of clarifying terminology for the Almoxy interface, indicating a need for translating certain hard-coded terms. The group addressed challenges with organizing data for Cabinet Vision import, considering solutions such as leveraging Netsuite for data reformatting. Action items were clearly defined, with Gary responsible for summarizing the current status and reaching out to Robert for further assistance, while Sarah would follow up on necessary adjustments related to the integration. The meeting fostered collaboration to resolve existing issues and align future steps for successful integration.

engagements.body: Peacock & Allmoxy All Hands Meeting 8/1/24 Aaron, Juan, Sarah and Madison Robert D, Kathy, Joe, Carrie & Jim Meeting Notes: Concerns: Dynamic attributes within product attributes needed for overall better designer experience, order level functionality is heavily utilized and validations are too clunky Needs timeline Needs confirmation on Dynamic Attribute feature as well as that it won't impact Gary's Netsuite work Done - https://allmoxy.atlassian.net/browse/CI-5 Folders not talking to Cabinet Vision Issue on the production side and doesn't feel like a better process than what Peacock is currently using Need to be able to price within one version/file on Allmoxy level Don't want separated within Allmoxy, wants to go to three rooms in Allmoxy with respective finishes, still wants one invoice for customer - Invoice is on Gary/Netsuite side Can Allmoxy help here? Feature request Verbiage Concern/Translation Request - to make the system more user friendly for designers Estimate was given to Peacock and Kathy expressed it was off putting to get a bill when they've invested this much Bringing this to attention of Allmoxy leadership Next Steps: Final estimate of when they can go live with this project - timeline and cost Outstanding Services Invoices to be revisited after final project decision Escalation ticket - https://allmoxy.atlassian.net/browse/CI-3?atlOrigin=eyJpIjoiMTBjMmFhNWJkNzVkNGMwODk5YzQ1MWNkYTc4NmVmMTYiLCJwIjoiamlyYS1zbGFjay1pbnQifQ

engagements.title: Follow up with Christopher Peacock
engagements.body: Regarding email: Outstanding Balance Assistance Needed, sent on Friday, April 12, 2024 3:36 PM

@Samantha Alexander - Please let me know what's going on with this invoice with Peacock as it's from last month. https://allmoxy1.harvestapp.com/client/invoices/aef4a02066fa7cd69f3be9b48aa209efd5375bf7

Internal Services Notes April: 4/18/23 Aaron completed catalog/update work for Peacock! CS running with next steps for launching. Joe should be primary but for now Kathy is still involved. I have finished everything on my end but they are keeping my appointment in 2 weeks so we can go over anything they come up with between now and then.Apparently someone in some company tech support capacity did something that has caused some significant issues with their Cabinet Vision. I don't know if it was a version thing or a license or whatever but Carrie is having to do a lot of work to get it functional.I'm really glad we did export testing before this happened because they are considering us to have completed our end. I may need to go make adjustments to accommodate whatever fix is implemented later. Training more on order processing and custom field configuration. 4/5/24 Update to panel products and price summations for invoices

Internal Services Notes March: 3/25/24 Kathy rolled out Allmoxy training to their designers last week on company sales call. I checked in to offer training and she said she'd like to wait until she has more feedback from the designers. 3/22/24 Joe and Jim new main POCS leading up to launch. Becoming more involved. Joe over department that designers report into. Aaron says Joe is fantastic to work with but hasn't spent a lot of time with Jim yet. 3/8/24 Aaron: got through 4 more exports that needed to come out which is good. Bunch more products made. Finally, they gave Aaron a new spec book to revamp pricing bc when they luanch the project will go to someone else who is managing- Aaron will manage that. They are 100% on board that this is what they are paying for, they aren't behind and Aaron thinks they are happy with everything.

Internal Services Note 2/22/24 "The operations manager that will be taking over (Jim Morrison) was testing by replicating real orders and they came back within half a percent. They are very happy about that. Jim is travelling to Carrie's location this week and has limited availability but Kathy is going to work out his schedule and book a time for introductions and we will go over some revisions to their pricing scheme that he is currently implementing." Jim is the Director of Special Projects and is the one responsible for their original and upcoming pricing schemes. From what I gathered Kathy is going to transition away from the project at or around launch

Internal Services Update: 2/9/24 Aaron built out export for what Gary needed even though we disagreed with the ask - either way exports for the product are working now Finished up backend on 93 outsource products Added in 60 some new products this week and fully functioning other than exports, going to work through Carrie for this info

Internal Services Upate 1/31/24 Aaron: Cabinet Vision Exports Escalation: Some of the limitation is on us - dev ticket(https://allmoxy.atlassian.net/browse/DEV-7666) - ORDX(xmml file) limitations Aaron met with Drew Lewis from CV/Hexagon software to discuss best way to approach, helpful conversation Limitation on their side is how CV looks at rooms Outcome - Separate orders for each room, hoping to get the process down to one step and not multiple People involved: Carrie(warehouse manager/processing of orders), Joe(and designers), Kathy and Cabinet Vision POCs Not sure if Carrie or Joe will be main POC for Allmoxy once this is done Other Catalog Some late additions of products, not complicated Aaron says and after he can fully focus on export Pricing is good Successful exports with ORD(csv file) Next meeting - tomorrow 2/1 Over $100k in services work right now

Internal Services Upate 1/29/24 Aaron: "My meeting with Kathy went very smoothly. We should be able to move forward problem-free. Likely some friction on the design team but as far as management and shop/warehouse go we are good. I'll be meeting with them again Thursday and I can gauge then how they feel across the board."

Internal Services update: 1/19/24 Aaron A lot to fix with what's going on with their exports, waiting on info form their Cabinet Vision developers(issues with files when there's more than one product sending same information) Would have to do separate orders for every room if they can't get this to work From Peacocks perspective they are upset with CabinetVision in this situation, but still not happy overall because we're stalled - ORDX is sold to Cabinet Vision customers and not working as expected Kathy pushing to hit launch within next month or two(End of February/End of March)

Weekly Services & Peacock Sync 1/11/24 Kathy, Aaron and Madison Netsuite creating child product in netsuite for all of their products so they can create bill of products Kathy needs to give them a list of parent/children products Susan is creating "children" Aaron getting this information for Kathy Need all products in Allmoxy even if flow is not complete ORDX part of this(Gary section) - doing something so they are congruent - Aaron working with Carrie independently for this and Kathy approved this will cost more/add services hours Utilizing folders discussion Confirmed pulling in room info at product level - the export formulas split into header, product part and then footer "Room" usage in product is for purpose that ever product it will get its own folder name and Image updates still need to be done - Kathy/Joe is trying to get someone internally to get those updated for Aaron Discussed starting employee Allmoxy training as they near launch - setting task to follow up with Aaron/Kathy on this Less than 20 people using Allmoxy at CP Need to determine Invited Kathy to WTS Event and asked to pass along to others - said Carrie and Mary could be good to attend

Aaron's Catalog Update 1/4/24: Kathy opted to skip today's meeting They were to come in today with results from their own order testing. I assume they want to spend more time gathering data I did meet briefly with Carrie to go over their new export requirements and have that to work on for them in the meantime Sarah API/Netsuite Update:

Internal Sync @Aaron Dundas & Madison 11/9/23 Aaron: "Next meeting will be rescheduled for a different day as it falls on Thanksgiving but I think it will be a great time to hop in and shake some hands. They are really frustrated with Cabinet Vision right now and showing some extra care along with my next set of deliverables could go a long way" Aaron adding Madison to next meeting as soon as rescheduled 11/8/23 Build is still going well Working on sample file, going with ORDX to differentiate rooms using folder names. Regular ORD is not able to pass room information. ORD is specific to Cabinet Vision and the same as a csv file, but their own version with slight differences. Export will build order in CV, data will be in Netsuite and Allmoxy is price book that will send back to Cabinet Vision Using Allmoxy to lessen man hours, improve accuracy and have better efficiency - their designers will use Allmoxy to estimate orders Aaron has bi-weekly meetings currently One of their designers was testing and found the B2B marketplace, wanted marketplace turned off but it's not possible. They aren't too upset with it Still unknown about the hardcoded concerns Kathy has. Aaron hasn't heard anything.

10/20/23 Meeting- CANCELED: The 10/20/23 meeting was canceled due to Jake and Sarah being out. Sarah asked if Donald or Gary had any questions that needed to be addressed prior to the 10/27/23 meeting and Gary confirmed they had enough to work on and could wait until 10/27 to meet again.

NetSuite Integration Meeting 10/13/23 NetSuite team's focusing on their current work on managing the production process and accounting for materials. They also mentioned Aaron's helpful guidance on data related to orders and expressed the intention to further explore it in the call. Gary Started Screensharing Gary, Sarah, and Aaron discussed the integration between Cabinet Vision and Next Week, focusing on the relationship between products, attributes, and bill of materials. They also explored how to handle non-cabinet items and ensure alignment between Yomoxi schema and Next Week for special order items.2:21 Aaron and Gary discussed the export process from various products to Cabinet Vision, including the categorization of hardware and accessories. They also talked about the need to capture additional parts for certain cabinets and the challenge of matching special order items with cabinetry components in NetSuite.8:05 In the discussion, Aaron, Gary, Sarah, Jake, and Donald were trying to figure out how to associate different items with specific categories in an order. They discussed using product categories and creating a new endpoint to simplify the process and make it more efficient.14:10 Jake, Gary, Donald, and Aaron discussed the categorization and breakdown of product data for filtering purposes. They also clarified that joinery items and certain other components would not be sent to cabinet vision, and Donald had no further questions.19:18 Donald mentioned that he had a few open questions, but most of them have been answered. Gary and Jake discussed the progress made on connecting the API points and the upcoming release, with Jake mentioning that he may not be available for the next meeting. Gary Stopped Screensharing

For line items Donald says Gary will have more information. Donald believes there is some inventory they will be tracking and needs Gary to confirm. @Sarah Pierce to break down a list of explanation for this and relay how it will correlate to the one endpoint ticket Went through Donald's API questions with Jake Making filtering an array rather than one filter at a time Postman question for updatestart filter Logical schema question - Sarah working on, documented internally and Jake can send over for related to questions. Netsuite Integration Meeting 9/29/23 Donald Arona @Sarah Pierce Jake Questions from Donald Limiting attributes (ex. contact ID as reference point) Need to differentiate between Individual(Contact) and Company Mask over to company Suggestion around documentation - re-arrange attributes in alphabetical order (Jake confirmed in process right now, Mack working on) Date in progress & Time Stamp(going away) Filter by multiple attributes - numeric field only one right now, feature request Sarah asked about the necessity for status' in Allmoxy to match Netsuite/Peacock and Donald said it's a better question for Gary Donald focused on API work, currently pulling companies and synchronizing in Netsuite

engagements.title: Call with Christopher Peacock
engagements.body: Called again no answer

Services Check-In: 9/28/23 Meeting attendees: Kathy Conroy, Joe Pascarella, Aaron Dundas Met to go over progress on the catalog buildout. Kathy is happy with the progress made since the last check-in (9/14/23). The third-party team working on Peacock's NetSuite integration has made internal comments to Peacock leadership about the amount of data working on the back end of the products. Kathy's impression is that this is relieving some of the pressure the project team is under about the investment in services. Kathy has restated that she expects to be fully finished with the catalog buildout in the next few months but expects the NetSuite team to take another 6 months (roughly) to finish. I am waiting for information about products to add to the Outsource category to come back from Joe and will be completing work on validations in the meantime.

Heads Update/Escalation: 9/28/23 @Sarah Pierce Next Steps: Once we get list from Kathy we will see if restricting employee permission is an okay route for how Kathy's wants employees interacting for pricing. Additionally, for status word changes we will need to check with dev on possibility. Additionally, need to understand more about Netsuite and Allmoxy working together via API. By sending everything over to Netsuite what will their actual customer base see from Allmoxy and how much do we weigh the status change request? Could their employees equate status like bid to estimate. Sales notes in demos they always let prospects know the status' are not an option to change/customize as these are hardcoded. Ultimately, we need to know exactly how their employees will engage with Allmoxy and what words/statuses are pertinent. How serious is the "deal breaker" of status words being in their verbiage If moving forward on status of words is this a feature request using translation or what Potential On-site customer candidate for Brady

Intro Meeting w/Kathy & Joe 9/27/23: Kathy Background and Vision for Success: How they will use Allmoxy is as a price book ONLY(estimates only.) Netsuite is doing everything else(reporting, shipping, customer accounts, etc.) Present pricing to customer and finalize/export out of Allmoxy to CabinetVision. Can't produce complete order at this point. Not sure how they will role this out. Need internal go to person for their entire staff to use Allmoxy program and Kathy is working on finding and hiring that person. Kathy and Joe handing over reigns eventually. Kathy is go to point of contact for Peacock as of now between Netsuite and Peacock. Kathy and Joe working on front end. Need to get into beta testing with other people in their company(working with Aaron for feedback.) Want to have another call in a few weeks and have a lot of pressure with leadership. Designers all around country. Last week sorted out billing with Sam. Invoices need to continue from @Juan Ridgeway and continue as they are. @Aaron Dundas to continue making information clear on Netsuite versus catalog for billing purposes. Action Needed ASAP: They have a major concern over the difference in Allmoxy's verbiage and need that "changed" eventually. Kathy's leadership cannot see this kind of verbiage in our system or it's a "DEAL BREAKER"(ex. bid.) Want to know what can't be changed for sure? Goal is to make it more in their vernacular. Would API work read these words in Allmoxy and pull for what they need? Kathy and Jim going through to get examples of other words. Open to even turning off/hiding these words/drop downs is another good option. @Sarah Pierce Talked about setting as customer view vs employee can be much more customizable - they have all employees(not customers), but they are open to the "customer use" for their employees. Kathy says they feel a little confused here. Starting an order(we call bid they call estimate) their employee is doing the pricing. Shipping is a number they quote and ballpark, don't need within Allmoxy - shipping currently set as will call Launch date - Need to get more of their employees through beta, need to ask Aaron for an updated ETA on product launch(they think they are 80% there and think around 2-3 months left) @Aaron Dundas Netsuite Integration launch date - expected 6 months out from after product launch date. Other: Discussed what will happen for bugs specific to what they report - we provide update Version updates - Appcues Would benefit from seeing a customer facing Product Facing Roadmap - @Ashley Schuurman I'm committing to a Weekly Email update to Kathy and Joe to keep organized on the catalog and Netsuite project, planning to send on Wednesday's - Kathy requested I keep it simple and clean - @Aaron Dundas @Sarah Pierce for your awareness Separate Netsuite team has access into their internal current system. The netsuite contact went to Peacock leadership and expressed the positive investment that has been done on the Allmoxy side on the product build out side of things.

engagements.title: Churned customer re-engagement consideration
engagements.body: See Churn playbook and retention notes for full context. For more information reach out to the former assigned CSM.

Churn Playbook Please select all of the reasons that apply for why the customer is churning. Add any additional churn context in the note - the more the better here. If there's escalation notes that exist on the account be sure to reference them and include the data of the escalation note in the playbook context. Business Model Change Notes: They are moving into a different industry (hospitality) out of necessity. They had hoped their new customers would want to use Allmoxy, but there wasn't any appetite. They go to the sites and give quotes based on what they see. They appreciate our team and personally love our platform. It just doesn't make sense to keep Allmoxy where they are headed. IF this is the customers main/only instance that's churning, change the Customer Lifecycle property(company property) to "Other." DO NOT DO THIS if this is a second instance and the churn does not completely eliminate their status as a customer. Other What's the catalog implementation status? Yes
@Josh Baker Can you fill out the Churn Playbook here when you get a chance please?

Churn Risk Playbook Please share the most up to date health pulse from the CSM's perspective. Red(this customer is not in good standing and is on track to churn) Has this customer asked to pause or cancel their account or is this playbook being utilized preemptively? Requested to cancel Notes: Joel emailed me saying that they will not be renewing their contract next month. How much is the customers monthly subscription? Please note if the customer has received any reductions in MRR during their lifetime with Allmoxy as well as any significant, recent implementation costs. $778.00 Industry Segment (Data Confirmation) Closets & Storage Designer, SKU Cabinet Manufacturer Business Size (Data Confirmation) SMB How long have they been a customer of Allmoxy? 04/01/2020 Is this customer launched? Yes Is this customer working with our implementation team now, if so please elaborate on the nature of the work/project. No Is this customer a legacy/strategic account? Yes Notes: Legacy, I wouldn't say very strategic. Has the customer proposed any solutions that would eliminate or delay their churn risk? This could be a price reduction or delivery on a specific feature. Notes: We made a plan last year to have a few meetings to discuss Allmoxy strategy with their new hospitality market. Set several meetings with Joel and Sarah, but he always rescheduled because he was too busy working on getting into a new market. Please share any other strategic context that we should consider related to this account. (Ex. Industry influence/connections, personality details, known expansion opportunity, competitor tech stack, etc.) Notes: They are pivoting to a new customer base that doesn't see the benefit of using Allmoxy. They weren't doing enough business with their current customer base, so they have been working hard to break into the hospitality market. I thought that market would be good for Allmoxy, but most people they talked to said they wouldn't find value in that. I think they are just going to be going out and quoting jobs manually. They like Allmoxy and have enjoyed working with us, so no bad blood. Their new customers just won't use it. Who's the main customer contact associated with this escalation? Joel For reporting purposes, please check that this playbook is completed. Yes

Renewal Lifecycle Playbook What's the contractual outcome of the annual renewal process? Please add any relevant notes. Yes, in contract Was expansion revenue captured in this renewal? No If the customer's subscription did not increase, what reasons attributed to the same contract amount or any level of churn? Other, Subscription amount high for customers verified order amount, New features required for full value perception/adoption Notes: Joel has had issues with validations, too much work that needs to be done and doesn't particularly want to pay us (it would be a large bill). Really wants the dynamic attributes. New business opportunity came up with hotels, his main focus is on that and doesn't know if Allmoxy will be the right fit. Tried to meet with Joel 4 or 5 times with Sarah but he kept rescheduling. Wanted to pay less than $778, but we kept him the same. Launch status Yes CS Health Pulse Red(this customer is not in good standing and is on track to churn) Bid Abuser No

Renewal Call with Joel 1/15/25 When he talked with Kenzi, is 778 the exact price? For the most part in back end Allmoxy was not working with their shop with inventory tracking (they way their shop was set up) Everything is computer generated, draw online and do g-code. He only wants to keep it open is the online storefront. Only want Allmoxy for online ordering, doesn't like the idea of spending 778 for that Wasn't working with the inventory side. Last time we discussed redoing his catalog. We can link CV and Allmoxy which would work great. Validations aren't working in the new instance. If they were working, they would do away with the old one. They are thinking of just keeping the closets in the new line. But also cabinets. We made all the cabinets with proxy variables. They are close, but he just needs to find the time to do it. They have shifted their use case, and they have been approached by hospitality projects. These are bigger projects for them. Seems very promising so far with these big clients. KEEPING CLOSETS AND CABINETS IN NEW INSTANCE Cabinets created by proxies, which they like. Problem is the closet catalog. They are going to keep some of it. It will really narrow down the amount of stuff we need make validations for. The initial plan was to have that done in late fall. But during all that, the new business opportunity came up. I let him know that the opportunity with the hospitality clients sounds awesome, but we need to work closely together to stay on top of the project. He wanted less than $778 but I let him know that is the best we can do since he is already getting a really good price. Will schedule some time two weeks from today to get a project plan moving.

Renewal Meeting 2024 In attendance: Kenzi, Joel, Josh Fluctuation in revenue. The major change is they went to Quickbooks online, so they are running their invoices through. What they plan to use Allmoxy for is setting up with lumbar yards where they are setting up pricing, etc. has 3 or 4 lumber yards waiting on them, they goal is to use the online store. They have a huge business opportunity coming down the pipeline and they are unsure if they are going to want to utilize Allmoxy. Discussed the Quickbooks online feature coming down the pipeline super soon. Using the inventory tracking program right now but there are a lot of improvements they believe should be made there such as tying a PO to an order. They invested in Webcap to track parts, that came out with an inventory module. Allmoxy is essentially going to be an online store front for them. Made a decision to get rid of all the cabinets, they have two sets of cabinets, the new instance would have all the cabinets and the old instance will just be closets.

Renewal 12/2/24 with Joel & Kenzi Right now they only want Allmoxy for online ordering Potentially getting rid of all cabinets in new instance and keeping the closets The issue has been with the validations (lot of manual work) they are planning on getting rid of those cabinets and just using closet units Just waiting on Joel to get us the information on what we need to do on our end. They were having too many small jobs, not enough money in the small jobs so now they are going after higher volume customers. Fluctuation They went to QBO and ran their invoices through there (that is why there is such a fluctuation in revenue. Setting up lumber yards so they can price and submit 3 or 4 that are waiting on him Overwhelming support of their customers send them a file in CV, they process they send an invoice through QBO Goal is to use the online storefront of Allmoxy Working on a big opportunity right now, not sure if that company will want to use Allmoxy but TBD. He wants his big customers to use Allmoxy, but they might have hesitancy He wants them to order online but right now they send a drawing. Improvements Inventory tracking They have WebCab 2 to track parts through

CS Onboarding Playbook Is this a VIP/Legacy customer?(If you're unsure check with leadership) Yes Is our Services team being utilized for this build? Yes, Services owning entire build Who is the main POC within the customer? Joel Wollman Confirm the customers desired Outcome for using Allmoxy Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) When is the customers goal launch date? 03/31/2024 Notes: Sounds like they could be launched within the next month or so. What cadence of meeting schedule did you set with the customer? Notes: as needed. Working with services. Ask the customer to define their benchmark for success unknown What Software(s) does the customer have? How will Allmoxy work with these softwares? Unknown Is the customer looking to fit Allmoxy around an existing ordering system or is this their first?(Utilize your Services Rep to more deeply understand how this impacts the catalog build.) Notes: First for 2nd instance Any initial concerns with the customer and their ability to launch(personnel, resources available, understanding of product, etc?) Notes: not necessarily since they already have an instance

Launched Customer Playbook Does the customer define launched as running and processing orders through Allmoxy? If not what is their definition? Notes: Yes When did the customer officially "launch"? 05/19/2020 What has been the most difficult part of this customers onboarding experience? Other Notes: unknown Does the customer want to continue meeting on a regular weekly, biweekly or monthly basis? Notes: as needed What is next for the customer in terms of Allmoxy? If they don't know, what are you going to work towards with them?(Further feature adoption, another product line/services exports, machining data(Mechanlinks), getting their customers to use system, paid dev work, third party/API work, etc) Notes: Further feature adoption What is the customers health in your opinion? Green(this customer is in good/great standing and would advocate for Allmoxy - churn would be shocking) Could this customer be good to use for marketing Customer Testimonials/Case Studies?(Now or down the line - think personalities, how customer is using system, technical strengths, innovative thinking) Please tag Cheneil & Madison if so. Notes: yes

Internal Services Updates 2/9/24 Re-building catalog with proxys. They were a self set up so original catalog was not consistent. Aaron working with Joel on this and it has stalled but Aaron is reaching out continue the project. Does all of the woodworking for the Amish community. Family ties to get business for cabinetry, closets, prefab homes, etc. Joel is very busy due to this and patient with pace of how we work because he can't work too quickly. @Josh Baker for visibility
Adding a new product line with proxy Variables. Joel is going to review it after Aaron builds it.

engagements.title: Call with Courtney Hogue
engagements.body:
• Doesn't feel like there is a lot of support with pricing formulas, on the building side there is a lot of things that you have to go in depth to do. It is not a simple process. It could be the way it was set up on their end but Courtney isn't entirely sure. They had an issue with how their labor was calculated which created some frustration for them.

  • Overall she is trying to demand to make the system more user-friendly. They are having customers not want to touch it and have Bella fill out their orders for them. They are worried that they'll lose customers over it.


  • Hasn't seen a lot of game changers for their end to the customers' end in the last year that they have made it better or made it more user-friendly.


  • Hasn't heard from the CS team this year. Doesn't believe she ever heard back from Gavin back in July of last year. I didn't see that he replied to her last email but could have missed it.

  • None of the backend stuff makes sense to her. When she looks up the video on how to do pricing formulas, nothing explains what anything is from her perspective. She kept repeating that the system is not user-friendly. She has been using the training resources and still feels overwhelmed. If there is something that has changed to improve systems or processes or something that could be easier then she would appreciate the help.


Kenzi will loop Madi in and connect the two of them together. Because of their experience this last year and where they currently are standing they qualified for an increase in price. Initially pitched at $718 but dropped them to $658. Courtney felt valued at this price.

DO NOT DO THIS if this is a second instance and the churn does not completely eliminate their status as a customer. Other What's the catalog implementation status? Yes

@Beau Lewis I know there was a pause granted to Maxx back in December, but it looks like their subscription payments have not started back up, according to installer https://share1.allmoxy.com/v1uzKo55. Their payment required is turned on in Admin settings. Do you remember how long they wanted to pause for? Here was an email from Broc back in November Hi Max Distributors Team, I hope you are all well and enjoying your holidays. I received a note from Beau to contact you about a pause on the subscription. Our team is happy to assist in that request. I have Mekenzi, your account manger and Josh your Customer success manager, both here with us. Your last payment ran on Nov 20, I will ensure you won't see another charge per our stakeholder (Beau) approval for next month (December). To ensure any additional time be extended we suggest having a meeting with our team to understand exactly how long is needed to bridge a gap of time to support the Max Distributors team. Can we please get on a call soon to secure an understanding of what time you're needing and for what purposes so our team can support whatever motion is needed?

Meeting with Motty Gottlieb 3/20/25 Had some attribute questions, we covered those Their catalog is an absolute nightmare (long formulas, giant attribute tables, etc) Offered a scope for next week Scope scheduled We will give an estimate and then they will decide if they want to proceed

engagements.title: Meeting with Neil Storey from EuroContempo
engagements.body: Meeting with Neil Storey from EuroContempo

6/4/25 Catalog Meeting - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/EuroContempo-Allmoxy-Custom-Cabinet-Work::01JWVJCJD2J2GWK64W24SVVPYJ Agenda: Assembled pricing Finished Interiors Validations Corrected Next Steps? Ray to finish the assembled and finished interior pricing. Neil to review and confirm if the product is ready for cloning Determine how we want to tackle cloning Move onto the CV ORD export file configuration Notes: Question Is everything pricing correctly as is now? (Ray sharing) Assembled Questions Meeting Summary The team reviewed updates made to the Allmoxy cabinet configurator, focusing on assembly logic, UI validation improvements, and pricing calculations. Ray implemented several fixes based on issues shared earlier by Neil, particularly around selection inputs, pricing visibility, and configuration logic. Discussion also covered pricing structure for assembled cabinets, handling of hinge/glide types, inclusion of hardware and accessory options, and future enhancements such as cubic feet calculations for logistics. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Updated the “Assembled” input from a checkbox to a dropdown with clearer options: Flatpack and Assembled. Default state now requires explicit selection to avoid pricing omissions and configuration inconsistencies. Clarified terminology in UI: changed duplicate “Hinge Drilling” labels to Hinge Type and Glide Type for better end-user clarity. New accessory insert options (e.g., trash can pullouts, lazy susans) added to base products and tied to assembled state. Revised validation logic for door styles and vertical/horizontal selections—several now working correctly. Solid no grain option added for both doors and drawer fronts with corresponding validations. Allmoxy is set to calculate and show proper pricing based on current inputs and logic layers. Technical Issues & Fixes Validation was previously failing when no selection was made for "Assembled" type, resulting in pricing not appearing. 5-piece shaker was not allowing door match panel—fixed via style-based validation logic. Issue where “no door supplied” still triggered a recommendation error if cabinet width exceeded 36”—adjusted validation to factor in door status and hinge drilling. Ensured edge banding, construction method, and all panel logic are properly priced and validated. Added placeholder pricing for glide and hinge types, with logic to charge only if assembled is selected. Drawer boxes and rollouts now correctly account for quantity in pricing; assembly markup structure replicated from drawer catalog. Decisions Made Allmoxy users must now explicitly select Flatpack or Assembled—no default value allowed. Drawer box assembly will follow existing drawer catalog logic: flat rate per drawer regardless of size. Hinge and glide pricing are included only if cabinet is assembled. Leg levelers will be charged based on user selection regardless of assembly state. Assembled pricing includes: Cabinet flat rate per type Box multiplier based on type (e.g., base, tall) Assembly charge per drawer box Hinge/glide hardware cost (if assembled) Accessory inserts (if selected) Global cost table “Global Cost Cabinets” will control flat cabinet rate and multipliers for flexibility. Placement and sizing adjustments made to UI for improved readability and alignment. Challenges or Concerns Cubic feet calculation is currently not implemented but would be valuable for estimating delivery logistics and trailer space. Ray will test this feature outside of billable time. Some redundant field naming was causing confusion during internal configuration (e.g., hinge drilling naming). Action Items Ray to test and attempt to implement cubic feet calculation for assembled cabinets. Ensure validation forces selection of hinge and glide types to avoid incomplete orders. Add accessory inserts to standard products (not just clones) and ensure their pricing is tied to assembled status. Continue refining UI layout—especially room name and assembly dropdown widths—for clarity. Neil to test current version thoroughly and report any additional issues. Next Steps Neil to validate drawer, door, and cabinet configurations and pricing across different assembly states. Ray to monitor for any validation or logic gaps post-implementation. Finalize and fill in actual hinge, glide, drawer assembly, and cabinet rates in “Global Cost Cabinets” table. If cubic feet summation proves feasible, implement as a core order metric. Carson to distribute meeting recording for reference and future alignment.

05/12/25 Next Steps Meeting (Sarah, Ray, and Carson) https://app.fireflies.ai/view/EuroContempo-Allmoxy::01JTY7ME6BK9DTWPBK49A9J3GF Meeting Summary The call focused on aligning all stakeholders on the final requirements to bring Neil’s cabinet line product to launch readiness within Allmoxy. The group clarified the current project status, budget constraints, validation challenges, pricing alignment, and export needs. Participants acknowledged past complications stemming from changes in personnel (from Juan to Ray), overly complex configurations, and scope adjustments. The immediate goal is to finalize and test accurate pricing to ensure consistency with Neil’s spreadsheet system, then move forward with cloning and Cabinet Vision export preparation. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Scope Adjustments: Veneer and paint-grade doors are being removed from the product line to simplify user experience and reduce validation needs. Product Strategy: The cabinet box configuration is the key focus; door and drawer box setups were more complex and are now being streamlined. Pricing Structure: Flat pack vs. assembled options will be integrated into the pricing model via multipliers. Configuration Simplification: Reduced product lines and validations are aimed at minimizing user friction and streamlining catalog maintenance. Export Expectations: Cabinet Vision exports still need configuration; current door export (standard only) is partially complete. Technical Issues & Fixes Extensive configuration work was required due to the original complexity of Neil’s door and drawer setup. Retroactive integration and understanding of Juan’s original work created inefficiencies when Ray inherited the project. Validations caused recurring issues during pricing tests, especially with mixed materials (door, drawer, and cabinet box). Export functionality (especially for custom doors) remains incomplete. Decisions Made Continue with simplified cabinet product offering (lambda doors only) and flat pack vs. assembled construction. Ray will proceed with final pricing tweaks, targeting 3–4 hours of work, with the understanding it may expand to 6–8 hours due to validation refinements. Cloning is expected to be straightforward, with time primarily required for adjusting part quantities. Target remaining work scope is estimated at 10–15 hours, including validation, cloning, and export setup. Challenges or Concerns Budget Overrun: Neil expressed concern that project costs already exceed the original $12,000 quote, yet a final master product isn’t delivered. Scoping Clarity: Initial project scoping lacked technical detail, contributing to delays and rework. Expectations Management: Neil has been expecting completion for months, creating trust concerns and business impact. Maintenance Outlook: Neil is concerned about future ease of updating pricing and product configurations. Consultative Support: Neil feels Allmoxy has been too deferential rather than guiding him with best practices. Action Items Ray: Finalize pricing updates (glides, style/rail formulas, construction multipliers). Validate against Neil’s spreadsheet for direct price comparison. Sarah & Carson: Present updated scope and time estimate (15 hours) to leadership for consideration. Follow up with Neil regarding Cabinet Vision export requirements. Neil: Review current state and email Carson with any outstanding issues or confirmations. Team: Reconcile Neil’s original cabinet product list with current catalog to ensure complete offering. Prepare product cloning based on finalized master configuration. Next Steps Ray to complete pricing configuration and share comparison-ready product with Neil. Carson to follow up with Neil post-leadership review to confirm remaining steps and timeline. Begin Cabinet Vision export planning in parallel to final product cloning. Use feedback from this experience to inform more detailed future scoping and onboarding processes.

05/02/25 Cabinet Line Overview & Next Steps meeting - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/EuroContempo-Allmoxy-Cabinet-Catalog::01JT64SX45RHV73YNAYQCEVCHG Agenda: Recognize where the project currently stands and that every customization that has been requested has been added Neil, thanks for continuing to test and share your feedback. We’re here to ensure we’re aligned on where things stand, what the finish line looks like for you, and what we can do to get you there as efficiently as possible We understand this project started with a lot of moving parts, but to move forward, we need a shared definition of what ‘complete’ looks like to you so we can help you get there decisively. Most Recent Feedback/Testing Results Reference email Ray to take the conversation Meeting Summary This meeting focused on aligning perspectives between Neil, Ray, and Carson regarding the current cabinet product buildout in Allmoxy. The key objectives were to review and resolve Neil’s recent feedback on product logic and pricing inconsistencies, clarify what constitutes a “complete” state for the cabinet system, and determine a more maintainable strategy for managing door material options, validations, and assembly pricing. The team explored simplification strategies for handling TFL and veneer materials, introduced structural changes to the product setup, and discussed future scalability. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Carson emphasized that from his and Ray’s perspective, the product was nearly ready to clone, while Neil expressed concerns over lack of clarity and completeness. The team agreed to use this meeting as a reset to align on a shared definition of “done” and finalize steps to reach it. Ray confirmed the product is close to functional completion, with some minor validations and logic updates pending. Neil outlined his testing process and revealed discrepancies in expected cabinet pricing when combining box, drawer, rollout, and door components. Ray confirmed that validation conflicts were causing issues and will be resolved quickly. The group discussed the challenge of managing a large list of door materials, especially given the company’s business model of offering extensive customization. Technical Issues & Fixes Ray acknowledged that validation errors and conflicting logic caused incorrect or missing pricing in certain scenarios. Proposed solution: separate TFL and veneer materials into distinct tables to reduce validation complexity and improve maintainability. Cabinet products will be simplified to only support TFL materials, eliminating underused veneer and HDM options. Complex door pricing logic has been retained and replicated within the cabinet structure without proxy reuse to ensure accuracy. Assembly vs. flat pack logic will be integrated via “Box Construction” dropdown with pricing modifiers and validations accordingly. Assembly charges can be flat-rate, multiplier-based, or hybrid, and will be customizable per cabinet type or category. Decisions Made Eliminate hardwood veneer and HDM cabinet options from the primary product line. Create a new veneer wood type table for veneer doors and reroute applicable door products to it. Cabinet logic will rely on the current wood type table, which will be purged of veneer/HDM rows once updates are made. Introduce assembly pricing logic using both multipliers and flat fees, adjustable by cabinet category/type. Use Box Construction as the selection point for assembly vs. flat pack, with logic-driven pricing and validations. Accessories will only be available for fully assembled cabinets, with pricing and labor installation included per selection. Specialized cabinets (e.g., farm sink, microwave) will require custom input fields for opening height/dimensions. Challenges or Concerns Neil raised long-term maintainability issues stemming from dynamic changes to TFL material offerings by suppliers. Large attribute tables and the need for frequent overrides led to the decision to split material tables. Concern over customer confusion due to too many dropdown options and validation errors if all materials are shown. Assembly pricing needs to accommodate variability in cabinet complexity without overcomplicating setup. Action Items Neil will: Finalize and reassign veneer and HDM door products to the new “veneer wood type” table. Delete veneer/HDM rows from the current wood type table post reassignment. Determine a strategy for assigning assembly multipliers and flat charges for each cabinet category/type. Ray will: Implement validation fixes for current logic issues (especially door and drawer pricing logic). Override cabinet material selections to restrict to TFL options only. Add and test Box Construction logic with dropdowns for “Flat Pack” and “Fully Assembled” selections. Set up assembly pricing formulas (multiplier, flat fee) based on Neil’s forthcoming inputs. Ensure accessory input and pricing logic is active only for assembled cabinet selections. Add logic to calculate leg leveler quantity based on cabinet width. Next Steps Neil to notify the team once the door product updates and table cleanups are complete. Ray to begin validation and logic updates as outlined and monitor for any emerging inconsistencies. Carson to facilitate status check-ins and ensure continued alignment as components are finalized. The team will reconvene or communicate as needed after Neil submits the assembly pricing model.

04/25/25 - Escalation Call - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/EuroContempo-Project-Overview::01JSMMC3HD2N5PHEYH99FC70HX Meeting Summary Carson Ward met with the client to discuss the status and challenges of the cabinet and door catalog project. The client expressed concern over project delays, unexpected costs, and the long-term manageability of the system being developed. Both parties acknowledged the complexity of the product offerings and the need for clearer guidance, tighter collaboration, and a more sustainable path forward. Plans were made to escalate the issues internally at Allmoxy and schedule a meeting with broader team involvement to resolve outstanding challenges. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Client feels the project has been complicated due to the uniqueness of their cabinet and door offerings (600+ material SKUs, exclusively engineered products). Original door products required significant rework due to prior build issues with a third party (Ogman) and limited initial consultation on system design. Concerns were raised about how the door build complexity has negatively impacted the cabinet catalog project. There was an overall lack of consultation during the build, where the client repeatedly sought advice but received minimal feedback. Carson acknowledged that Allmoxy’s earlier implementation structure (third-party builders, lack of regular consultative meetings) contributed to project challenges. Current cabinet form is functional for box input and pricing but still lacks validations and deeper functionality testing. Technical Issues & Fixes Duplication of tables (e.g., box materials, edge banding) is creating future manageability concerns. Lack of dynamic table filtering (e.g., selecting a door type should filter available materials) identified as a missing feature, requiring validation rules as a workaround. Client’s complicated door pricing structure likely made the cabinet build more difficult. Pricing fixes for cabinet boxes are now matching the client’s spreadsheets exactly after recent adjustments by Ray. Material update processes for frequently changing materials (e.g., Egger, Tafisa) are identified as a future maintenance burden without a streamlined system. Decisions Made Carson will escalate the project concerns to his manager (Madison) and the head of implementation (Aaron). A follow-up meeting is to be scheduled with Carson, Ray, Aaron, and the client to: Recalibrate the project plan. Identify critical remaining tasks. Explore solutions for long-term manageability and potential simplification of the catalog structure. Focus on finishing the flat stack cabinet input form and assessing the opportunity to seamlessly adapt it for fully assembled cabinets as well. Challenges or Concerns Project has exceeded the original budget estimate ($13,000+ spent vs. $12,000 initial estimate) and the client estimates they are only halfway complete. Client’s shops are waiting for the system to launch; currently relying on manual Excel spreadsheets for quoting. Managing multiple separate material tables may cause significant operational overhead in the future. Lack of proactive feedback and strategy discussions over the course of the project have hurt project confidence. Action Items Carson to escalate concerns to Allmoxy management immediately. Carson to coordinate internally with Ray and Aaron for a deeper technical and operational review. Prepare an agenda for a new client meeting focused on: Remaining technical validations and completions. Long-term catalog management strategy. Potential restructuring or simplification where possible. Carson to ensure next steps offer a clear timeline and resource plan to the client. Next Steps Carson to meet internally with Madison and Aaron within the next few days. Set up a follow-up meeting with the client, Carson, Ray, and Aaron to realign on project goals and delivery expectations. Develop a clear and realistic project timeline including milestones for validations, launch readiness, and future catalog maintenance strategies. Present potential options to simplify material management (consolidation of tables, automation strategies if possible).

04/25/25 - 1:1 sync with Neil Agenda: Project overview/review? Talk about the status of the project curious about - from my perspective, would we consider this an overly challenging. Feels like this has been a theme throughout his time in working with Allmoxy. (our cabinet product is just enough different then the template so they're trying to force a fit) Frustration - from day 1, this is my product, how does your system work to input my product. Next Steps Revise the door products (needs to understand the logic of what Augmon built and how it can be optimized) Maintenance - how is he going to maintain this at scale (validations, selections, tables)

04/22/25 - Cabinet Catalog Review Session - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/EuroContempo-Allmoxy-Cabinet-Pricing-Review::01JSAQP2KN32TCQZTD4PR0E93S Agenda: Neil's feedback items Door type attribute - Default should be "select one" and first option should be none supplied Door type and drawer front type should have default "select one" and validation to make the user make a selection. Validation errors - trying to create a box with no boxes, doors, or drawer fronts but getting validations firiting. Pricing - $152.10 for this box but Neil is seeing that it should be 123.50. Gameplan: Confirm the pricing and selections are working as expected Determine if we want to start on the export or if Neil would like to build out the rest of the cabinet line products Export - Get Ray whatever information he needs to build the outline for the export Schedule follow up testing meeting for the export to do live troubleshooting Here’s the structured HubSpot-ready meeting summary based on the transcript: 📝 Meeting Summary Purpose: The meeting focused on refining the cabinet pricing formula within Allmoxy by aligning it more closely with the client’s real-world cost expectations. Ray, Carson, and Neil collaborated to review discrepancies, discuss formula adjustments, and determine next steps for replicating and scaling accurate pricing across different cabinet types. Context: Due to recent inconsistencies in email-driven changes and unclear documentation, the team emphasized the need for a standardized baseline model to streamline future modifications. The meeting included a detailed walkthrough of how the system calculates cabinet box and edge banding costs, highlighting areas requiring correction. ✅ Key Takeaways Important Discussions Baseline Establishment: Ray and Carson agreed on starting from an example provided by Neil rather than building from scratch, to avoid misalignment and wasted effort. Change Tracking: The team acknowledged that recent change communications have lacked clarity, reinforcing the need to "lock in" specifications before implementing updates. Technical Issues & Fixes Pricing Calculation Discrepancies: Initial price mismatch identified: Allmoxy showed ~$108 vs. Neil’s expected ~$123.50 (including markup and edge banding). Issue traced to missing top deck calculations and improper markup applications. Edge Banding Formula Errors: Original formulas underestimated footage (e.g., 0.4 ft vs. expected 9.08 ft for both cabinet sides). Adjustments made to account for both long and short edges, correcting footage calculations. Stretcher & Nailer Logic: Neil clarified stretchers are rarely used in their construction, except drawer stretchers and specific nailers for floating sink cabinets. Ray will disable edge banding calculations for nailers and unnecessary stretchers unless otherwise required. Material Cost Referencing: Incorrect reference to Allmoxy’s cost tables for back materials was replaced with customer-specific values for consistency. Spreadsheet Integration: Neil provided an updated spreadsheet with several cabinet scenarios and parts usage highlighted for clear modeling and formula verification. Decisions Made Use a hybrid approach—retain some Allmoxy formulas but override with Neil's where exact values are required. Confirmed that formulas do not need to match exactly to the penny, as long as margins are within an acceptable range (preferably slightly overestimated). Edge banding calculations across all parts will be revised using the customer’s quantity logic. Challenges or Concerns Need to maintain simplicity while ensuring accuracy—balancing exact custom calculations with system efficiency. Formulas must adapt to variable component use across cabinet types (e.g., which parts are toggled on/off per design). 📌 Action Items Owner Task Deadline / Notes Ray Finalize edge banding formulas using Neil's spreadsheet logic. In progress Ray Adjust material cost referencing to ensure consistent pricing across materials. Immediate Ray Disable pricing logic for stretchers and nailers where not applicable. Immediate Neil Review the updated pricing once Ray implements the changes. Awaiting notification from Carson Carson Notify Neil once the updates are implemented and ready for validation. Post-implementation Team Discuss responsibilities for cloning cabinet types after pricing validation. Next meeting 🔮 Next Steps Testing: Once Ray completes updates, Carson will alert Neil to conduct testing across various cabinet scenarios using the new spreadsheet. Cloning Product Types: If pricing is validated, the team will proceed to clone cabinet variations (base, sink, wall, tall) and configure relevant parts per design. Responsibility Review: Neil may handle cloning on his end if system setup allows; otherwise, Ray may assist depending on implementation complexity. Ongoing Adjustments: Any exceptional edge banding cases (e.g., floating sink cabinets) will be handled with conditional logic or manual toggles as needed. Let me know if you'd like this exported into a shared doc or formatted for internal wiki/reference.

04/17/25 - Cabinet Catalog - "hopefully finishing" - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/EuroContemp-Allmoxy-Cabinet-Catalog-Review::01JRGBW5KRRMK3M5PJFENHCAF2 Meeting Summary This meeting focused on aligning the current box pricing formulas within Allmoxy's system with Carson’s updated cost calculations from a shared spreadsheet. Carson, Ray, and Neil reviewed the differences between existing system logic and Carson’s spreadsheet-driven approach. The conversation centered on how parts, edge banding, waste yields, material markups, and shop rates are being calculated, with a goal of standardizing logic and ensuring more accurate pricing across products. There was also discussion around the need for a separate global cost structure for cabinet products, as the existing one is optimized for doors and drawer fronts. A follow-up meeting is scheduled to validate changes and test the hybridized pricing logic. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Current Pricing Logic Overview: Ray explained how Allmoxy’s current system calculates box pricing based on square footage per part (e.g., left end, top, deck) multiplied by material cost and waste yield. Spreadsheet vs. System: Carson clarified that his spreadsheet simplifies adjustable shelf calculation by factoring only 25% of their square footage for shop rate costs due to minimal labor involved. Edge Banding Specifics: Carson emphasized that edge banding on cabinet sides (left/right) must account for both face and bottom edges (i.e., both long and short axes), differing from Allmoxy's current single-axis logic. Static Dimensions: Some parts like stretchers and nailers have fixed dimensions regardless of cabinet size and need to be hardcoded in the system. Component Relevance by Cabinet Type: Carson outlined which parts apply to specific cabinet types (e.g., base, wall, tall), highlighted in his spreadsheet. Technical Issues & Fixes Edge Banding Logic Gaps: Currently missing markup and waste yield on edge banding. Needs to be modified to match spreadsheet logic and pull correct values from the global cost table. Shop Rate Logic: Not currently implemented in the Allmoxy formula. Needs to reflect square footage-based rates and linear edge banding multipliers (e.g., 1.0 for material, 0.25 for adjustable shelves, and 0.4 for edge banding). Global Cost Structure Conflict: The existing global cost table is used for door products. To avoid attribute conflicts, a separate “Global Cost - Cabinets” table will be created for base, wall, and tall cabinets with their respective default values. Decisions Made Proceed with creating a new cabinet-specific global cost attribute to handle separate pricing logic. Adjust the box pricing formulas to: Include shop rate calculations. Incorporate dual-axis edge banding where needed. Add missing waste and markup logic to edge banding. Continue using the spreadsheet as a reference to validate Allmoxy logic alignment. Challenges or Concerns The current system can’t apply two sets of cost logic simultaneously due to attribute conflicts (e.g., door vs. cabinet multipliers). Ensuring all edge banded components are captured and processed properly, including accounting for items that should never be banded (e.g., grayed-out parts). Action Items Owner Task Deadline/Status Ray Add edge banding waste yield and markup logic. Before Tuesday's meeting Ray Integrate adjustable shelf calculation (25% of SF). Before Tuesday's meeting Ray Create and configure new "Global Cost - Cabinets" attribute. Before Tuesday's meeting Ray Match cabinet pricing logic to spreadsheet (shop rate, markup, fixed dimensions, dual-axis banding). In progress Carson Send calendar invite for Tuesday afternoon review meeting. Done Ray Validate default values for each cabinet type (base, wall, tall). Before next meeting Next Steps Testing: Ray will complete the formula updates and test the hybrid model. Validation: Review updated pricing logic against Carson’s spreadsheet in the next meeting. Structure Finalization: Ensure all components and multipliers (shop rate, edge banding, global cost) are aligned and appropriately referenced per cabinet type. Continued Review: Follow up to ensure changes are effectively implemented and accurate before broader deployment.

04/07/25 - Cabinet Catalog - Clarifying Session Agenda: Neil's list of what needs to be addressed with the current product setup, see email: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/4910812/company/1129301720/?engagement=76621386621 Recording: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/EuroContempo-Cabinet-Catalog-Sync::01JR43Q465XMENWMEFZBY9ZWTG Meeting Summary The meeting was held to review and finalize structural and pricing configurations for cabinet and drawer products in Allmoxy. Carson facilitated a working session between Neil and Ray to streamline communication and clarify implementation strategies. Neil had submitted a list of configuration and pricing concerns ahead of the call, which served as the agenda. The conversation focused on resolving technical attribute issues, setting up validations, and aligning product data with pricing logic. There was a strong focus on maintaining back-end clarity while simplifying front-end user experience. A follow-up meeting was proposed for April 17th to review final configurations and confirm pricing logic. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Attribute Configuration Strategy: Ray will break out certain shared attributes (e.g., door style and rail width) into new attributes to avoid conflicts with existing catalog elements. The new attributes will be customized without affecting the current product lines. Box Construction: Adjustable leg leveler height options (e.g., 3.5", 4", 4.5") will be added to improve flexibility. Lockdown construction tables will be consolidated to centralize pricing updates. Attribute Mapping Clarification: Ray will provide a cheat sheet showing which attributes control pricing, streamlining updates. Existing attribute mappings were reviewed to ensure correct connections between dropdowns and pricing tables. Technical Issues & Fixes Door Material Validation: Allmoxy currently lacks dynamic attribute linking. Ray will implement table-based validations using Neil’s provided door material table. Validation will be based on columns like door_TFL, veneer, and HDF, with logic that throws errors if a material is not valid (e.g., marked as 99). Grain Direction Options: New dropdowns will be added for door and drawer grain direction: Solid – No Grain, Vertical Panel, Horizontal Panel. These selections will not affect pricing but are essential for accurate Cabinet Vision exports. Decisions Made Use centralized attribute tables for easier management of leg levelers and lockdown construction pricing. Implement cheat sheet for pricing attributes to reduce confusion and user error. Split door and drawer grain direction into two new dropdowns for better clarity and export control. Separate attributes will be created when shared tables cause unintended overlap across product lines. Challenges or Concerns Adding dropdowns may increase complexity for end users, but was deemed necessary for flexibility and precision. Ensuring validations are intuitive and don't overwhelm users is a key focus. Care must be taken when reusing attributes across products to avoid breaking catalog logic. Action Items Action Owner Notes Due Date Break out shared attributes (e.g., door style/rail width) into new custom attributes Ray Ensure no cross-contamination with existing catalog Before April 17 Create and share a pricing attribute cheat sheet Ray Clarify what drives pricing across dropdowns Before April 17 Implement validations using door material table Ray Use columns like TFL, veneer, HDF with 1 or 99 logic Before April 17 Add dropdowns for grain direction (door and drawer) Ray Include "Solid No Grain", "Vertical", and "Horizontal" options Before April 17 Build pricing logic for rollout shelves Neil Add lockdown components and update existing pricing Before April 17 Build spreadsheet showing current pricing logic Neil To compare with Allmoxy pricing formulas during final review Before April 17 Next Steps Schedule follow-up meeting for April 17 at 8:30 AM to review: Finalized product structure Pricing formulas Validation logic Neil to prepare a pricing logic walkthrough spreadsheet for comparison during the meeting. Once finalized, begin product cloning and rollout of the new configuration structure.

02/28/25 Cabinet Catalog Line - Review Session Agenda: What needs to be reviewed & confirmed Drawer box pricing - working as expected? Edge-banding and reveals setup - good to go? Additional items - What you both have been going back and forth on. Separate project Door materials referencing the same table - need to be handled later on as we need to focus on the original project scope. Notes: Reveals - Good to keep them in the current location Door type - No door option - Want them to have that option Alert the customer with validation for hinge drilling Missing attributes Box construction Box material Drawer boxes (Ray displaying these and doing the reorganization) Flat stack vs fully assembled https://app.fireflies.ai/view/EuroContempo-Allmoxy-Cabinet-Project-Review::01JN45N9CE21BH3YCY9E2RXTSY

01/21/25 Cabinet Catalog Line - Check-in & Review Meeting - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/EuroContempo-Allmoxy-Cabinet-Catalog-Progress-Review::JmFHVZXL1EOR7C2n Agenda: Ray walk Neil through the items he's completed so far Get confirmation on anything that needs to be adjusted Get approval to continue work to prepare for actual testing Notes: Long term goal - Ready to install cabinet line First piece Pricing - focused on getting the drawers and door pricing of neils original products to match what the pricing calculation for the new cabinet line. Door type Occasionally sells cabinets with shaker doors and slab drawer fronts Stile/rail & Stile width group and line item attributes Need to show the stile width for the formulas to work - use validations to correct incorrect entrees by customer. Action items Split out door and drawer front selections so customers can choose different options for both Panel options & door material - still need validations Stile/Rail width attribute - add a "no" option Panel wide and high - need validation 3pc door - remove the stile width attribute and combine with the stile/rail width Glass ready & glass option - hid and only display for the wall cabinets Share with Neil any recommendations for making the calculations/formulas easier to maintain or update. Validation list for door material and door type. Add the drawer box selections ( MDF or upgrade for baltic birch) Option for (no drawer fronts, boxes, or doors) Drawers Would need to be included at some point - should we include these now? Need to correct the pricing - currently if quantity of 2 is entered it doubles the price and not the subtotal. Once this formula is corrected the drawer box selections need to be added to the cabinet line.

Cabinet Line Scoping Meeting Notes 11/20 Notes: Scoping spreadsheet: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1FrmO1lbto1-jNEQFpbuf9M3zs66ARcsDfcKwj8zupZI/edit?gid=0#gid=0 Lives close to black desert Cost per cubic foot - Seems to remember he was told this could be used and was confused by this. Feels much more comfortable with the cost per square foot. Customer options Neil wants to offer: Just box full kit cabinet Export - wants an export or output page that shows the dimensions of what the doors and drawer fronts need to be. Estimate: 80 hours $150/hr = $12,000 Timeframe: 10 weeks (considering current workload) Start at 5ish hours each week and ramp up as other project are completed Neil: Wants to do the hybrid approach, feels comfortable making changes and updates on his end as long as he has instruction Meeting Recording: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/EuroContempo-Allmoxy-Cabinet-Catalog-Scope::ur2R4hCuQwUeWOvi

Regrouping Meetings - Launch Attempt 2nd Meeting 04/10/24 Agenda: Meeting with Juan - how'd it go? Confirm Shipping & financial settings Triggers Confirm who should be notified of what, and when. Account creation Contact creation order submitted payment submitted Order verified In progress? Completed Shipped Price Adjustments How you can add surcharges or discounts for certain products or customers. Don't need to add anything now but good to know for the future. Tags How you can group things together for shipping, price adjustments, triggers, customers, companies, and orders. Would recommend include tagging customers in your account creation trigger if you're limiting shipping etc. Customer Creation & Editing Your customers should create their own account You can create customer accounts for them How to edit a customer's permissions & account settings Output pages Review the default setup of the invoice and packing slip. What other output pages do you need for your shop operations? Next Steps Work through output pages and triggers. Schedule the next meeting to continue working through these items until completion. Notes: Shipping set up for now Good enough to get started with for now Financial settings Current speculation - thinks people will be paying for their order up front. Trigger https://eurocontempo.allmoxy.com/settings/triggers/edit/6/ - Still needed tex box editing Text message notifications - why aren't there any employees selectable. Tyson's user name: tysongarritson 1st meeting 04/08/24 Context: Neil went cold in October after our first meeting. Their catalog should be complete other than their cabinet vision export. Need to have Neil review the catalog to see if things look accurate, have Juan build the export, and then work on steps to start utilizing Allmoxy. Agenda: Current Status Services team completed phase 1 of the build except for the CV exports. Neil needs to review and test the catalog to ensure pricing is correct so we can move on to the exports. Ready to start back up?   What's your timeline and bandwidth look like right now? Is there a goal date we have to start utilizing this for order processing? Rolling this out to customers or internally first? What's needed to get started? Catalog review & training with Juan - how to adjust material cost, supplies, etc Juan build the cabinet vision export Shipping? - Your own truck, freight, will call? Financial settings - Pay before processing or credit limits? Triggers, Tags, and Adjustments Internal operation process - order entry, verification, etc. Next Steps Schedule a recurring meeting to put us on a path to launching Share what can be completed on Neil's end Get a training session scheduled with Juan Notes: Main thing - needs next steps and needs modifications made to the build out. Credit limit - Bring this back up, check in with Neil. Wants people to put a 50% deposit down for payments. Shipping Typically uses their own truck Their shipping will most likely be based on Distance (choose the max distance they're willing to go) Neil wants to get launched as quickly as possible Meeting with Juan tomorrow to go over small adjustments he needs made for their catalog, then he'll need to create their export.
