engagements.title: Churned customer re-engagement consideration
engagements.body: See Churn playbook and retention notes for full context. For more information reach out to the former assigned CSM.

Churn Playbook Please select all of the reasons that apply for why the customer is churning. Add any additional churn context in the note - the more the better here. If there's escalation notes that exist on the account be sure to reference them and include the data of the escalation note in the playbook context. Features, Moved to Other Solution Notes: Key Takeaways Important Discussions Kurt confirmed that they will be switching back to Drawer Lister due to unresolved challenges with Allmoxy’s production and reporting tools. Key frustration stemmed from the plant schedule and lack of accurate daily totals, which hindered delivery planning and overloaded their delivery vehicles. Communication breakdowns were highlighted — notably, there was no contact from Allmoxy between May 21st and July 28th, despite unresolved issues. Kurt emphasized that while Allmoxy’s front-end order entry experience is strong, the back-end manufacturing support falls short for their needs. Kurt's team had to outsource shop sheets, which led to confusion and inefficiency on the production floor. Complexity and customization of their products made implementation difficult — Zach had done extensive development that was not fully supported by Allmoxy’s engineering team. Allmoxy acknowledged these issues and shared that the internal development focus had shifted this summer away from key manufacturing features. Sarah and Josh acknowledged Allmoxy had not met expectations but reaffirmed their commitment to developing these features for future benefit. Technical Issues & Fixes Production scheduling and plant schedule functionality were lacking and caused daily operational inefficiencies. Shop sheet generation was insufficient — required third-party development, resulting in confusion and limited adoption on the production floor. Back-end limitations undermined the value of the system despite strong order entry capabilities. Invoice and acknowledgment formatting issues led to customer backlash and loss of clients due to unclear or incorrect order information. Lack of order summation and total calculation tools hindered efficient planning. Change the Customer Lifecycle property to "Other" and create a task for Kylie to review after 1 year. Customer

Big Valley Wood Products (cancellation meeting 11/19/25) Meeting Summary This meeting served as a cancellation discussion between Kurt’s team and Allmoxy, represented by Sarah Pierce and Josh Baker. Kurt’s team expressed significant frustration over key production and reporting features that Allmoxy did not support, prompting their decision to revert to Drawer Lister. Sarah and Josh acknowledged the issues, clarified internal development prioritization problems, and expressed a strong interest in retaining the relationship in the future. The conversation remained respectful, with both sides agreeing to keep communication open and data preserved for a potential return. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Kurt confirmed that they will be switching back to Drawer Lister due to unresolved challenges with Allmoxy’s production and reporting tools. Key frustration stemmed from the plant schedule and lack of accurate daily totals, which hindered delivery planning and overloaded their delivery vehicles. Communication breakdowns were highlighted — notably, there was no contact from Allmoxy between May 21st and July 28th, despite unresolved issues. Kurt emphasized that while Allmoxy’s front-end order entry experience is strong, the back-end manufacturing support falls short for their needs. Kurt's team had to outsource shop sheets, which led to confusion and inefficiency on the production floor. Complexity and customization of their products made implementation difficult — Zach had done extensive development that was not fully supported by Allmoxy’s engineering team. Allmoxy acknowledged these issues and shared that the internal development focus had shifted this summer away from key manufacturing features. Sarah and Josh acknowledged Allmoxy had not met expectations but reaffirmed their commitment to developing these features for future benefit. Technical Issues & Fixes Production scheduling and plant schedule functionality were lacking and caused daily operational inefficiencies. Shop sheet generation was insufficient — required third-party development, resulting in confusion and limited adoption on the production floor. Back-end limitations undermined the value of the system despite strong order entry capabilities. Invoice and acknowledgment formatting issues led to customer backlash and loss of clients due to unclear or incorrect order information. Lack of order summation and total calculation tools hindered efficient planning. Decisions Made Kurt’s team will officially cancel Allmoxy by December 20, 2025, with the final billing to run on November 20, 2025, covering service through December 20. Allmoxy will store all customer data for one year post-cancellation in case the team decides to return. Team agreed not to burn bridges and to revisit a potential partnership if and when backend improvements are implemented. Challenges or Concerns Lack of proactive communication and updates from Allmoxy significantly impacted customer trust. Internal disconnect within Allmoxy between customer success advocacy and development prioritization. Customers like Kurt’s team felt neglected during a critical implementation phase, which resulted in them having to sacrifice efficiency and customer satisfaction. Allmoxy’s engineering team lacks consistency in experience and domain expertise, which affected the buildout for specialty manufacturing customers. The product is not currently viable for manufacturers with complex, custom backend workflows. Action Items Josh to send Kurt's team the cancellation form with optional detail submission. Allmoxy to store and preserve customer data for up to one year post-cancellation. Sarah to share parts of the meeting with leadership and development team as feedback and to advocate for prioritizing missing features. Allmoxy team to update Kurt's team if meaningful reporting, scheduling, and backend improvements are implemented within the next 4–6 weeks. Next Steps Kurt’s team to complete the cancellation form and finalize billing for final access through December 20, 2025. Allmoxy will reach out to demonstrate improvements, especially in order summaries and backend production tools, if completed within the next 4–6 weeks. Both teams agreed to reconnect at the next industry trade show (IWF in Georgia or Texas) and reassess partnership potential. Potential third implementation attempt in the future if Allmoxy delivers on missing backend capabilities.

Retention note 11/12/25 Reached out to BVM in August to renew their contract (no increase) Current rate: 1,999 Proposed rate: 1,999 for one year Kurt (owner) said that he would have to get back to me in October I followed up at the end of the month about the contract. Kurt asked about the remaining hours left from implementation (I thought he was going to try to get a reduced rate based on hours left, but we never charged him for a bank of hours) Kurt emailed this morning saying that "unfortunately, they will not be renewing their Allmoxy contract and want to cancel at the end of the month." Between November 2024 and November 2025, they have done around 2.6 million in verified revenue through Allmoxy. B2B Drawer Box customer whom Steve Harmon referred Waiting on this feature (Order Total Summations), but it has taken a back seat for Allmoxy Lite They are using the system fully, but they are still entering orders themselves (this is how they wanted it) Juan and Zach (Kurt's son) did a hybrid build that went well. Some IMP issues but we offered them one free meeting /week, one billable meeting /week until they were running orders. Not sure if this is a serious cancellation request or an attempt to get a lower rate. According to Bobby's new customer payment chart, they are paying less than the 2-3 million rate. Scheduling a retention meeting with Kurt next week if possible

Big Valley Wood 4/3/25 Meeting Summary This meeting served as a final planning and alignment session to support the customer's transition from their legacy system to Allmoxy, targeting full cutover by April 18. The team reviewed critical checklist items related to batch order workflows, output formatting, and visibility needs for production. A primary goal was to design a workflow that replaces the customer's current plant schedule with a custom batch export in Allmoxy, ensuring it is easy to use, totals drawer counts, and hides pricing for floor visibility. Discussions also included label setup, order report customization, timestamp issues, and output page sorting. Additional enhancements, such as implementing a code switcher for output page types and reusing formatted HTML, were explored to maintain consistency. Key Takeaways Important Discussions The customer's legacy system is being phased out, with complete retirement scheduled for April 18. Production is currently running in parallel. The current plant schedule is a printed report used by all departments. The team aims to replicate this in Allmoxy using the "Batch Orders" tool and a custom CSV export. The batch report provides line-level detail and drawer piece counts, but lacks a total count summary, which is essential for planning. This solution should eliminate the need for the "Scheduled Capacity" feature. Technical Issues & Fixes The batch orders view does not currently total drawer counts; this may be addressed with a CSV export or UI enhancement (e.g., pop-up total). The order report reveals pricing, which needs to be hidden for production use. Output page sorting by height/width is inconsistent. Items do not appear in the same order as entered. Timestamps on internal notes show a 4-hour discrepancy, likely due to theme- or user-level time zone settings. The ID fields on output pages are confusing and inconsistently ordered. Some IDs correspond with original line entries; others do not. Decisions Made A custom CSV export for batch orders will be built by the engineering team. Deadline for CSV readiness is April 16 to support the full launch on April 18. The export will include additional attributes to mirror the current plant schedule. The customer will no longer pursue schedule capacity if the batch report/export solution works as intended. Output pages will use the same formatting by duplicating HTML and adjusting titles dynamically with code switchers. Challenges or Concerns No immediate way to total drawer counts in the batch report UI — CSV export is a workaround but introduces a delay. Sorting inconsistencies and ID confusion in output reports may create fulfillment and printing errors. Timestamp misalignment could affect audit trails or communication. Need to ensure labels and output formatting are clean, duplicable, and avoid rework across products. Action Items Action Owner Deadline Build and deliver custom batch CSV export with all required attributes Engineering (via Sarah) April 16 Confirm data fields on export match current printed plant schedule Sarah ASAP Report sorting inconsistencies and ID mismatches to developers Josh / Sarah ASAP Confirm time zone settings at user and theme levels Josh / Sarah ASAP Send original order forms and acknowledgments for jobs 95 & 102 Kurt ASAP Schedule Stripe/Saison setup session Josh Scheduled for April 8, 3–4pm ET Share sizes for merch order Josh / Sarah Optional Next Steps Sarah will review and confirm the plant schedule via email. Engineering will develop and deliver the batch CSV export. Josh will set up a follow-up session with Aaron for live CSV testing. Zach and Kurt will verify usability of the CSV export as a full replacement for the current plant report. Josh and Sarah will monitor sorting and timestamp bug reports. Output page formatting will be standardized using HTML source and code switchers. Team will reconvene after implementation to assess workflow improvements and readiness for customer onboarding. If the batch export resolves core issues, the scheduled capacity feature will no longer be pursued. The team is also prepping for Stripe/QuickBooks invoicing workflow validation on April 8 with real invoice data. Title: Saasant Mapping with Dena Date: Fri Dec 06 2024 15:30:00 GMT-0500 (Eastern Standard Time) Participants: kurt.stauffer@bigvalleywoodproducts.com, joshb@allmoxy.com, sarah@allmoxy.com, dena@bigvalleywoodproducts.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 46 mins Overview In the Technical Implementation and Training meeting focused on Saasant mapping with Dena, Sarah and Zach collaborated to set up Saasant for QuickBooks Desktop integration, addressing initial challenges related to file downloads and permissions. The team walked through the mapping of Almoxy fields to QuickBooks fields, specifically discussing key elements like invoice numbers, customers, dates, and sales tax, while troubleshooting issues with the PA sales tax code. Further refinements were made to the mapping process, examining taxable versus non-taxable items and checking the import functionality with varying configurations. Ultimately, they successfully demonstrated the import of invoices from Almoxy to QuickBooks, ensuring data accuracy and outlining regular maintenance procedures. Action items included Dena committing to import invoices and conduct monthly spot checks, while Sarah was tasked with clearing test invoices from the export queue. Notes 🖥️ Setting Up Saasant Sarah and Zach start setting up Saasant for QuickBooks Desktop integration They encounter issues with file download and permissions Team discusses the need to link Saasant to QuickBooks 📊 Mapping Almoxy to QuickBooks Sarah guides Zach through mapping Almoxy fields to QuickBooks fields They discuss various fields including invoice number, customer, date, and sales tax Team encounters issues with sales tax mapping 🔍 Troubleshooting Tax Code Issues Team investigates why PA sales tax code is not recognized in QuickBooks They discuss the differences between tax codes and tax items in QuickBooks Sarah and Zach attempt different mappings for tax-related fields 🛠️ Refining the Mapping Process Team continues to adjust mappings for tax-related fields They test the import process with different configurations Discussion on how to handle taxable and non-taxable items ✅ Finalizing and Testing the Integration Sarah and Zach successfully import invoices from Almoxy to QuickBooks They verify that imported data matches the original Almoxy data Team discusses the process for regular use and maintenance of the integration Action Items Dena Regularly import invoices from Almoxy to QuickBooks using the established process Spot check one invoice monthly to ensure accuracy of the integration Sarah Clear test invoices from the Almoxy export queue to prevent accidental re-sending

Onboarding #5 with Big Valley 12/4/24 Pricing terms solution Enabled cash discount and set it at 2% When you get their check > pull up their company page > open order Acknowledgment > check the ordered date If it is within the 10 days, apply the 2% cash discount Shipping Flat rates for customers in certain states? Are we still doing this? They have every first and 3rd Tuesday of the month is Virginia Lead time Is 5 business days good? Triggers S Lead time Individual or business days? Options company exclusive? Follow up on capacity 240 orders a day. If they hit that capacity do can it go to the next shipping cycle?

Onboarding #4 with Big Valley Team Checklist of things they has set up Theme (yes) Add employees/ customers (yes) Set up Stripe? (yes) Basic trigger setup (no) Production lines (no) Test order Financial order settings (determine when your customers pay) Default checkout flow (use credit limit or pay before processing) You can set this globally or per customer If some customers refuse to do pay before processing, you can change their individual checkout flow Credit limit is extending a certain amount of money to place an order, and set up payment terms (pay when they ship or the order is marked complete) Order limit/ build limit: the amount of product you will build but NOT necessarily ship to your customer. You set those terms They are going to do credit terms 2% discount if they pay for the order within 10 days of shipping OR full amount in 30 days If they don't pay they will start charging interest. Default interest rate- if you are doing terms and they haven't paid by the end of the month, a fee is attached and will charge them a finance charge. Daily is every day something is past due, it attaches a fee Sales tax presence Where are you doing business, just AZ or regional? 5K a year threshold- when you collect 5K in revenue from a state, you need to start collecting sales tax Statement editor (credit limit): goes out to your customers at the end of every month if they have an outstanding balance. Overhead management can be tracked here: lease payments, etc. Go to the order page, click on the customer to show the dashboard If pay before processing, it will just show their order history If there are credit limits, they can log in and pay from here (or you can do an offline payment- customers don't have access) Could be a walk in, pay in cash, or use square. Refund payments (demo) Undo transaction (transaction history report, then refund that payment on your stripe dashboard) Sales tax report (demo) A lot of customers file sales tax quarterly You would select the state and filter the dates- it will show what you owe to the sales tax commission Helpful report for knowing how much you need to pay in taxes Financial integrations (he uses QBD) We recommend using the export invoices queue function This will get invoice information out of Allmoxy with an export you can use Transaction Pro or Sassant to map the Allmoxy export to QB (which we can help with- typically a one-time set up) The more manual route is using the Export Transactions Queue (while having QBO pulled up) and reconciling payments Notes HW: Assign products to DB production line 75% of their customers pay by check in the mail (you will give these customers a credit limit) Is there a way to turn off customers seeing their credit limit? NO

11/21/24 Onboarding #3 with BVW Companies are set up Looks like you created some tags Triggers? Which ones do you want to use. Let's set one up now for testing. How an order moves through Allmoxy What is your current workflow? Order comes in, enter in drawer lister, double check, looks good they send the acknowledgement with the delivery date From there they figure out what date they want to run the shop sheets to get out to the guys Delivery days worth of orders at a time 200- 300 drawers then they send all those shop sheets out. I will do a test order There is an expected workflow in Allmoxy Bid --> Ordered --> Verified --> In progress --> completed --> shipped When an order is verified, it is locked (the customer can no longer make changes) Customer's can make changes to Bids and Orders SHOW TRIGGERS AGAIN Test order Production lines/ work schedule report (Does he want to add a Drawer Box production line?) Production lines allow you to mimic what is happening on the floor in Allmoxy. What are the steps in making a drawer box? Show Production Lines and Work Schedule Report (easy way to see jobs in verified status) We don't need to set up production lines, but it can be useful. We can even just make one category called "in progress". Or we can get more granular. (Show barcode scanning) You can start jobs for each production line --> moving them to in progress (which will drop off from the report) It will show you a start by date and a ship by date (based off lead time) From there, you can mimic what happens on the job floor in Allmoxy by using the scanning station OR you can move orders to in progress manually Shipping report Once an order is completed, you're ready to get those out the door. You're looking ahead. It shows you what method is being used, company name, etc. Shipping Methods 90% their own trucks M-W-F 1 method, TUE-THU 2 method Virginia: 100 dollars Deleware: 60 dollars Use tags here! Notes Height validation Drawer bottoms need to say drawer inset Different kinds of plywood.

Onboarding #2 Big Valley To review They do not want customers creating accounts, they would like to create accounts for them (and give login information) Theme looks good Any issues adding employees, or giving permissions? Is Stripe set up? Today we are talking about Tags, Price Adjustments, and Triggers. We are also going to do some live testing to see how things look right now. Tags These are ways you can group customers together for a certain purpose These can be referenced in triggers and price adjustments Ex. you have a group of customers that are cabinet shops, and they get a certain discount. You can give this group of customers a "cabinet shop" tag on their company profile that can then be referenced later. You can also put tags on products How do you think you might group certain customers together? Price Adjustments Do you have customers that are priced differently than others? Why? Use price adjustments to achieve this Say you have a customer that is your oldest and most loyal customer, you always give them a 10% discount no matter what. Price adjustments are basically discounts or surcharges that can be applied to customers, groups of customers, or products. They are adjusted PER ITEM, not the total order amount Priority For when there are multiple price adjustments competing with one another If one discount has a higher priority than the other, the highest priority will override the other discount Ex. if you have a new customer discount and a summer sale discount going on at the same time, you would want the new customer discount to have a higher accumulate than the other. Tip: Use the Always setting, so you know if you are discounting or surcharging every time. You can also discount customers or products with certain tags Triggers How are you currently interacting with customers? Is it manual calls, emails, etc? Triggers can automate that process for you Notify you, and your customers of something happening in Allmoxy Triggers to edit: 1, 6, 12, 16, 18, 19 Does he want to be notified of a bid? Or an order? Notes 1/8 inch increments (selectable) Have their customers type in height and then have a validation? HW: add some customers to Allmoxy, find a few triggers you want to utilize off the bat and make some edits to the text.

(paid by you) The underwriting process is very important Theme Action items Edit Kurt Stauffer Add Dina, Mitch, and Zach as employees with logins and permissions (13:40) Consider how to handle customer account creation (manual vs. self-signup) (15:55) All Big Valley Wood Products team Review and test product catalog as it's being developed (31:18) Set up Stripe account for payment processing (35:20) Customize Allmoxy theme with company branding (38:33) Josh Baker Send follow-up email with Stripe setup as homework assignment (35:20) Juan Complete product catalog development by November 20th (42:20) ️ Notes Site Navigation and User Permissions (00:02 - 10:08) Josh Baker introduced basic site navigation in Allmoxy Explained difference between internal employee and customer views Discussed creating accounts, adding employees, and setting permissions Kurt experienced login issues, resolved with temporary password Adding Companies and Contacts (10:12 - 23:25) Demonstrated how to add companies and contacts in Allmoxy Explained process for creating customer accounts Discussed options for customer account creation (manual vs. self-signup) Showed how to set customer permissions Briefly introduced order placement process Order Statuses and Product Catalog (24:55 - 35:07) Explained various order statuses in Allmoxy Demonstrated how to place a bid/order Discussed current state of product catalog (work in progress) Introduced Stripe as payment processor for credit card transactions Stripe offers 2.9% fee across all credit card types Theme Customization and Training Plans (35:07 - 43:15) Explained how to customize Allmoxy theme for branding Discussed upcoming training for salesperson and secretary Planned to review catalog progress in two weeks Set tentative training date for week of Thanksgiving or first week of December Aim to have completed catalog by November 20th for review

Chris Barton I would like to review this project with you and have you do it. This one will be a good project that I would need a decent time commitment for. You up for it?

Beau Lewis we need to get back to Kurt. He is emailing me about pricing, and I am using him for feedback on the new pricing model. With that though, he’s begging for services and wants to get going. The tools for the price increase should be ready by the end of this week.

Background - Been with Allmoxy since 2012 How do you leverage Allmoxy currently? Internal or external tool? Exporting? Using our API? Current challenges & the rest of 2024 What's top of mind for you right now? Would meeting more regularly be valuable to you right now? Anything new coming down the line for H2? Next Steps Schedule a follow up meeting Follow up on any necessary items Notes: Limitations (had these since day 1) API - communication with other software Allmoxy pricing and configuration tool CRM tool - production and customer manage Majority of orders are entered by their internal team Using Allmoxy to quote API fills out the pipeline Few customers that have access and can configure their own orders. Cadence having our email is best Most issues they submit via the support chat Challenge Reporting issues - weak Deleted old quotes Setup of the database (locations seems to be the lowest common denominator) Output pages Can't control these enough Check in on previous requests in Jira and provide any updates Takeaways: Happy with the system, been using it for an extremely long time and has it almost completely worked out. Two main limitations Database - how allmoxy is built and the ability to manipulate the data Output pages - page breaks and page view control Next Steps Continue to check-in - Quarterly Follow up on any JIRA tickets that have seen movement He'll reach out if he needs support.

5/30/25 Export Testing Cont. - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Meeting-with-Nick-Thompson-from-Reid-MFG-LLC::01JWBXEECTS2VQ6GCXA28K85HP Meeting Summary Carson Ward and Nick discussed ongoing issues with material data mapping and export consistency in their Cabinet Vision integration process. The conversation covered the misidentification of several door materials (e.g., Crystal, Atlantico, Pharaoh, Ingrain), organization of folders to isolate materials correctly, and steps taken to improve naming consistency and fix attribute mismatches. They also discussed operational workload challenges and plans for follow-up testing and collaboration. Some problems were linked to naming conventions and missing table data for certain five-piece and three-piece doors. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Clarification of email confusion regarding a previous file sent on 5/22; resolved and forwarded. Folder structure and naming cleanup was implemented to isolate and identify materials correctly (e.g., separating Pharaoh, Ingrain, Shade). File naming inconsistencies (e.g., extra spaces in names, missing attributes) were flagged as causes of export/import failures. Material misidentifications observed: Atlantico, Bianco, and Perla coming through as Crystal High Gloss. Pharaoh entries coming through as Razio or Urban White. Five-piece door entries defaulting to Phoenix Beige Luxor. Crystal Piano and some mats failing to map correctly. There is improvement in three-piece Nora material mapping, with some lingering issues (e.g., Salt Cut Sand incorrectly mapped as Terra Loft Oak). Technical Issues & Fixes Updated attribute fields to fix mislabeling (e.g., Pharaoh names). Identified missing product attributes in tables for many five-piece entries. Noted that Cabinet Vision export may require specific file naming and folder structure to function correctly. Problem with scratch-prone material (e.g., Aegean Anchor) due to ultra-thin surface layers. Proposed workaround for diagnosing import failures: create isolated test orders (ORDs) for problematic materials and share them directly. Decisions Made Nick will create a dedicated ORD with all the problematic materials over the weekend for testing. Carson will review ORD files and assist with attribute fixes based on the test data received. Another engineer (Ray) may step in next Friday if Carson is unavailable. A meeting is tentatively expected for follow-up, but dependent on Ray's availability and review status. Challenges or Concerns Significant time pressure on Nick due to being the sole engineer managing multiple responsibilities including training, design validation, and production troubleshooting. Persistent five-piece mapping issues caused by incomplete or incorrect data in the source tables. System pulling fallback material (e.g., Phoenix Beige Luxor) for unmapped or misconfigured entries, causing widespread mislabeling. Action Items Nick to create and send a test ORD file including all currently failing materials (focus on five-piece, three-piece, Pharaoh, Ingrain, and Piano). Carson to review and correct material mapping and attributes based on the provided ORD. Nick to send updated screenshots and ensure consistent folder structure to eliminate conflicts during import/export. Carson to prepare documentation for Ray and evaluate his ability to take over support during Carson’s absence. Both to monitor and test corrected mappings (e.g., Pharaoh, Bianco Matte) to confirm effectiveness. Next Steps Nick will deliver the test ORD file to Carson by Monday. Carson and Ray will review the file and determine corrective actions. Coordination will be made to schedule a follow-up session if Ray can take the lead next Friday. Continue refining the folder structure and material naming conventions to prevent fallback mislabeling issues. Track and resolve lingering issues with five-piece and three-piece configurations, especially Piano, Bianco Matte, and the Nora new entries.

05/16/25 Export Testing Cont. - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Reid-MFG-Allmoxy-Export-Testing::01JV7VW16J1A962R1G51J1NF6J Agenda: Nick shared what didn't come through Where do we want to start? Notes: Robert joined - Currently working on a chatbot for their main website and wants to direct traffic to their Allmoxy. Consideration - Need to revisit their catalog to see what's currently in there, how it's pricing, and if any adjustments are needed. Meeting Summary The team conducted an in-depth troubleshooting and update session to address multiple export issues related to Allmoxy product codes, especially within five-piece and glass door configurations. The discussion involved real-time collaboration across screens and involved refining export attributes, correcting naming inconsistencies, and confirming updated behaviors across the product catalog. Robert Jacobs also introduced plans for a future chatbot integration with Allmoxy. Key Takeaways Important Discussions The group reviewed discrepancies between expected and actual exports, particularly within five-piece doors and five-piece glass doors. Crystal Atlantico slab products were identified as missing or misattributed, prompting attribute updates and testing of refreshes. Naming inconsistencies (e.g., capitalization differences like “PHOENIX” vs “Phoenix”) were determined to be a key source of failed product code recognition. Allmoxy export configuration and product setup behaviors were clarified, with emphasis on avoiding inconsistent data entry to streamline downstream processes. Discussion of Allmoxy export structure included frame-only distinctions and surface material accuracy within export columns. Aaron introduced process improvements, such as isolating “frame only” values into their own export column to reduce error potential. Technical Issues & Fixes Errors in Crystal five-piece glass doors were caused by double “glass” references in code names, which were corrected. Slab products like Crystal Atlantico and Charred Ebony were fixed by correcting export references and attribute mappings. Products like Phoenix Base Luxor (no longer supported by Rayhow) were being incorrectly assigned; suspected default fallback behavior was adjusted. A broken or empty attribute reference in “mat read five piece export” was identified as the cause of misassignment to Phoenix Beige Luxor. Multiple updates were made to refresh and test changes, including fixing a typo that prevented a large group of five-piece door products from referencing correctly. Capitalization standardization efforts were confirmed to be ongoing for future consistency. Crystal Bianco High Gloss and related matte versions were reviewed and corrected where needed. Some product exports (e.g., shade five piece) required manual recalculations or column refreshes due to attribute structure limitations. Decisions Made Use all-uppercase naming for product consistency moving forward. Shift “frame only” options into a dedicated export column for easier identification and separation. Continue the approach of reviewing products one-by-one, but optimize wherever cascading fixes are possible. Send PDF and ORD files for Nick to cross-check updated product codes and verify integrity. Schedule a follow-up meeting next week to assess progress and continue refining the export logic. Challenges or Concerns Export code mismatches due to inconsistent or incomplete attribute setup were time-consuming to troubleshoot. The volume of product entries (over 100) made it difficult to ensure clean mapping across all scenarios. Visual limitations during screen-sharing made it hard for all participants to follow complex troubleshooting steps. Robert noted the challenge of following along during real-time export/code updates and plans to improve this in future setups. Action Items Aaron to: Finalize product code updates, especially around Crystal five-piece and slab products. Send updated PDF and ORD files to Nick for review. Nick to: Begin checking the refreshed files for export accuracy and report back any discrepancies. Verify if codes for Crystal five-piece frame-only are complete and add missing attributes if needed. Carson to: Coordinate the next meeting and keep the team aligned on timing and deliverables. Robert to: Continue working on the chatbot integration concept and gather information on linking to the Allmoxy catalog. Finalize list of services Allmoxy offers to support chatbot functionality (e.g., thermofoil, edge banding, panel stock, custom machining). Next Steps Validate all Crystal five-piece and shade product exports now that structural changes are in place. Continue phased verification of slab products and non-standard materials (e.g., matte finishes, special surfaces). Review and clean up naming and formatting inconsistencies across all active export folders. Ensure screen-sharing and meeting logistics are improved for technical walkthroughs in future sessions. Meet again next week to finalize testing results, review any residual errors, and plan remaining updates.

Nick provided updates on completed tasks and ongoing training efforts. Aaron shared technical observations regarding product name inconsistencies and file generation logic. The group also planned for next steps, including continued filtering of completed items and scheduling another review session next week. They briefly discussed recent severe weather events and personal experiences with storms and natural disasters before returning to task coordination. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Nick has completed a significant portion of the glass and three-piece product configurations but still has several outstanding. Training of another team member has been ongoing, slightly delaying some progress but helping overall efficiency. Nick is preparing and organizing screenshots and product data into edge folders by material type: shade, crystal, and billion. Aaron emphasized the importance of testing crystal slab products due to inconsistent spacing in product names. Technical Issues & Fixes Several product configurations (e.g., Nebia slab, Terra products, Pharaoh group) are pulling incorrect material data (e.g., Phoenix Beige Luxor appearing across different entries). Folder duplication as a workaround for refreshing data was deemed ineffective due to caching issues. Issues in product material output appear related to method implementation within the generation logic, particularly affecting frame-only methods and multi-row datasets. Aaron and Ray are developing a faster method for updating order data and refreshing folders. Identified that the CSV export process might be failing due to naming column inconsistencies or logic flaws in the file generation. Decisions Made Nick will review the latest email and confirm which items came through correctly and which did not. Carson will update the fabrication order based on Nick's feedback. Aaron will continue verifying and refreshing folders, especially focusing on high-risk areas like crystal slab and certain Terra entries. Challenges or Concerns Difficulty in managing large, segmented product lists, especially when each variation (e.g., color) requires a separate CSV file. Some unexpected products (e.g., three-piece Nora) were included without prior awareness, indicating potential communication gaps. Persistent mismatches in material naming causing erroneous file outputs. Acknowledged need for more streamlined communication from fabrication teams (e.g., Ray’s team) regarding available product types. Action Items Nick: Finish reviewing the full product list and report which entries came through correctly. Send updated status to Carson for order revision. Continue training the new team member on the floor. Carson: Update the fabrication order to reflect products that came through correctly and exclude problematic entries. Prepare and send a refreshed PDF version of the order. Aaron: Continue folder refreshes and fix naming/spacing inconsistencies. Check the raw file and method logic for the Phoenix Beige Luxor mislabeling issue. Coordinate with Ray to finalize and deploy the improved update method. Next Steps Schedule the next review session for Friday, May 16, with a contingency to delay if needed based on Nick’s availability. Nick to send item status feedback before the next session. Carson to prepare updated materials (CSV and PDF) for final validation. Aaron to complete testing and adjustments for the remaining folders and methods before the next sync.

This should help fix the Revazio issue where "Urban White" was being pulled as the default color due to lack of specific entries. Aaron confirmed that styles such as RAO Bianco and Urban White were being incorrectly pulled due to this. Style Clarification: Questions around certain style names (e.g., “Asia Oak” vs. “AA Oak”) were clarified during the meeting. Manual Review & Reprocessing: Nick began reprocessing the slab, five-piece, and glass orders using a more efficient method to avoid earlier mistakes caused by following a more complicated route through the door process. Interim Communication Plan: Nick will notify Carson and Aaron once all updates are complete and files are added to the shared Drive, rather than requiring another interim meeting. ✅ Action Items Task Owner Due Date Notes Finalize five-piece and glass door updates Nick Before next meeting (Tentatively next Friday) Will include missing color data and updated file organization Notify Carson and Aaron when files are ready Nick As soon as updates are uploaded Email confirmation and Drive link will be shared Schedule follow-up meeting Carson Done Tentatively for next Friday to maintain momentum Continue cleaning and filing processed orders Aaron Ongoing Moved completed files into a “today’s work” folder for easy tracking 🔄 Next Steps Await Nick’s completion of the five-piece and glass order updates. Review shared Drive for uploaded screenshots and finalized order data. Monitor for the next meeting confirmation, currently scheduled for next Friday. Long-Term Fix: Continue ensuring new styles and color sets are properly mapped in the system tables to prevent incorrect default behavior in future orders.

04/17/25 - Export check-in meeting - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Reid-MFG-Allmoxy-Export-Testing::01JRXEBR4225WQPE58SZE5FCSF Meeting Summary The team convened to validate and troubleshoot issues with product codes and material assignments within Allmoxy’s system, specifically focusing on the 2022 and 2024 data sets. The meeting was primarily a collaborative audit between Nick, Aaron, and Carson to verify which materials were correctly linked and functioning, and to identify the ones needing updates or re-mapping. The session was prompted by a recent update confirming 2024.4 is operational, but the project is still being finalized in the 2022 version. An in-depth walkthrough of 212 product items was conducted, reviewing each material's system match and identifying discrepancies—especially with the shade, slab, and five-piece product types. A high volume of incorrect mappings, particularly defaulting to “Urban White” when no code was available, drove the discussion toward re-structuring the input folders and prioritizing which materials need updates. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Team reconfirmed to finish the implementation in the 2022 environment despite 2024.4 functioning. Nick is responsible for capturing screenshots and organizing the product folder for upload review. Files were sorted and reviewed in batches by category (e.g., Brilliant, Ravisio, North Slab, Terrace Lab). Identified several categories consistently defaulting to "Urban White" due to missing or invalid codes. Technical Issues & Fixes Primary Issue: Multiple materials defaulted to "Urban White" because the system could not find a valid match, especially in the Vizio, Shade 5-piece, and Pharaoh categories. Shade 5-piece Mapping Failure: System repeatedly pulled "Urban White" instead of the correct shade; the corresponding column appeared empty. North Slab and Ravisio: Certain entries (e.g., Midnight Dash, Dusk Pine) were misaligned or duplicated incorrectly. Three-Piece and Five-Piece Failures: Specific five-piece items, especially with glass, consistently failed to match (e.g., Brilliant Bianco mapped incorrectly across multiple entries). New Colors Not Initially in System: Items such as Indigo Pewter, Chalk, Canvas, Lighthouse Oak, and Fireside Walnut were missing in Nick’s system but have since been added. Decisions Made Aaron duplicated the 212-piece order to preserve working references while creating a filtered version focusing only on items needing updates. Carson confirmed folder delivery and passed off the session to Aaron for continuation. Items with verified matches were actively removed from the update list to streamline next steps. Scheduled a follow-up session for Wednesday at 9:00 AM (Central Time). Challenges or Concerns Repeated failures with Vizio, Pharaoh, and Shade collections highlight the need for more granular folder separation. Several items shared duplicate references or mislabels, complicating validation. File sharing restrictions (e.g., Word doc uploads into Zoom chat) slowed down information transfer. Need to break complex product families (e.g., Ravisio Shade, Ingrain, Pharaoh) into more manageable folders for testing. Action Items Task Owner Details Due Date Finalize 2022 version with current verified data Team Continue validating materials based on 2022 references Ongoing Send updated list of items that failed validation Aaron Compiled and emailed list of 110 materials needing code updates Sent post-meeting Update and re-upload missing or misassigned materials Nick Reformat, correct material codes, and send updated files back Before next meeting Review and revise Ravisio, Pharaoh, Ingrain folders Team Break out folders to isolate problematic material groups Prior to next sync Schedule and prepare for follow-up review Aaron & Nick Wednesday meeting invite confirmed for final review of broken items Wednesday @ 9:00 AM CT Next Steps Aaron will monitor returned files from Nick to confirm corrected mappings. Continue testing to ensure newly updated materials map correctly without defaulting errors. Reorganize problematic folders (e.g., Ravisio, Pharaoh) for cleaner testing workflows. Prepare a "final match" list of successfully validated materials to use for future batch orders. Long-term: Evaluate system handling for missing codes to avoid fallback to Urban White in future builds.

03/28/25 Export Meeting: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Reid-MFG-Rehau-Export-Working-Session::01JQ9PCV4J8C52PJ0QCZQ0YRAE Here is a HubSpot-ready meeting summary based on the detailed discussion: Meeting Summary This meeting focused on refining material testing, file structuring, and code validation for Allmoxy’s Cabinet Vision integration. The group reviewed progress on cleaning up outdated material data (especially from 2022), adding material folders for slab, five-piece, and three-piece product types, and cross-checking material codes against current availability. Discussions also included logic structure for edge banding, setting up consistent test files, and preparing for final validation before involving stakeholders Scott and Steve. The overarching goal was to streamline the material mapping process, reduce missing code issues, and ensure that test orders comprehensively validate all supported combinations. Clear plans were laid to finalize schedules and testing before the next meeting. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Material Cleanup & File Structuring: Nick removed outdated materials from 2022 Cabinet Vision, significantly improving system performance. Organized materials into folders (e.g., Slab, 5-Piece, 3-Piece) to simplify testing and future updates. Plan to restructure folders further, e.g., splitting Razio slab into a dedicated “Slab Doors” folder. Testing & Validation Focus: Carson built a large test order (212 items) covering a range of materials and product formats to drive validation. Test file included slab, five-piece, and three-piece for Brilliant, Shade, Crystal, Noir, Terra, Pharaoh, and Ingrain where applicable. Ongoing alignment between what’s supported in Allmoxy vs. Cabinet Vision material schedules. Code Availability & Syncing: Several materials (e.g., Magnolia, Cappuccino in Brilliant 5-piece) were identified as missing and flagged for removal or update. Discussion around missing SKUs for some Crystal and Noir variants, with a plan to test and update as schedules are created. Edge Banding & Material Matching Logic: Crystal materials require additional logic for matching edge banding (matte vs. polished vs. V-groove). Current method: material schedules dictate banding explicitly to ensure consistency. Technical Issues & Fixes Resolved dropdown loading issues within Cabinet Vision by waiting for reloading. Identified locked dropdowns when products were added too quickly; resolved via delayed refresh or new product entries. Cleared out and archived older test folders for clean reference going forward. Decisions Made Nick to continue material schedule creation on his end. Carson to finalize test order structure and send it for testing. Aaron confirmed all necessary previous folders have been archived and are not deleted. Final meeting with Scott and Steve to be scheduled after one more round of internal validation. Challenges or Concerns Many newer shade and crystal color options do not have corresponding material schedules yet. Several product variants still need code assignments, particularly for new lines like Shade and Noir. Ensuring consistency across different product formats (slab, 3-piece, 5-piece) remains complex due to inconsistent naming and missing schedules. Action Items Task Owner Details Due Finalize test order with all relevant products Carson Ensure all slabs, 3-piece, and 5-piece items are in, excluding known missing codes Done Create material schedules for missing items (e.g., Shade 5-piece, Noir 3-piece) Nick Focus on materials that didn’t transfer or failed in test file Before next meeting (April 4) Send Nick list of missing codes and test feedback Aaron Helps Nick identify what schedules need to be created or corrected ASAP Schedule final internal validation meeting Carson Set for Friday, April 4 at 9AM CST Scheduled Prepare for final stakeholder review with Scott & Steve Team After Nick’s schedules are completed and Aaron re-tests Following week Next Steps Testing: Nick will run the test order and validate which items pass or fail based on material schedules. Material Schedule Creation: Nick to create remaining schedules before the next internal review. Documentation: Carson and Aaron will support Nick with updated code lists and folder access. Final Validation Meeting: April 4th, to ensure all mappings are complete and prepare for stakeholder meeting. Stakeholder Sync: Meeting with Scott and Steve will follow to demonstrate updates and align on process improvements for future materials and naming conventions.

03/21/25 Export Testing Cont. Meeting Summary - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Reid-MFG-Rehau-Export-Working-Session::01JPQNMAZSRRRHWFD5DRBD2YCJ This session focused on validating and finalizing the product data integration between Rayhaus and Nick’s Cabinet Vision system. The team worked through persistent issues with material naming inconsistencies and product export mismatches—particularly around five-piece and slab door configurations—and confirmed successful syncing of several test exports. Significant progress was made on ensuring all material and construction types are pulled correctly across systems, laying the groundwork for future streamlined fabrication workflows between Rayhaus and Nick’s shop. The conversation also included plans for final verification steps, an upcoming transition to Cabinet Vision 2024, and the importance of clear documentation and long-term maintenance. The team emphasized the importance of shared understanding between Rayhaus, Nick’s team, and implementation contacts to avoid future data mismatches. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Microphone and Audio Setup: Nick experienced initial issues with his Yeti Orb mic not being recognized until toggled—similar to problems faced last week. Gemba Docs Usage: Nick shared how his team uses Gemba Docs to create walk-through PDFs for shop operations, simplifying on-machine instructions. Product Testing: Nick and Aaron conducted live testing of several materials and configurations including Brilliant Bianco, Pharaoh Slab, and Loft Oak 3-piece HG. A major breakthrough was achieved when five-piece and slab Pharaoh materials finally synced properly with Allmoxy, confirming naming consistency. Technical Issues & Fixes Material Naming Mismatches: Pharaoh materials were previously failing to sync due to mismatched names like “Rah Pharaoh” vs. “Revassio.” Resolution involved adjusting both system exports and Cabinet Vision imports to standardize on the correct string formatting. Frame-Only Configurations: The export process needed to differentiate between “five-piece with glass” and “frame-only” formats. Aaron added a dedicated FO column in the export table for frame-only use cases to avoid future confusion. Horizontal Door Issues (HG): Past issues with 3-piece configurations pulling incorrect materials (e.g., PL11 EB118) were retested and resolved. Allmoxy Color Syncing: Final test exports confirmed that Allmoxy now pulls the correct material colors and configurations from Cabinet Vision. Decisions Made Moving forward with full material syncing based on Nick providing: Separate lists (screenshots) for 3-piece, 5-piece with glass, and slab door configurations. Rayhaus export setup will remain static unless intentional updates are communicated to Nick’s team. Naming conventions across systems are now standardized and should not be altered without coordinated changes. Challenges or Concerns Trailing Spaces in Material Names: Even minor whitespace discrepancies could break the sync; Aaron recommended using Notepad++ to spot invisible characters. Versioning Differences: Nick's team is moving from Cabinet Vision 2022 to 2024. Material schedules must be carefully verified during migration to prevent mismatches. Future Configuration Drift: Carson raised concerns about Rayhaus unintentionally changing formats or tables, which could disrupt the entire integration if not monitored closely. Action Items Task Owner Details Due Provide screenshots of material lists (3-piece, 5-piece glass, slab) Nick Include naming format as seen in Cabinet Vision. Separate for each product type. ASAP Notify team when material screenshots are uploaded Nick Post notification in shared drive/chat for Aaron and Carson. Upon upload Integrate received material names into Allmoxy export table Aaron Ensure all materials, including ¼” and ¾” slab, are aligned in export config. Before next meeting Run asynchronous testing of final material sync Aaron, Nick Test multiple colors and configurations to verify full compatibility. Week of Mar 24 Schedule and prepare for final validation meeting Carson Placeholder meeting set for Friday, Mar 28, to run through final integrated test with team. Scheduled Next Steps Nick will finalize and send all material lists with correct formatting. Aaron and Carson will update the system tables and run a full round of sync testing to confirm everything is pulling correctly. A final internal meeting on Friday, March 28 is scheduled to verify success across all scenarios. If successful, a broader meeting including implementation and Rayhaus stakeholders will be planned to validate process handoff. Post-meeting, Rayhaus will begin sending live orders to test continuous production and verify reliability in real-world use. Once validated, the team will begin exploring Phase 2—which includes offering Nick’s cabinet products with Rayhaus materials.

03/14/25 Export Testing Cont. Notes: Nick sharing his screen - issue encountered creating one large ORD file. (would only do it a room at a time) Recording: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Reid-MFG-Rehau-Export-Working-Session::01JNXXMYTKDJDD2BKP0S7XDR8Q Meeting Summary Purpose: This meeting was held to review and test the export and transfer of OOD files related to various cabinet styles (Three-Piece, Five-Piece, Frame Only, and Slab), ensuring that the correct materials, grain selections, and file configurations were being properly generated and imported into the system. The session also addressed a backlog due to a previous meeting cancellation and served to organize progress for future testing. Context & Urgency: Nick encountered challenges creating a single OOD file due to system constraints and provided multiple files for testing. The team prioritized verifying that the exports worked properly across configurations to avoid time-consuming manual corrections. Accuracy in file naming, material options, and grain direction was critical to ensuring the exports function properly in production workflows. Key Takeaways Important Discussions: Nick shared screen and explained issues when trying to generate a unified OOD file; files had to be broken up by room. Google Drive folder was used for transferring files, with Aaron assisting Nick in navigating access/setup. Various styles and material configurations were uploaded for testing: Three-Piece, Five-Piece, Frame Only, and Slab. Multiple materials across these styles were tested including Terra Loft Oak, Mission Maple, Crystal Bianco, Empire Skyline, etc. The team reviewed and discussed material codes, naming conventions, and grain orientation (e.g., horizontal grain for center panels in Three-Piece styles). Technical Issues & Fixes: Crystal Bianco file did not import correctly due to an extra space in the filename; fix is simple (adjust space if necessary). Some files defaulted to incorrect materials (e.g., PL10 018 PVCEB) when main materials weren’t defined—identified as a system fallback issue. A misspelled “Midnight” value was causing a mismatch; it was corrected during the meeting. Confirmed grain direction logic for Three-Piece (center panel horizontal, styles vertical). Noted the need to account for material suffixes like -HG (horizontal grain/high gloss) in exports. Decisions Made: Horizontal grain (HG) will be appended where required for Three-Piece styles. Materials will be accepted as-is if the system requires specific naming conventions (e.g., spacing in names). Nick will provide updated material lists for Crystal and Pharaoh in Five-Piece Frame Only formats. Export test files will follow a standard naming structure based on Nick’s folder setup for consistency and tracking. Challenges or Concerns: System constraints only allow room-by-room exports, increasing file management complexity. Some materials default incorrectly due to lack of defined main material in setup. Pharaoh and Crystal are more likely to be problematic in slab configurations—highlighted as areas for close testing. Action Items Nick: Compile and upload a complete list of Crystal and Pharaoh materials available for Five-Piece Frame Only. Confirm all entries are properly configured in the system to prevent fallback to default materials. Continue organizing materials by style and upload to the shared Google Drive folder. Aaron: Continue verifying each file against internal tables and identifying any discrepancies or missing attributes. Maintain naming convention across folders and exports for consistent tracking. Carson: Send updated meeting follow-up with direct link to shared folder for Nick. Coordinate next meeting and maintain shared thread with updated export files. Next Steps File Testing Progression: Completed: Three-Piece, Five-Piece Next: Frame Only and Slab (ongoing) Closely monitor Pharaoh slab imports for potential errors. Material Table Updates: Awaiting new material codes from Nick for Crystal and Pharaoh (Five-Piece Frame Only). Placeholder rows will be added to the table for these materials until full data is received. Future Meeting: Scheduled for next Friday at 12:00 PM to continue testing and review. Will focus on Slab validation and finalizing frame-only materials once updated lists are provided. Ongoing Monitoring: Continue validating export imports for accuracy. Track any inconsistencies in material codes, grain settings, or system fallbacks to ensure fixes are implemented proactively.

engagements.body: 11/22 Meeting Prep Notes: Current operation - Rehau receives an order from a customer which they decide they want Reid to manufacture. Rehau takes the export file, fabrication order, and packing slip, screenshot of the resource allocation tab, and their own internal purchase order file and attaches these to an email they send manually to Reid. What we're trying to solve: Automate the transfer of information (order details, export, etc) A place to manage these Rehau orders (inside Reid's allmoxy) Allow Reid to offer Rehau's products in their instance without it pushing the orders through the Rehau's instance (b2b connection) What are our options: Data transfer - we can set up a trigger on Rehau's side to automatically send the order data and attachments to Reid. (only thing that wouldn't be automated is Rehau's purchase order) Order Import - It's possible to build an export that captures Rehau's order data and import that into Reid's instance. (somewhat manual as the customer information has to be entered manually) Allow Reid to offer Rehau's products - Rehau's products must live within Reid's instance, these either have to be built manually or we can technically clone Rehau's products to your instance but we'll lose all of the catalog data that's been built up until this point. Absolutes: For an export to work (instance to instance) the products would have to match exactly The only way to "resell" Rehau's products would be through B2B which you wouldn't be able to edit the products If we copy Rehau's instance into yours you'll lose all of the past product data (Clean slate that replaces everything) Notes: Bryce - joining in a few minutes Catalog Data - is there a way to save their catalog data? Determined a plan of action What we're going to do: We will be keeping the existing Reid catalog and need to expand the currently built Rehau product line within Reid's instance. The expanded products must match exactly what's in Rehau. Set up training on how to build a Rehau product within Reid's instance, using the newer method which includes proxy variables. Modify the currently built export to work within Reid's instance (eventually)

11/15 Meeting - Steve and Bryce Agenda: Export/Rehau updates Aaron working on, he'll confirm once he's completed and ready for testing B2B Show how to connect - Bryce can set this up Will allow us to start selling from Reid once the Rehau work is completed Bryce & Steve's thoughts? Notes: Reid's desired behavior for using Allmoxy Rehau receives an order from their instance and determines they want Reid as the manufacturer Rehau sends the order information over to Reid Reid can import the order information into their Allmoxy instance It can generate all of the desired paperwork/exports Roadblocks Can't import an order from Rehau into Reid's instance without having Rehau's catalog cloned inside of Reid. Rehau would need to have Reid set up as the "Allmoxy supplier" through B2B and then this connection could work but Rehau wouldn't ever do that. Resource allocation and supply management wouldn't be possible without having the catalogs duplicated. Any time Rehau makes an update to their catalog the exact same update would need to happen in Reid's "cloned" catalog. First Steps Get the export for Cabinet Vision working with Reid's CV and from the orders generated by Rehau Need a trigger setup within Rehau to send Reid the necessary paperwork (export, fabrication order, packing slip) Unable to attach the supply allocation but isn't necessary right now Unable to attach Rehau's PO for Reid to do the fabrication Second Step Determine and outline what is possible and isn't possible Use B2B? (most likely not, doesn't solve the problem) Clone Rehau's catalog into Reid? - Could potentially work but someone would have to maintain any updates that Rehau makes.

Reid & Rehau Export meeting 11/8/24 Agenda: Meeting goal - share with Reid and Rehau team's the current challenge we have with building 1 single export that can work with all products. Challenges discovered by Aaron Aaron to share how the differences between these products make it difficult for us to be able to create a single export. Action Plan Steve and Scott need to decide who will be making the updates (Allmoxy or Scott's team) for the products so they can be exportable on one file. Once updates are complete Aaron can finish the export file and Reid should be able to fabricate any/all products from Rehau's instance Next Steps Work needs to start on the products (Adding the missing attribute options) Scott sharing a list of all products that should be touched/Reid will be fabricating.

Reid Export Issue: Looking through Rehau's products, there are individual attributes set so it won't allow us to make an export that uses proxies. (Every product would need it's own formula or have their products edited) Time estimate: 135 products that would need formula's added X amount of time to add formula to each product 15 hour project - estimate? Basic function test: Need to test this first before the next step but next steps will be larger then what they might be anticipating.

Reid - Export notes with Aaron Export needs: Issue - Group attributes are different Because attributes aren't shared from product to product, it's going to be editing every individual product and adding the reid product and editing the reid export formula. Can't write a formula that will do everything in a proxy - Would be a larger lift.

Review homework - create an account, contact, and test order Tags, triggers, and price adjustments - demonstrate how these all can work together Next steps Set up a tag on your test company create a price adjustment create a trigger Test these all with your test company Notes: 5 piece doors for Rehau 8/29/24 Notes: Went through the order page gave homework of creating a test company and test order 8/23/24 Agenda: Cabinet vision export file - Juan needs a sample order of what you're processing through cabinet vision. Training Customer View What they see Order Page Required fields What items are called How to get order information (output pages and exports) Homework Create a test company Create a test order and export the CV file and the output pages Next Week Review your testing, answer questions, and move onward to automation training (Triggers, tags, and price adjustments) Notes: Cabinet vision versions (11 & 22) Importing process work the same on both imports

How can we get Rehau's orders to Reid so they can manufacture but stay within the system. We're going with the B2B option but also creating an export within Rehau so once they get an order they can send it more easily to Reid for fabrication. (limitations - payment through system) Build the products you want to offer - Recreate the main products you want to offer (best option because it would allow you to fully use the system - customer payments, invoicing, inventory, labor processes, etc) Action Plan Products Confirm if we can start with this cabinet line (with Rehau's door materials) Determine if we need to separate the door style and color. This would impact timeline and cost if we separate the doors out and make them their own category/stand alone product. If we are trying to copy what's in Rehau we'll face further delays and cost which we want to avoid. Determine which products under the doors category should be hidden Operations Confirm if the standard invoice we have will suffice and if there is any other paperwork needed? (provide us with an example if possible) Are customers going to be placing orders? Will they pay through Allmoxy? Would you like them to receive notifications from Allmoxy? Who's going to be the Allmoxy power user? Processing the most orders? Next Steps   Schedule a follow up meeting for next week to progress on whatever's decided from above Run through a complete customer order scenario to make sure all pieces are set up correctly. Get Jamie or the power user scheduled on a weekly training meeting with myself to go through the platform and understand the basic operations. Notes: door style/color option - 6 hours Door material pricing: Linear foot price - price would change depending on which group it is (plus cabinet type and drawer boxes) Wants to offer exactly what Rehau is offering Wants to get this started first Requested Meeting cadence 30 minute meetings to confirm weekly updates of what needs to be completed. (easiest to do this with two screens) 10th Meeting 3/22/24 (Missed previous week due to WTS and the week before we cancelled do to nothing to talk about) Agenda: Catalog Progress How have the training sessions with Juan been? From your view how soon do you think you want to roll this out? Still needs work Output pages Need the products to be complete so I can help work on the configuration Exports Juan will need to train you on this part. It looks like Rehau has a specific export for you, I would share a copy of it with Juan. Creating customers What do you want to do with the existing customers in the system? My recommendation would be to have customers start placing orders under the accounts they create themselves and then when you start seeing duplicate accounts you delete the incorrect one. (they don't cause any issues just sitting there) Launch date What's a date you feel comfortable picking to start with? Next Steps Complete the catalog and product exports Set up the output pages as desired Launch and officially invite a customers to start using the system to place orders. I can help make a quick video for customers if needed Notes: Catalog - Believes they're close and almost ready to launch! Export - Discuss this with Juan. (Services can build this or Juan can walk you through this setup) Next Steps With Susan's and Juan's goal of having customers enter their bids/orders into Allmoxy, we generate the packing slip, fabrication PDF, and the export, and then they take over shipping and handling the order movement from that point there isn't much else for us to do other than wait on the products to be completed by Juan so we can start testing with customers. Notes: No notes, hoping to be launch-able within the next 2 weeks. 8th Meeting 2/27/24 Agenda: Triggers Did Susan get a chance to review these and provide input? Any triggers she sees as necessary we don't have? Shipping methods Custom Methods Connected - UPS, FedEx, & Freight Quote Order Flow Work schedule report Labor processes Scanning Next Steps Finish Triggers Connect/complete desired shipping options Friday we'll review financials and output pages Notes: Shipping convo Trigger New account New quote/bid New order Job scheduling Each machine has a schedule they follow Output Pages   7th Meeting 2/23/24 Agenda: Catalog Update How did the call go last week? Rehau? Pricing? Anything else? Homework Check-In Review/edit the marked triggers with Susan. We also discussed tags and pricing adjustments This week's objective Shipping method What do you want to offer to your customers? How orders (can) flow through Allmoxy Work schedule report Shipping report Labor Processes Bar code scanning Homework for next week Shipping methods & company workflow (how will the team adopt Allmoxy) Notes: Changing pricing Catalog - can't copy and paste their database Recommendation - edit what they have in there, simple and easier. Cabinets taking presidence here Need to confirm how they want to price Determined they'll be using per linear foot 6th Meeting 02/16/24 Agenda: Detour (Rehau) Need more context on what Steve is looking to accomplish Goal: Sell Rehau's products How: B2B or create a new separate instance and clone Rehau's catalog Homework Trigger flow: Review with Susan what notifications need to be shared with her team and customers. Tags & Price adjustments - review those links/walk-throughs Triggers, Tags, and Price Adjustments What's the notification path we want to use (account creation, to order shipment, etc) Price Adjustments - Retail, builder, dealer Tags - Retail, Builder, Dealer Customer account creation They'll create their own Output Pages Share examples of what is currently there. If you want it to appear like Rehau's then I'll need an example of which ones you're referencing. (can be specific per customer) Next Steps Work on products with Juan Complete the triggers Create price adjustments for retail, builder, and dealer. Next week - Shipping, financial settings review, output page follow up Notes: Rehau topic Reid partnership - makes doors for Rehau Floating shelves and counter tops Is it possible to transfer or copy specific products from one instance to another? Need to figure out how to share the attributes Homework Triggers, tags, and adjustments New account is created - employees Other topics Account creation Output pages 5th Meeting 2/2/24 Agenda: Homework follow up Theme, add an employee, and add a customer - Did you get a chance to try this? This week (Finance & Automation) Financial settings We can connect to your processor if you want them to have the option to submit their payment while ordering. If we don't then all customers will need to be set up on credit terms - credit limit. Triggers how you want your internal team and customers notified of changes Pricing Adjustments Increase or decrease pricing for customers and products. Tags Group/identify customers and or products for triggers and pricing adjustments Next Steps Review the automation links - Activity: add a tag to a customer, create a trigger and pricing adjustment that would impact the customer. Review the products after your training session with Juan. what else would you like to discuss? Notes: Went well with Juan but realized it's going to be a decent amount of work. Last week's Homework - Added an employee Issue - timezone Can't find something for central time Fourth Meeting 01/26/24 Notes - Covered the order page and customers. Shared homework for the theme, creating an employee and customer. Next week we'll focus on finances, triggers, tags, and pricing adjustments. Third Meeting 01/19/24 Call Goal - Get confirmation on the catalog discussion (Juan's perspective, Susan's goal, etc) Agenda: Catalog conversation Susan doesn't want to rebuild Existing catalog is still there but would need to be "made available" IDEA Schedule a meeting with Juan, me, Juan (services), and Susan. Juan share with us what has been built in the past, clarify how we can utilize it, and confirm if something else would need to be built for Juan's functionality - export for CV Once we're all on the same page we can move forward with a plan (Services showing Juan how to make products available So Juan can review the catalog and determine what's available. Next steps Schedule follow up meeting Determine the direction we want to take - (doors only, doors and cabinets, editing anything, leaving as is) Build onboarding around this new goal outline steps needed to get the instance active (processing orders) What else would you like to cover? Notes: His notes - Juan recommended that we should start from scratch (quoted that a new catalog would be $8k roughly) Second Meeting 01/05/24 Call Goal - Review Onboarding Steps Agenda: Catalog/Services team - Still need to schedule with Juan to review the catalog and see what needs to be fixed. Homework/Setup work   Initial steps Automation Customization Next Steps Meet with Juan Get familiar with the platform, create test orders, create companies, etc. Review homework items shared in recap Schedule call for next week to review Notes: Wednesday the 10th 1:30 - Can we not charge for this first scoping call? Their Goal - Want to have a portal online where customers will be able to order whatever cabinets they can provide. Wants a CSV file export and be able to upload it into Cabinet vision Juan needs to map their catalog Replicate what Karhu is doing - a fabrication order Quickbooks - Interested First Meeting 12/15/23 Call goal - create a plan to get Allmoxy activated Agenda: Background Looks like there were a lot of moving parts (people leaving, some pushback about Allmoxy, etc) Can you tell me what happened? Current State What's your familiarity with Allmoxy? Catalog looks complete - Any known issues? No shipping methods other than Will Call. Stripe isn't connected or even authorize.net - How are you processing payments? Action Plan Determine a timeline of when we can go live with orders. Address any roadblocks Schedule regular meetings once we're ready to activate the account Notes: Susan's information: had an employee (trent) he started the setup but then he left. Steve, the owner asked Juan to take this on as his project Juan doesn't have a ton of experience with Allmoxy (looked into it a few years ago) Juan has been working at the company for 7 years and is going to get this going!

Title: Meeting with Manuel Arteaga from Create More Space Date: Wed Jun 18 2025 15:00:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio App: Meeting Recap Generator Prompt: Create a meeting recap with a brief summary of the call. Make sure to highlight the following details: 1. overall... Meeting Recap: Brief Summary: The meeting involved discussing the current state of Manuel's Almoxy catalog for Create More Space, addressing pricing issues, and planning next steps to get the catalog functional. The conversation also touched on Manuel's business expansion plans and the potential use of Almoxy for multiple business entities. 1. Overall tone: The meeting had a positive and collaborative tone. All participants were engaged and focused on finding solutions to move forward with the Almoxy implementation. 2. Customer sentiment: Manuel appeared optimistic and eager to get the Almoxy system working correctly. He showed enthusiasm about the potential benefits of using Almoxy for his expanding business operations. 3. Customer objections: - Past experiences with inconsistent advice from different Almoxy team members - Concerns about pricing accuracy and consistency across different product categories 4. Features mentioned: - B2B catalog vertical for separate business entities - Integration with Cabinet Vision and QuickBooks - Ability to have different pricing tiers (retail, wholesale, component parts) - Customer communication features for order status updates 5. Action items: - Manuel to create benchmark pricing examples using Cabinet Vision for various product sizes and types - Manuel to compile and send pricing information to Madison (CC Aaron) - Almoxy team to review the backend of the current catalog setup - Schedule a follow-up meeting to go through pricing adjustments together - Almoxy team to prepare for potential Cabinet Vision export integration The meeting concluded with a clear plan for next steps and a commitment from both parties to work towards getting the Almoxy catalog functional for Create More Space and potentially other business entities.

engagements.title: Schedule Report Card Consultation
engagements.body:
This customer is an active user of Allmoxy. Review their account, see what functions they are NOT using, then schedule time with the customer to review the following: 

1. Why aren't they using certain functions? Are there pain points we can eliminate? Other software we could integrate better with? 
2. Check in on any must-have features they're requesting.
3. Follow up on any open tickets they currently have. 
4. Ask for one or two industry partners/customers/suppliers etc that they'd love to see in Allmoxy.
