Julian Woods LLC Julian Woods LLC December 19, 2025 9:00 AM MST 64 mins bobby@allmoxy.com madison@allmoxy.com jaya.krishna@julianwoods.com View Meeting Recording Production Order and Output Page Customization The team confirmed that Almoxy’s output pages can be tailored to match the simplicity and clarity of the current Handsome system’s production orders to improve usability and reduce redundant data (00:08). Phillip Kim and JayaKrishna highlighted missing key attributes such as font size, door side (left/right), finish details, and color categories in Almoxy’s production order outputs compared to Handsome’s system (08:14). Madison Theisen explained that output pages are highly customizable, with adjustable font sizes and attribute displays, but increasing font size reduces the number of products per page (12:40). Bobby Lewis noted that many current output pages contain redundant repeated information, and removing such data would free space for larger fonts and more cabinets per page (13:19). The team agreed that order-level information should appear once at the top, not repeated per cabinet, freeing space for cabinet-specific details like hardware (14:15). Adding color categories specifying wood type (maple or cherry) to cabinet labels is feasible by updating or creating new label attributes in the catalog (27:00). The group clarified that custom drawing orders can be attached as files to orders and shown as internal-use products in Almoxy, but attachments do not print on production orders—they must be accessed separately (16:40). This workflow allows the production team to reference custom drawings during manufacturing without cluttering the printed output pages (19:09). Overall, Madison and Bobby emphasized that output page layout customization is extensive but requires time and collaboration during implementation and training, which will be provided under contract (23:10). Production Process Tracking and Automation Almoxy supports real-time scanning and labor process tracking integrated with barcode scanning to automate production status updates, similar to the current Google Sheets automation (29:45). Bobby Lewis confirmed that Almoxy’s system can track multiple labor steps such as pull out, staining, spraying, sanding (twice), top coating, assembly, and shipping, updating status live as operators scan barcodes (30:22). The system supports barcode scanning per order, per cabinet, and per production station, enabling granular tracking of each step and worker involved (36:30). Barcodes can be configured to submit multiple fields automatically (station, user, order, etc.) to speed up scanning and reduce errors (37:21). Almoxy offers integration options via API or Zapier to push scan data into external tools like Google Sheets for real-time visibility, with Zapier recommended for ease of use (30:50). The team noted that internal technical expertise is needed for API integration, but Almoxy can suggest external consultants if required (33:11). Bobby assured that the system is always live with no downtime or scanning pauses, ensuring continuous production without data loss even if temporary connectivity issues arise (48:00). Phillip Kim raised concerns about data lost during potential system outages, but Bobby confirmed Almoxy’s real-time updates prevent such losses and no scanning pauses occur (48:05). The labor processes feature includes setting up production steps and scanning stations with corresponding barcodes; scanning triggers update order progress and can send automated notifications like customer alerts upon job completion (49:10). A shipping report shows which items have not yet been scanned complete, helping production managers monitor outstanding tasks (50:42). Pricing and Catalog Engineering Almoxy can support complex pricing formulas and adjustments down to the penny, but this requires detailed catalog engineering by specialized engineers (52:10). Bobby Lewis emphasized that Almoxy’s pricing engine can handle variable price formulas based on size, product options, and other variables, but formulas must be properly configured (53:30). Madison clarified that price issues and formula updates are engineering tasks not solved instantly on calls but handled by dedicated implementation engineers (52:54). The team agreed that engineering efforts are critical to replicating and improving pricing accuracy currently managed by Handsome (53:51). Implementation Process and Support Structure The next steps after contract signing involve setting up a clear ownership model between the customer team and Almoxy’s engineering and support resources to ensure smooth implementation (54:00). Madison outlined two main support channels: billable implementation engineers ($150/hour) for catalog and formula setup, and general product training from customer success managers like herself and Sarah (54:13). JayaKrishna is expected to lead internal efforts coordinating with Almoxy’s engineers (54:40). Almoxy can help with training on core system features like triggers and integrations, but configuring external tools like Google Sheets requires customer involvement (55:30). Madison stressed the importance of consistent communication and stable points of contact on both sides to avoid delays and confusion during the project (42:28). The team discussed adding a numeric pick list field next to accessories to track quantities needed per order, which is possible through customization (55:55). Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and Inventory Management Almoxy supports resource allocation and MRP workflows, but these require engineering setup to tie product orders to supply lists and inventory thresholds (57:40). Bobby explained that the resource allocation feature can be configured to generate supply lists for purchase orders based on confirmed orders (58:00). The system can handle minimum and maximum inventory levels and ordering quantities per supply item, enabling automated material planning (58:26). Engineering will define how supplies are allocated per product and variable to ensure accurate MRP outputs matching current business needs (58:54). Data Retention and System Reliability Almoxy is a cloud-based system with unlimited data storage and historical access, enabling retrieval of order and production data for at least one year or longer as needed (39:01). Bobby confirmed there is no cap on data storage, supporting retrieval of detailed production and order history (39:14). Madison noted that data accuracy depends on consistent use and maintenance of the system by the customer team (39:33). The support team will respond quickly to bugs or issues, with escalation processes involving screen recordings and direct developer attention (41:00). Average resolution times vary based on issue complexity, but Almoxy’s support team is highly responsive and reliable according to Bobby and Madison (41:30). Action Items JayaKrishna Confirm continuous team involvement throughout the project to maintain consistent communication with Almoxy. (42:40) Share detailed production order print samples and attribute requirements like font size, color categories, door sizes, and accessory quantity fields for engineering review. (08:14) Coordinate screen sharing of current Handsome system labels and production order examples to facilitate customization discussions. (28:44) Follow up on MRP and resource allocation requirements with Almoxy and engineers for purchase order and inventory management setup. (57:08) Continue evaluating proposed production tracking and barcode scanning workflow setup and coordinate necessary training. (31:00) Phillip Kim Provide examples of custom cabinet orders and drawings to Almoxy team for attachment and printout feasibility. (16:43) Deliver detailed door size information and printing expectations for output page adjustments. (59:57) Validate continuous data capture ability and production tracking robustness during scanner or system outages with Almoxy team. (48:00) Support setting up labor process flows and barcode station connections on production floor. (49:00) Bobby Lewis Assist with defining attributes and label customization to incorporate missing color categories and product details in output pages. (27:20) Provide technical advice on integrating Almoxy with Google Sheets via Zapier or API for live production tracking. (30:00) Demonstrate Almoxy labor process and barcode scanning setup for real-time order tracking. (49:00) Inform about availability of implementation engineers and engineering support for custom pricing and catalog configuration. (52:00) Madison Theisen Facilitate discussion on output page customization capabilities, attachment handling, and production process tracking during software vetting. (06:00) Provide training and product support coordination post-contract. (54:00) Address concerns about project continuity and support process, ensuring stable communication. (42:30) Clarify data storage, support, and system bug reporting procedures. (39:50) Meeting Recording: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KCPY8E5YJCB5X6QVTTY20WA9 📎 Time markers in this document can be used to navigate to specific points in the meeting recording Processed by:

Office/Production Team Training 4/29/24 & 4/30/24 Madison, Juan and Mass Team Juan changing Cabinet labels to show modified dimensions and comments - Done Production Order CSV - Done Juan focused on 4/30: Deactivating validations that have to do with the modified dimensions Production order should include everything from order page ('No' answers should be Hidden if Chosen) Show Pair option in Hinge Drilling Using purchase orders without supplies built out? - Madison to check with Ashley and Sarah Interested in building out supplies Juan needs pricing from suppliers Importing the dealers and suppliers(want suppliers for POs but don't have supplier built out?) - in progress Components output page request: Everyday at 3:30 purchasing runs MRP. After that a purchase order is created. Currently purchasing can see the purchase order for chemicals and edge bands but nothing else. We need the PO’S for doors, Laminates, moldings, panels and the TUK (touch up kit). Attached is an example of the door PO. The PO’S are what gets sent to our supplier, and I think we spoke briefly about this yesterday but if not only a few of our suppliers are listed, so we also need them added, is that something you can do or do we do that? And if we do it, is it done one by one or can a file be uploaded? - Juan working on this 4/30 Lizette had concerns about: validations/red errors showing when mins/max's reached, said she doesn't want validations, Madison consulting with Juan Next step 4/30: Value add of validations is so you don't have your team spend so much time verifying orders - Juan needs to know which validations they want turned off, Juan can go through and set them to inactive incase they ever want them used for customer facing orders - Juan turning these to inactive 4/30, done Production Order sheet printing in mass daily - Madison sent two options(trigger with zapier or filtering by orders) - email and video followed up 4/29 - https://share1.allmoxy.com/bLubD68X & https://share1.allmoxy.com/geu7lJG8 Interested in setting up UPS integration and weight formulas - need to revisit Process for order entry will be sales, then purchasing orders from suppliers and the production team builds - lead time is 8 weeks How are they differentiating the two different buildings - tagging and production lines suggested but have not set up Laminates and assembly Wood materials and paints Suggest two different production lines(unsure if it makes sense for product level) or order tags(Lizette would add building tags - run orders report by tag)

engagements.body: Regarding email: Allmoxy Service Invoices - Overdue, sent on Monday, November 27, 2023 1:38 PM

QB/Account Conversation 11/30/23 Heriberto, Chris(accounting), Sarah and Madison Invoice payment needed - COMMUNICATE TO HERIBERTO We’re 9 invoices unpaid now. We need an ACH account on file so we can charge the account every Tuesday for the previous week’s services. If we can’t get the payment info we will stop services labor as of next Tuesday until all invoices are paid in full. We have been very vocal with Heriberto and their finance dept but they made promises they didn’t keep such as having a payment method on file. Heriberto updating us by end of day or tomorrow morning @Samantha Alexander @Broc Hill Quickbooks conversations Wants to keep up to date with QB integration as we progress Hanseem doesn't have financial capability, running everything in Hanseem and adding to QB When orders are marked shipped in Allmoxy this is when we want to set up QB(mapping or integration) They're just going to keep remakes in Hanseem for orders that originated in Hanseem and then start running new orders in Allmoxy. Eventually, all of the remakes will come from new orders in Allmoxy and Hanseem will be phased out Services = Remakes for Massachusetts

Weekly Meeting 11/29/23 Aaron, Juan and Madison Heriberto, Lizette, Michael and Grace Augmon met with Mike last Wednesday 11/22 Differentiation between Framed and Frameless lines Once framed line complete then frameless will be cloned and it's a quick process Skew codes the same, just a few cabinets not available in framed lined vs frameless, cloning needs to be done here in order for use on two different production lines Last check on output pages Output pages not related to products still being worked on Labels/bar codes not matching yet - Michael test printed 1)text showed light so want it darker/bigger, 2) a few inches of blank space showing(Juan asked for a picture to email to Augmon) 3)showing extra page Augmon meeting with Lizette tomorrow 11/30 for data clarification Is a print log possible to show when it was printed and who printed it - follow up in recap email Export data still needed from Michael for CSV export Need data from Massachusetts now, waiting on Michael for more info and examples Used for internal Massachusetts information Quickbooks mapping next steps Heriberto and Chris meeting with Madison and Sarah on 11/30 Running orders goal Need to know if export csv needs to be completed in order to run orders Running test order from sales to production this week Heriberto says he's working with Finance to get half of invoices paid asap @Juan Ridgeway @Broc Hill @Samantha Alexander

@Madison Theisen @Juan Ridgeway We need to stop services work on this account until payment is received for services. Broc and I have gone back and forth with them for 2 months trying to resolve their payment method. Waiting to hear back from Heriberto but we need to stress this on the call today. They have 9 open invoices currently

11/21/23 Augmon and Madison Heriberto, Michael and Grace Recording - https://fathom.video/share/Vvhs2DWLxtV_edzsgJSuetsRx8NDYhxP Beginning of December want to start running orders Integrating to Quickbooks Quickbooks Mapping Tools: Saasant - https://www.saasant.com/ TransactionPro - https://importer.transactionpro.com/ Wants to finish production and shipping before tackling accounting Need to bring in account POC Catalog Status/Output Pages: Augmon finished output pages and team tested, going through on this call to get feedback from Mass team Feedback: @Augmon Jolley pulling in @Juan Ridgeway Variables to include: Production date, delivery date, customer, delivery method Labels need to be printable Multiple requests for adding to labels request Bar code needs to scan for a unique item for every single label, Augmon checking with Juan QR code - number next to QR code is what scanner is looking for, Michael says it should be okay to do as bar code but checking with manager and will get back to Augmon tomorrow Comment section to show up "All Parts" - Cabinet label tab and parts label tab Group attributes to show once on PDF Printable PDF to establish line breaks Exports Discussed creating a CSV export, on product create a part that will export certain data Asked for what info to be exported and in which way, Michael sending Augmon an example Next Steps: Meeting tomorrow afternoon to test feedback on output pages - Augmon and Michael

11/15/23 Sarah, Augmon and Madison Heriberto, Michael and Grace Beginning of December want to start running orders Integrating to Quickbooks Transactionpro and Sassant mapping tools Wants to finish production and shipping before tackling accounting Catalog Status update: Augmon halfway through output pages and letting Massachusetts production team review later this week, will either schedule meeting Friday or early next week

Meeting 11/10/23 Production team - Michael Heriberto and Lizette Juan, Augmon and Madison Catalog Status: Current work Augmon is doing Finished ends pricing - Augmon needs more clarification on Friday call On off product and pricing for finished ends - review in meeting Friday @Augmon Jolley dissected production pages with Massachusetts team and is working on these builds Checking in next week on Wednesday 11/15 Customer might need rescoping for all modifications Augmon is doing @Juan Ridgeway 25 custom attributes and output pages - based on original scope do we need to rescope?

11/1/23 Massachusetts Weekly Meeting & Production Team Training (First half catalog run through, second half production team training) Catalog Updates: Lizette had met with Augmon on Friday(10/27) No updates needed related to the call in this meeting. Lizette is working on sending data Augmon requested and will send that over today. Set meeting with Augmon this Friday. Production Team Training: Grace and Michael from the Production Team attended training Michael schedules orders in system and by hand, sorts labels and distributes to shop floor Sarah covered orders going from bid to ordered status, output pages, bar code scanning and time tracking Notes/Action Items: Desired Output Pages/Cut Lists: Finished ends, finish materials, assembly for cabinets, any GEDs(not automatically part of a cabinet) - @Augmon Jolley Heriberto sending current versions of cutlists to mimic what they currently use Also, Sarah suggested a combo of a cut list and manual export to limit manual data entry Checklist paperwork Adding check off steps/table in order for them to manually track steps as it moves through shop floor - @Sarah Pierce Googlesheet being utilized currently, potentially export out info from Allmoxy to Googlesheet for production lists Two different shops Massachusetts has two buildings and is thinking they would want to differentiate the buildings. Physically the buildings are 10 minutes a part from each other. One shop does cutting for panels and pieces and the other does all wood materials(ex. stain) and then everything is shipped back to other shop for assembly. Sarah suggested order tags to differentiate(Lizette to add tag to order and sort by production line/tag but want to confirm if this is best route...) - @Augmon Jolley to discuss and train with Lizette Friday if this is the best route and then next week we will cover implementing this process with everyone on Wednesday's weekly meeting Two Euro lines and one American classic line - two different lines for assembly and Heriberto is thinking it would be best to label as Euro Line and American Line Bar Code Scanning Completed stage is suggested to add bar code scanning for quality protection step/QA check Discussed adding less tracking stages rather than every stage to limit too much scanning Time Tracking Identify any bottlenecks(need for additional machine/changing of process) Shipping Report used once order is marked completed Automation possibility here - once an order is marked shipped it can email the customer the invoice and add to invoice que for accounting software Printing Labels Discussed output pages, grouping together in batch print and generating labels per product or per order basis(not part level) Part Remakes One off parts added as an attribute for this situation Distinguish in order name that it's a remake and then add one off part and zero out pricing if customer is not charged Could also create output page for one product to show remake and would only show if part was selected... Currently print remakes on different color piece of paper to differentiate Need to follow up on this next week

10/25/23 Catalog Sync - CS/Servcies/Mass Team Augmon, Juan, Sarah, Madison, Lizzette Went over Augmon's product changes/updates Want to make sure we are all confident on directions of catalog Way we have built is off spec guide so far. To mirror current order entry, do we need to pivot? Shared a screenshot of a product from another customers skew catalog(not showing pricing or name) to show the full potential modifications once their products are finished @Augmon Jolley working on updates and modifications specific to this discussion Claim Process - Lizette shared this process and concerns she has with current understanding of Allmoxy. Augmon starting work here and next meeting is Friday. @Augmon Jolley

10/18/23 Lizette Heriberto Sarah Madison Concern about where the build is as far as modifications needed. The customer shared their current system(Hanseem)/screen and explained how they want orders/output pages to look based on the catalog. Everything they shared is in line with what Allmoxy can do on the functionality end, but we have a concern around if this catalog should be a custom template. It's currently being built on the sku template... The customer doesn't think they are far off but current state needs modifications ASAP as they are wanting to be launched as soon as possible. Ultimately,  they need the system to be a seamless transition and usable for the sales team as bottom line for launch. Need to set up a meeting with Juan, Augmon, Mass team, Sarah and Madison - @Augmon Jolley @Juan Ridgeway Can you join a meeting next Wednesday(10/25) at 11am MST? Internal: It's worth noting this customer has been very hard to get information from and has only had a sales person assigned for a technical POC during the catalog build. Many calls no one on the customer side took the lead and it was a lot of silence. It's potentially less modifications than Sarah and I understand, but we need an all hands on deck meeting with the customer so we don't risk building any further with a lack of information

Next meeting: Want to cover output pages - "We are looking in wanting to print out work orders for the “in progress” status soon. But when we go to sample a work order it seems we are missing parts." Answer any lingering questions from re-make conversations/modifications with Lizette Check in if Mass sent list to Augmon for "add on" products and cover making them only visible to employees and on orders and remakes

Working Session w/Services & CS Augmon, Madison, Gavin Lizette and Heriberto 10/11/23 More order form training needed for Lizette Augmon covered product specific questions and requests Part remakes Using one off parts within categories Need list of what parts they would need to be listed here - List of products, if any drop downs are necessary or fields Don't want to have one of parts showing to customers, need to utilize the employer tag

Onboarding Completed - Now meeting as needed until launch 10/3/23 Madison & @Gavin Flitton supporting call Most of the focus is on the catalog build out now, so we're moving our calls to a monthly/as needed cadence now and I'm joining more of the services calls to stay on the same page with the build out and address instance questions that arise in real time during those calls Asked about remakes when an order is messed up Discussed how "customers"/employees of Mass. can request remakes in Allmoxy and showed in instance. If they split cost with dealer of remake they can notate in name Wants to know if you can do part remakes of products. Explained this is not possible now but is a feature request and can explore work arounds. Workarounds discussed: - need to discuss further with @Augmon Jolley Convert to itemized order and put as "other costs" Create part/supply as finished product for the customer to choose to add - heavily services Asked about adding as supplies as skew - services Set meeting for Mass team with Augmon next week. Gavin and I will join to further explore remake solutions

We are trying to get payment dialed in with Massachussets Design as the owner is pushing back on a payment method on file. They submitted the payment of the initial services (invoice 4169 in harvest) but it is not showing up in stripe as received. As far as we know, they have not sent the second round of service payments over yet (invoices 4181 and 4195). They now have another balance due as well that needs to be paid off. We have established that we need a form of payment on file and they are aware of this. We need to get their payment method and track down the initial payment, then get all of the invoices cleared and up to date. @Broc Hill for visual

Onboarding Working Session Agenda/Notes: 9/27/23: Catalog Status: Met with Augmon last week and has action items from his recap email Okay to launch with frameless line(launching in phases) Have a product request and need pricing - @Augmon Jolley please see snippet from recording here - https://fathom.video/share/h/3qS6tNPs_8uurwCze7-XD4681hNCwUNr What they want to cover today: -How to add Vendors in the supply tracking system? -Set up stripe - @Augmon Jolley the customer is set up with stripe but doesn't expect to be utilizing stripe as they will not be ordering themselves through Allmoxy. They are utilizing QB currently for tracking and know about the upcoming API. -Start working on training for launching - their frameless SKU line is about complete. - referring to order processing for skew line(identify any gaps running through A to Z) Add in multiplier, discount rate(Price adjustments, tags and priority) Lizette running point on this type of work and confirming all orders with dealers Discussed projected shipping and creating a trigger internally versus work schedule report Philip is choosing to work out of Work Schedule Report versus a trigger for verified every verified order Next week make sure they are comfortable to move to monthly cadence and introduce what that will look like/success plan.

engagements.title: Follow up via email or call post sales call
Churned customer re-engagement consideration
engagements.body: See Churn playbook and retention notes for full context. For more information reach out to the former assigned CSM.

Churn Playbook Please select all of the reasons that apply for why the customer is churning. Add any additional churn context in the note - the more the better here. If there's escalation notes that exist on the account be sure to reference them and include the data of the escalation note in the playbook context. Features, Failed Implementation Notes: 1. Implementation & Development Process Breakdown Expectation: David anticipated a collaborative, phased catalog build — developing and reviewing product sections one at a time (e.g., walls, then bases, etc.). Reality: The build process, primarily handled by Juan, was executed as a full catalog all at once with minimal discussion or review until after completion. Impact: This approach caused significant delays, inefficient reviews, and eroded confidence early in the project. The perceived “black box” style of development led to frustration and loss of trust. 2. Inadequate Quoting Capabilities for Sales Team Major Concern: Sales reps could only generate quotes at list pricing, with no built-in mechanism to adjust or factor pricing to stay competitive with dealers. Process Gap: To generate competitive quotes, reps had to manually calculate discount factors outside the system, relying on Excel-based tools — defeating the purpose of a digital platform. Outcome: Sales team was confused, lacked confidence in the tool, and couldn't effectively quote or win business, making Allmoxy ineffective for a core business need. 3. Lack of Internal Confidence Across Teams Customer Service Issues: Carla (Customer Service Manager) had to manually verify and correct product configurations post-order due to catalog inconsistencies. This created extra workload and delayed adoption. Sales Team Pushback: When introduced to the tool, the sales team did not feel confident using it, largely due to quoting limitations and confusion during the rollout. Leadership Confidence Lost: Without internal buy-in from customer service or the sales organization, David felt he couldn’t confidently endorse or mandate Allmoxy’s use. Change the Customer Lifecycle property to "Other" and create a task for Kylie to review after 1 year. Other Additional properties: Pipeline

Demo Playbook 2.0 Who's on today's call? (Update contact levels for each contact: Buyer Persona and Buyer Role) Notes: David - Sales and Marketing VP What product lines do you currently manufacture? Man. - Doors, Man. - Full Cabinets Of the product lines you manufacture, which are you wanting to run through Allmoxy? Are you wanting to sell all of those product lines through Allmoxy or just one/a few to begin? Notes: Starting one line of doors through Nova south Eventually will do another agreement and catalog for WF cabinetry and implement those products and copy over the door line to make a b2b. connection of those products. Are you outsourcing anything or manufacture everything in-house? Cabinets and Molding and Trim: Is your offering a semi-custom repeatable(most of the jobs are repeatable other than some changes) or is this a Pinterest worthy/Martha Stewart level custom product offering? Notes: Everything manufactured in house for the most part What optimization programs are you using, if any? Cabinet Vision, NetSuite Who is your ideal end customer? Notes: Builders, cab shops, gc's What is your current process? (From order entry, to production and inventory tracking and shipping and delivery) - tell me about this. Notes: Everything is very manual, order entry people do all of the orders and input them What gaps are you experiencing in your current process? What pain points are you looking for Allmoxy to solve? (Select top outcome and add additional in notes.) Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) Notes: Automate orders, organize systems, online store What is your pricing structure and do you have documentation of pricing? Notes: sqft pricing, all documentation provided How are you currently managing inventory and is inventory something you're interested in tracking through Allmoxy? Notes: not wanting to track through Allmoxy Additional features customer liked or wants to utilize? Triggers, Validations, Price Adjustments, Cutlists, Part Lists and Labels, Payment Processing How much revenue do you run through each product line? Notes: doors 0-1 million Cabinets (dfferent product line/agreement) 50 million Are the products this company is offering in line with Allmoxy's view of a Target Account? True How ideal of a fit is the customer?(1 Highest and 3 Lowest) Tier 1

(wood doors) Made-to-order cabinetry - semi-custom cabinets Opti - 2020/insight, pro kitchen, cabinet vision Currently Process:  receiving the orders through the design, cs rep receives the order, verifies it, goes back to the customer, creates a folder, sends it to order entry (manual), proofed by two more people, then after these steps it is sent to the customer for first acknowledgment, also gets kicked to credit at this time then moves to production, then scheduled and produced. Price based on the item Base 30 is one price and base 36 is another price Shipping cost is calculated per product and by where it's going.

customers.name: Precision Elements
customers.churn_reason: Moved to Other Solution; Paid and Never Used

engagements.title: Churned customer re-engagement consideration
engagements.body: See Churn playbook and retention notes for full context. For more information reach out to the former assigned CSM.

Churn Playbook Please select all of the reasons that apply for why the customer is churning. Add any additional churn context in the note - the more the better here. If there's escalation notes that exist on the account be sure to reference them and include the data of the escalation note in the playbook context. Moved to Other Solution, Paid and Never Used Notes: Customer churning. They are a gym membership customer paying 600/ month with no ROI (they are always too busy to get Allmoxy up and running). We offered a subscription pause and a few hours of implementation on the house and they still don't want to move forward. Total spend on Allmoxy was 24,000. IF this is the customers main/only instance that's churning, change the Customer Lifecycle property(company property) to "Other." DO NOT DO THIS if this is a second instance and the churn does not completely eliminate their status as a customer. Other What's the catalog implementation status? No

Churn Risk Playbook Please share the most up to date health pulse from the CSM's perspective. Red(this customer is not in good standing and is on track to churn) Has this customer asked to pause or cancel their account or is this playbook being utilized preemptively? Requested to pause Notes: Summary: Gym membership customer, never launched and paying around 600 a month for no ROI. Not really on us but he needs to make a decision to keep Allmoxy or find something else. This is one I think we can save! Meeting Summary The client expressed significant concerns about the lack of return on investment from their ongoing use of Allmoxy, having spent approximately $24,000 with little to no revenue generated. They indicated dissatisfaction with its capabilities compared to a full MRP/ERP system and are considering pausing the subscription while they reevaluate their technology stack and operational needs. A pause is being requested, and both parties agreed to reconnect after the holidays to assess the potential future of the partnership. Key Takeaways Important Discussions The client has invested $24,000 into Allmoxy with minimal tangible ROI and is currently paying $600/month. They originally hoped to use Allmoxy as a sales tool and for stock product management but now feel it falls short as a full MRP solution. The client’s CFO advised pausing Allmoxy usage as there's no immediate revenue opportunity, and internal resources are limited. The client emphasized the need for a single system for manufacturing to avoid inefficiencies caused by running multiple platforms. Technical Issues & Fixes The client requires advanced job-level time tracking (e.g., scan-in/scan-out) which their current system doesn't support. They are currently using barcode scanners for inventory but are evaluating broader MRP options. Allmoxy’s functionality is seen as geared more towards basic stock products than custom manufacturing processes. Allmoxy's integration capabilities with Zapier and webhooks were discussed as potential solutions for interoperability with other platforms (e.g., Katana MRP, QuickBooks Online). The automation webinar scheduled for next week was recommended, especially for the client’s tech lead to attend. Decisions Made The rep will advocate for a 2-month pause (January & February), understanding the financial and operational context. The client’s in-house tech team member will review the automation webinar to evaluate future integration paths. Both sides agreed to reconnect after the holidays to reassess direction and alignment with Allmoxy. Challenges or Concerns Pricing in the current Allmoxy catalog is outdated and may be impacting profitability. Client feels the platform isn’t currently providing sufficient value to justify continued monthly costs. Client is considering multiple tools (Katana, QuickBooks Online, Tama) and is unsure if Allmoxy can remain part of the stack. Concern about continued payment without corresponding business value or usage. Action Items Allmoxy rep to submit a request for a pause on billing, ideally covering January and February. Client's tech team member to attend the Allmoxy automation webinar. Implementation team to potentially provide 1–2 free hours to review product catalog, pricing, and attributes. Allmoxy team to review client’s current product offerings and pricing setup. Both teams to discuss possible integration with Katana MRP if supported by Zapier. Next Steps Rep to follow up next week on the pause request status and confirm the final duration. After the holiday season, schedule a strategy session to: Review implementation progress and any necessary system adjustments. Reassess the value of continuing Allmoxy usage. Explore system integration options to support job tracking, costing, and inventory management. Client to internally evaluate the future tech stack (QuickBooks, Tama, Katana, etc.) and decide whether Allmoxy can still fit within the broader strategy. How much is the customers monthly subscription? Please note if the customer has received any reductions in MRR during their lifetime with Allmoxy as well as any significant, recent implementation costs. $598.80 Industry Segment (Data Confirmation) Custom Cabinet Shop Business Size (Data Confirmation) SMB How long have they been a customer of Allmoxy? 01/24/2022 Is this customer launched? No Is this customer working with our implementation team now, if so please elaborate on the nature of the work/project. No Is this customer a legacy/strategic account? No Has the customer proposed any solutions that would eliminate or delay their churn risk? This could be a price reduction or delivery on a specific feature. Notes: Yes, they are a gym membership customer paying ~600 month since 2022. (20,000) They want to pause and re-engage with our team to see what they can do to get their products to where they need to be. Currently everything is pricing really low. Please share any other strategic context that we should consider related to this account. (Ex. Industry influence/connections, personality details, known expansion opportunity, competitor tech stack, etc.) Notes: He hired a new technical person that will help with building automations to other software (Katana) Who's the main customer contact associated with this escalation? Clifton For reporting purposes, please check that this playbook is completed. Yes

Churn risk 10/30/24 Last login 10/1/24 Not launched I spoke with Clifton in May and had a good conversation, he really wants to get Allmoxy up and running He left off with Gavin and Juan trying to get info from him but he never got back to them Asked if there was anything we can do with the price, Kenzi & Broc proposed new agreement with the first 3 months at no cost so he can build the catalog Hasn't gotten back to either of us

Re-engagement call 5/8/24 Really wants to get Allmoxy going again, has been spending every month and hasn't built out his catalog yet. He acknowledged that we left off at Gavin and Juan trying to get some information for the catalog from him. He didn't respond to those emails or requests. Looks like he has been paying roughly $600/month but hasn't used the system @Mekenzi Falslev he was wondering if there was anything we could do from a sales perspective to alleviate some of the cost of the catalog build. I didn't promise anything but said I would ask and follow up with him. From there we could maybe do a re-scope with Juan if needed He is on the fence about canceling, and would rather get the ball rolling again

CS Onboarding Playbook Is this a VIP/Legacy customer?(If you're unsure check with leadership) No Is our Services team being utilized for this build? No Notes: gym member customer Who is the main POC within the customer? Clifton or John Confirm the customers desired Outcome for using Allmoxy Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) When is the customers goal launch date? 03/03/2024 Notes: gym member customer What cadence of meeting schedule did you set with the customer? Notes: none Ask the customer to define their benchmark for success unknown What Software(s) does the customer have? How will Allmoxy work with these softwares? Unknown Any initial concerns with the customer and their ability to launch(personnel, resources available, understanding of product, etc?) Notes: gym member customer (lack of engagement)
@Gavin Flitton @Josh Baker Is this one where we want to reach out to?

Meeting 10/3 Juan and I met with Clifton. Here is what we discussed: Phase 1 of the catalog was completed by the services team after working with John Davies. Talked about the benefits and disadvantages of SKU vs custom. Clifton was looking for advice here and for us to be the industry experts here. Clifton expressed wanting to get things up and running quickly to get value from the system. Juan talked about the pricing formula for custom products. Juan expressed that the most efficient way to get things running would be to 0 out all formulas and create a new master formula that would apply to all products. Clifton said that usually, they price by the lineal foot. Talked about inventory and what comes into play there. Talked about payments and Stripe. Juan needs spreadsheet with materials and retail pricing Include poles pricing We are going to do SKU catalog and shoot for launch by the end of october

engagements.title: Build out Thermofoil Doors
engagements.body:
When a customer calls us for thermal foiled products, we typically need the following:

1) Door Style

2) Door Size for each door

3) Thermal Foil color choice (attached) need the full list and pricing on there

4) Do they want hinges bored? What type? How many? Hole placement? We provide hinges?

5) Color matched backs? Closest back? White back? Black back?

We also provide other thermal foil products.

1) 1/4 - 3/4 panels flat press 1 side all edges (1.5 inches wide by 96 inches long is the smallest)

2) 1/4 - 3/4 panels 2 sided no edges

3) 1/4 - 3/4 panels custom shapes and sizes

4) 1/4 - 3/4 panels full wrap (closest back, white, black, color matched?)

Currently we would like to offer:

1) 15-20 door styles

2) Ability to request a custom door style

3) Edge Profile default choice (1/16, 1/8, 1/4, 3/8)

4) Ability to add as many doors/panels as necessary

5) Ability to customize edge profile on any door (so default edge when door style is chosen, but can change edge on any door necessary and it overrides default)

6) Ability to change color/texture on a per door/panel basis.

7) Thermal foil color/texture automatically changes sqft pricing appropriately. (ie. solid colors are cheaper than gloss etc.)

engagements.body: I had a conf. call with Brian, Jamie and Phil. I reiterated what had already been discussed in email. They are thinking about how serious they are in building the bridge.
@Sarah Pierce

3D Meeting 12/13/24 They see a gap in the market that aren't looking for 300K kitchen but good quality They want to put together a standard but nice library that is open to the public A whole line of garage cabinets and a whole thing of closets Luxury homes is their main customer They might just want to wipe the slate clean He wants to pull in our template and work from there Garage cabinets are going to be frameless but limited to TFL Scrap what they have built, in the big cab projects that goes on for a year, Allmoxy isn't the best for. Lester worked at Panhandle for 10 years They do want to remain custom They are targeting higher end luxury, 6 figure cabinet projects. They have learned a lot. A lot isn't hard to do. But you can get fantastic cabinets that you can afford. He can expect designers could become clients. Designers may not like this as much, but contractors and cabinet shops would find value in it. The designers strength is simplicity. Contractors that would normally be buying a box cabinet, this would be awesome for. New router and bander have been put in, they are getting more efficient They could do more with their capacity. Everyone up there buys box cabinets, they can build a way better cabinet than a box cabinet and for cheaper They get their doors from Panhandle, they could B2B their doors. They use Mozaik for nesting Not super robust (name, width, height, depth, and some of the materials) As far as using panhandle for doors and drawer fronts, we don't know if Jeff has Mozaik- Lester doesn't mind manually ordering His thoughts are lets get this going first. Build in iterations. Iteration 1: customizable catalog but a little more standard with 3D Lester will go through the library now Lester to send us what products, materials, colors, etc over to us Going to be limited If a customer wants super custom they can still get that service from Martin Wood products if a customer wants their hands held Can we send him the cabinet template? He doesn't think they are using their current instance at all. He needs to confirm that they can wipe the catalog clean. They are going to brand this a different name. So open a new instance and close Martin Wood products when they are done. Another question, lets say they did really well with this new build They are also a man door/ moulding shop Can that be added later? yes. That could come down the line. Lester will reach out to us for next steps when they are ready. Next steps Lester will come up with a new name, reach out to us We set him up on a new template instance When we get that set up (with 3D) we can phase out the Martin Wood Products instance The new instance will capture the new market

engagements.title: Call with Martin Wood Products
engagements.body:
Called Lester to schedule a renewal.


He said they are trying to decide what direction to go with Allmoxy, they don't feel like Allmoxy will do all the things they need and want it to do due to the complexity of their company. But Lester made the comment that there may be things that we can do for him that he does not know about.


They are trying to re-evaluate what they are wanting and needing and what makes sense. I talked with him about hopping on a call regardless to meet with his Customer Success Manager Josh as he has built out product road maps, etc., and for us to get a firmer grasp on where they are as a company and where they are headed which will allow us to see if/how Allmoxy can be a value add and fit for their business needs and future goals.  Lester agreed to the meeting and was very cordial.


We are scheduled to meet this Friday. The hope is to hear Lesters concerns, questions, etc. and to take the next couple weeks to do some discovery (if needed) then likely have Josh and/or Broc follow up with Lester when he attends the show as he plans to stop by the booth.

engagements.title: Follow up with Kyle on next steps

engagements.body: Retention Call 11/22/24 Kyle, Broc, and Carson Notes: UX never lent to being a direct to consumer system Hurdles and roadblocks along the way As Allmoxy grew our goals changed but Kyles goals remained the same 9 months ago - Jess and Kyle changed gears to make the system work well for internal estimates Last 6 months - sales have tanked so theirs no need for the system Wanted 3D but it's too clunky and doesn't work well in his eyes Remaining work Maybe 20 hours of work left Has been cancelling or reducing other systems as well over the last few months as well. 3 month grace period Need to document what the last touches are for Kyle to be able to use the system after the pause Schedule meeting with Kyle and Jess for January Pause starting November (pausing Nov 25th payment) and ending January - Payment resume in Feb. Meeting Recording: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/The-Cabinet-Face-Allmoxy::56kC0CJDZwzixPmt Call Summary: In the recent Customer Support and Account Management meeting regarding The Cabinet Face's implementation of Allmoxy, key challenges were discussed, particularly Kyle's struggles with the platform since 2019 and the shift in Allmoxy's business focus that has hindered progress towards a direct-to-consumer system. Financial constraints prompted Kyle to request a 2-3 month pause on subscription payments, which was agreed upon by Brock and Carson, allowing for a grace period through January, with access to the system remaining intact during this time. The meeting emphasized the completion of a catalog with over 500 SKUs and the development of an efficient estimating process. Next steps include Kyle reconnecting with Jessica and Carson in January to finalize system implementation, while Carson will enhance his involvement in future discussions, ensuring all decisions are documented and communicated effectively. Action items were assigned to ensure continuity and clarity moving forward. Action items Kyle and Jessica Reconnect in January to discuss remaining system implementation tasks (32:55) Carson Join the meeting between Kyle and Jessica in January to understand final implementation steps (29:58) Send a recap email including the recording and payment pause details (31:20) Broc Send a summary approving the payment pause amount and timelines (31:59) @Mekenzi Falslev @Broc Hill for vis.

engagements.title: Call with Kyle Kwiatkowski
engagements.body:
The biggest thing is to agree to move forward or not, he can't even pay the people to work on it for him. He can't even figure out how if they can pay to get


Allmoxy is no longer doing the buildout, he was strong-armed into using a third party.

engagements.title: Call with Kyle Kwiatkowski
engagements.body:
Kyle has requested to put his account on pause for a few months due to slow business and the inability to market as much as they'd like. I spoke with Jess Ampleman who is doing their buildout and she said there isn't anything they are waiting on Allmoxy for to propel them forward.


After speaking with Broc, we agreed to offer a free two-month pause with the acknowledgment that they would move into an agreement. I provided the first 2 at no cost and the remaining 10 at $300 per-month. Additionally, I went over the new pricing model and what it typically looks like for customers. Kyle was hesitant to sign an agreement as he still doesn't know if he is going to get a functioning system. He understandably doesn't want to get locked in only to find out that Allmoxy won't work for them.


Kyle has put in about 30k into the system over 5 years and still hasn't used it, he said he was "promised the world" when they signed up for Allmoxy. When Kyle initially approached Beau about Allmoxy he said he wanted to create a direct-to-consumer system. They have put much effort into the system which hasn't yielded high results. A part of it has to do with the slowness of the season and the service they are providing to their customers but from what Kyle said they have been hitting a lot of roadblocks with their build-out.  I asked if they have a realistic timeline for launch and he said he is completely unsure. Kyle said he still doesn't know if it will turn out well for a DTC system. This was interesting info to hear because when we spoke over the phone initially he said there was nothing Allmoxy was doing to hold them back from launching/Jess said we didn't need to do anything on our end. So those things don't align with what Kyle had mentioned on our call.


I let him know it might be a good idea to have a expectations realignment/strategy meeting with Kyle, Jess, Carson, and whoever else makes sense to figure out what the best next steps are. The next subscription payment comes out on the 25th. If we could meet before then that would be ideal. Kyle also requested if we don't meet before then that we waive this month's payment.


Next steps:


Kenzi is going to get with Carson on this situation and figure out who makes sense to involve in a meeting for the best next steps and expectations regarding alignment.


@Carson Ward @Broc Hill for visibility

Premier Custom Millwork - Cancellation Meeting Takeaway 1/2/25 Context: After Jesse's initial request to cancel, Sarah and I scheduled time with him to discuss the main obstacles he presented in hopes to keep him from wanting to cancel. His original objections were the cost & time for services work, the remaining product lines that would need to be completed for full system usage, and pricing flexibility. 01/02/25 Meeting Recording: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Premier-Custom-Millwork-Allmoxy::z4BqlC8hMtM2uzwe Throughout the conversation with Jesse, Sarah and I got a lot more insight into these issues: Services Cost & Time - His catalog doesn't have all of the products he would need to run both sides of his business and the lift it would take to get this implemented is beyond what he's able to do right now. Custom Door & Millwork businesses - Each of his businesses operate off of his current system (excel pricing sheet) and he doesn't want to manage two separate systems. From Jesse's view the time and energy it would take to move both businesses to using Allmoxy would be at least another year or more. Bandwidth - Jesse has tried to implement Allmoxy Since October of 2022 and hasn't been able to get there partially due to his own lack of time. He'll get into a sprint and we'll make some progress but then he'll go silent for a few months and then we try to pick up the pieces again. He recognizes this falls on him. We asked Jesse what his thoughts are on tabling Allmoxy and doing a short-term pause (3 months) and then revisiting or if the lift is too great to just cancel as a whole. Jesse felt that it'd be better to just cancel. Contract Status: Monthly payments - $899 on 13th of each month Payment history - Never missed a payment in the 27 months since signing Total Paid: (Oct 22' - Dec 24') Subscription: $23,624 Services: $16,911 Total: $40,535 Since Jesse is still technically under contract until May he would need to pay out the remaining 75% of his contract amount which would be $2,967. However, after discussing with Sarah, our thoughts are that we should wave this cancellation fee since he's been so consistent with payment and still never got any value from utilizing the Allmoxy system. I think this would go a long way for Jesse and show that we truly do care about our customers. @Madison Theisen @Beau Lewis - please let me know how we should proceed with Jesse's request and what should come of the remaining 4 months. @Sarah Pierce for vis.

Retention Meeting 1/2/25 (Sarah, Carson, and Jesse) Context: Jesse requested to cancel his Allmoxy account and shared these main objections as to why: Due to the $150/per hour service fees the time needed to build out the rest of our product lines the limitations in flexibility of pricing (aka - freeform pricing) Jesse is still under contract until May so he would still owe Jan - Apr ($3,600) Goal: Have Allmoxy train his in-house technical person how to build/edit products so he can get the benefits from Allmoxy and without wasting additional investment. (cancellation fee 75% contract amount $$2700) Agenda: Better understand the obstacles Jesse shared Present recommended route - training someone that works directly for Jesse to operate his Allmoxy catalog Secure a action plan for next steps needed Notes: Delivery vehicle broke down - everyday something comes up Pricing Flexibility Issue with shipping was taxed - has been resolved Pricing structure - daily basis (seat of their pants pricing - Adding percentages to certain line items on an order) Pull back on moving forward wants to go all in or all out (doesn't want to sit between two systems) Door side Every time there's an issue they don't want to have to bring Juan or services back into the fold. Feedback New they didn't fit the exact business model Layout template In discussing with Jesse the challenges he's faced over the last 2+ years in trying to launch, it came down to the following items: Managing two systems - doesn't want to manage two systems but in his view it would take at least another year to build out and set up the catalog to meet the needs of his two businesses (custom doors and millwork) Cost & Time - Currently has to be reliant on services team for new builds or fixing issues, leads to more cost and time. Bandwidth - Jesse himself doesn't have the time he would need to put into Allmoxy to get it to function. There wouldn't be anyone else able to fully set up his pricing and get this functioning the way it would need to in order for it to make sense.

Cancellation Request 12/12/24 Jesse reached out before our scoping meeting with the following: Hello Carson,  Please cancel our meeting today. After careful consideration, we have decided to stop pursuing our build out with Allmoxy. Due to the $150/per hour service fees, the time needed to build out the rest of our product lines and the limitations in flexibility of pricing (aka - freeform pricing), we will be terminating our services with Allmoxy. We have decided to hire an in-house tech guru to build out an excel/access based ecosystem to better fit our values and flexibility. I thank you for all your efforts and wish you well. Please confirm autodraw payment will stop on our account. Account Context: Over the last year I've been reaching out and following up with Jesse to work on completing his catalog so he can utilize Allmoxy. Currently he has his molding product built and we were getting ready to start working on a few other Millwork products. After two previously missed scoping meetings, (due to Jesse missing the meeting), we had finally gotten rescheduled for today 12/12 and I confirmed the morning of that we still had the meeting so Jesse would remember. There are three other products that he needs built out and from Juan's description these products would be easy to complete. Prior to these last few meeting attempts, Jesse did request to have a service provider come in-person to set up and build the remaining products he needed which at the time wasn't a possibility so it seems as though the challenge of scheduling a meeting, along with the cost of each meeting, has deterred him from wanting to move forward. I definitely believe this account could be saved with the right conversation. I can provide as much support as possible. Please advise on how we should move forward. @Madison Theisen @Sarah Pierce @Juan Ridgeway @Bobby Lewis for vis.

engagements.title: Jesse Schmitt, Premier Custom Millwork & Surfaces, Inc- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations
engagements.body: Jesse Schmitt, Premier Custom Millwork & Surfaces, Inc- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations

Services & CS Sync - Planning Agenda: Cover Premier's history (Juan's memory of their builds) What's your memory of working with Jesse? Current state Is extremely busy, running both his doors and millwork businesses. Knows that it's been his issue regarding the build (you'll meet, start working on things and then he'll get busy and fall off) Plan of action Build an outline of what needs to be completed within the catalog for him to run orders. What's missing (pricing, products, etc) Get a realistic timeline based on if information is being shared timely. Work through 30 min meetings and email communication. (If I can be CC'd on these I can help with reminders for Jesse) Next steps We'll need to get a meeting scheduled for all three of us to talk about his exact build status, what parts are missing, and what information he still needs to provide. (if we can share the questions with Jesse ahead of time that would allow us to get certain things answered more quickly) After the meeting I'll outline the steps and I'll basically facilitate and help move the conversations along so we don't get lost again. Notes: Door catalog Built pricing to match his spreadsheet Issue - The doors where built in a way that doors were built in somewhat of a skew with drop downs rather than door ordering (hand typing dimensions) Still didn't like the rebuild so they switched to the molding Millwork catalog/pricing works perfectly and was confirmed with Jesse in a previous meeting Created a prototype of every type of molding (Issue if the master product isn't perfect and then is cloned, it'll create a big issue for all subsequent) Door catalog - Waiting on (putting on back-burner) Millwork catalog - ready, just need him to test the product and ensure it's good for testing.

Call with Jesse ACE Agenda: Intro & background New to Allmoxy - your main POC moving forward Looks like things started earlier this time last year but hit some slowdowns during spring & summer. Met with Juan in July and August. Current challenges/roadblocks preventing order processing What's been the biggest challenge? Time, resources, bandwidth? If you were able to get this operating, what would that mean for you and your team? Stripe not connected, how do you currently process orders with customers? Credit limits, checks, etc? Next Steps Catalog review - Need to confirm the catalog is completed Stripe not connected, how do you currently process orders with customers? Credit limits, checks, etc? Accounts need to be created Test a few customers and have them use the system. - Troubleshoot issues further and then release to all customers. What else would you like to cover? Notes: Solid wood interior and exterior doors Not cookie cutter Challenges Door pricing - hard to build square foot pricing model Bought another business along the way and that has a better pricing model (started bringing this along with Juan but is limited with bandwidth) Swamped - Doesn't have the time to sit down and set up pricing correctly. Feeling stuck - Brings in new employees, something else happens, constantly pulled in multiple directions. Also building a house right now Maintenance on the new business - having to go fix every single issue Catalog Pricing should be correct but there might be a few additional items that need to be added Next step is testing the pricing What would help email communication - dialog back and forth Seasonality 2 businesses Millwork - Casing - local area (B2B cabinetry casing) Base frame material (not sure on the seasonality of this business) Doors - NYC - Season never ends, no real slowdown during holidays

engagements.title: Panel Types and Pricing
engagements.body:
Panel cost:

The easiest may be to start retail prices off as flat panels instead of 1" panels that we currently have as default. Then yes, we can add percentages per below.

1" panel - add 10% over flat panel cost

1 1/4" panel - add 15% over flat panel cost

1 1/2" panel - add 20% over flat panel cost

1 5/8" panel - add 25% over flat panel cost
