Overview During the Allmoxy Sales Q&A Demo, Spencer Turner, representing a custom cabinet door manufacturing company in Northeast Oklahoma, explored how Allmoxy can streamline his operations and enhance customer satisfaction as he aims to expand into the Florida market. The session began with an introduction to the company and outlined current operational challenges, including the need for real-time order tracking and better inventory management. Key features of the Allmoxy system were demonstrated, showcasing its integration capabilities, customizable order entry, real-time pricing, and resource allocation functionalities. The discussion also covered production processes, system maintenance, and the pricing model tailored to company size, providing insights into the potential business impacts, including increased revenue. Next steps were established, with Sam Alexander agreeing to send relevant information to Spencer for further evaluation with his business partner and a follow-up scheduled for the following week. Notes 🏢 Company Introduction and Background (00:00 - 03:57) Spencer Turner's company makes custom cabinet doors Located in Northeast Oklahoma Trying to establish market in Florida Current delivery radius is about 3.5 hours drive Use local trucks for delivery to control breakage 🛠️ Current Operations and Goals (04:03 - 12:06) Use Work Straight for task and work order management Use Door Pro for cut list generation Looking for real-time order tracking for customers Want to improve inventory management Aiming to track work and get information on work orders and cut lists Want to give customers exposure to the process Lost a customer due to lack of order status visibility 📊 Allmoxy Demo Introduction (12:06 - 25:31) Allmoxy integrates with existing website Customers can create accounts and place orders Dashboard shows outstanding balance, invoices, job statuses Order entry system with customizable fields Real-time pricing calculation based on square foot and labor charges Ability to set up different account types (e.g., cabinet shop, dealer) 🔍 Resource Allocation and Inventory Management (25:45 - 34:28) Resource allocation feature shows supplies needed for each job Inventory management with minimum stock levels and automatic reordering Purchase order generation and tracking Ability to receive partial orders and track remaining quantities 📋 Order Documents and Production Process (34:28 - 49:18) Cut list generation for different components (fronts, panels, styles) Barcode scanning for job tracking and employee time tracking Packing slip and invoice generation Integration with optimization software (e.g., Cabinet Vision, Mosaic) Ability to import cut lists from customer's design software 💻 System Functionality and Pricing (50:49 - 01:08:27) Triggers for automating emails and notifications Task assignment system for employees Tablet stations in the shop for job tracking Pricing based on company size (0-1 million, 1-2 million tiers) 15 for 12 pricing model for the first year Implementation cost of $150/hour for catalog building (estimated 70-100 hours for doors and drawer boxes) 🔧 System Maintenance and Data Management (01:08:28 - 01:18:46) System updates handled on the backend Some maintenance required for price adjustments and adding new products Full record of previous orders maintained Data can be exported if needed in the future 📈 Business Impact and Considerations (01:18:57 - 01:24:54) Potential to increase business (example of BNB doubling revenue in 4 years) Concerns about shipping custom-built products Current business serving northwest Arkansas, Tulsa, southeast Kansas, southwest Missouri, and Oklahoma City Consideration of expanding into flat-pack cabinets 🤝 Next Steps and Follow-up (01:24:58 - 01:28:07) Sam to send demo site, subscription agreement, and onboarding steps Spencer to review with business partner Follow-up scheduled for end of next week Spencer interested but needs to ensure it fits business needs Action items Spencer Turner Review Allmoxy demo with business partner to assess fit for business needs (01:26:32) Consider potential for flat-pack cabinet business expansion based on discussion (01:24:19)

05/29/25 - 3D designer Review - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Tahoma-Closets-Allmoxy-3D-Designer-Review::01JW9B1CDJGFR75A966DBBJYWQ Agenda: Aaron to display what's been built in 3D. What's left? Next Steps start testing? Notes: Wyatt can't join - moving equipment Scott here to review Meeting Summary The meeting focused on the ongoing development and testing of closet and cabinet design functionality within Allmoxy, aiming to transition Rainier Woodworking’s team away from their current design tools, such as Cabinet Vision and Rain Dog, and toward a unified Allmoxy workflow. Demonstrations and discussions covered catalog access, product selection in the designer, design visibility, sharing proposals, and workflow integration. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Catalog Navigation & Product Selection: Users can now access a product catalog in Allmoxy's designer via the cabinet icon. The system allows for selecting floor-mounted and color-matched components based on MRP headers. Product Visibility in the Designer: Not all added products show up in the designer; only those designed within the designer (marked with a cube) will appear. Aaron is addressing syncing and visibility issues. Proposal Sharing Methods: There's a share link feature in the designer, but it currently returns errors likely due to unsaved orders or admin session conflicts. This will be investigated further. Product Layout Tools: The designer allows for customizable wall/floor settings and layout previews, aiding in accurate visual representation for customers. Room Configuration Limitations: Currently, rooms are boxed-shaped with configurable dimensions, but overly long dimensions (e.g., 50 ft walls) can appear visually distorted. Adding more walls or dividing elevations may be needed. Technical Issues & Fixes Designer Sync Bug: Some selected items are not yet “talking” to the system. Aaron is working on backend updates to ensure full integration and visibility. 3D Rendering Errors: Certain configurations trigger clipping issues in the designer. Aaron identified integrated ends as a temporary fix. Shared Link Access Errors: Errors arise due to session conflicts or unsaved orders. Needs further debugging and QA. Order Save Requirement: Orders must be saved before certain functions (like link sharing) work properly. Decisions Made Internal Testing Framework: Scott will create a shared Google Sheet for the Rainier team to log bugs/issues rather than sending ad-hoc emails. Aaron will share access via a shared drive. Team Enablement Strategy: Rainier Woodworking’s closet designers will begin using Allmoxy for drawings and internal testing. Goal: eliminate reliance on Cabinet Vision and Rain Dog. Product Expansion: Plan to build out cabinet components (base, tall, wall units) within Allmoxy to support closets, pantries, garages, and laundry rooms. Reactivation of Discontinued Products: Carson confirmed that pre-built cabinet templates exist in the system but were marked discontinued. These will be re-enabled and updated. Challenges or Concerns Legacy Tool Dependency: Designers are defaulting to Rain Dog for pricing and design due to missing cabinet components in Allmoxy. There’s a push to kill Rain Dog by making Allmoxy fully functional. Transition Hurdles: Manual PDF order processing remains a temporary stopgap. The goal is to shift to link-based order sharing. Product Setup Complexity: Proper configuration of options and pricing in the MRP is necessary to ensure accuracy. Visibility Gaps in Testing: Initial test links failed due to system setup issues; must ensure environments are properly staged. Action Items Aaron: Resolve visibility and link sharing bugs in the designer. Enable integration features for 3D rendering. Add cabinet product templates back into the catalog and verify MRP configurations. Prepare for post-surgery handoff and follow-up on product configuration post-recovery. Scott: Share the Allmoxy testing link with the Rainier design team. Create and maintain the Google Sheet for issue tracking. Encourage internal team to adopt Allmoxy for closet and cabinet drawings. Coordinate with Carson for post-testing evaluation and launch readiness. Carson: Send Zoom recording and recap to Scott’s team. Follow up on catalog organization and coordinate upcoming touchpoint. Schedule next meeting for operational review and roadmap planning. Next Steps Rainier Woodworking: Begin full testing of closet design workflows in Allmoxy. Log all bugs and challenges in the shared issue tracker. Follow-Up Meeting: Scheduled for Friday at 8 AM to review operational feedback, discuss further catalog development (especially cabinet products), and confirm implementation timeline. Post-Surgery Coordination: Aaron will be out for surgery but will follow up on all issues upon returning June 11. Product Roadmap: Review existing discontinued templates and determine which cabinet components to reactivate. Begin working sessions to build out and refine new product configurations with Aaron post-return.

04/15/25 - 3D check in meeting Agenda: Aaron demonstrating what's been done so far and the complexity of the backend of 3D. Schedule next check in meeting https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Tahoma-Allmoxy-3D-Designer::01JRGBW5HPN69E2Z0EHTZTFPKX Here’s a fully structured and formatted summary of your meeting, ready for pasting into HubSpot: Meeting Summary This meeting served as a progress check-in between the Allmoxy team (Carson, Scott) and Aaron Dundas to review early-stage backend and visual developments for the 3D Designer integration. The session focused on explaining the architectural foundation of the 3D system, its connection with the MRP, current limitations, and upcoming steps for configuration. A demo preview was provided to help visualize how the system will eventually function, although the full 3D interactivity is not yet ready. The team discussed catalog preparation, attribute mapping, image configuration, and the need for centralized texture and product libraries. Key Takeaways Important Discussions The current 3D designer implementation is significantly different from the MRP's setup. All products must be manually configured and mapped between the MRP and the 3D system. Visual previews and configuration interfaces are in development but not fully interactive yet. Users will access the 3D space directly through the MRP interface via an "Add 3D Order" option. The 3D system supports custom textures and panels, but users must provide matching images and data. Aaron showcased how the configuration system will allow selection of textures, construction methods, and product variants like drawers and cabinet types. Technical Issues & Fixes Mapping attributes and selections from MRP to 3D requires synchronization through integration settings. Data flows one-way (MRP → 3D), meaning updates on MRP must be pushed manually to update the 3D space. The current system lacks public-facing tools to directly add or manage catalog records in 3D; backend involvement is required. There are visibility discrepancies in how certain parts (like end panels or fixed shelves) appear in the 3D visualizations vs. actual construction logic—this will need to be accounted for during product configuration. Decisions Made The Allmoxy team prefers to batch product and attribute updates for efficiency and minimal processing delays. Floor-mount cabinet base height will default to 96.5", with 32mm increment adjustments per Cabinet Vision standards. End panels should be treated as separate assemblies for greater construction accuracy in the 3D space. Future check-ins will be based on milestones—next meeting will occur when full 3D interaction is possible, not before. Challenges or Concerns Aaron noted that allowing clients full CRUD access to their 3D catalog is not currently feasible due to backend complexity and risk. Scott expressed a desire for more ownership over the 3D catalog content, ideally with the ability to create/edit/delete products independently. Action Items Task Owner Details Due Date Continue configuring and mapping 3D products to MRP attributes Aaron Dundas Include image matching, attribute syncing, and ensuring MRP alignment Ongoing Finalize drawer configurations in the MRP Carson, Scott Needed to prepare matching 3D representations This week Provide updated cabinet vision file Carson To align Aaron’s configuration with the latest specs ASAP Begin documenting construction details for each cabinet type Scott To prepare a future full catalog with locked/adjustable notes Upcoming Prepare and send texture files for custom rendering Allmoxy team Will enable realistic wood grain/pattern visuals in 3D space TBD Next Steps Aaron will continue configuring the catalog, mapping attributes, and preparing the system for live 3D interactivity. Allmoxy team will focus on finalizing MRP data (e.g., drawers, base sizes, etc.) and preparing visual assets. The next meeting will be scheduled once live 3D interaction is ready, allowing drag/drop demos and round-trip MRP validation. Long-term goal includes building a complete cabinet/closet catalog with detailed construction references for each configuration. Let me know if you want a condensed version or want to tag this summary to a specific HubSpot record.

04/08/25 - 3D Designer Meeting - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Tahoma-Allmoxy-3D-Designer::01JR60S7EXA4X26ZASZFFJ0WT6 3D designer asset folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1GvnvTU4hhiU8-mPMXfDf4KRfizl3F8XA Meeting Summary Purpose: This meeting was initiated to align Allmoxy and the Tahoma Closet team on 3D design integration within Allmoxy, particularly focusing on expectations, capabilities, and initial limitations. Aaron Dundas, from the Allmoxy team, met with Carson Ward and Scott Reader to discuss design constraints, product visualization, and integration workflow between Allmoxy, Cabinet Vision, and 3D designer tools. Context: The goal is to ensure Tahoma Closet's products can be effectively visualized within the 3D designer tool, with proper configuration, manageable expectations, and a streamlined export process to Cabinet Vision for detailed production work. The group also discussed operational logistics, texture and image sharing, and process suggestions for long-term success. Key Takeaways Important Discussions 3D Designer Expectations: The tool is for visualizing standard product configurations, not for custom CAD-level modifications. Users will drag and drop pre-built product models into a 3D room and get basic layout visualization, selections, pricing, and exports. Visualizations will help builders and clients quickly validate room layouts and configurations. Functionality Limitations: Challenges arise when attempting advanced geometric changes (e.g., rotating shelves, integrating shoe fences at angles). Stacking complex functionalities (e.g., drawers that extend like hampers with custom animations) may be inefficient or costly. 3D vs MRP Data Flow: The 3D designer → MRP data flow is one-way. Changes made in MRP will not reflect back in the 3D space unless manually reconfigured. Cabinet Vision Integration: Cabinet Vision will handle precise dimensioning and detailing, while Allmoxy manages visual configurations and order creation. Products will be finalized in Cabinet Vision using standard formats (plan view, cross-section, face view) with dimensioning. Image/Color Asset Sharing: Scott will share a Google Drive folder with high-resolution, borderless texture images for accurate product rendering. Aaron and Carson now have shared access for image imports. Future Touchpoints: A recurring 30-minute weekly check-in is being scheduled to review progress, align on functionality, and validate model behavior. First follow-up meeting set for next Tuesday. Technical Issues & Fixes Height Adjustments & Metric Alignment: Cabinet Vision handles automatic adjustment to metric increments (e.g., 32mm), avoiding fractional errors. Allmoxy will be configured to only allow standard set heights to avoid misalignment during MRP → Cabinet Vision transfers. Drawer Box Logic: Drawer configuration (e.g., one drawer with tall hang above) is feasible, and will be finalized after initial configurations are built. For now, drawer setups will be deferred to avoid wasted effort. Corner Closet Pricing & Configuration: Ongoing discussions on how to price and display 90-degree corner units in Allmoxy. Still under evaluation. Decisions Made Start with Hanging Sections and Shelves: Focus on configuring and validating hanging units first, leaving drawers for a later phase. Standardize Product Configurations: All configurations will use Tahoma's stock standards — no dynamic customizations inside the 3D designer. Texture/Image Format: Only high-res, borderless images will be used for finishes to avoid rendering issues. Product Snapshot & Order Link: Orders from the 3D designer will include visual snapshots and order links for client sharing. Folder Access: Wyatt from Tahoma Closet has been added to the shared folder for asset exchange. Challenges or Concerns Expectation Misalignment Risks: Historical issues with clients expecting CAD-level design behavior from a visual tool were highlighted. Alignment on scope was critical before development begins. Texture Mapping Nuances: Borders on texture images can lead to ugly repeat effects in 3D wrapping — vigilance in asset formatting is required. Cabinet Vision Coordination: Effort is needed to ensure all configurations in Cabinet Vision align with what's visualized in Allmoxy. Action Items Task Owner Deadline / Notes Start configuring hanging and shelving units in 3D Aaron Dundas Immediately Delay drawer configuration until decisions are finalized Aaron Dundas Post initial review Upload high-res, borderless texture images Scott Reader ASAP Share Google Drive folder for asset management Aaron Dundas ✅ Completed Set weekly recurring 3D check-in Carson Ward ✅ First touchpoint next Tuesday Develop Cabinet Vision standard dimensioned drawings Scott Reader In progress Finalize standard heights/increments in Allmoxy Carson + Scott To align with 32mm system Next Steps Tuesday Check-In: Initial follow-up scheduled to review first round of 3D implementations and validate configurations. Image Asset Import: All texture images to be reviewed and added to the system once uploaded. Finalize Drawer Configurations: After initial shelf/hang sections are live, revisit drawer box structure for integration. Continue Cabinet Vision Import Testing: Scott to finish testing group imports and wall growth logic; feedback will shape structure moving forward. Ongoing Collaboration: Weekly meetings will serve as checkpoints to avoid missteps and allow minor course corrections without major rework.

03/24/25 - Export Testing Agenda: Review how export testing went/is going If export is complete - next item is the designer Need Aaron to confirm and clarify what he'll need Notes: Item is coming through but the machining wasn't coming through. Information is coming across and correctly Wants to get the actual products on the wall when importing 3D Defined All attributes have all selections Prod configuration are locked in (1=1 match) https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Tahoma-Export-Live-Testing::01JPZCTCMXXK62N8A7KPY0145X Meeting Summary This session served as a follow-up to prior testing efforts focused on the integration between Allmoxy and Cabinet Vision, particularly around the export functionality and item rendering within design walls. The key objectives were to review progress with wall configuration issues, test item placements, and identify next steps for product setup in the 3D designer. The discussion involved troubleshooting item export behavior, wall scaling, and determining the most effective strategy for placing cabinets properly within Cabinet Vision. Team members explored formula logic, offset calculations, and system limitations, with Aaron providing live adjustments and testing support. The meeting concluded with alignment on remaining configuration steps and preparation for transitioning into designer functionality. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Initial testing confirmed item exports were partially successful but faced challenges with wall scaling and cabinet placement offsets. Team identified that machining failed to fire due to items not being properly tied to materials—this was corrected and verified to work. Wall lengths and placements were initially extreme (e.g., 15,000 inches), and new increment strategies (e.g., 30–50 inches per item) were explored to reduce over-scaling. Discussed using group ID and line ID combinations in export formulas to control item spacing. Attempted to define wall attributes at the product level to simplify scaling but faced limitations in Cabinet Vision due to how walls are interpreted and rendered. Aaron explored potential logic to assign individual offset depths (Z-axis) to walls so they could stack neatly without overlap. Technical Issues & Fixes Issue: Items were not appearing in Cabinet Vision due to incorrect material assignments and proxy logic. Fix: Adjusted the item-to-material mapping and simplified proxy formula to use group ID + line ID. Issue: Multiple walls with the same name were being created in the same location. Fix: Identified Cabinet Vision treats repeated wall names as unique walls; team to consider generating unique wall names or using one long wall. Issue: Cabinets were appearing far off from the wall. Fix: Reworked placement logic to limit offsets and bring items closer to intended positions. Formula Constraints: Certain data points can’t perform math operations in Allmoxy due to contextual limitations. Workaround: Use proxy variables and backups to test placement behavior iteratively. Decisions Made Team will continue using a larger single wall with better spacing logic if individual wall naming introduces too much complexity. Agreed to start building out remaining exports and test them item-by-item. Confirmed that products must be placed on walls for machining to trigger correctly. Will simplify catalog structure by creating separate configurations instead of using attributes to define options (e.g., hang rods vs. shelves). 3D designer development will begin once product selections and configurations are finalized. Challenges or Concerns Cabinet Vision imposes rigid interpretation of wall names and layout, complicating dynamic wall creation. Proxy math limitations restrict real-time wall scaling based on cabinet width or item count. Still unclear why some cabinets failed to appear during later tests, indicating possible missing data (e.g., hinging, finished ends). Need for customer education around 3D designer limitations—visuals may not reflect technical details like added drawers or hardware. Action Items Owner Task Due / Notes Ian Continue testing ORD file with updated exports; validate that cabinets appear and fire machining properly. Ongoing Aaron Explore logic for stacking walls via Z-axis and fine-tune group/line ID offset formulas. In progress Carson Share recording of the session with Ian for reference. ✅ Confirmed All Team Build out export templates for remaining cabinet components and configurations. Next phase Aaron Connect with Bobby to review backend setup for 3D designer. Before next meeting Scott & Team Finalize all configurations and ensure they’re properly defined in Allmoxy. Near completion Ian Begin experimenting with ORD exports via WordPad edits and proxy backups. Encouraged Next Steps Aaron to meet with Bobby to finalize technical requirements for launching the 3D designer phase. Carson to follow up post-Aaron/Bobby meeting to align timeline and expectations for next integration steps. Ian to continue testing with updated ORD exports and refine logic for wall and cabinet placements. Once all exports are validated, transition will begin into the 3D designer setup, ensuring selections and visual representations are consistent. Team to document any issues found during testing and report back with findings and proposed refinements.

03/17/25 - Export Live Testing https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Tahoma-Export-Live-Testing::01JPDC18RHPF5EXF04GBNS89SS Meeting Summary The meeting focused on troubleshooting and refining the export process between Allmoxy and Cabinet Vision. The goal was to ensure that the data transfers correctly, with accurate materials, selections, and wall assignments. Aaron Dundas led the discussion, providing insights into how the exports function and addressing specific issues that were encountered during testing. Key Takeaways Export Process & Troubleshooting The team reviewed how Allmoxy generates exports and the structure of the Cabinet Vision ORD file. Issues with unnecessary parameters in the export were identified and addressed. The team discussed moving catalog tags to the header to streamline the export structure. Wall Assignments in Cabinet Vision A dynamic wall-length solution was implemented, ensuring all items are assigned properly within a predefined space. It was confirmed that walls will be created dynamically with a standard width, accommodating all items without manual adjustments. Adjustments were needed to correct item placement within the 3D space in Cabinet Vision. Material & Selection Mapping Ensured that order-level selections (e.g., box materials, drawer box materials) are correctly assigned in Cabinet Vision. Identified a discrepancy where default selections were pulling from the wrong dataset, causing material mismatches. Adjustments were made to ensure the export correctly maps to the correct material codes in Cabinet Vision. Drawer & Door Style Configurations Discussed aligning selections such as slab vs. five-piece drawer fronts to correct variables. Confirmed that adjustments to the export file would allow for accurate representation of selected materials and styles. Next Steps in Testing The team will manually test the updated export file to verify if the changes resolve the material mismatches. Adjustments will continue to be made for aligning product selections with the correct catalog in Cabinet Vision. A follow-up meeting was scheduled for next Monday to review progress and address any remaining issues. Action Items Aaron Dundas Adjust export file to ensure selections map correctly to Cabinet Vision presets. Remove redundant standard portions of presets to prevent incorrect default selections. Send updated export file through Carson for further testing. Scott & Team Conduct further testing with the revised export file. Verify that material selections and wall placements function correctly in Cabinet Vision. Identify any additional discrepancies in selections and share findings before the next meeting. Carson Ward Distribute the meeting recording and export file for testing. Schedule and facilitate next week’s follow-up meeting. Next Steps The team will spend the week testing the revised exports. Any findings or unresolved issues will be reviewed in the follow-up meeting next Monday. Further refinements will be made based on test results to ensure seamless integration between Allmoxy and Cabinet Vision.

Kickoff Call Confirm the key stakeholders: - Know who signed the contract(Executive Buyer) before the call(from sales) and if/how they will be involved in onboarding -Who will be the key point of contact for the build? -Sales POC? -Production POC? -Financial/Accounting POC? -Any other stakeholders? What were they hoping to achieve when they signed the contract and how does Allmoxy fit into that? If their answer is too broad, ask them to expand on how they envision their business changing when they purchased Allmoxy. Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) Notes: Rainier Woodworking is the parent company of Tahoma Closet and Tahoma is a newer venture for them. Wants a more customer friendly user interface, needs the front end. Wants to be able to price and provide proposals and track opportunities. Has a dealer network they want to give access to Allmoxy so they can submit their own orders while using the "drawing tool" 3D designer. When does the customer want to "launch" and what would a successful onboarding look like? How do you know when you're there? Ex. Door Product Line is Launched and Cabinet Line is started. Notes: Successful onboarding will be having a complete catalog and 3D matching catalog that they can use to submit a proposal to a customer.

Let them know we resolved to allow them out under our cancellation terms of 75% of remaining months contract and if they choose to return we will return this cancellation amount in form of a subscription credit for their return.

Retention Meeting 2024 Broc, Kenzi, Josh, Yasir, and Paul in attendance. Hallmark home interiors signed 6/6. The agreement started 7/30 they still have 11 payments at $699 left to pay. So a total of $8,388. Buying out at 75% would be $6,291. Broc says we will look and see what we can to do mitigate the cancellation amount to have a strategic relationship with Hallmark. Broc and Kenzi will work together with one of the stake holders to come up with some options for Paul to exit his agreement. Kenzi will include on the cancellation email, there was one last thing we weren't able to cover on the call. These are the accounts that are using Hallmark, what is the strategy and what are the resources we can use to help, essentially whats the plan for the B2b accounts using Allmoxy. Kenzi can't reference their names in the email. Josh says that Intermountain Wood Products in Oregon is reselling Hallmark. Sam says that there are 3 agreements pending signature to work with Hallmark on the B2B level... They have enjoyed their relationship with Allmoxy and have appreciated us working to get them what they need. API- how much longer will they need access? They are currently not using API through Allmoxy How much longer will they need access to the Allmoxy site itself? Paul says 90 days will probably be good just to be safe. On the 2020 side of things it's them not us. There were a number of things that led to this. The aspect of showing people stock inside their units, they needed to think how far they wanted to go down that trail. They write the APIs for Pro Kitchen and Net Suite. How do they do an internal check on stock that they are doing? Another issue is the credit function. They need to have that labeled in. We have it but not quite what they need. They have had to weigh how long and how much it would cost for them to do this. They are not ruling out coming back to Allmoxy. It really comes down to performance. End of September, beginning of October they will have stock in house to work with and that's really when they will start moving the needle. Showing stock within their units- what does this look like? They have 25 thousand boxes in-house, within that 25k they have multiple doors and colors to work with and they stock those in one door one skid. To check stock they would need to have continuous updates on the skus. They have to put together a configurator to display what they have. We have said we could work with it but the cost is what we would need to explore. They currently have 5 colors in stock. They are moving to thermofoil which is another aspect that will need to be configured within their system. Credit functionality within Allmoxy- what does this look like? Trying to integrate the front end of the credit side into their business. Part of that will be a credit check on their end to authorize that program to move ahead. Need people to be able to look in there and be 100% up to date on the credit function of things. Outside of these things is there anywhere we missed the mark? The biggest frustration has been 2020. They have the capacity to do things but necessarily the will to do them. Who are their end customers? Distributors and retail dealers. Eventually, they will have other channels. @Broc Hill @Josh Baker for visibility.

engagements.title: Allmoxy CS | Sales Retention Meeting - Hallmark Home Interiors Inc.
engagements.body: Allmoxy CS | Sales Retention Meeting - Hallmark Home Interiors Inc.

Meeting agenda 8/21/24 (tentative) Josh's questions digging into why they want to decided to build their own solution/ what we could have done better to support Hallmark What is the main driver for wanting to build their own system? Based on our renewal call in June everything was seemingly going great. What features/functionality will they require for their new system and do any differ from Allmoxy? What was the most valuable part of Allmoxy during their time using the system? What held them back from fully(shipped) running more orders in Allmoxy once the catalog was completed? Who are their end customers? Retail dealers and distributors. Also do OEM channel. Did the 2020/recent API conversation take part in shaping the decision to build their own internal system? Notes They enjoyed the relationship and we've tried hard to get them what they need been pretty responsive. 2020 saying its not you its them Internal side of things, the aspect of showing people stock inside of their units. Part of what they have to work with (25K boxes in house) with that 25K in house. They have multiple doors and colors and they stock those in one door one skid (white 15x24) will have own skid in the shop. In order  to check that you would need to do continuous updates of all the SKUs. Basically have to put together config to pull together all components. Simple look up configurator. Too many software being pulled together, including Allmoxy. They currently write the APIs for 2020 and ProKitchen internally in Netsuite. How can they move quicker towards 2020 goal, how can they do an internal check on stock for every order they are doing. We do it but all the SKUs need to be locked in. Credit function, they need to have that labeled in. Trying to integrate front end of credit side with their business, which they will have to do anyways. Credit check on their end. Our credit feature is not very user friendly. Corporate side of things. He has a quote from a team with how much it costs to develop in house. Not ruling out coming back. His frustrations is something outside our purview, which is 2020. They are they guys that have the capacity to do things but not necessarily the will. People higher up than Paul are making the decision. They currently aren't using the Allmoxy API. Want access to Allmoxy, to be safe, 90 days. Kenzi: Explaining the terms of canceling the 12-month contract Broc: seeing if there is anything we can do to keep them onboard with us.

Call with Paul Lantz 8/19/24 Want to cancel Allmoxy They said our team has been great to work with and appreciate the ease of use for their customers Right now they think it is best for them to move in a different direction and invest in an internal solution for Hallmark. They are going to build their own ordering system They just resigned their contract in June (and everything was going well) so we will need to have another cancellation meeting with them I have been working with Yasir on getting him the necessary data from Jake on the 2020 integration and he was great to work with. No red flags. They ran 158 orders in Allmoxy and were launched

engagements.title: New Sign Up - Ajia Group Inc.
engagements.body: Signed up for an account in the installer, check to see if a card is on file.

Initially, a meeting between Sam Alexander and Jeremy focused on Jeremy's company, which had been in operation for eight years. The business had recently expanded into manufacturing partnerships to cater to builders and contractors, with plans to target homeowners in the future. Discussions covered internal processes, including utilizing Cabinet Vision and QuickBooks for manufacturing and accounting tasks, respectively. Integrating Almoxy functionalities like order processing automation and inventory tracking, linked with QuickBooks, was explored for operational efficiency. They proceeded to delve into incorporating barcode scanners in the moxie system for product management, pricing, and inventory tracking. This discussion emphasized leveraging barcodes for time tracking to streamline job statuses efficiently. Sam Alexander proposed a B2B feature within moxie enabling manufacturers to outsource products and connect with suppliers easily, enhancing white-labeling and inventory management. Pricing structures were detailed, indicating subscription costs based on company size tiers ranging from $1099 to $5000 per month, emphasizing scalability and growth alignment. The meeting also touched on creating customer-facing online ordering portals customizable by account type. Detailed discussions showcased how orders could be generated within Almoxy, integrating validations for precise data entry before production initiation. The seamless export of data to Cabinet Vision was highlighted to reduce manual inputs, improving production planning efficiency. Implementation details were shared, requiring users to devote 10-30% effort in setting up catalogs, supported by assigned integration representatives and customer success teams for guidance over a four to twelve-week period. Ongoing support from dedicated resources was emphasized for smooth transition and continued operational success.

engagements.title: Churned customer re-engagement consideration
engagements.body: See Churn playbook and retention notes for full context. For more information reach out to the former assigned CSM.

Churn Playbook Please select all of the reasons that apply for why the customer is churning. Add any additional churn context in the note - the more the better here. If there's escalation notes that exist on the account be sure to reference them and include the data of the escalation note in the playbook context. Catalog Unidentified, Customer Stakeholder Misalignment, Paid and Never Used Notes: Customer churned over a year ago. Vendors aren't using Allmoxy No one is placing orders like they expected, their customers didn't adopt Allmoxy Can we hold the data there and pay a lower monthly fee? Doesn't want to pay the 800 anymore Offered more training but at the end of the day their customers aren't using the system IF this is the customers main/only instance that's churning, change the Customer Lifecycle property(company property) to "Other." DO NOT DO THIS if this is a second instance and the churn does not completely eliminate their status as a customer. Other What's the catalog implementation status? No

Sarah sent them a scope doc after out 10/17 meeting. His response to Broc was very non-commital, Broc responded by asking him how he wants to move forward. Morris never responded. Likely going to churn

10/17/24 Meeting with Morris, May, Sarah Action items Send them a proposal for training/ additional asks (Sarah) They want to be able to self serve and know how to add colors, materials, and product lines (shouldn't be hard with the way their catalog is set up) Notes Right now they have Swiss Corner, Cleaf, Fenix want to add Chenoke. Want to add several colors and want to offer other kinds of doors. Are they happy with the things in there now? Yes Continuing to add product line Current catalog is pretty relevant, those product lines constantly need to add more colors. Pretty complicated. They could use some training to continue to add for themselves (adding and discontinuing colors) All colors live on one table, we need to make sure it is similar enough for them to maintain They price labor plus material. Services training, we can also make them a video to make it clear how to maintain Declutter as well. They started Allmoxy with Royal Plywood. They stopped using it then Soliton had to take all the cost. Now they want to add other products for other customers. Timeline: as soon as possible! For Labor only product, the system helped them charge the labor charge. When they get all the labor charge, they have to add up the material cost and send the codes (cost?) back to the customer. They have to manually add in Material cost and send back to customer? Sometimes customer provides the material, sometimes not. Sometimes they purchase materials for the customer, but that is a separate cost. Fast forwarded to 16 minutes in (Sarah's recommendation) May liked this recommendation: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Soliton-Crossing-Allmoxy-sync::REnW9UVwugd1uL6g They want to add Chenoke and POD. Additional work, they don't want to pay more. They want to go the training route it sounds like. Services is going to have to set some of it up, they need to be able to add color lists!! By themselves. They don't really charge different labor costs for materials.

engagements.title: Follow up on Soliton Crossing
engagements.body: Regarding email: Allmoxy Subscription Pause, sent on Friday, September 27, 2024 10:16 AM
From retention meeting September 2024: 6 month subscription cost pause as per Brocs direction. Subscription payment pause goes from September to February.

Retention Meeting September 2024 In attendance: Broc, Kenzi, Josh, Morris, May. Currently out of contract. Paying month to month. They are a distributor. Was actively working on Allmoxy back in April. They had Royal Plywood asking them to sign up for the door program as well as EB Bradley. They are some local distributors who have a lot of customers under their network. The door program business has dropped which is why they got Allmoxy. They need to pay $800 a month but there is no business. They have been talking to Royal Plywood for several months to get them to do something with Allmoxy but they are not wanting to help Morris out. For his sign up he has paid 10-20k so far. They don't have a lot of resources to do the marketing for the platform right now. Morris said they weren't sold that Allmoxy would do the marketing in their behalf just that it is difficult for them to do right now. For his current book outside of Royal Plywood, etc. are there other customers that are apt to put in their orders if they are incentivized to do so? Is there value in us having a shorter term discount that allows them to get up and use the program that might be an opportunity for us to work together continually? Broc ask: Aside from the money is there any other reason why you wouldn't stick with Allmoxy by working with our team, especially customer success? We have seen many smaller door shops come in with the same issues who we have been able to help as well.  Broc is open to looking at another pricing model or possibly a few free months to get things up and going. If we could find someone else to use their system (outside of Royal Plywood) they would be happy to switch to them. They don't see themselves getting any other customers but would be interested in it being sent to someone else. Advanced Hardware has asked them to work with them as well and make some doors for them. Broc told Morris that he is a perfect fit for what we do. We have a great relationship with Royal Plywood. Especially Broc. He feels like they are pretty responsive to him. Broc thinks we would have a really good idea of things within 90 days to see if we can add value. To see if there are partners in our ecosystem to see what connections we can make, we would be willing to give at least the next 90 days at no cost. Then come back together and regroup. Morris does not feel like he has the time or energy to market things. He is really burned out and doesn't have a lot of energy to build the platform. Morris said maybe we can keep on the three months pathway then regroup. He really likes the setup they already made within the Allmoxy platform. Broc ask: When could you say with certainty that you would have more customers in the flow of their business? Morris says they are talking with other guys like Swiss Corona and another Spanish manufacturer. If we could keep all his data and not pay during six months he would take the time to meet and speak to those guys and try to create connections and business. Morris said: "I'm thinking if we transfer everything to our website, our Allmoxy multi-platform things to our website and also open the Allmoxy platform to our portal to other like swiss corona or some other door program door some other panel producers, maybe we do have a chance." It would be helpful if we could show them and teach them how to update colors, etc. with their products. They were under the impression they had to pay for all those things to be done. Broc said it can be things that we simply train on without having them pay for it. We also need to look at adding additional products to their catalog like sheet goods, slab doors, etc. Morris mentioned Shinoki products on several occasions to add to the catalog. Broc ask: Is there anyone in the company that they could train to input those things? Morris says May is there and understands the platform. She can work with us to supply us with the colors and door type, etc. Next steps: Josh to set up a training with Juan to show how to change colors, etc. not necessarily a training to add individual product lines but something more general. Josh to connect with May as well. Kenzi will follow up in an email that Soliton will have the next six months at no cost for their subscription cost.   Morris to think creatively about how Broc can help be strategic with partners like Royal Plywood and provide him assistance.

engagements.body: Regarding email: Allmoxy Next Steps, sent on Friday, September 20, 2024 10:33 AM

Cancellation request meeting 9/27/24 Requesting to cancel Vendors aren't using Allmoxy No one is placing orders like they expected, their customers didn't adopt Allmoxy Can we hold the data there and pay a lower monthly fee? Doesn't want to pay the 800 anymore Offered more training but at the end of the day their customers aren't using the system Let's get a better idea of their business model and end customer/ dig into this a little more on the call Notes at Vegas meeting, they were interested. They wanted to set someone up on their door program. They are a big local distributor and that's why the set up Allmoxy. Main reason of cancellation: the business (door program) has dropped. They didn't update their business with Allmoxy. They have one customer who uses it. Royal Plywood- they wanted to use it initially and they don't want to anymore. Their door program is still ongoing, but customers will rather email an excel sheet to them. No business using Allmoxy, they are paying 800 per month. They have been reminding Royal Plywood for 3 months to do things in Allmoxy and they don't want to do it. Allmoxy doesn't bring customers to the platform. They don't have the resources to do the marketing. Soliton makes the excel calculator for Royal. Royal Plywood isn't training their customers to use Allmoxy. Soliton does not have the bandwidth to promote Allmoxy like Rehau. Royal appreciates their effort, but they still don't want to use it. At the end of the day, their customers aren't there and not using it. Can we reach out to our network to help them? Broc's proposal, 90 days at no cost and try to get them the resources they need. Morriss says he doesn't have enough time or energy to put into Allmoxy. Another option is to save their data for them and run their server (while Morris tries to get more customers) Pain point: our system doesn't allow for them to maintain things themselves. If they need a color added, our services team has to do it. They don't know how to put products into the system. Chenoke? They want to put all Chenoke. They want to know how to add colors, sheet goods, slab doors. Is there someone we can train at Soliton? --> May would be that person. Next steps, get May on a meeting with me. 6-month Allmoxy at no cost.

engagements.title: Tirssa Machado, Qualita Property Services- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations
engagements.body: Tirssa Machado, Qualita Property Services- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations

Onboarding Meeting Notes Week 10 03/11/24 Catalog Concerns Address Tirssa's concerns Drawer pricing Hinges, slides drawer boxes pricing Get them on the same page (what does Tirssa need from Juan in terms of communication and education regarding the catalog and how things are built) Launching today Any hesitations? Are you ready on your end? 6 month goal What can we consider success over the next 6 months? Expanding the catalog, securing customers, etc? Meeting Cadence - bump this to bi-weekly and then eventually monthly. Catalog growth - What benchmarks do you need to achieve in order to secure more funding to expand the catalog? Troubleshooting Please keep Juan and I both CC'd on email communication. Next steps I'll update the meeting cadence to bi-weekly Reach out to Juan and I via email for any major issues/concerns Notes: Got confused when they tested prices previously it worked correctly but then were broken for some reason - caused the confusion. Juan - template was our starting point and it was programed to price drawer boxes a certain way with a certain material schedule. (Juan needed to change the attributes so the drawers would price correctly) Teaching/Training session - need to get this scheduled B2B - interested in expanding here in the future Wants to allow regular clients purchasing in the future (retail) Gahida's focus - Future planning Wants to ensure Beside processing orders, what else can Allmoxy assist with? Materials - show how the product works (advertising/marketing materials) Planning to execute an email marketing campaign to give people a discount for the first 3 months. Product information - updating products with their instructions Next Projects Cabinet Vision Export Inventory/Supply Management Part or Cut lists (reports) Next steps Tirssa reviewing checklist of adjustments she requested Tirssa schedule a training session with Juan regarding what is adjustable and what isn't Meeting every two weeks to discuss any issues or concerns Next project will be creating the cabinet vision export and moving onto any other reports needed. Underside panel with LED - PRODUCT (we didn't build, Tirssa added it - good product for Juan to train on) Week 7 02/29/24 Agenda: Catalog update - Juan finishing the pieces needed and expect them to be completed today. Once confirmed by Juan we can allow account registration per Tirssa's confirmation. Triggers Welcome email: https://formacabinets.allmoxy.com/settings/triggers/edit/6/ - clarify when payment is due and that it can be applied from their home page. Payment email: https://formacabinets.allmoxy.com/settings/triggers/edit/30/ - Confirm the payment amount covers the entirety of the order, if it does then move the order status to in progress. Shipping charges If someone does want delivery, are you going to charge them within Allmoxy? If so you'll need to do so before you move the order to completed. This will allow you to add the additional shipping cost to the order which the customer can then pay through their login page. Notes: no notes Week 7 02/26/24 Notes: Catalog - Juan is fixing the pricing that Tirssa broke. Triggers - Completed Output pages - good for now Next steps - launching this week. Week 6 02/15/24 Agenda: Catalog Review Does everything look good from your end? Any last things to adjust with Juan? Homework from last week Review the financial and shipping settings with Gahida Financial Settings (Credit & order limit) Shipping Settings (How you want to offer shipping) Output pages - what do you need? Overview Checklist Financial Settings Shipping Settings Triggers Output Pages Catalog Complete Internal process (who's doing what) Customers Next Steps If everything is complete we can pick a date and have a few test orders come through and see if we have any challenges. Ideally a beginning of week launch. Notes: Catalog - Creating orders as they go in cabinet vision Website - launching this week (tomorrow or Wednesday) Financial Gehida - setting customer credit limit 30,000 Shipping Working with a third party later For now they'll use pick up Their delivery - value $250 any location around Vancouver Discount - How can we show the discount on the invoice? Week 5 02/08/24 Agenda: Last Week & Homework Tirssa editing the body of the triggers Need to build triggers to model after Duke - Tirssa's objective Wrap up Tags, pricing adjustments - Any lingering questions? This Week Shipping, Financials, and output pages if we have time Homework Follow up on Ship Date Recreate the in progress trigger Notes: Reviewing catalog updates with Juan (Tirssa didn't finish reviewing everything just yet) Triggers - Gahida reviewed the triggers and said they look good. Trigger when they make a payment (Notify Tirssa and Gahida) Still needed - build trigger steps to replicate duke's process Order email - letting the customer know it will be reviewed, once the customer has paid then it'll move forward into production. Week 4 01/29/24 Agenda: Previous Week Went through the general function of triggers This week Catalog - Sharing more data, did you share this yet? Review the triggers I recommended and what you discussed with your team Go through and mark these triggers so you can update from your end Tags and Pricing Adjustments Homework Build out your triggers (all of them if possible) Review with your team how you'd like to use triggers Determine if there are any price adjustments you know you'll want to pass to customers (if any) Next Week Confirm the trigger completion Move onto shipping and finance What else would you like to discuss? Notes: Has been trying to go over triggers with Ghidaha (still needs to determine which ones they want to use) Has worked with Duke and ordered from them. Wants to copy their order and verification process (they review the order, then they send an email asking to confirm the order information, then they send a payment) Catalog - it was hard for them to have an estimate of what it would cost to build the catalog because they don't know the scope of the entire catalog. Quoted them a very high price, Tirssa told Juan they want to start very basic and she needs to provide pricing. She added some information to the spreadsheet but isn't sure if it's what Juan needs. Juan said he would put something there for testing Week 3 - 01/22/24 Agenda: Catalog - First sheet is filled out, the services team has started working on the catalog. Previous week Reviewed how to create a customer (from your end) Logo/Theme adjustments Test ordering Website links for customers (login/sign up) This week Triggers Tags Pricing Adjustments Homework Think through your customers sign up and order flow, we need to determine the touch points/notifications you want to utilize internally and with your customers. List out every step from account creation to order shipment. Review the tags and pricing adjustment support links and make some practice tags/adjustments (start thinking of how you'd want to utilize these) Next week We'll work through the triggers and follow up on tags and pricing adjustments Dive into shipping and financials What else would you like to discuss? Notes: Didn't dig around, a little bit after we talked but didn't go much further after our initial call. Her header isn't showing even though she has it added to the header content. Hasn't been notifying customers previously since the cabinet business just started Tirssa's perspective - interested to add the invoice after they verify Some customers will be paying per job, some companies might get longer credit limits Week 2 - 01/11/24 Agenda: Catalog How was the conversation with Juan? From Juan's notes he'll need more data from your end to give a timeframe. When do you think you'll be able to provide him that information? Previous "homework" Initial tasks - Were you able to start on these? If so, any challenges? Theme/Logo - Set up, needs some slight tweaks Stripe - Connected Test order - no test order How did you feel going through these steps? How do you feel about navigating the site? Any areas you're specifically curious about? Let's create a test account together and a test order. This week Automation - Triggers, Tags, and Pricing Adjustments how do you currently update customers and employees? Do you group customers or products together? Easily identify the customer based on internal criteria How custom is your pricing for customers? Review the automation links throughout next week and we can review together Notes: Wants to confirm something - link to their Allmoxy account Catalog - starting on the spreadsheet tomorrow Three sheets - she'll be working on the first tab and try to have it completed by the weekend Homework links Share the login links Need to fix the logos Creating customers - need to decide if they want to create the accounts or have the customers do it. Week 1 - 01/03/23 Agenda: Introductions Their intro - manufacturer out of BC, outsources from D3/Duke. So you're familiar with Allmoxy? Built out your cabinet vision catalog over the last several months. My intro - Go to person for your account, I'll be able to guide you to becoming an Allmoxy pro. (set the expectation of services vs. CS) Goals   When are you looking to launch? Specific date? Integration estimate is 60-90 hrs, have you scheduled your time with Juan yet? Do you have an hour or two each week you can dedicate to working on your instance? Getting Started Week 1 training overview and will provide you with homework. Next Steps Set up stripe, customize your instance, and use the set up guide to move ahead if you have time. What else would you like to cover? Notes: Had a convo last year about allmoxy and that was the first time they were made aware of the company. Has lots of doubts - Quote Used Allmoxy as a client of a company - Their company started with renovations (bathroom and kitchens) They draw the cabinets by hand, the manufacturer would send the detailed drawings with the specifics Had trouble finding good suppliers in the past, has changed quite a bit due to pricing, quality, etc. Has future ideas and want to make sure we can align with. Just started using Cabinet vision Launch date - No specific date as of yet. Machines arrived and were installed before Christmas Still working on their website

01/08/25 Customer Refund Request High-Level Overview: Customer originally signed with Allmoxy to utilize our 3D designer. They claimed they were told by sales when signing that improvements were coming to the designer. (Signed and started May 1st 2024) During the onboarding/Catalog building process the customer found the designer difficult to use and doesn't meet the needs they were originally anticipating. Felt like they were oversold on the capabilities and our plans for the designer. Carson and Sarah tried to reinstate the value of the designer with it's current functionality but the customer continued to poke holes in the use case. Customer met with Broc on 11/21 and pushed the objective to focus on potentially using closet pro in conjunction with Allmoxy. Payment was paused and the next steps given by sales was to meet with Sarah and I to talk about how the connection with Closet Pro can function. Customer also requested at this meeting a refund of what they've paid to date with Allmoxy. Sarah and I met with customer on 01/09 to talk about the challenges they faced and if they are interested in moving forward with leveraging closet pro and Allmoxy at any future state. Customer shared that at this point their main concern is recouping their loses on the project and after that point they would consider a conversation of how they could utilize Allmoxy.   Total Spent to date:   Subscription: $5,894 (May - Oct) Services (Harvest report attached): $14,592 total: $20,486 Total that Ryan mentioned in recording with Broc: $16,374.20 Call Recordings: 02/01/24 (Sam): https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Allmoxy-Sales-Q-A-Demo-Crown-Cabinetry::k3V4odI2yoRXw1FP First designer discussion: 40:00 - to end of call https://app.fireflies.ai/view/k3V4odI2yoRXw1FP?t=2403.67 03/08/24 (Sam & Juan): https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Cabinets-by-Crown-Allmoxy-Designer-overview::dNJ6Pf5ZfekvCVUY 11/21/24 (Broc): https://allmoxy.zoom.us/rec/share/K4pFukahVBG_UkSiw4U-CndHxLofe2KA7Ad59rnibRJC-ZX3NIzKU551qb3p2flf.SZ_ARMf1pJamweQW Passcode: d=vta+u9 01/08/25 (Carson & Sarah): https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Crown-Allmoxy::5OFPNR13lppMP9de I have reviewed both calls and I see both sides of the issue here. Sam does make claims that we're building a new designer but while that's being built we would be able to get them access to our current designer which does have limitations. Overall I think the customer definitely didn't vet our designer as thoroughly as they should have but they were also lead astray by sales with a hope for a "completely new designer within 12 - 18th months". They're currently awaiting a decision from our end on if/what we're able to refund them. After reviewing the original calls with Sam, I feel like this is in their court and we never mislead them on what the current functionality of the designer was. They put their faith in the person they hired to drive this closet program and he didn't do his proper due diligence. The only other kicker here is that Ryan is close with Troy from Westwind, which is where a lot of his experience with Allmoxy comes from so if we come back with no refund whatsoever there is a chance he spreads this experience to Troy and others. @Madison Theisen @Sarah Pierce

Crown - Action Plan 12/17 Call goal - The goal for the meeting on Jan 8th will be to share all options for Crown and find the best move forward plan that they're excited about. Options: Closet Pro - Utilizing closet pro would allow Ryan's customers to design orders in closet pro and then pass these orders to Allmoxy for the remaining solution of managing orders, paying invoices, and converting orders to cabinet vision for production. Needs: Would need to finish or build the desired catalog within Closet Pro Need to finish the Allmoxy MRP catalog Need an export from Closet Pro to Allmoxy Need an export from Allmoxy to cabinet vision Benefit: This would allow crown to deliver a design software to their end customers and get into the closet business. Easily manage orders within Allmoxy, receive customer payments, and communicate designs from Closet pro to Allmoxy to Cabinet Vision. We could keep the Allmoxy subscription paused for a 3-6 month period while closet pro work is completed. Once closet pro, allmoxy, and cabinet vision are communicating we could reinstate the subscription at a reduced rate and lock into a more suitable contract. No Closet Pro & No Allmoxy - If there's no desire to explore closet pro as a possibility or using Allmoxy in any way we would need to escalate this to our executive team to see what would be needed to settle the remainder of the contract. Goal - want to maintain the relationship and make this work for everyone, trying to find the best middle ground. @Madison Theisen @Sarah Pierce for vis. Please let me know if there's any other key items I should include in this outline. 01/09/25 Call Notes: Good holiday break Eric - was the person to run it, he choose Allmoxy and when we found that our designer wouldn't meet their needs Eric had to leave. Doesn't want to start the whole process over. At this moment Ryan is looking for a refund and that's it. Doesn't seem interested in pursuing closet pro or using Allmoxy.

Important Note: Ryan has a good relationship with Troy from Westwind so we need to be careful how we handle their cancellation request and ensure that we're not giving him a reason to bad mount Allmoxy to Troy or any other industry contacts he might know that utilizes Allmoxy.
@Madison Theisen and @Carson Ward am I missing the follow up on this cancellation request with them?
@Samantha Racker Please see response here for Crown cancellation request.

Cancellation Request 11/14/24 Context: Ryan and Anessa reached out to me on 11/14 to request to cancel their subscription. Full email context can be seen here. Hubspot Email Link Background: During the building of Crown's catalog it was a struggle to get the catalog information from the first point of contact I had, Eric Tyrell, as he found it hard to provide the exact details the service provider needed to build the MRP. Eventually the service provider started a weekly recurring meeting in hopes this would help get us all of the information needed. Aug 13th: the owner Ryan, joined the services meeting and we found that there was a misalignment between Eric and Ryan so we put a plan in place to correct the situation. (Hubspot Note Link) Sept 10th: After Ryan shared concerns with how the 3D designer functioned I recommended we have a meeting with myself and Sarah to demonstrate how it works and within that meeting Ryan pointed out several ways that he thought the designer failed to achieve what he originally thought it could do. (easily calculate all room and product dimensions, all products configurable and editable) The conclusion of this meeting was that Ryan wanted to talk to another customer that has used the 3D designer and gain insight from their experience and after searching I recommended Kiwi Closets. Sept - Oct: I sent an introduction email between Kiwi Closets and Crown after Kiwi said they'd be willing to share their insight/feedback. I checked in multiple times with Ryan and Anessa to see if they've been able to meet with Kiwi and during this time Ryan was partially out of office and Kiwi wasn't responding to them. Nov 14th: Anessa and Ryan reached out with their final request to cancel (see email link above). Their main points were as follows: We were told that the Designer portion of the software was under development for improvements or that it would be in the near future. the Designer has significant limitations. Those details were left out of the sales demonstration. Besides difficulty manipulating a drawing, the tool cannot produce accurate measurements required for operations. I've responded to their email and included Broc and Mekenzi so we can work on getting a retention meeting scheduled before full cancellation. @Madison Theisen @Broc Hill @Mekenzi Falslev

Slight Change Order/Update on Catalog Progression 8/13 Aaron has started recurring weekly meetings with Eric and Anessa to help expedite the finishing of their MRP catalog. Today (8/13) Ryan, the owner, joined the meeting and while reviewing what Aaron was sharing with everyone he said "these aren't a good fit for what they do". Aaron stated that Ryan wasn't mad and it seemed that he was motivated to get this corrected and back on course. Part of the issue is that it appears Eric Tyrell did the initial scoping call with Juan and I, whereas if Ryan was part of that scoping it could have impacted the build and got us started closer to what he wanted. (side context: Eric has been difficult to get homework done or provide product information in a timely manner. The progression we've made has been achieved by Aaron and I's follow up and constant reminders of the need to share data) Next Steps: The next meeting is Thursday morning where I'll be attending to ensure I have a grasp of what Ryan is looking for and how this will change what Aaron has built up until this point. Ryan and Eric are putting together a document of what they actually need to start with, we're hopeful it won't be too complex/custom from the template. Meeting Recording: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Crown-Cabinets::oH3CSmr9djpWK6dq

engagements.title: Allmoxy & Crown Cabinetry
engagements.body: Allmoxy & Crown Cabinetry

Sam Alexander and Ryan Johnson discuss their previous experience with a demo of all moxie, a system for ordering cabinets and closet systems. Ryan explains that they had tried to set up their cabinets on all moxie in the past but encountered roadblocks. However, they are now developing a new line of closet systems and want to expand into an outdoor line as well. Sam asks about the roles of Vanessa and Eric in the company, who are also present in the conversation. Ryan mentions that they use Cabinet Vision for manufacturing and that they will be outsourcing drawer boxes and potentially doors from other companies, some of which are not all moxie users. Sam discusses some changes since their last interaction with all moxie, including direct integration with Cabinet Vision and an in-house team for third-party services. They go through a demo showing how customers create accounts, place orders, make payments, track inventory, generate invoices, manage suppliers using purchase orders (POs), export data to QuickBooks for accounting purposes, access reports, use triggers for automated actions like sending emails or creating tasks based on specific conditions or events within the system. The discussion touches upon features like b2b marketplace integration where businesses can collaborate by selling white-labeled products to each other's customers while maintaining transactional records within all Moxie; design capabilities (presently under development) allowing importation of designs from software like Closet Pro 2020 or Cabinet Vision; pricing models based on company size; implementation timelines typically ranging from 4-12 weeks depending on requirements; enterprise pricing options beyond $10 million revenue range etc Sam Alexander explains the pricing structure for their services, starting at $1,099 a month for the zero to 1 million tier and going up to $5,000 a month for a $10 million customer. The first year cost for the one to 2 million tier is $1,699 a month but can be reduced with discounts. The monthly fee includes unlimited access and a dedicated customer success representative. After twelve months, the price may increase based on revenue growth but will be negotiated. Integration costs are billed weekly at $150 per hour and typically range between 50-100 hours. Additional build-out time may be required for the 3D designer feature. The potential client wants more time to consider and discuss their needs regarding the designer before making a decision. They plan to follow up with Sam in about a month's time. Summary: Sam Alexander discusses pricing options ranging from $1,099 to $5,000 per month based on revenue tiers and provides details on included features like unlimited access and customer support. Discounts are available along with integration costs billed weekly at an hourly rate of $150. The potential client requests more information on the 3D designer feature before finalizing their decision.

Please note if the customer has received any reductions in MRR during their lifetime with Allmoxy as well as any significant, recent implementation costs. $315.20 Industry Segment (Data Confirmation) Custom Cabinet Shop, Door Manufacturer Business Size (Data Confirmation) SMB How long have they been a customer of Allmoxy? 02/04/2021 Notes: He's been paying since 2021 Is this customer launched? No Is this customer working with our implementation team now, if so please elaborate on the nature of the work/project. No Is this customer a legacy/strategic account? Industry influence/connections, personality details, known expansion opportunity, competitor tech stack, etc.) Notes: One man operation, older and not very technical. Who's the main customer contact associated with this escalation? Mark For reporting purposes, please check that this playbook is completed. Yes

Meeting 8/28/25 with Mark Meeting Summary Josh Baker and Mark met to review Mark’s existing Allmoxy setup and determine how to proceed with product configuration and pricing. Mark explained his goal to simplify his product offerings by initially selling cabinet boxes only — no doors, drawer fronts, or finished elements — and eventually expand to more advanced offerings. The conversation explored technical issues with existing product pricing, outdated catalog configurations, and how best to rebuild or clean up the catalog structure to support Mark’s evolving business model. Josh provided insight on attribute configuration, product customization, pricing models, and how Allmoxy engineers can assist once Mark provides a clearer product/pricing plan. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Mark wants to start by offering basic cabinet boxes only, without doors, drawer fronts, applied end panels, or finished elements. Josh and Mark reviewed product attributes (e.g., drawer box, edge banding, toe kick, door type) and discussed which to hide or retain. Discussion around integrating edge banding options, including renaming and adjusting thickness (e.g., changing from 0.5mm to 1mm). Mark is considering offering cabinet parts flat-packed and unassembled as a simpler service model. Josh suggested that Mark outline products, desired options, and pricing in a document (or marked-up printout) to provide to Allmoxy engineers. Technical Issues & Fixes Corner cabinets were not pricing properly — workaround discussed (changing settings like retail pricing, default attributes). Many attributes (e.g., door type, drawer front) are tied to pricing formulas and may require reworking to remove dependencies. Existing pricing setup is outdated and overly complex — last updated in 2021 and built with legacy practices. Josh demonstrated how to hide product attributes and explained the impact on pricing behavior. Toe kick configurations (no toe, integrated, flush) exist, but they don’t currently influence pricing meaningfully. Decisions Made Mark plans to start simple, offering only boxes without doors/drawer fronts, and gradually scale offerings. Will not offer doors initially; decision may change based on early results. Will submit a marked-up document or spreadsheet to define product offerings and pricing preferences. Intention to price by sheet cut + material, possibly with a margin or simple cost-plus model. Challenges or Concerns Existing catalog was set up by a third party who is no longer involved, leaving uncertainty around current configurations and payments made. Legacy pricing formulas are bloated and difficult to manage, affecting software performance. Mark is unsure of how best to structure pricing within Allmoxy and wants to avoid overwhelming complexity. Action Items Mark to: Review current product offerings (e.g., base one drawer, one shelf). Mark up pages or create a spreadsheet outlining: Products to offer Attributes to include or exclude Desired pricing method (e.g., per sheet, cost-plus) Email documentation to Josh for review. Josh to: Await Mark’s documentation. Coordinate with Allmoxy engineering once scope is defined to estimate time/cost. Next Steps Mark will mark up existing product printouts or compile a list in a spreadsheet detailing his product and pricing approach. Once Josh receives the materials, Allmoxy engineering will review and provide a scope of work and pricing estimate. After approval, Allmoxy team will proceed with cleaning up and rebuilding the product catalog, aligning it with Mark’s streamlined model. Future enhancements (e.g., doors, commercial-grade options) can be layered in after successful implementation of the simplified model.
