Allmoxy Demo - : Pinnacle Millwork Inc Allmoxy Demo - : Pinnacle Millwork Inc December 19, 2025 10:00 AM MST 33 mins bobby@allmoxy.com accounting@pinnaclemillworkinc.com production@pinnaclemillworkinc.com jeremy@pinnaclemillworkinc.com View Meeting Recording Product Fit and Customer Experience The meeting confirmed Almoxy software as a perfect fit for Saving Design’s core product lines, especially inserts, drawers, and doors (07:46). Almoxy offers a fully customizable portal branded for Saving Design, allowing customers to log in and see real-time pricing and order status (09:27) It supports multiple user roles: owner, customer, and employee, enabling tailored views and access Bobby emphasized the system’s ability to handle complex variables for cabinetry and millwork pricing instantly Customers can self-serve, reducing manual order input and improving ordering speed and accuracy Real-time tax and shipping cost calculations adapt to customer location and shipping choices The portal improves payment processing and credit management by integrating with Stripe and supporting ACH transfers (13:29) Customers can pay directly within Almoxy, consolidating account snapshots and payment history Bobby shared his experience reducing average accounts receivable aging from 62 days to 14 days by simplifying payments (13:29) Almoxy automates credit handling, making it easy to set and apply volume-based discounts per customer The system syncs payment data automatically to accounting software, aiming to eliminate manual double entries (17:18) Integration and Workflow Automation Almoxy's integration capabilities and automation reduce manual tasks and improve operational efficiency for Saving Design (12:32). Almoxy integrates with accounting software like Aspire through APIs or Zapier, facilitating seamless data exchange (12:32) Bobby explained they aim to automate payment status updates inside Aspire to avoid manual updates The system pushes order and payment info automatically, reducing errors and saving time Order entry triggers comprehensive automated workflows that streamline production and shipping (20:18) Orders automatically update inventory, generate cut lists, packing slips, shipping labels, and invoices without manual effort Almoxy communicates directly with plant floor machines to generate necessary files, cutting down production prep time Barcoded packing slips enable labor tracking and real-time order status updates, improving customer communication Automated email triggers notify customers at key points: order verification, shipment, and payment reminders, reducing collection efforts Operational Reporting and Production Planning Almoxy provides detailed reports that improve production scheduling and shipping coordination (25:14). The system generates daily and future shipping reports showing what items need to ship and by which method (25:14) This gives shipping and finance teams clear visibility on outstanding collections before shipment Bobby showed how production scheduling and capacity reports help optimize workflow and prioritize orders Reporting on sales mix and customer segmentation allows Saving Design to identify best-selling products and key customers (26:32) These insights support sales strategy and inventory planning by highlighting where to focus efforts Implementation Approach and Pricing The implementation includes a setup phase involving customization and ongoing control for Saving Design (29:22). Setup costs are estimated around $5,000 in engineering hours to customize product catalogs, pricing, and workflows (30:20) Bobby stressed the goal of empowering Saving Design to manage product changes independently after setup The monthly subscription pricing depends on the annual revenue run through Almoxy, with no upfront license fee Bobby will send a pricing matrix and demo access to facilitate further evaluation and decision making Next steps include Saving Design’s internal review by their president, with Bobby available for follow-up discussions (32:02) The team aims to streamline processes and reduce reliance on Excel and manual communication between departments (27:15) This integration and automation are expected to significantly reduce operational bottlenecks and improve cash flow Strategic Business Impact Adopting Almoxy will modernize Saving Design’s ordering and production process, enhancing customer satisfaction and financial control. Bobby highlighted that Almoxy was designed specifically for cabinetry and millwork companies, making it a strategic fit (10:13) The system’s automation reduces manual labor, speeds up order processing, and improves order accuracy, supporting growth without proportional headcount increases Real-time payment and collections features are expected to improve cash flow and reduce days sales outstanding dramatically Improved visibility across departments and automated customer communications will enhance customer experience and reduce errors Action Items Bobby Lewis Share meeting recap and AI-generated notes with Pinnacle Millwork. (32:00) Send demo access for Almoxy portal for hands-on review. (32:00) Provide pricing matrix and outline monthly subscription plus setup fees. (30:20) Prepare for potential follow-up meetings after client review. (32:00) Accounting Department Pinnacle Millwork Verify Aspire accounting software API capabilities for integration assessment. (12:45) Present Almoxy proposal and demo to company president for approval. (32:02) Provide feedback or requests for further information after internal review. (32:10) Meeting Recording: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KCPQAJEDGXCMFRFVMP7RCW5V 📎 Time markers in this document can be used to navigate to specific points in the meeting recording Processed by:

Retention meeting 11/19/25 with James & Sarah Meeting Summary The purpose of the meeting was to evaluate the current usage and ROI of Allmoxy by Pinnacle Cabinets, and to discuss potential options for restructuring or pausing the subscription. James and Jacob expressed concerns about the lack of inbound sales through the door line originally envisioned for Allmoxy, and emphasized limited use of the platform outside quoting and internal cost calculations. The Allmoxy team (Sarah and Josh) proposed several flexible options to reduce costs while preserving data and system structure for future use. Both parties agreed to follow up on revised pricing options and the potential for future re-engagement based on business restructuring. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Original Use Case vs. Reality: Pinnacle Cabinets originally intended to use Allmoxy for both custom cabinets and door line sales. The cabinet use case was abandoned early due to the complexity of custom work. The door line has not generated anticipated business, with only minimal sales coming via Etsy and direct quotes. Current Usage: Allmoxy is now primarily used internally for quoting and pricing. Occasional customers are routed through Allmoxy for payments. Some orders are processed fully within Allmoxy; others are quoted in Allmoxy and invoiced via QuickBooks. Customer Behavior & Market Fit: Pinnacle’s customers (builders, contractors) typically send floorplans and expect design work, not to self-service orders online. Market feedback suggests a lack of fit between Allmoxy’s order-based model and Pinnacle’s custom workflows. Regional (Texas/Southern) market challenges were discussed, including territorial business dynamics. Value Acknowledged: Despite challenges, Pinnacle sees value in what’s been built in Allmoxy and wants to preserve it. Open to future potential, possibly by launching a standardized cabinet offering. Technical Issues & Fixes Allmoxy was determined early on to be incompatible with Pinnacle’s fully custom cabinetry workflow due to too many variables. The company moved to using Allmoxy for doors only, but sales volume has been too low to justify the current contract. Payment flow between Allmoxy and QuickBooks is currently manual; upcoming QuickBooks integration may simplify this. Decisions Made The Allmoxy team agreed to bring this request to leadership and return with options, including a lower subscription rate or temporary pause. (or a mix of both) Sarah proposed a follow-up with Aaron (Allmoxy’s lead engineer) to revisit catalog viability if Pinnacle wants to explore cabinet productization in the future. Josh will present multiple pricing options for consideration, factoring in reduced usage. Challenges or Concerns Lack of ROI due to underutilization of Allmoxy. Concern about losing existing system setup if cancellation is immediate. Uncertainty around when/if Pinnacle will launch a separate brand for cabinet doors. Regional sales resistance and online sales model misalignment. Action Items Josh to send revised subscription options (pause, reduced rate, or long-term restructure) to Pinnacle Cabinets. Sarah to propose possible re-engagement with Allmoxy engineer Aaron, potentially in 3–6 months. Pinnacle to review rate proposals and decide on preferred option. Evaluate future cabinet catalog creation for internal quoting and limited external use. Consider exploring Allmoxy examples (e.g., Greyloch) for how semi-custom cabinet lines can be structured effectively. Next Steps Josh will follow up with pricing proposals and flexible options for a 6-month pause or reduced functionality use. Pinnacle will review options and confirm path forward (pause, lower rate, future rebuild). Potential meeting with engineer Aaron scheduled later if Pinnacle decides to pursue cabinet line integration into Allmoxy. Continued evaluation of launching a separate cabinet door brand to resolve current market fit challenges.

Retention meeting prep Context They have been a customer for 2 years, overpaying at 1799/ month They are launched, and the catalog looks great, just didn't get as many sales for their door line as they anticipated The previous sales team put them in the 2-3 million tier, as expected revenue through Allmoxy I offered them the same rate for 2025/26 (approved by Madison), but they requested to cancel after discussing with their team (however, they are open to a rate that is fair to both parties) Looks like they are only using retail bids at the moment. Purpose of the call Build up a case to get them a better rate in Allmoxy moving forward Better understand how they are currently using Allmoxy and what they would like to get out of it if they moved forward with a reduced rate. Get a better feel for their business model. Are they truly millwork? Originally, they anticipated 2-3 million in revenue. What is a more realistic goal for this next year?

Triggers Looks like they got a lot of their triggers activated, they look good! Do they want to attach invoices to customer notifications? Notes FedEx shipping not working (freight economy, ground is working) Qty of the items ordered, big orders you want them to not use ground. (restrict the customer from choosing that option) Or by weight, if Weight exceeds X weight than don't let them select an option. Wants the marketplace to be optional for end customers. (not a current function) They want an Allmoxy audit from our team. General lookover. Did they audit correctly?
Services update 6/21/24 Still working on getting ready to launch Last thing to do is shipping, then they can launch Need to meet with CS to talk about FedEx.

engagements.title: Jacob Conner, Pinnacle Cabinets & Millwork- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations
engagements.body: Jacob Conner, Pinnacle Cabinets & Millwork- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations

@Josh Baker I had a renewal reminder task set for these guys but I bumped it out another month where they are still working on launching. I don't dare try and renew them right now where they are paying $1799 a month. Please let me know if I can assist on this one in any way.
Services update 5/31/24 Juan fixed the exporter for them I have been reaching out for CS training

Meeting 5/1/24 Today: Shipping Getting on weekly schedule with them Shipping notes Most of what they are doing right now are full cabinet jobs and installation. Shipping doors is going to be something new. They think they will just be flat-packing They are in TX, and most of their products are centered around doors They are doing installation as well Collect shipping after estimated, or do a flat rate. How do people typically weigh materials? (lumber supplier great resource) FedEx: rough estimate, our integration sends raw information of weight of the order based on sq ft of material- FedEx says, based on this weight we are going to charge X amount of dollars. Typically when you are quoting out an order, you also need to include width and depth, so it isn't incredibly accurate. One option:  go the route where they notify customers that shipping is variable, and they will calculate the rate of the shipment and they might have an adjusted invoice. Other option (they liked this one): some customers that use that integration frequently audit it. For example, cross reference what it will cost in Allmoxy and compare it to FedEx--> use the Shipping Price variable (shipping_price*1.30) so they get a little bit more money than what Allmoxy is evaluating. Next week: Triggers (Jacob will create some, then we will review) How an order flows through Allmoxy They are going to book some time with Juan to build out material weight

CS Onboarding Playbook Is this a VIP/Legacy customer?(If you're unsure check with leadership) No Is our Services team being utilized for this build? Yes, Services owning entire build Who is the main POC within the customer? James Conner and Jacob Notes: Confirm the customers desired Outcome for using Allmoxy Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) When is the customers goal launch date? 03/31/2024 Notes: They could possibly launch by the end of March What cadence of meeting schedule did you set with the customer? Notes: As needed now. They have been trained fully. Ask the customer to define their benchmark for success Unknown What Software(s) does the customer have? How will Allmoxy work with these softwares? Mozaik Is the customer looking to fit Allmoxy around an existing ordering system or is this their first?(Utilize your Services Rep to more deeply understand how this impacts the catalog build.) Notes: First ordering system Is the customer letting you drive the meeting and follow an agenda or do they want to drive and take charge of meetings? Either can be okay, but dependent on the customers approach to onboarding you will need to pivot your approach to working with them. Notes: We drove in the beginning and now are letting them drive as they sort stuff out with Services. Any initial concerns with the customer and their ability to launch(personnel, resources available, understanding of product, etc?) Notes: Possibly understanding of the product and ability to integrate with other systems.

engagements.title: Todd Lewis, Organizetosize- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations
engagements.body: Todd Lewis, Organizetosize- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations

Call with Todd 12/20/23: Sarah met with O2S team two weeks ago to help with QB questions and work Todd and I dicussed weight problem for SR-AS product they're B2Bing with Lewis, set up meeting for Todd, Suzanee and Juan for tomorrow(12/21) Canceled this week and next weeks meeting due to Todd's schedule/holiday and meeting again in new year

Meeting with Todd 11/29/23 Todd, Sarah and Madison Worked through order confusion and reconciled it's not a bug and how Lewis is splitting, cloning and deleting orders. Planning to meet with UltraShelf soon to start that reselling partnership. Todd and Ross meeting in upcoming weeks. Next week to follow product/order all the way through to financial to make sure all looks good there.

Quick chat with Todd 11/15/23 Todd and Jenn have worked on the mapping for Sassant and they have good progress on it Both Suzanne and Jen can't make it so canceling meeting today Having a good amount of orders through etsy Jardee planning to push the marketing campaign this week(joint push with Ultrashelf) Looking for any other companies GoDaddy offers ecommerce platform and could be easier than implementing direct to consumer Would it be a conflict for Todd to B2B with Ultra? Reselling floating shelves. Todd to run idea by Jardee - @Sarah Pierce do you see any conflict here? Resuming meetings on Nov 29th @Sarah Pierce for visibility

11/8/23 Quickbooks O2S: Jen, Suzanne and Todd Allmoxy: Sarah and Madison Covered mapping using Sassant from Allmoxy to QB via exporting queue Sarah sending Jen the directions she uses with customers for mapping - @Sarah Pierce Export of data Cut sheet dimensions calculated on an order form, currently hand typing this information into excel spreadsheet(exactly what customer is putting on order form and then formulas in spreadsheet are converting to cut sheet) Open to changing their process(google application preferred) Known data needed: Order number, Width, length and number of dividers Formula would need to populate before or after export but needs to be considered Options: Make Allmoxy Output Pages cleaner(PDF) Build out export on every product Build export formula in trigger that would send the data to an email or use a webhook to put on spreadsheet(limitation here is part data) Sarah suggests starting with simple CSV line/export and starting simple. Suzanne and Todd will need to meet with services to get this built - @Juan Ridgeway scoping call needed here. I've sent the customer your link to schedule this call Other: Waiting for Jardee to push marketing email campaign and the should have real orders come through. Open items below. Quickbooks Triggers and shipping we will consult on as orders come through and we have live/real life feedback Sarah to look into how other customers are using UPS @Sarah Pierce Bar code scanning - cover when Wayne is on meeting Is there another company with similar scenario/process to OrganizeToSize with Etsy and Roger Keeping 30 minute cadence for now and Madison checking in on Tuesday's to confirm/check attendance

11/1/23 Quickbooks Training O2S: Jen and Todd Allmoxy: Sarah, Madison and Carson Training on Quickbooks export Categorizing Allmoxy sales separate from Etsy sales They differentiate in Etsy to show in Setting up export class/mapping Tax structure - tricky part Allmoxy sends tax code(hard coded based on abreviation of state) Set up sales tax item with same name so it matches and pulls correctly Suggested Sasant for mapping tool Discussed API coming down the line @Sarah Pierce sending a video overview of export que process and CSV so she can see the headers @Sarah Pierce Lets save this internally in the CS Product Training google doc for future reference and use(https://docs.google.com/document/d/1O4s_Gpox92cyrbLgjzzdpmtSbBW8j5w8RdIgrH2Awdk/edit) - it could be a good future article but also understand the API will hopefully resolve a lot of these trainings Waiting for Jardee to push marketing email campaign and the should have real orders come through. Open items below. Quickbooks Triggers and shipping we will consult on as orders come through and we have live/real life feedback Sarah to look into how other customers are using UPS @Sarah Pierce Bar code scanning - cover when Wayne is on meeting Is there another company with similar scenario/process to OrganizeToSize with Etsy and Roger Keeping 30 minute cadence for now and Madison checking in on Tuesday's to confirm/check attendance

10/24/23 Todd Office Visit Wants to connect Shopify to Allmoxy instance for purpose of tracking on back end. Wants to be introduced to Ultrashelf for B2B opportunity and best practice conversation around instance.

10/11/23 Sarah, Carson, Madison Todd No Suzanne or Wayne This meeting: Shipping B2B Other connections for B2B - needs refreshed version of list from what Broc gave him Sarah says focus should be B2Bing with Manufacturers Has store on etsy now and was wondering if Allmoxy plays with Shopify - https://www.etsy.com/shop/OrganizeToSize - Sarah mentioned would need CSV from etsy/shopify. Would need set parameters on how products are being measured. Orders would funnel through allmoxy so production is centralized. Add Wayne to meeting invite ongoing Next meeting: Quickbooks Triggers and shipping we will consult on as orders come through and we have live/real life feedback Sarah to look into how other customers are using UPS @Sarah Pierce Moving meetings to 30 minute cadence as we need more information once orders are running Bar code scanning and how to implement is something to cover after Quickbooks - Sarah touched on how less is more for bar code scanning stations. Suggests quality check station and shipping

Meeting with Todd & Suzanne 10/4/23 Madison and Carson @Sarah Pierce for visibility. Set up weekly cadence to get necessary training and ready for launch when B2B is released. Note: Suzanne has Fortune 500 corporate background. Highly skilled in process and technically strong. Also working on Design to Size business. Overall very laid back and close knit team that is passionate about the product. Recap: Todd and Jardee have created an email marketing campaign for Lewis customers called "built to size cabinet accessories" for when B2B is ready. Trying to use the B2B marketplace functionality with Lewis when B2B features are launched. Ultimately want to keep it simple and get it working with Lewis and then expand to other B2B customers How can they prep for re-selling when B2B is turned on/they are launched? If they find they are keeping up with orders with Lewis partnership they will be ready to expand to the next Allmoxy customer to re-sell with. Head of manufacturing is Wayne. Going about considering other manufacturers to B2B in the following ways: Likely Primary Kitchen, Todd has meeting out in Denver through Roger in upcoming month Wants to know others we would suggest so they can do research @Broc Hill @Samantha Alexander - who would be a good intro/fit. Want to know this now Has a friend in Denver that has Cabinet shop connections and is going. to be introduced, not sure if they are Allmoxy users - let Todd know to tell us if he likes any that are not customers and wants us to reach out Leveraging local NY relationships Todd has once he's comfortable/ready to take on What Todd/Suzanne would like to accomplish: Triggers - Suzanne worked with Jardee to do initial trigger work. Wants to know if they have anywhere to grow in this area? Since they haven't received orders they might just have to wait to find out was is missing but want a consultative eye here. - @Sarah Pierce Triggers make sense for notifying and sending emails. Suzanne feels less clear on the "guts." Not sure they need to change anything on the product level but want to make sure the way they have things aligned with their business processes. Voiced they internally may need to have conversations on business processes and make sure they are being smart about using the features in Allmoxy they are needed. Open to consultation here. They might not know the best way to do things here until they start doing it but want to mitigate any issues that could arise Shipping Struggles - @Sarah Pierce For Lewis they will receive orders and ship all items back to Lewis, at some point may need to look into this, not a ton of concern now with Lewis shipping set up since it's one address and might not do through Allmoxy. More concern over UPS account set up within Allmoxy, Suzanne not confident they are setting up good shipping estimates based on what they set up in Allmoxy - is it API call from Allmoxy to UPS that determines - wants more visibility in what is going on between shipping numbers they receive and feel confidence - shipping price is where she's wanting confidence - since their business model is drop shipped is this different than other customers business process? Are there other companies that use Allmoxy that have similar/unique business model? Any that could also sell direct to a consumer. Wants more clarity on how other companies use Allmoxy. - @Broc Hill @Samantha Alexander any thoughts here? Quickbooks - wants to understand what is the struggle customers have had here(specifically Lewis) and what do they need to know for a successful launch - how should Allmoxy speak to Quickbooks? - @Sarah Pierce Future - barcoding, and other features to make the manufacturing process easier between the shop and Allmoxy - have Wayne Fisher join for that conversation(manufacturing floor guy/shop partner) Wants Wayne set up as an employee of Allmoxy Interested in Employee/User Permissions for Wayne and another potential future manufacturer... could be tricky on the pricing side for multiple manufacturers Other/Admin Waiting on functionality for tagging in B2B to make this work - wanting a weekly update - Madison When do we push version updates? - Madison checking with Cheneil on Marketing Newsletter highlighting these, also discussed Appcues for UI feature changed Interested in Courtney's services in long term future Wants to start the first 15-20 minutes next meeting getting to know each other/professional background

Todd called me to introduce himself and thank for intro email. 10/25/23 Context of call/history of partnership: Todd and Jardee have been talking about the soon to come B2B release. Has another design company with 70 projects in the pipeline. Goes back and forth between the two companies. Waiting on B2B newest feature push and we discussed status of that push. Previously never had the time to invest in Organize to Size until Covid since he was out of the world of deadlines. Drawer dividers/spice racks, behind the door accessories is speciality. During that time he tried to create his own "Allmoxy" and realized what a feat it would be. That is when a peer in the industry referred to Allmoxy. B2B was most appealing to him for the partnership. Has been 3 years of investing in Allmoxy and feeling like now is the big push to officially launch. Opportunity - In a position where they just want to see how things start with Lewis in regards to B2B. If they feel they can start to handle more orders he's very interested in other Allmoxy customers to do business with. Has always appreciated @Broc Hill thoughts in this area! Wants us to keep them in mind for working with another Allmoxy customer. Training - could use some more help here. Suzanne worked on a lot of trigger work. Todd feels he has a better handle on the triggers. Would love to have more training around where it could be utilized better(B2B with Lewis) and other future companies. At a point now where they have the time to invest in making this work with Lewis and potentially other opportunities. Month of October schedule time with him and Suzanne. Previously, worked with Peyton Wells and then hired Roger Roberts on the catalog side, have him available if needed for dynamic pricing/other services work. Peyton began process and Robert picked it up. Built it from a spreadsheet Todd created to a formula to work in Allmoxy. Connectivity to quickbooks is also training he would be interested in. Would prefer a consultative approach to training. @Sarah Pierce Tools Used: Asked about how Sarah is and we talked about her new role. Asked: "Is Sketchup in conversation for API Integrations?" @Sarah Pierce Allmoxy Summit - @Cheneil Garrett Could be interested in attending especially with any B2B sessions. Other: Todd is very friendly and chatty! Coming to Utah in October tentatively and will reach out to grab lunch while in town and see Lewis team. Has friends in SLC and PC area. Also interested in a local trout stream for fly fishing. Mentioned he has discussed fly fishing a lot with Bobby, Beau and @Brady Lewis!

engagements.body: Services Training/Scoping Call with Micaela & Mauro: Juan & Madison 10/3/23 Recap: Micaela re-emphasized their intent on how they want to use Allmoxy. Since they are a dealer they will be building multiple product lines/manufacturer lines to use to design and quote with Allmoxy. Want to know exactly how each of those catalogs can be "uploaded/built" and distinguished. Should someone want to choose between different manufacturers/lines(difference in skews, colors and pricing) how does the build look? Juan spoke to build out process for different product lines and when it's needed for a whole new product and when it's not. Micaela asked how quoting works with the designer with products. Suggested starting with most complex product line and then clone to next line. Micaela said they might start with easiest line rather than most complex. Discussed using categories/grouping for their customer base Is there a way to compare quotes with other lines? Yes, using order level settings. Wanted to verify no way of uploading a file to make the product build process easier. Juan said they can upload the skew pricing matrix but not much else. What do they do for nontraditional products that are not templated, specifically within the designer and storefront(ex. wine cabinet) - Juan recommends starting in storefront, start with a closet close and clone(ex. standard wall cabinet) so you don't start from scratch. 3D issues Morian has run into, not registering - Juan says could be intersections or script being off telling when to snap(on product level code needing to be changed) Mauro watched 40 minute video(likely demo) where Broc did a walk through with Augustine and is the extent of his backend knowledge of Allmoxy Discussed next step options. Determining if priority is to take their time and build out on their own over months, pay for services to train and guide them of pay for services to complete full build. Micaela is not wanting services to handle full build.   Next Steps: Sending over recommended Knowledge Base articles and videos Indy considering training/consulting route with services team @Juan Ridgeway , discussed at $110/hr rate

Re-engagement Meeting Agenda w/Micaela 9/22/23 @Gavin Flitton @Carson Ward Agenda & Meeting Notes: Onboarding Tasks they have completed: Week 1: Stripe set up, Branding Uncompleted tasks: Test order, Triggers, Shipping(zip/conversation), company workflow, Credit Limit What does Micaela know? Need to start training/onboarding from the beginning is likely. Target Launch Date = by end of year they need this working, ideally by Thanksgiving, have been paying since beginning of 2023

engagements.title: Call with Agustin Molini
engagements.body:
@Gavin Flitton @Madison Theisen context behind the Indy Cabinetry email.


How we arrived...


Indy Cabinetry was behind 2 months worth of payments. Broc and I have both attempted to reach out and have not been successful in outreach.


Micaela (Augustins sister) called to update her card on file. We updated it then caught them up on their payments.



Where Indy Cabinetry is...


Micaela is not involved in Allmoxy like Augustin is. She saw the bills and wondered why they are paying so much and not using the system.


After looking through our correspondence we both agreed that we need to meet to evaluate where we left them, then see what is necessary to reengage and get them running successfully.


Micaela was very kind and understanding that Augustin let Allmoxy fall by the wayside. She is not upset. They don't want to cancel. She just wants to see movement.



Next Steps....


Micaela wants to talk with Augustin to be caught up to speed on their side.


I encouraged Micaela to have a re-engagement meeting with CS/Services. She was in agreement.


I emailed her the link and will follow up next week.


Biggest Takeaway..


At this point she is pretty unsure where things got left on both sides and wants to pick it back up.

engagements.title: Allmoxy & Northland Woodworks
engagements.body: Allmoxy & Northland Woodworks

Kickoff Call 8/22/24 Confirm the key stakeholders: - Know who signed the contract(Executive Buyer) before the call(from sales) and if/how they will be involved in onboarding -Who will be the key point of contact for the build? -Sales POC? -Production POC? -Financial/Accounting POC? -Any other stakeholders? Have a few contacts listed here but ideally want to hear from you directly on who is overseeing what Can you confirm who will be my POC for the following roles Executive Buyer: Leah, Matt - Owners Production: Matt Financial/Accounting: Leah, business operations Ethan - Project Manager for Allmoxy internally (Allmoxy Ninja) Why did you buy the product? What were they hoping to achieve when they signed the contract and how does Allmoxy fit into that? If their answer is too broad, ask them to expand on how they envision their business changing when they purchased Allmoxy. Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) This is a newer venture so there's no process in place and the goal is an online storefront for your customers to come and order from you, correct? Can you expand upon what this ideally looks like to you? Do you want the general public to see your catalog without signing into Allmoxy? Or would you like to vet new sign ups before giving them catalog access? Ethan Hunter: 09:45 So our current process is we work with, like, I want to say higher profile, but that's kind of a bad way of saying it. Architects, exclusive designers. Yeah, like, we work with kind of the best people in the area, and this is like, we still want to work with people like that. Architects, interior designers, people who kind of have fish together, not just like a, like a customer, you know, not retail. So we're expecting them to come in, like, with a good idea, have kind of that premium mindset that we kind of like to work with, but just kind of cutting out the back and forth of like, oh, should we do this? Oh, no, that's not going to work. This is kind of like people that are. Leah: Only setting up customers they know with specific logins (Doesn't want this to be a public ordering system that anyone can just create an account) (Define success) When does the customer want to "launch" and what would a successful onboarding look like? How do you know when you're there? Ex. Door Product Line is Launched and Cabinet Line is started. Getting this to work Getting a whole job out the door Triggers working (seemless and nice) Making Money   Ethan Hunter: 13:24 I mean, I'd say very basic, basic milestone is getting this to work because that's what happened last time was we went through all this and it didn't work and it was a whole issue. Oh, God, my cat's stuck in the closet. Sorry about that. Getting it to work and getting it to run through cabinet vision or CNC. Get it. Get it built. Just a cabinet. And then, yes, number two is getting a whole job out the door. Somebody picked it up. That whole process worked. All of our triggers work. The process is seamless and nice, and we delivered a product that is good. And then three is making money. So we put a significant payment for TechMD, and then also we have the payments here for all moxie. So I think that's the ultimate goal, is to make money there. Past experience with Leah: Leah Krig: 14:47 Well, we were using the 3d design software or whatever that's called. I can't remember. But, you know, the almoxy three d. And so I would. I would be drawing a kitchen, and then I would export it into almoxy or however that worked, you know, so it create a parts list and things wouldn't match up, like the edge banding wouldn't match the material on the outside of the cabinets, and just. Just different things like that. It was just like we didn't even get where we were running anything. It was just like, wow. It was like the ordering part just wasn't working. And it was frustrating me, and at that time, Ethan wasn't working for us, and I was the only one that was really working on the integration. It just felt like we would never be able to get there. Catalog) Going to be using the TEKMD catalog, is that correct? What communication have you had with them so far regarding customizing the catalog to fit your needs? Are they building your exports, output pages, and production paperwork as well? Did they give you a timeline? Has two meetings with TEK next week - waiting to see how those meetings go. Also recommended that he bring any production paperwork and export information to the table so TEK can build these for them. Ethan Hunter: 17:38 I am honestly not sure exactly, but I do know that they kind of sell and we purchased everything after. Everything after getting on the website is techMD's thing. So all moxie is just like running the website, making sure that our orders are fulfilled and like the UI is there and then technology seems like it's pretty much everything after that. So I guess I'll answer that question once, like after I have those meetings. What's your familiarity with the system? How comfortable are you with Allmoxy in general? Does anyone on your team have experience? Ethan has ordered from Primer Eurocase and also seen Bella IMC so he's familiar with the customer ordering process but not the backend stuff like updating order statuses or setting up a trigger or output page. (Technology) What financial software do you use? Quickbooks What machining software do you use? Cabinet Vision - 2021 ( TEK requires 2023) looking to transition due to TEKs requirement For production, what cutlists, exports, and labels do you anticipate being available? Do you currently have any lists/reports that you love? If so, share them with us! Can we get a breakdown of how they price deliveries? (Training Cadence) Based on your familiarity, what cadence works best for training? Would you like to start training now or wait until TEK has completed the catalog so we know what we're working with? Who should be involved in the training sessions? Ethan is going to be the point person for most items and trainings. We'll need to get Matt involved regarding the production lines and verifying orders. Next Steps: Schedule first training session based on their needs Confirm TEKs timeline for customizing the catalog to your needs Get everyone that should be involved a login for Allmoxy Follow Up Items/notes: General navigation of the site - Tried implementing before (never got to the point of understanding the interface and how to customize it to their needs) Triggers - Heavy focus (automation) Wants to see data (where costs are going, how can they make this more efficient) Cadence : Monday's 10 - 10:45 MST (11 - 11:45 CST)

2026-06-17 | CS + IM | Precision Millwork Utah — Envision Exporter Walkthrough & Go-Live Prep Fireflies transcript: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KV6KTSAR80NAQSG68329KB2N Meeting Summary Ray walked Dave through the full Envision exporter setup in Allmoxy including proxy variables, attribute references and export file naming Dave confirmed the Envision export is in good shape and just needs final testing before go-live Energy export was deferred — Jose was out; Dave and Jose will review together upon his return Crown molding product images (3 products) are missing — Dave to send image files to Ray for upload Dave confirmed the split knife logic: anything under 3/4" width = TRUE, anything above = FALSE — Ray implemented dynamically via a new column on the Width Select attribute Team agreed Precision Millwork Utah is very close to go-live; remaining steps are Envision testing, Energy export fix and a team training session Key Takeaways Important Discussions Proxy variables: Ray explained the workflow — build and test the formula directly in the product first, then move to the proxy once verified so broken references don't get silently masked Attribute CSV export/import: Ray showed how to export attribute options to CSV, bulk-edit values (e.g. split knife TRUE/FALSE), and reimport — significant time saver for large option sets Adding columns vs rows: Columns must be added inside Allmoxy first; rows can be added in Excel/Sheets during the CSV workflow Export file naming: Dave can customize the file name format in the Exporters section under Settings Scripting language: Allmoxy formulas are a diluted version of PHP; Ray offered to assist if Dave needs help Technical Issues and Fixes Crown molding product categories (Cove, Crown Verse 1, Bevel) have missing images — no image was ever assigned, not a broken reference Energy export formula has a broken "price" part reference — Ray flagged this as still needing correction; deferred to Dave and Jose Decisions Made Split knife logic will use a new column on Width Select attribute (TRUE for < 3/4", FALSE for >= 3/4") rather than a hard-coded formula Envision exporter proxy updated with the new dynamic split knife reference Challenges or Concerns Dave's team gets busy and implementation stalls — Dave acknowledged this; no blockers currently Action Items Dave Bleazard: Send image files for broken crown molding products to Ray for upload Dave Bleazard: Run tests on Envision exporter and report any issues back to Ray Dave Bleazard: Review and update Energy export with Jose upon his return Dave Bleazard: Coordinate a team training session and record a concise "how to add a product" demo Ray Cantu: Upload crown molding images once received from Dave Ray Cantu: Continue supporting Energy export fixes as needed Connor Wright: Send meeting recording and go-live checklist doc to Dave and team Connor Wright: Coordinate and facilitate team training sessions on exports and Allmoxy Next Steps Dave will test the Envision exporter and report back Dave and Jose to work through the Energy export together when Jose returns Once both exports are confirmed working, schedule a full team training session (beginning to end workflow including how to add a product and set up new companies)

[2026-05-19] | CS + IM | Precision Millwork — Output Pages, Width Attributes & Validations https://app.fireflies.ai/view/01KREE08QJDPKNR59KS4TVMWW0 Meeting Summary Working session with Dave and Brian F focused on Allmoxy platform training: adding global widths to the width select attribute, updating banner/validation text on the interior jam product (P RAB INT JAM), and an in-depth walkthrough of building output pages (cut lists/part lists) Dave confirmed output pages are critical to launch — he needs a rip list and molder list before production testing can begin Ray walked through how to create parts, part attributes, and tie them to output pages, including how to add a rip width column to the width select attribute as a global/dynamic variable Key Takeaways Important Discussions How to add global widths: navigate to the width select attribute, add a row, default to not available, then override per product How to change banner text: validations tab on a product → find the relevant validation → edit the message text; changes are real-time, no reload needed Output pages are saved/generated only after an order is saved; can't be previewed on new orders Parts vs. product profiles: "parts" in the parts tab are the data building blocks for output pages, not physical profiles Rip width as a global variable: add a "rip width" column to the width select attribute; formula in the part references width select + rip width column instead of a hardcoded value Technical Issues and Fixes Existing "pricing" part on products is not useful for the customer-facing output page and should be hidden/turned off Some products showed calculation errors — Ray noted these are likely due to previously removed/deleted references, not urgent Decisions Made Ray will build the rip list output page for Precision Millwork once Dave provides the list of fields and the formulas needed Agreed to keep output pages as universal as possible rather than per-product to simplify maintenance Challenges or Concerns Dave and Brian are not HTML-literate; Ray suggested AI coding tools (e.g., Claude) for any custom HTML output page work Dave opened the question of whether Allmoxy could just build it for them ("even if we hire you") Action Items Dave: Send Ray a list of the fields and formulas needed for the rip list / molder list output page Ray Cantu: Build out the rip list and molder list output page once Dave provides the field list and formulas Connor Wright: Send recording to Dave within 30 minutes; send action item recap; schedule next meeting to review output page + cover Envision/Energy exporting Next Steps Schedule next working session: review Ray's output page build, then cover Envision and Innergy/Energy API exporting Dave to provide field + formula list to Ray asap to unblock the build Team training (order entry, API, exporting, adding products/clients) still deferred to after launch

5/4/26 — Working Session | Connor Wright, Ray Cantu Working session with Dave Bleazard and Michelle Reidhead. Completed majority of pre-launch catalog checklist live — product category moves, width attribute overrides, category image replacement, priority sort updates. Output pages partially cleaned up; width attribute still showing as text and linear footage connection to parts list needs verification. API setup and Envision exporter deferred — still open pre-launch items, no timeline. Dave is technically capable and self-sufficient. Connor to send recording. Good session, positive sentiment.

Allmoxy To-Do List — Precision Millwork (Dave Bleazard, 5/1/26) Hey @Raymundo Cantu these are the notes Precision is wanting to talk about on the call Monday morning.  With being so new I wasn't sure what needed to be discussed on a call or not so had them send over everything holding getting launched. Catalog Fixes Needed (Admin Required): PT&G 1 — Move to Tongue and Groove category (currently under Door & Window/Interior Door Jamb) PT&G 9 — Add 8.964" width PT&G 10 — Add 8.964" width PCORNER 4-0.813-90 — Cut list width: 0.843" PCORNER 5-1063-90 — Cut list width: 1.196" PCORNER 6-0.75-90 — Cut list width: 0.931" PCORNER 7-1-90 — Cut list width: 1.285" PCORNER 8-1.313-90 — Cut list width: 1.55" PCORNER 9-1.25-90 — Cut list width: 1.638" PCORNER 9-2.25-135 — Cut list width: 2.405" Interior Door Jamb — Replace category image with Dave's attached image Jamb widths — Enable typed-in values or expand dropdown (exclude "number-number" format entries) Topics for Monday's Meeting: Production lists: Parts List, Rip List (items #4–10 have wider cut dimensions than names suggest), Moulder List Innergy API EnVision Export (partially reviewed — needs full walkthrough) Team Training: order entry & processing, Innergy API + EnVision export, adding new molding products, adding new clients Notes: Dave requested short reference videos for some processes. After completing the above, Precision Millwork is ready to begin production testing.

IMP meeting with Precision 3/18/25 Meeting Summary This meeting focused on clarifying wood species pricing updates, reviewing the export process for Envision, and discussing pending tasks related to Energy CSV integration. Key discussions revolved around setting up an efficient process for updating material pricing and ensuring smooth data transfer between systems. Key Takeaways Important Discussions: Wood Species Pricing Updates: The pricing table in Allmoxy was reviewed and structured to align with supplier data. Gary confirmed that an average pricing approach will be used for the three provided data columns (Intermountain, Sierra, High Mountain). Ray demonstrated how to update pricing manually within Allmoxy. Users can adjust pricing via a dedicated table without deleting values; availability toggles can be used instead. Any additional species can be added manually without disrupting the system. Envision Export Process: The Envision exporter is already integrated into the system and accessible from the order page. Users can export data by selecting "Export Data for Envision." Testing recommended using small orders first to ensure smooth integration. Identified a known issue with molding orders in Envision, where the system miscalculates width adjustments. Winig is working on fixing this issue; continued pressure may be needed. Pending Energy CSV Integration: The team still requires a CSV export from Energy to map data properly. Michelle will reach out to Katie Mitchell for information on the availability of this file. If no direct integration is possible, an external process may be used for data transfer. Technical Issues & Fixes: Molding Order Issue in Envision: Envision currently miscalculates width adjustments on molding orders. Dave and Winig are working on a fix. Energy CSV File Requirement: Awaiting confirmation on file availability for seamless import. Decisions Made: Use an average of the three pricing columns for Allmoxy pricing updates. Proceed with testing Envision export using small sample orders. Await response from Energy regarding CSV file availability before proceeding with further integration steps. Challenges or Concerns: Ensuring accurate pricing updates across all products within Allmoxy. Verifying that Envision exports correctly map data to the corresponding fields. Resolving the molding order width calculation error in Envision. Action Items: Gary: Implement pricing updates in Allmoxy using the average column approach. Conduct initial testing of the Envision export process with small orders. Ray Cantu: Support Gary in updating and testing pricing adjustments. Address any issues encountered in the Envision export process. Michelle Reidhead: Contact Katie Mitchell regarding Energy CSV availability and share updates with the team. Coordinate with Dave and Grace for reviewing export files. Josh Baker: Send a tutorial video covering pricing updates and Envision export process. Assist in troubleshooting any encountered issues during testing. Next Steps: Confirm pricing updates and validate changes within Allmoxy. Test Envision export with small orders and document any issues. Follow up on Energy CSV availability and determine next steps for integration. Address Envision’s molding order miscalculation issue with Winig.

Onboarding #4 with Precision Millwork Team Checklist of things they has set up Theme (no) Add employees (yes) Set up Stripe? (no) Basic trigger setup (no) (important for customers on payment terms) Production lines (yes) Price adjustments (no) but they are starting small Test order Testing notes/ services notes Does every single pattern in here correspond to a specific thickness and width? If this is a must-have, our team would have to break down each pattern into an individual product. Which we can do but it would add an additional 10-14 hours to be on the safe side. Financial order settings (determine when your customers pay) Default checkout flow (use credit limit or pay before processing) You can set this globally or per customer How do they want it set? If some customers refuse to do pay before processing, you can change their individual checkout flow Credit limit is extending a certain amount of money to place an order, and set up payment terms (pay when they ship or the order is marked complete) Order limit/ build limit: the amount of product you will build but NOT necessarily ship to your customer until they pay down the balance. You set those terms Default interest rate- if you are doing terms and they haven't paid by the end of the month, a fee is attached and will charge them a finance charge. Daily is every day something is past due, it attaches a fee Sales tax presence Where are you doing business, just UT or regional? 5K a year threshold- when you collect 5K in revenue from a state, you need to start collecting sales tax Statement editor (credit limit): goes out to your customers at the end of every month if they have an outstanding balance. Overhead management can be tracked here: lease payments, etc. Go to the order page, click on the customer to show the dashboard If pay before processing, it will just show their order history If there are credit limits, they can log in and pay from here (or you can do an offline payment- customers don't have access) Show check payment Refund payments (demo) Undo transaction (transaction history report, then refund that payment on your stripe dashboard) Sales tax report (demo) A lot of customers file sales tax quarterly You would select the state and filter the dates- it will show what you owe to the sales tax commission Helpful report for knowing how much you need to pay in taxes Sales by Product report Financial integrations QBD or QBO? We recommend using the export invoices queue function This will get invoice information out of Allmoxy with an export you can use Transaction Pro or Sassant to map the Allmoxy export to QB (which we can help with- typically a one-time set up) The more manual route is using the Export Transactions Queue (while having QBO pulled up) and reconciling payments

CS Onboarding Playbook Is this a VIP/Legacy customer?(If you're unsure check with leadership) No Notes: Return customer, they tried to build Allmoxy themselves the first time and it didn't ever get up and running. Is our Services team being utilized for this build? Yes, Services owning entire build Who is the main POC within the customer? Michelle & Gary Confirm the customers desired Outcome for using Allmoxy Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) Notes: Triggers, automations, tags. They use Innergy for inventory and PM. When is the customers goal launch date? 01/24/2025 What cadence of meeting schedule did you set with the customer? Notes: 1X/ week Ask the customer to define their benchmark for success Online ordering Notes: Trying to capture a new market with certain customers with a 48 hour turnaround time. What Software(s) does the customer have? How will Allmoxy work with these softwares? Quickbooks Notes: Innergy Is the customer looking to fit Allmoxy around an existing ordering system or is this their first?(Utilize your Services Rep to more deeply understand how this impacts the catalog build.) Notes: No, this is take 2 for them and they are trying to tap into a new market. Online ordering is perfect for that. Is the customer letting you drive the meeting and follow an agenda or do they want to drive and take charge of meetings? Either can be okay, but dependent on the customers approach to onboarding you will need to pivot your approach to working with them. Notes: I'm driving, they will start driving soon. Any initial concerns with the customer and their ability to launch(personnel, resources available, understanding of product, etc?) Notes: No, pretty simple catalog build and no inventory tracking. They are already happy with our test product.

Onboarding #3 12/12/24 Jose: operations/ project manager (in Argentina) jose@precisionmillworkutah.com Capri: Office office@precisionmillworkutah.com Ted: yes Kaylin: not Jake: not John: no Talarie: no Lynell: not working in business, Gary's wife Dave: lead engineer Dynamic attribute question? Juan Valley Hardwoods, sell distribution and they deliver to homeowners. People will place their order with distribution and then place order with Valley hardwood which ships directly. Homework check-in Stripe (no) Theme (no) Triggers (no) Have they put any thought into triggers? Should we set one up? Notes from previous calls Customers will log in (correct) Payment terms for some customers (50% down, 50% upon completion)- for larger orders, longer turnaround. Pay before processing for certain customers with a 48 hour turnaround time How will they know which customers have the 48 hour turnaround? Most customers have been paying by check (going to be a mix, most of generals will pay by check) Price adjustments Small price adjustments to start and then if they order more volume they will create new ones How an order moves through Allmoxy What is your current workflow? I will do a test order There is an expected workflow in Allmoxy Bid --> Ordered --> Verified --> In progress --> completed --> shipped When an order is verified, it is locked (the customer can no longer make changes) Customer's can make changes to Bids and Orders SHOW TRIGGERS AGAIN Test order Production lines/ work schedule report Production lines allow you to mimic what is happening on the floor in Allmoxy. What are the steps in making a drawer box? Show Production Lines and Work Schedule Report (easy way to see jobs in verified status) We don't need to set up production lines, but it can be useful. We can even just make one category called "in progress". Or we can get more granular. (Show barcode scanning) You can start jobs for each production line --> moving them to in progress (which will drop off from the report) It will show you a start by date and a ship by date (based off lead time) From there, you can mimic what happens on the job floor in Allmoxy by using the scanning station OR you can move orders to in progress manually Shipping report Once an order is completed, you're ready to get those out the door. You're looking ahead. It shows you what method is being used, company name, etc. Shipping Methods How do you usually ship? Most of the time they do will call (48 hour) Not UPS or FedEx Do shipping based on QTY and Distance, higher quantity the cost goes up.

Onboarding #2 with Michelle (Jose, Capri, Gary) Tags These are ways you can group customers together for a certain purpose These can be referenced in triggers and price adjustments Ex. you have a group of customers that are cabinet shops, and they get a certain discount. You can give this group of customers a "cabinet shop" tag on their company profile that can then be referenced later. You can also put tags on products How do you think you might group certain customers together? Other man companies for tiers With moulding cab shops and finish carpetners There are going to be small price adjustments for different customers UNTIL they become more high volume Price Adjustments Do you have customers that are priced differently than others? Why? Use price adjustments to achieve this Say you have a customer who is your oldest and most loyal customer, you always give them a 10% discount no matter what. Price adjustments are basically discounts or surcharges that can be applied to customers, groups of customers, or products. They are adjusted PER ITEM, not total order amount Accumulation For when there are multiple price adjustments competing with one another If one discount has a higher priority than the other, the highest priority will override the other discount Ex. if you have a new customer discount and a summer sale discount going on at the same time, you would want the new customer discount to have a higher accumulate than the other. Tip: Use the Always setting, so you know if you are discounting or surcharging every time. You can also discount customers or products with certain tags Triggers How are you currently interacting with customers? All manual, very excited about triggers Is it manual calls, emails, etc? Triggers can automate that process for you Notify you, and your customers of something happening in Allmoxy Triggers to edit: 1, 6, 12, 16, 18, 19 Does he want to be notified of a bid? Or an order? Notes Asked what the best way to add products were --> training or have us do it

Onboarding meeting #1 with Michelle 11/22/24 Notes from previous call End customer: cabinet shops, builders, GC (other businesses) Starting with a select few products and building from there Follow up on email for feedback from Ray Covering today Site navigation What he sees vs what his customer will see Creating an account Does he want their users to log in? For a customer Individual and Company Adding employees Permissions Creating an order How will customers be paying? Pay before processing? Or use a credit limit? They have credit limits, everyone pays as soon as they deliver Most of them pay 50% down 50% upon completion (they will want to do this for some) Market is having them do 48 hour turnaround, they want to have an option with some of them paying in full since there is such a short turnaround time. ^^ pay before processing for 48 hour customers How do they process payments? Usually they pay by check New market that they are chasing with 48 hour turnaround time. Stripe set up 2.9% fee for all cards, so it is consistent. (paid by you) The underwriting process is very important She has used Stripe in the past and will likely set it up

Launch plan meeting 11/13/24 with Gary & Michelle End customers: Cabinet shops, builders, GCs Due date: 12/30/24 Goals for the second time around with Allmoxy Already outlined Before they had someone setting it up for them in-house. He switched companies. Not much knowledge with Allmoxy Having us set it up will make it successful for them. Sales and marketing ideas? How do they get people to the website? Their main customers are other businesses. They never got it set up all the way. We need to do a really good job this time Do they want us to add hardware for them? No Can she get an estimate on adding hardware? Rocky Mountain Hardware is the hardware they want to sell They had an Allmoxy person but they don't anymore Should we start on a regular cadence of onboarding? Yes, Tuesdays 12:30

Demo Playbook 2.0 Who's on today's call? (Update contact levels for each contact: Buyer Persona and Buyer Role) Notes: Gary and Michelle What product lines do you currently manufacture? Man. - Millwork, Man. - Other Notes: Knives/moldings Of the product lines you manufacture, which are you wanting to run through Allmoxy? Are you wanting to sell all of those product lines through Allmoxy or just one/a few to begin? Notes: Moldings and man doors. Starting with about 150 options of their 1000 to start Are you outsourcing anything or manufacture everything in-house? Cabinets and Molding and Trim: Is your offering a semi-custom repeatable(most of the jobs are repeatable other than some changes) or is this a Pinterest worthy/Martha Stewart level custom product offering? Notes: not outsourcing What optimization programs are you using, if any? Quickbooks Who is your ideal end customer? Notes: Cabinet shops, builders/gcs/etc What is your current process? (From order entry, to production and inventory tracking and shipping and delivery) - tell me about this. Notes: Excel sheets, emails What gaps are you experiencing in your current process? What pain points are you looking for Allmoxy to solve? (Select top outcome and add additional in notes.) Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) What is your pricing structure and do you have documentation of pricing? Notes: Excel sheet they are putting together What are your current shipping practices? Notes: Local trucks/delivery How are you currently managing inventory and is inventory something you're interested in tracking through Allmoxy? Pause & Potential Cancel Request 7/26/24 Abraham is considering canceling Allmoxy, in his words "I’m really concern if I will need to quit the hole Allmxy as the limitations is so much for that amount of money I’m paying for it." Main Issue: The main issue is the formatting of his output pages, he doesn't understand why we aren't able to simply display the order level settings once at the top and then have each line item below. Here's an example from another customer of ours: Part of his frustration is that this is technically possible if he would have had his catalog build in a way, similar to QualityKitchen's where the catalog is basically stored within one attribute dropdown. Overall our current formatting allows to show all of the information for his products so this doesn't seem like a limitation but rather an unwillingness for him to use a different outline of output pages. Related Issues: In addition to the order level settings, he also desires to have our Allmoxy product IDs hidden, his IDs before the quantity, and have folders display on the output pages as well. I previously requested for a quote (dev - 8481) to see how much it would cost for us to build the order level settings into the output pages but due to our limited bandwidth we aren't able to give a quote. Even if we were able to give a quote it would still be a large cost ($5k-$10K+) which if we told him that I don't believe he would be willing to pay that amount. He requested that we pause his next months subscription, you can see linked here, and I have looped in Broc and Mekenzi. Potential solutions: He uses our default output pages and we edit them as much as we can to match his formatting request but still limited in multiple ways. I get an actual quote to see how much in total it would be for Jake to make some magical output page (not a good solution) We rework his catalog to function similarly to QualityKitchens - (not great but would get him what he wants) Any other ideas are welcome. @Madison Theisen @Mekenzi Falslev @Broc Hill - for vis on Abraham's email from this morning requesting to pause while he reconsiders things.

@Samantha Alexander @Carson Ward @Madison Theisen @Aaron Dundas  The scope call with Abraham went well but he did push back when I gave him the estimate because he said Sam told him it would be 40-60 hours. A full cabinet line will almost never end up taking under 60 hours once EVERYTHING is built out. He is open to take on some tasks once the ground work is done for the catalog.  He does not need us to build out inventory tracking right away but wants it in the future. The catalog needs to be built in a way that will map well to Mozaik with all of its limitations so there will be more program needed to have a single product in Allmoxy map to multiple (singular) products in Mazaik depending on what's selected in Allmoxy.  Some of their cabinets need to scale with the height differently than what is built in any product in the custom template so extra programming is needed on select products. for example, he has a 3 drawer base cabinet that has 2 fixed height drawers at the top and only the bottom scales with the overall cabinet height. That is not how the 3 drawer base cabinet works in the template.  I gave him and estimate of $8,000 (a little over 70 hours) and a timeframe of 6-8 weeks.

engagements.title: Abraham Markowitz, Kingwood Cabinetry- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations
engagements.body: Abraham Markowitz, Kingwood Cabinetry- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations

Onboarding Meeting with Abraham New Meeting Cadence - Week 1 - 6/3/24 Agenda: Regrouping Paused for 2 months - finished your busy season after passover, correct? What's happening on Abraham's end? Allmoxy - What's needed to get started Catalog - Is this functioning as anticipated? Any work left to do with Juan? Pricing Inventory Export file - Moziak Specific output pages or files where product specific information is needed? Operational Financial settings Not processing any payments online - customers given the ability to place an order and Abraham will need to log offline payments if we aren't accepting payments through Stripe. Output Pages Invoice Packing Slip What else is needed on your end? Notifications Review all triggers Edit and keep the desired triggers deactivate unnecessary trigger notifications Next Steps Meet with Juan on Thursday and finish any catalog/product items still remaining. We need a list if possible Finalize the output pages Invoice, packing slip, etc... Complete the trigger notifications Start pushing customers to place orders in the system I'll plan to join the Thursday call with Juan We can schedule a follow up meeting for next week. (10th or 11th) Notes: Things are going good - has been busy Customers want to get the login and get started Juan - scheduled Agenda: Catalog - almost done 85% and then started with inventory tracking Now - pausing the inventory tracking for now Right now Going through a real life customer testing Bug Seeing the green email notification of who is receiving an email notification when an account is created. Flag as bug or bug feature. Tax 8.13% for 10950 need to flag with the team - 0.01 off. Week 8: Agenda: Refocus on getting you launched (what's needed still?) Catalog complete Exports complete Triggers set up Customer's created Output pages confirmed Last topics Order & folder level settings (Let's keep this simple, the more specific we get the longer it can delay our goal of having customers enter their own orders) Payment Processing If we don't allow a gateway then customer payments have to be tracked manually on your end to manage outstanding balances. Is there a different processor you'd like to use? Next Steps Confirm the catalog and exports are complete with Juan Review the triggers and output pages Confirm the payment process you want to follow. (manually updating customers accounts, add a processor, or don't track order revenue/account balances in Allmoxy) Identify a few customers to try this with and either have them create accounts once we're ready. Notes: Cancelled an hour before. Week 5/6: Agenda: Notes from meeting with Sarah Order/folder level settings Order level setting - We can control whether this is expanded or not. Folder level settings - These follow order level settings, these don't automatically expand so order level settings would be better. Folder Output pages - Has been requested previously   attributes will need to show up per group or would be removed completely (Difference between invoice and packing slip) Is this for the customer or for you? Try to reframe the message of how this will simplify his workload (revisit these once we're getting value from the system) Custom color - could change this to a checkbox option and inform the customer they need to add the custom color to the description. (since it's a text box, this type of group attribute isn't applicable across order/folder level settings which is why we would need to use the checkbox) (Create a custom field for this) Takeaways Confirm if you'd like to use the checkbox for the custom color code or not? if so please confirm the wording you'd like to be used. We need to use the order level settings (it's what we can control) and we can have this always expanded We can either show the attributes on the output pages (invoice and packing slip) or hid them, this is at the group level, not possible with the folder level. (has been requested) Payment processing (manual payments) What we experienced was an anticipated function - we don't want customers to be able to process offline payments because they could say they paid but really didn't. This portion needs to be controlled by you or whoever is managing customer checks, cash, zelle, etc. Customer's can submit the orders in an ordered status though, even without the payment gateway setup, they'll just receive the error message that the gateway isn't completed. We'll need to slightly adjust your triggers so once someone submits an order and you verify it and then the customer needs to contact you and make their payment. Once you've received the payment you can log against the order that it's been partially or completely paid and can move into production. Takeaway - edit the triggers for this process Customer submits order -> You Verify the order -> customer receives notification to contact you to submit partial or full payment -> Once payment is received on your end you mark what has been paid against the order -> you can leave the order in verified or move it to "in progress". Notes: Week 5: Agenda: Follow up items (These are my responses after discussing with Ashley & team) Removing Output Page Totals - I found a reason why you shouldn't remove the totals from the invoice page. I attached a screenshot here of one test order that has multiples of the same product which then totals the amounts within that group. If we remove the totals you won't have a calculated total for that group. If you still want the totals removed I can ask my team what it would cost to customize that specific page. Output Page Order Level Settings - This has been previously requested however due to the priority of other requests this one hasn't moved forward. I've ensured that the team is aware of the need so in the future when this is able to be scoped you'll be one of the first to know. For now, we either don't show the attributes or they will be part of each line grouping. Sequential Numbers for Each Line Item - This is a completely new feature request that hasn't been asked for before. The number IDs that are currently on each group and line were specifically designed to be this way, grouping multiple parts together. I've created the feature request but based on the volume of features that are currently being worked on this would be at least 6 months before any progress. For now, our best move for the sequential numbering is what Juan recommended. We should include the ID attribute that allows a manual entry and while you or your team is verifying orders the numbers can be added to each line. I know this isn't ideal but it would allow you to have the sequential numbers for each line. I'll follow up once I hear a price for removing the totals from the output pages. What's the status of the catalog? Financial settings We'll need to set up a baseline credit limit for your accounts (otherwise they won't be able to move past the bid status) Determine your tax presence - Only New York? Default status to charge customers - I'd used verified so once you verify the order the balance will reflect on their account. Next Steps Once the catalog is completed you can start adding customers and running orders Notes: Just met with Juan - talked with him about inventory and supplies He's still working on the formula for the export (mozaik) Showed Juan other customers setup and is frustrated that his setup isn't the same (cabinwoodny.allmoxy.com) Urgent New issue - Folder level settings should be showing on the invoice and packing slip but not the order level settings No longer using the order level settings - can this be hidden? Customer must select choices from the folder level settings The folder level settings should be visible in the invoice and packing slip Color code type-in box - should be on the folder level settings   Permissions - Make payment isn't selected as a default permission, how would they be able to make payments? Why isn't it letting us move forward with an offline payment? - Week 4: Agenda Review the questions Abraham had Confirm when the catalog is completed Launch Notes: No show Week 3: ACE Agenda: Catalog Update How are things progressing with Juan? Any insights you can share? Recap of Last meeting Covered triggers and how you can automate your notifications. Still need to outline the entire customer process. (account created, order placed, payment submitted, etc) You can determine these steps and I can help you pick which triggers to setup. This Week Shipping Will call/pickup - previously confirmed Order flow review (start to finish) Related to the ordering process Output pages - what specifically do you want to share with customers on orders? Next Steps Outline your customer journey/process (account creation, order, payment, etc) Edit existing triggers to compliment this journey Work on output pages to incorporate with triggers Schedule follow up meeting for next week, I'll be OOO starting tomorrow (in the meantime review the articles listed in the support section. you can also reach out in the chat support and if I'm available or my team is they can answer questions) Notes: Catalog - By Tuesday the catalog should be completed (Jan 2nd) Complement from Abraham - having him navigate Triggers Review the triggers we set up 1, 6, 12, 16, 18, 19, 20, Payment how can we allow the customer to make a note that they've submitted a payment Output pages How can we block the attribute settings from showing on the invoice and packing slip pages Packing slip not showing barcodes - how can we add these? Invoice is including barcodes - how can we remove these WizzWood example - Abraham's action items: Complete the triggers Complete the output pages Complete the catalog & testing Create customers Launch Week 2: ACE Agenda: Step 2 Catalog check-in Looks like you've shared quite a bit of information with Juan. (great to see) Last week's homework Did you get a chance to review the recap email I shared, along with the videos and resources? Customer experience, adding new customers. Adding your logo and custom imagery Triggers, Tags, and pricing adjustments? Next Steps Shipping options Order movement through Allmoxy Send follow up recap and Abraham will meet with Juan on Friday What else would you like to discuss? Notes: Doesn't want just anyone to create an account. Share this: https://kingwoodcabinetry.allmoxy.com/public/create_account/ Week: 1 Agenda: Confirm goals & timelines of getting this active Differentiate Services from CS How was the meeting with Juan? What timeline did he give you? Did you confirm with him you're using mozaik and you'll need exports from Allmoxy for it? Review familiarity with Allmoxy (which parts are you familiar?) Starting with cabinets and then exploring closets later, correct? Step 1 & 2 starting points Creating an account Adding employees Set up Stripe Creating an order Tags Triggers Pricing Adjustments Next Steps Work through the initial tasks and the automation training. (If you're familiar with Allmoxy you can skip the test order, tags, and pricing adjustments, those will come into play once your catalog is done. Schedule a call for next week to move onward to shipping, labor processes, and financial settings. Notes: Wants to launch by beginning of January Wants to setup Allmoxy to work well with Mozaik February is the busy season Used Allmoxy to place orders for a few vendors

engagements.title: Craftline Cabinets.ca  renewal ready
engagements.body: This is the first year renewal for Craftline Cabinets.ca proceed to prepare data and engage the customer to execute a renewal meeting.

Cancellation Request 02/24/25 Customer emailed Sam's old email address on 2/24 requesting to cancel. "Hi Samantha        We would like to Cancel our subscription as we are not utilizing the software enough to justify the cost currently. I hope to get back on in the future if we can build our online presence. Please confirm cancellation, thank you Regards" I reached out to the customer and pushed for a meeting to better understand why they aren't using the system and if they're aware of the potential danger of losing the progress they made over the last year. Customer confirmed they're aware of the investment and hopes to be able to return in the future but right now with the volatility between CA and the US he can't justify the cost of the system when he isn't utilizing it. Shared the cancelation form with them, removed the payment method from their account and confirmed their data would be secured for 1 calendar year. See email chain here: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/4910812/company/19368621724/?engagement=73946277502

Call with Chris Doyle 11/1/24 Context: Call focus is production lines, time clock,  scanning and reporting stations I'm setting up in the shop. Part of the scope for that phase is the user accounts for the floor staff. With regards to the errors that I'm getting when I set the flags, a simple trial and error session on my part should flush out any glitchy flags which I will pass over to you guys with screenshots. I'll use the DEV mode in the browsers to get the error codes. I need to document the SOP for the ship shed with the focus on completing orders and setting a "shipped" state in Allmoxy, with triggers. Its fairly ambitious and i could use some guidance. Notes: Call recording: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/carson-allmoxy-com-Fri-01-Nov-2024-19-31-47-GMT-Untitled::akrDHmqyQBWVOsOM
Chris Doyle Refferal
