engagements.title: Churned customer re-engagement consideration
engagements.body: See Churn playbook and retention notes for full context. For more information reach out to the former assigned CSM.

@Josh Baker Overview The Project Re-scoping and Requirements Gathering meeting for The Cabinet Warehouse focused on addressing the challenges faced during the Almoxy implementation, which has been ongoing for six months. John and Susanna outlined difficulties in organizing data and inconsistent direction for reformats, prompting Beaulewis to propose a rescoping of the project, drawing on their expertise in manufacturing. The team analyzed the existing Excel-based catalog system that includes 650 SKUs and requires complex pricing mechanisms. Technical discussions confirmed that integrating production paperwork generation with Almoxy is feasible, and an initial plan was established to develop a client portal emphasizing variable pricing. Action items were assigned, with a primary focus on refining the Excel catalog and creating a proposal for implementation while a follow-up meeting is scheduled to finalize the next steps. Notes 🏢 Project Background and Challenges (00:00 - 08:57) Beaulewis and Bobby reconnect with John and Susanna from Cabinet Warehouse. Cabinet Warehouse has been struggling with Almoxy implementation for 6 months. John explained they struggled with organizing their data properly for Almoxy. Despite multiple attempts at reformatting their data, they never received clear direction. Beaulewis acknowledged organizational changes at Almoxy and proposed rescoping the project. Beaulewis emphasized his team's industry expertise (previously manufactured 90 kitchens/day). 🔍 Current Cabinet Warehouse System and Requirements (08:58 - 18:11) Susanna showed their current Excel-based catalog system with 650 individual SKUs. Their catalog tracks: cabinet specifications, components, doors, drawers, and hardware. Cabinet Warehouse needs a system for pricing with multiple variables: overlay options, door styles, finishes, hardware. Current pricing is based on four tiers with additional charges for features like soft-close. Cabinet Warehouse uses QuickBooks for estimating but it doesn't handle assemblies well. They need a system that can handle dealer sales with variable pricing. 📊 Technical Discussion and Catalog Examination (18:11 - 29:13) Susanna demonstrated their Excel spreadsheet that generates production paperwork. The system includes component listings for frames, drawers, doors, and raw materials. The spreadsheet generates cut lists and assembly instructions for production teams. Beaulewis confirmed the catalog implementation seems highly doable in Almoxy. Juan (previous integrator) is no longer with Almoxy, explaining some of the past issues. Beaulewis requested the spreadsheet to review with Aaron (current Almoxy team member). 🔄 Integration Plan and Next Steps (29:15 - 39:23) Discussed whether Almoxy should generate production reports or integrate with existing Excel system. Agreed that Phase 1 should focus on client portal with complex pricing that feeds their existing sheet. Identified important requirement: ability to sort production outputs by specific parameters. Decided to pause the current subscription until the project is properly scoped. Established a plan: Susanna will send the Excel spreadsheet to Bobby for review. Follow-up meeting will be scheduled to discuss implementation approach. Action items Susanna Send the current Excel spreadsheet to Bobby and copy Beaulewis (38:42) Continue refining the Excel catalog for 2-3 more days to ensure all data is accurate (22:29) Beaulewis Review the Excel spreadsheet with Aaron to understand implementation requirements (23:17) Set up a follow-up meeting once Susanna finalizes the spreadsheet (26:05) Create a proposal for implementing the catalog in Almoxy (22:10) Bobby Build a sample working cabinet in Almoxy to demonstrate functionality (23:28) Coordinate with Susanna on receiving and reviewing the Excel spreadsheet (38:49)

@Josh Baker and @Madison Theisen  I had a productive discussion with John, during which he conveyed his reluctance to continue investing in a solution that does not appear to be yielding results. Through our conversation, we identified a disconnect between his business decisions regarding catalog structure and the associated setup costs of Allmoxy. After some back and forth, John agreed to reconvene with Bobby and his office staff to further explore the feasibility of aligning their catalog with our templates. He also acknowledged that pursuing a fully custom solution would likely be more costly than an Allmoxy setup while delivering unsatisfactory results. To move forward, I have scheduled a follow-up meeting for Monday.

1/14/25 Suzannah reached out to me and wanted to have services look at reducing the product offering and giving us another estimate They have a really big catalog, over 20K I think John William wants to start with a slimmed down product offering He also seems to be hesitating on pulling the trigger on the build, may need to meet with Bobby

Call with Suzannah 1/13/25 The hesitation that whole project will achieve what they want Have another demo conversation? They want to decide if it is worth the investment for them They might want to do a limited product offering at first to cut down on the cost.

Note 12/31/24 I have reached out to John William a few times to review how the Allmoxy catalog is implemented a few times, per his request Haven't heard much back from him but he is signed up We did a scoping call with him, just waiting for him to sign the scoping agreement

engagements.title: John William Mooney, The Cabinet Warehouse- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations
engagements.body: John William Mooney, The Cabinet Warehouse- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations

Kickoff call 8/27/24 Introductions Purpose of this kickoff call is info gathering so we can hit the ground running when you meet with our services team for the official scoping call. Identify key stakeholders and who is going to be involved with the day to day John Has Suzanne, bookkeeping and data stuff. You mentioned to Broc that the main goal creating an online storefront for your customers, what are some of your other goals with Allmoxy? (automation capabilities & streamlining dealing with contractors) Identify a launch date and what does that look like? How many Drawer Box lines are we anticipating? Then we will get into some catalog specifics from a high-level What's next Schedule a scoping call with Juan Sending Juan resources needed to get started (specbook, catalog, pricing, etc.) When our team gets halfway through the build, we will start doing product training Meeting cadence Notes Their main goal is to get better production process. They buy doors, face frames, build kitchens individually, they use 2020 and make a QB invoice, then take the QB, then production. 3 points of data entry. Better internal paperwork and production processes down. Initially, they want to put in orders, then move onto the customers. What we need: How they input orders, they have an excel spreadsheet that has 700 different lines, enter qtys, then based on that it populates informational tabs. Those output pages are their cutsheets. They design from 2020 in house, not importing to machines. No other software, QB desktop, They make drawer boxes, order doors, and outsource the other stuff. Homeowners and contractors and their main customers. Pricing by the item, they have all inputs for cabinet (sqft of plywood) Then cost multiplier. Send us some invoices in QB so we can see the pricing structure. They have everything based on list price, they have different lines (semi custom, diff door styles, paint color, etc selling at one price point) Less custom is a different price point. We can start with Sierra White line (the most basic) Just changes depth and dimensions. They started as an RTA cabinet dealer. They aren't set on pricing- open to ideas. Shipping, own delivery trucks They charge by distance. They do cheap deliveries to encourage their customers to ship.

My friends from Fitzgerald!! We talked about the Up and Vanished podcast, it took place right around there :-O John William has a good head on his shoulders and knows he needs software to scale. He said he has a pretty efficient shop, but he has to babysit it and it's stressful to take a work day off. Can't wait to hear his feedback in a year :) @Josh Baker
@Samantha Racker I'm making sure this deal has booked with CS and Juan to begin integration.

engagements.title: Call with Norman Minkoff
engagements.body: Called Norman to confirm we are still on for tomorrow.

This is a long one. Please let me know if you have any questions! @Madison Theisen @Gavin Flitton @Brady Lewis How we arrived... Norman's monthly payment was disputed. Broc reached out to him to see why it occurred. Norman replied saying he wants to cancel: "Yes. I requested in the past and no one responded. I also called every phone number I have and no one answered or called back. The shame is I really wanted this catalog and no one even bothered to follow up or take the initiative to make sure to get it done. " This was a long call. I ensured to hear Norman out and validate his frustrations. Overall he has many claims against us for not responding to his communication. He was driving and I encouraged him to bring those examples to the meeting. He was hesitant and felt like it was pointless to show them... so I am unsure if it's valid or not. Here are a few things he mentioned on the call... Norman repeatedly said he reached out to a couple of people via email and text and was not responded to. He is upset because we never delivered the product. Claimed he had a few meetings to finish his simple catalog and we kept telling him that we were almost done, that it would be a couple more weeks, etc. then it kept dragging on with no result. Claimed that he has sent us customers who have had to go elsewhere for services because they were having the same problems with not getting things done. Claimed that he talked to one of the brothers via email saying if things didn't move then he wanted to cancel. Didn't get a response. He acknowledges we have a great product but feels like our delivery is not there. Feels like he couldn't get proactive and consistent responses from anyone at Allmoxy until he stated he wanted to cancel. Result At the beginning of our call, he wasn't even willing to sign the cancellation form. He didn't feel like he needed to because he was already done with the system and didn't want to spend one more second on it. Saw no point in signing. I talked him into a more neutral state where he is open to meeting to see where the disconnects happened and how we can move forward with his account.

engagements.body: Churn Playbook Please select all of the reasons that apply for why the customer is churning. Add any additional churn context in the note - the more the better here. If there's escalation notes that exist on the account be sure to reference them and include the data of the escalation note in the playbook context. Moved to Other Solution Notes: Looking to move to Odoo or 2020 Insight. TEK build, their customers want a more visual 3D aspect, not the list set up like Allmoxy. Customers never really adopted. Change the Customer Lifecycle property to "Other" and create a task for Kylie to review after 1 year. Other

Cancellation Note 10/7/25 Meeting Summary The client confirmed their intention to cancel both Allmoxy subscriptions, citing a lack of alignment with their industry needs. The discussion focused on their decision to discontinue use due to poor adoption among their customers, who require visual renderings rather than list-based configurators. The client is currently evaluating alternative platforms better suited for their business model. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Client intends to cancel both Allmoxy accounts. Primary reason: Allmoxy does not fit their industry niche or customer expectations. Customers prefer 3D visualizers, renderings, or drawings instead of list-based workflows provided by Allmoxy. Client was unable to get any users excited or willing to adopt the platform. Technical Issues & Fixes No specific technical issues were reported; the decision was based on feature alignment and customer feedback. Decisions Made Proceed with cancellation of both Allmoxy subscriptions. Cancellation forms will be sent for completion to initiate the process. Challenges or Concerns Lack of customer interest or engagement with Allmoxy. Visual configuration tools are a required feature for their market. Action Items Send cancellation forms for both Allmoxy instances. Process cancellation upon receipt of completed forms. Next Steps Await completed cancellation forms from the client. Client is exploring alternatives, including Odoo and potentially 2020 Insight. Maintain contact for potential future opportunities or collaboration.

Launched Customer Playbook Does the customer define launched as running and processing orders through Allmoxy? If not what is their definition? Notes: Yes When did the customer officially "launch"? 01/13/2025 What has been the most difficult part of this customers onboarding /implementation experience? Other Notes: No real challenges, they had a quick TEK build and a quick onboarding experience. They adopted quickly and wanted to launch quick. Does the customer want to continue meeting on a regular weekly, biweekly or monthly basis? Notes: No, they reach out as needed. Sometimes Michael will reach out with API questions. What is the customers continued benchmark for success? Utilizing the API, potentially doing B2B Notes: Eliminating double-entry What is next for the customer in terms of their business goals and initiatives and how can Allmoxy/CS support these? Notes: Potentially using B2B with a few customers (Eicher being one). Continue to use the API What is the customers health in your opinion? Green(this customer is in good/great standing and would advocate for Allmoxy - churn would be shocking) Could this customer be good to use for marketing Customer Testimonials/Case Studies?(Now or down the line - think personalities, how customer is using system, technical strengths, innovative thinking) Please tag Cheneil & Madison if so. Notes: No

CS Onboarding Playbook Is this a VIP/Legacy customer?(If you're unsure check with leadership) No Is our Services team being utilized for this build? No Notes: TEK build Who is the main POC within the customer? Josh Confirm the customers desired Outcome for using Allmoxy Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) Notes: Cabinet Vision exports and online ordering API When is the customers goal launch date? 01/08/2025 What cadence of meeting schedule did you set with the customer? Notes: twice a week Ask the customer to define their benchmark for success Online storefront, cabinet vision exports, payment processing What Software(s) does the customer have? How will Allmoxy work with these softwares? Cabinet Vision Is the customer looking to fit Allmoxy around an existing ordering system or is this their first?(Utilize your Services Rep to more deeply understand how this impacts the catalog build.) Notes: First ordering system Is the customer letting you drive the meeting and follow an agenda or do they want to drive and take charge of meetings? Either can be okay, but dependent on the customers approach to onboarding you will need to pivot your approach to working with them. Notes: I drove most of the time, they picked up really quickly. Any initial concerns with the customer and their ability to launch(personnel, resources available, understanding of product, etc?) Notes: No

@Josh Baker and @Madison Theisen  Friday Brady, Jake, and I came to the conclusion that when we grant an API access, we want to quickly have them get on with me to scope a quick review and how they plan to use the API.  This will help us get market feedback on what potential needs we will want to convert to our product road map, and also provide assistance in helping them put together a team that will use it properly.    Here is the link https://meetings.hubspot.com/beaulewis/api-access-quick-scope-training?uuid=d0f9f326-faf3-4e89-84d6-811717c8bcd3 .   Coming out of that meeting, I will give jake the green light and grant them keys.  This will allow market feedback to arrive in the appropriate place, me to join together Pro serve clients, and keep work off of Jakes desk that is not filtered.   Any objections to this?   If not, if you don't mind, let's get them on a quick scope call.  ?

Rigel Cabinetry Onboarding #3/4 with Rigel Team To do Triggers (some are made, they do need to set up a welcome email to customers and new company added to employee) Price adjustments (done) Stripe (done) Tags (done) B2B No drop shipping Amos is assembling and finishing, they send to him RTA Do they want individuals to log in or just businesses? How an order moves through Allmoxy What is your current workflow? I will do a test order There is an expected workflow in Allmoxy Bid --> Ordered --> Verified --> In progress --> completed --> shipped When an order is verified, it is locked (the customer can no longer make changes) Customer's can make changes to Bids and Orders SHOW TRIGGERS AGAIN Test order Production lines/ work schedule report Production lines allow you to mimic what is happening on the floor in Allmoxy. What are the steps in making a slab door for example? Show Production Lines and Work Schedule Report (easy way to see jobs in verified status) We don't need to set up production lines, but it can be useful. We can even just make one category called "in progress". Or we can get more granular. (Show barcode scanning) You can start jobs for each production line --> moving them to in progress (which will drop off from the report) It will show you a start by date and a ship by date (based off lead time) From there, you can mimic what happens on the job floor in Allmoxy by using the scanning station OR you can move orders to in progress manually Shipping Methods How are they doing shipping? LTL Brokerage company They use multiple different companies Based off weight and volume They will add a shipping charge after LTL, then add that to open invoice Financial order settings (determine when your customers pay) Default checkout flow (use credit limit or pay before processing) . You can set this globally or per customer If some customers refuse to do pay before processing, you can change their individual checkout flow Credit limit is extending a certain amount of money to place an order, and set up payment terms (pay when they ship or the order is marked complete) Order limit/ build limit: the amount of product you will build but NOT necessarily ship to your customer. You set those terms Default interest rate- if you are doing terms and they haven't paid by the end of the month, a fee is attached. Daily is every day something is past due, it attaches a fee Sales tax presence Where are you doing business, just MI or regional? 5K a year threshold- when you collect 5K in revenue from a state, you need to start collecting sales tax Statement editor (credit limit): goes out to your customers at the end of every month if they have an outstanding balance. Overhead management can be tracked here: lease payments, etc. Go to order page, click on the customer to show dashboard If pay before processing, it will just show their order history If there are credit limits, they can log in and pay from here (or you can do an offline payment- customers don't have access) Could be a walk in, pay in cash, or use square. Refund payments (demo) Undo transaction (transaction history report, then refund that payment on your stripe dashboard) Accounting Export invoices queue QB or QBO? Notes 50% down, 50% at shipped Internal use products tag

Call with Ivan 1/2/25 Want to set up B2B account for their customer Amos Eicher He gets things flat packed, he finishes and assembles then ships to the customer after putting in his upcharge. They are going to be managing the B2B catalog for him (Amos is Amish), but get him set up with trigger emails so he can see incoming orders.

Onboarding #2 with Rigel Team 12/30/24 Tags These are ways you can group customers together for a certain purpose Cabinet dealers and contractors Pricing based on number of units over a 6 month time period Bronze, silver, gold, platinum (discount off retail price) These can be referenced in triggers and price adjustments Ex. you have a group of customers that are cabinet shops, and they get a certain discount. You can give this group of customers a "cabinet shop" tag on their company profile that can then be referenced later. You can also put tags on products How do you think you might group certain customers together? Price Adjustments Do you have customers that are priced differently than others? Why? Use price adjustments to achieve this Retail vs Wholesale Say you have a customer that is your oldest and most loyal customer, you always give them a 10% discount no matter what. Price adjustments are basically discounts or surcharges that can be applied to customers, groups of customers, or products. They are adjusted PER ITEM, not the total order amount Priority For when there are multiple price adjustments competing with one another If one discount has a higher priority than the other, the highest priority will override the other discount Ex. if you have a new customer discount and a summer sale discount going on at the same time, you would want the new customer discount to have a higher priority Tip: Use the Always setting, so you know if you are discounting or surcharging every time. You can also discount customers or products with certain tags Triggers How are you currently interacting with customers? Everything is manual (phone and emails) Triggers can automate that process for you Notify you, and your customers of something happening in Allmoxy Triggers to edit: 1, 6, 12, 16, 18, 19 Does he want to be notified of a bid? Or an order? Notes TEK needs to make some adjustments with the pricing Testing today and tomorrow CV testing tomorrow Any other dealer they deal with, they would quote them and then they quote the customer In this customer's case (Amos) wants his customers to be able to quote it from Rigel and then Amos gets a cut or incentive out of it They are doing all the selling and quoting for Amos Which it will all go RTA to Amos, however, the customer is paying for them like they would be assembled. They want to set customers up to Bronze pricing He's doing the assembly and finishing Amos's customers pay the bronze discount, and Amos pays the platinum discount and Amos gets the difference. Is there a way to figure out what Amos should be paid? Solution This all needs to be done outside of Allmoxy- until he gets set up B2B and there is an upcharge for his services You'll need to figure out his cut based on the order before sending him the RTA cabinets, maybe include a check for his services with the RTA flat pack.

Onboarding meeting #1 with Ivan & Michael 12/18/24 Notes from previous calls TEK build SKU based catalog Currently lots of double-entry Doesn't want public ordering- they want to verify all customers LTL Shipments with rates negotiated with carriers UPS with smaller packages Sometimes will call but mostly freight shipped Covering today Site navigation What he sees vs what his customer will see Creating an account Does he want their users to log in? Yes- but verify all customers first. Just businesses? For a customer Individual and Company Adding employees Permissions Creating an order What are you imagining the order flow to look like? What is the current order flow? How are you currently or planning to communicate to customers? Has it been manual in the past? How will customers be paying? Pay before processing? Or use a credit limit? They have credit limits, everyone pays as soon as they deliver How do they process payments? Stripe set up 2.9% fee for all cards, so it is consistent. (paid by you) The underwriting process is very important

Call Ivan TEK is almost done, will start training next week. Testing next week. This customer has no access to internet (Amish). He only wants to get involved with payment and delivery. He just finishes and assembles it. For his customers will be slightly higher. His customers can log on, but send money to his bank account. They want a custom code that will talk with Allmoxy, Odoo, and CV. One of our customers, Eicher Cabinetry, would like their customers to place orders directly through Rigel's Allmoxy instance. While these customers (approximately 5-6 in total) would pay Eicher Cabinetry directly, Rigel would then bill Eicher Cabinetry through our ERP system.  This would be a large account for us, so we would like to make them happy if at all possible. Would it be possible to: Tag these specific customers in the system Set up their payments to go to a separate bank account?

(TEK BUILD) Kickoff Call DOC: https://docs.google.com/document/d/18nX1f69h-3ks050DASOzI96VgDb55NONIiOWh6NJoBQ/edit?tab=t.0 Confirm the key stakeholders: - Know who signed the contract(Executive Buyer) before the call(from sales) and if/how they will be involved in onboarding -Who will be the key point of contact for the build? -Sales POC? -Production POC? -Financial/Accounting POC? -Any other stakeholders? Notes: Michael: Software developer, systems, integrations Ivan: Part owner, production manager Kyle: Consultant, Cabinet Vision expert Why did you buy the product? What were they hoping to achieve when they signed the contract and how does Allmoxy fit into that? If their answer is too broad, ask them to expand on how they envision their business changing when they purchased Allmoxy. Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) Notes: Triggers, Validations, Tags, Price Adjustments, Export and Import, Cutlists, Part Lists and Labels When does the customer want to "launch" and what would a successful onboarding look like? How do you know when you're there? Ex. Door Product Line is Launched and Cabinet Line is started. Notes: ASAP but they are utilizing TEK build

Kickoff call 11/13/24 Sam mentioned they wanted to use TEK MD for their build. Correct They will want access to the API at one point Introductions Purpose of this kickoff call is info gathering so we can hit the ground running when you meet with our services team for the official scoping call. Identify key stakeholders and who is going to be involved with the day-to-day Michael: Software developer, systems, integrations Ivan: Part owner, production manager Kyle: Consultant, Cabinet Vision You mentioned to Sam that the main goal creating an online storefront for your customers, what are some of your other goals with Allmoxy? ) Triggers, Validations, Tags, Price Adjustments, Export and Import, Cutlists, Part Lists and Labels Identify a launch date and what does that look like? How many cabinet lines are you anticipating? Then we will get into some catalog specifics from a high-level What's next Schedule time with TEK MD Get them all the reference materials they need We will start onboarding in a few weeks Notes At sign-up, they will want their new sign-ups to automatically get tagged and then they can see a certain price They want people to sign up and then they verify. Keep me in the loop with TEK build so I know when to start training onboarding

engagements.body: Meeting w/Chris Notes: Chris, Gavin, Juan & Madison 1/17/24 Discussed considering training with services team so he can stay up to date in training Says he has a few accounts in the pipeline to pass along to Allmoxy and would have our services team execute build as they are too big of catalogs @Broc Hill @Samantha Alexander Panels issue that perviously occurred within Dibasco & LGA Solutions: Juan owned this and did a great job getting the point across of what Chris' action impacted negatively in the catalog and how this was NOT a bug. Summary of issue/action: In template there's an alternative to deleting anything, encouraged to never delete. This does not always look like deleting and can look differently but have the same impact, ex. inputting attribute after exporting and modifying, when this is done if yes is not yes in default, it can easily deactivate the default and impact the attribute globally, same goes for importing products and impacting on product level override which can cause to not price Chris is going to be very mindful of ID tags moving forward to avoid anything like this - we HIGHLY encouraged him scheduling training sessions to make sure his understanding of the catalog build out process/Allmoxy as a product is up to expectations Juan has major concerns of Chris' knowledge of Allmoxy from a programmer perspective. He's looking at the program in a way we would expect customers to look at the program, rather than a programmer/engineer. We need to keep a close pulse on Chris as his understanding is far better in the cabinetry world rather than Allmoxy/software. Exporting attributes specifically is where a big concern is as the risk of messing things up interconnected to the attribute when importing messes with settings on a global level. @Broc Hill for your visibility since he's an affiliate and @Brady Lewis for your visibility on the overall escalation and Chris' relationship to our company. DiBasco and LGA Solutions: Chris is an open book and is the voice of these two companies currently, gets passionate in a passionate but caring way. engagements.title: Lane 17 Cabinet, Co. renewal ready
engagements.body: This is the first year renewal for Lane 17 Cabinet, Co. proceed to prepare data and engage the customer to execute a renewal meeting.

02/11/25 Retention Plan Meeting Context: Alex asked when his contract is up and I confirmed with him it will end as of March 10th. He wants to keep his data for 90 days so he can come back and potentially get back into Allmoxy. The goal of this meeting is to determine a gameplan with Alex to see what will be needed for him to start using the system in 90 days and also confirming that his access will be removed during the 90 days. Agenda: Pause timeframe - looking for 90 days Do you feel that you'll have bandwidth in 90 days? Anticipating more time available? What's left to complete within the catalog? How can we hit the ground running once the 90 days are up? Removing payment method as of March 10th (loose access after that) Scheduling a time to re-sync after 2 months of pause to see how bandwidth is looking and if we can plan to resume the project. Notes: Meeting recording: https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Lane-17-Allmoxy-Quick-Sync::01JKR8NYAZ435KQC9MSDPE0CNA Takeaways: Alex hasn't had the bandwidth to invest into setting up Allmoxy as he needs it to function. Alex learned throughout the last year that he and his partner, Jason, need to first define their catalog offering within Microvellum so they can then use this outline to match up Allmoxy. Essentially they put the horse in front of the cart and weren't ready to actually implement Allmoxy. New plan I've removed their payment method from their account so once the next charge date hits they won't be charged. I'll be reaching out to Alex in mid May to see how the backlog of work is coming along and if anticipates having the bandwidth to dive back into Allmoxy. Confirming with product how long the data will be stored if someone hasn't logged in or submitted a payment (believe this is 12 months)

Check-in Meeting (Catalog progress) 10/3/24 Agenda: Catalog progress How has the work been going? Any issues? Platform familiarity Feeling more comfortable since you've been building the catalog? What's next? Our services team is waiting on information to build out the door only product lines, correct? What's your timeline look like for being able to start using the system? Next Steps Share the information with me and I can get it to Juan/Aaron Schedule a touch base call for the end of the month Notes: Still has multiple projects on his plate. Door only products - could use sooner rather than later Get clarity from Juan - what information is needed for the doors.

They are wanting to start with a standard cabinets line and then build another more customer cabinet line after launching the standard line. The goal for what they would call a successful launch is to use Allmoxy as a storefront where customers can place order that price similarly to how they are currently pricing using an internal spreadsheet. engagements.body:
CS Onboarding Playbook
Is this a VIP/Legacy customer?(If you're unsure check with leadership)
  • No
Is our Services team being utilized for this build?
  • Yes, hybrid build

  • Notes:

    Alex wants to have a hybrid build and work with Juan.

Who is the main POC within the customer?
  • Alex Forbes

  • Notes:

    Main POC and power user.

Confirm the customers desired Outcome for using Allmoxy
  • Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering)

  • Notes:

    Their main focus is getting Allmoxy working effectively for their internal team and then once comfortable they can start rolling it out to their dealers.

When is the customers goal launch date?
  • 05/01/2024

  • Notes:

    Alex shared in the first meeting he's looking for roughly 6-8 weeks, that's his goal for being done with onboarding/training and using Allmoxy internally.

What cadence of meeting schedule did you set with the customer?

  • Notes:

    Weekly 45 minute sessions.

Ask the customer to define their benchmark for success
  • Internal Usage

  • Notes:

    His first benchmark for tracking success is using the system internally to create orders and share the information through Microvellum. Essentially, having Allmoxy be part of their internal operations and feeling comfortable with the software.

What Software(s) does the customer have? How will Allmoxy work with these softwares?
  • Microvellum, Quickbooks
Is the customer looking to fit Allmoxy around an existing ordering system or is this their first?(Utilize your Services Rep to more deeply understand how this impacts the catalog build.)

  • Notes:

    This is their first ordering system, everything was done in excel sheets prior.

Is the customer letting you drive the meeting and follow an agenda or do they want to drive and take charge of meetings? Either can be okay, but dependent on the customers approach to onboarding you will need to pivot your approach to working with them.

  • Notes:

    They were letting me drive the first kick off meeting.

Any initial concerns with the customer and their ability to launch(personnel, resources available, understanding of product, etc?)

  • Notes:

    The only concern could that he's wanting to do a hybrid build which I've told him will impact this timeline of "launching".

engagements.body: Allmoxy & Lane 17 Cabinet, Co.

Onboarding Notes 2024 4th meeting 5/2/24 Agenda: Timelines/next steps What will your bandwidth allow for? After the information is shared and Juan's team has built what they can, do you think you'll have the time to do the pieces you originally wanted to do yourself? Essentially, will you have the bandwidth to even work on the catalog once it's ready to be turned over to you? Best case next steps Jason and Alex share the information with Juan to have us get started. Once Juan is as far as he's able to go we can get a regrouping session scheduled. Notes: Decided to push out until June. See Jason's email. 3rd Meeting 04/11/24 Agenda: Catalog catch up Shared the information with Juan Any updates so far? Last meeting Created an employee contact Created a company and company contact Edited permissions Updated branding Adjusted financial settings and connected stripe Today's Objective Automation Triggers Tags Pricing adjustments Shipping - if time permits Next Steps/homework Determine which triggers are needed and complete the setup of these. Understand how tags and pricing adjustments work - Tag your test customer, create a price adjustment, and then create an order and see how the adjustment impacts the order - Use the Dev tool to see it broken out. Continue your work with Juan on the catalog Notes: Jason - filled out some of the google sheet for Juan realized they don't know exactly what they offer Did whatever we had there and ignored a handful of the items next step - Jason adding a little more of the catalog Determined that there's too much going on for Alex and Jason to think about the onboarding steps. Going to spend more time focusing on building the catalog and then regrouping once ready. See recap email. 2nd Meeting 03/27/24 End Goal - Allmoxy Navigation   Agenda: Catalog Scope Meeting How was the meeting? starting with a standard cabinet line and then create a more custom line afterwards? Are you planning to do training sessions with Juan or is he going to build the master product with your pricing and then show you how to clone and duplicate to the full cabinet line? Want to stay on the same page with you and Juan's work.   Your View & Customer View (Account/contact creation & Branding) Add an employee, company, and company contact Permissions Branding Intro to tags - If time permits Payments Are you planning to have customer's submit payments via Allmoxy? Will customers need to pre-pay? How do you charge your customers? Is managing the invoices and payments from one location your ideal scenario? Order Flow What does your current order flow look like? (start to finish) (who's involved and at which times?) How do you see this changing with Allmoxy? Intro to triggers - how they can be used Homework Create a test order Connect Stripe Outline the basic order flow needed. (order entry, verification, production, shipping) Triggers/notification, output pages, exports Notes: Scoping call - Went through everything with Juan - waiting for Juan to give Alex a price but they need to fill out the sheet for Juan. Had questions about Rehau's order process (customers could only submit bids or something along these lines) No real questions, everything seemed to make sense. Set up stripe while we were on the call 1st Meeting 03/20/24 End Goal - Allmoxy Onboarding Introduction Agenda: Introductions and Background Alex - Owner/founder, you're based in Cleveland. Utilizing Allmoxy to input orders, correct? Online storefront for dealers? How do you envision Allmoxy working in your flow? Dealers/customers entering orders? Who is going to be your Allmoxy power user? Goals and Timelines What would deem this successful for you? What's your goal launch date? How much time are you able to dedicate to this weekly? Wanting to pursue a hybrid model for the integration/catalog build? Caution - the importance of the catalog build and how it impacts your launching ability Getting Started With Allmoxy What's your familiarity with Allmoxy? How comfortable are you with the system? Support Center (highlight the support sections) Review creating an order and have Alex review the template to get ideas of how he wants his catalog setup. Next Steps Set up a weekly meeting cadence Meet with Juan next week for the catalog scoping Homework - create test orders and review how the template catalog is set up so you can bring this feedback to Juan. Next week we'll kick off with our full week 1 onboarding What else would you like to discuss? Notes: Add Jason Zook as a contact in the account Alex will be the one operating Allmoxy Jason is the one operating Microvellum Goal launch Date - They know it can take a while, potentially a few months. Working model that they're using internally (6-8) Running order internally Order editing - Spreadsheet operation/system At first orders will start to go through their team and then eventually goiong to the customer's control. (Balancing their workload with a semi-custom category)

engagements.title: Build out Thermofoil Doors
engagements.body:
When a customer calls us for thermal foiled products, we typically need the following:

1) Door Style

2) Door Size for each door

3) Thermal Foil color choice (attached) need the full list and pricing on there

4) Do they want hinges bored? What type? How many? Hole placement? We provide hinges?

5) Color matched backs? Closest back? White back? Black back?

We also provide other thermal foil products.

1) 1/4 - 3/4 panels flat press 1 side all edges (1.5 inches wide by 96 inches long is the smallest)

2) 1/4 - 3/4 panels 2 sided no edges

3) 1/4 - 3/4 panels custom shapes and sizes

4) 1/4 - 3/4 panels full wrap (closest back, white, black, color matched?)

Currently we would like to offer:

1) 15-20 door styles

2) Ability to request a custom door style

3) Edge Profile default choice (1/16, 1/8, 1/4, 3/8)

4) Ability to add as many doors/panels as necessary

5) Ability to customize edge profile on any door (so default edge when door style is chosen, but can change edge on any door necessary and it overrides default)

6) Ability to change color/texture on a per door/panel basis.

7) Thermal foil color/texture automatically changes sqft pricing appropriately. (ie. solid colors are cheaper than gloss etc.)

engagements.title: Follow up on Allen Company - Set up
engagements.body: Regarding note logged on Tuesday, January 31, 2023 1:38 PM

@Samantha Alexander @Broc Hill I just had a meeting with Devin. He was asking about order import functionality. He wanted to be able to make attribute selections on the catalog page, then import a list of quantities, widths and depths. When I told him it didn't do what he's asking, he said he didn't need allmoxy then because it's not user-friendly. I asked him to clarify what he needs. He has clients who design orders in Mozaik, Cabinet Vision, etc and send him a door list (qty width & height) for him to produce. He said if that list has to be manually entered, then Allmoxy isn't helpful for him. Currently, there's no easy way to just import a door list. You have to configure your import template to match every single product with each attribute listed out per line item. I assured Devin that our product is made for door manufacturers, so it's completely plausible that there's an easy solution in place that our customers are using prior to importing the order in Allmoxy, I'm just not familiar with it, but I'd do some digging and get back to him today or tomorrow. @Beau Lewis @Bobby Lewis I would imagine this is a daily occurrence with Lewis/other door manufacturers, what do they do with the door lists that get sent to them from design software? Manually enter it? Or does the client manually enter it? Or is there a way to format the export so that it seamlessly imports door orders with little manipulation? @Juan Ridgeway
Discussed 5-10 hours of training
Augmon met with Allen and patched up some things with the catalog

Follow up on Mountain Shadow Woodworks
engagements.body: Regarding email: Mountain Shadow Woodworks​- Contract Saving Renewal, sent on Tuesday, April 23, 2024 12:46 PM

Renewal 2024 Kenzi and Carl in attendance. Started running revenue/orders through the system in January of 2024. This is their first year with Allmoxy. Things have been ok this last year. it has been difficult because Carl feels as if the system is pretty complicated. Taken months to get setup. They have been with Allmoxy for one year now. It was about January when they started getting things figured out. I asked if there has been anything that has been a value add and he said not really. He feels like it's extremely difficult to ensure they have all the things put together correctly. Almost feels like they have to know how do a lot of programming and he is having to do a lot on his own. It seems very difficult to setup triggers, invoicing, etc. so they just want to use it for orders. Carl recognizes Allmoxy has a lot of features but they don't use them. They only use Allmoxy because it's an easy way for them to have people order from them. Would be ideal if they could pay for specific features instead of having to pay for it all. Essentially a SKU based subscription. Carl claims he hasn't heard from anyone in the year of signing up. He feels like he is a small little guy and feels like we maybe spend more time with companies that are bigger instead of the smaller companies. I looked through the emails and asked him if he received the email regarding the CS rep change and WTS. He said that he got the WTS email and must have overlooked the email.   Overall he wasn't very happy regarding his Allmoxy experience. He says he goes home at night and tries to figure out how to set things up. I re-ephmasized how important and valuable it would be for him to meet with Josh to setup some time to go over the things and pain points that he has experiencing. To allow for Customer Success to set up some trainings for him to see if there are other components within Allmoxy that he could utilize and overall have more value added to his jar than what he has experienced this last year. He is open to the idea. Feels like they could pay someone to design a website, etc. for cheaper. than what he uses Allmoxy for. Doesn't like that as his revenue increases his price increases. I let him know that it's our pricing model. He also was unhappy that we continue to charge for their instance once their agreement is up.  He brought up cancellation and asked what that process looks like. I encouraged him to meet with Josh first before he commits to cancelling and he agreed to it. We are keep pricing the same as they have just started running revenue through the system. Staying at $699 for this next year. I will connect Josh and Carl together and set a task for myself to follow up with Josh post the two of them meeting. @Josh Baker for visibility. If we could set up some re-occuring meetings with him it could be a great value add or else I think we might lose them. @Madison Theisen in case you find value in meeting with Carl prior to Josh returning. @Broc Hill for visibility. Would you like this to be moved to "considering cancellation" or remain in contract sent?

12/19/25 - Implementation meeting - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Meeting-with-Stanley-Sommer-from-Evolve-Elements::01KCPM42BYKZWG1WSVAX3521YA Agenda: Stanley's agenda items - pricing help? Notes: Meeting Summary This meeting involved Stanley Sommer, Carson Ward, and Aaron Dundas discussing the setup, pricing, and product configuration for Rugby’s exclusive Allmoxy portal, specifically related to five-piece door panel materials and product visibility. The team focused on simplifying the setup, eliminating unnecessary product cloning, improving maintainability, and ensuring pricing logic and shipping configurations were in place. Rugby's operational requirements and regional pricing needs were central to the conversation. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Product Setup for Rugby: Focus is on creating a Rugby-exclusive product catalog in Allmoxy. Rather than cloning multiple products, a tag-based approach will be used to manage product visibility. Tags such as “Standard Offering” and “Rugby Products” will control what each group of customers can view. Rugby will only see products tagged for them, and the standard catalog will remain unaffected. Quarter Inch Panel Material: Need to differentiate quarter-inch and three-quarter-inch panels for five-piece doors. Cost difference is minimal but still relevant for product configuration. Panel materials will match the color of stile and rail materials — no separate color selection. Pricing Configuration: Setup completed to reflect quarter-inch sheet pricing and sheet size. Updates made to pricing formulas to calculate square footage accurately using the correct material. Shared markup and waste percentage between quarter-inch and standard materials unless modified later. Custom Product Naming: Allmoxy allows customer-facing names and internal "machine" names for Mosaic integration. Stanley needs naming consistency with Mosaic but can still show simpler names to customers. User Experience Improvements: Worth’s door ordering setup used as a UX benchmark — largely praised for intuitive visuals. Recommendation to use images and visual cues (e.g., highlighted selections) to aid customer understanding. Technical Issues & Fixes No critical bugs reported, but several backend improvements were made: Added quarter-inch sheet pricing and dimensions into attributes. Adjusted pricing formulas to accommodate the new material without complicating maintenance. Demonstrated the correct approach to cloning products and applying customer visibility tags. Set up future-proof structure for potential Rugby pricing adjustments (via meta fields and multipliers). Decisions Made No need for full product cloning unless strictly necessary. Rugby will be restricted from seeing the broader catalog via tag-based visibility. Quarter-inch panels added as attributes with proper pricing and size data. Allmoxy’s internal and external naming system will be used to maintain Mosaic compatibility while keeping customer-facing names simple. Challenges or Concerns Rugby's preference for iframe integration on their website could delay deployment. Regional pricing differences may require more custom configurations in the future. Risk of catalog complexity increasing if Rugby expands regionally with varying offerings. Shipping logistics still under discussion; freight partners not yet finalized. Action Items Stanley to: Receive final materials list from Brandon. Enter remaining products and validate pricing. Add quarter-inch material values for Tafisa and any smaller sheet sizes. Begin preparing visual aids for ordering (attribute images from Mosaic). Carson to: Push Rugby to begin customer onboarding once Stanley confirms pricing setup is complete. Support Rugby in tagging their own products/customers on their end. Advise against waiting for iframe setup to begin ordering. Aaron to: Finalize configuration changes to pricing and material attributes. Support any cloning/testing for future Rugby-exclusive product needs. Provide guidance on implementing shipping weight calculations if UPS/FedEx Freight is selected. Next Steps Stanley to complete product entry and confirm pricing accuracy by EOY. Carson to begin follow-up with Rugby early January to prompt account setup and B2B link sharing. Test orders to be created for Rugby to validate pricing, visibility, and shipping methods. Explore UPS/FedEx Freight integration once a carrier is selected. Revisit catalog structure in Q1 depending on Rugby’s expansion or complexity in regional offerings.

engagements.title: Meeting with Stanley Sommer from Evolve Elements
engagements.body: Meeting with Stanley Sommer from Evolve Elements

12/11/25 - Onboarding Meeting (Meet with Evolve) https://app.fireflies.ai/view/FW-Allmoxy-Evolve-Cabinet::01KC2EF6N2XAJYWZ7A04G5QGSH Meeting Summary The meeting centered around the integration and setup of Stanley's custom door products within the Allmoxy platform for Rugby to resell. Discussions covered remaining technical issues, product setup steps, pricing configuration, materials management, and shipping logistics. A phased rollout strategy was proposed, including a plan to finalize configurations before the end of the year, with potential go-live in early 2025. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Stanley is waiting for Allmoxy to resolve a pricing issue related to panel thickness before proceeding with catalog sharing. The issue centers on mispricing the quarter-inch center door panel as a three-quarter-inch panel, affecting cost accuracy. A new material line (Tafisa from Rugby) will be added, but only once current pricing issues are resolved. Stanley clarified that Rugby customers should only see materials Rugby stocks, to avoid unsupported orders (e.g., Wilson Art in some markets). Custom product setup for Rugby’s instance will be handled through cloned versions of Stanley’s existing products, with limited material selections shown. Discussion around controlling the user experience: avoiding overwhelming material dropdowns and maintaining clean product presentation. Technical Issues & Fixes Current technical problem: Allmoxy misprices quarter-inch door panels as three-quarter-inch. Solution: Either document changes to send to Aaron for implementation, or set up a direct meeting with Aaron for a collaborative adjustment session. Future product flexibility depends on setting up accurate material depth attributes (e.g., panel thickness) and logic to avoid material bloat in user selections. Custom shipping weight (dim weight) must be manually input due to system limitations. Decisions Made Stanley will hold off on adding Rugby materials until the panel pricing fix is completed. Stanley will enable products for B2B resale once pricing is accurate, and Rugby will then pull them into their instance. Products for Rugby will be cloned and limited to Rugby-approved materials only. A single universal price adjustment will be used for all B2B products (instead of multiple per product). Future customer-specific attributes will be enabled when available to eliminate the need for cloned products. Local delivery will be handled by Rugby for nearby customers, and out-of-region freight will be managed directly by Stanley. Challenges or Concerns Risk of Rugby customers selecting unavailable or unreleased materials (e.g., Tafisa color not in the U.S.). Laminating veneer to match stock colors for quarter-inch panels is unreliable due to color variation. Teams issues on Stanley's end prevented screen sharing for product configuration during the meeting. Shipping complexity due to lack of integrated freight calculation (dim weight must be added manually). Action Items Stanley to: Finalize pricing corrections in Allmoxy for quarter-inch vs. three-quarter-inch panels. Provide list of products to be cloned and prepared for Rugby’s instance. Await Brandon’s list of stocked Tafisa SKUs (both quarter-inch and three-quarter-inch). Work with Carson to schedule a meeting with Aaron next week for pricing logic finalization. Brandon Anderson to: Send list of Tafisa materials stocked at Rugby. Carson Ward to: Coordinate the engineering meeting with Aaron and Stanley. Guide Stanley through enabling B2B product sharing via Allmoxy. Support configuration of cloned products with limited attributes for Rugby. Josh & Rugby Team to: Prepare for eventual integration of Allmoxy instance into the Rugby website (iframe or direct link). Test the ordering process once Stanley's catalog is ready. Next Steps Carson to send Aaron’s availability for a pricing review and configuration session with Stanley. Stanley to complete product setup and confirm pricing accuracy before cloning Rugby-specific versions. Rugby team to test ordering through Stanley’s instance once products and pricing are finalized. Determine shipping strategy per region: local via Rugby; freight handled by Stanley with manual input. Evaluate if temporary direct-link access can be used instead of iframe for faster Rugby launch. Target: Internal testing by January 1, 2025; public launch potentially by March 1, 2025.

engagements.title: Follow up with JD on chat with Brady (last payment comes out May 17th)

engagements.body: Brady Call with JD on 5/8 Brady talked with JD for about 20 minutes. Brady wasn't too surprised about things. JD basically said in a nice way that I respect you but I didn't feel like he was heard by your people. He felt like Broc and Kenzi were all about the contract and were "hard asses" with him and he felt like he had a good old boy relationship where he couldn't get back to Brady. Brady can't blame him, Brady used to hand-deliver doors to him so he gets why he would feel disconnected from things when he used   Brady thinks what it boils down to is that in his mind we sold him a 3D designer that solved all the problems he wanted it to. He felt like that's what he was getting and he felt like it also made sense to hire someone and pay them 80k a year to work the Allmoxy system for them on top of several hundred a month for the Allmoxy product. So he feels like because of that he should get what he wanted and needed for what he was paying. Brady says most of our problems have been corrected. We don't allow people to hire someone to have complete access to our back end anymore and he thinks that is the main problem here. Now what would happen is that he'd be advised that we build it for him and we either do it and kill it or not. In that scenario of having us do things, he would have also not had the lost clients because he wouldn't have had a broken product. JD still want's to cancel. They talked about a possible comeback down the road. Brady mentioned we are making changes all the time and getting better and better. Left it at no hard feelings and we will always welcome him back. CTA Kenzi will follow up with JD to follow through on the cancellation process. @Broc Hill @Brady Lewis @Josh Baker @Madison Theisen for visibility.

engagements.title: Call with JD Lundbade
engagements.body: I had a good call with JD. I think the main problem here was that he hired a guy and paid him $80,000 a year to be his Allmoxy guru the expectation that that guy should be able to do everything, and so our services team was not engaged. His employee tried to get 3-D to work, with all kinds of problems. He lost clients in the process grew frustrated, and wanted out of his contract. He felt slighted because he could only ever talk to Brock and Kenzie. Madison Theisen

engagements.title: Follow up on call with JD
Call with JD Lundbade
engagements.body: Called and set up a meeting for cancellation/retention on May 8th.

engagements.title: Call with JD Lundbade
engagements.body:
Doesn't want to a pay dime. He just wants to vent to Brady about what kind of company that he owns. Doesn't feel like he should have to pay the remaining amount of his contract that would be in June. Will pay the $700 IF Brady gets on the call with him. If we try to go after him for the last month of his agreement, there will be lawsuits abd if we go there and he will be very active in that because he is just beside himself and is just appalled.


He said he has already made his decision. He is completely done with the account and wants to cancel. Does not want to pay another dime. Unfortunately, the way it's gone the last few weeks is he was wanting to save his data but now he wants nothing to do with us or our company. He is not looking to salvage anything. He would still love to talk with Brady but for the rest of the team he thinks it's pointless. I even used Madis talk track.


His only intent is to let Brady know the kind of company he owns and his only concern is for Brady at this point. He ultimately wants Brady to know what he has gone through. He feels like the communication break is there within the company. He has never done business with a company like this.


He feels like he had to employ a guy he paid 80k to teach our people how to use our system. Then he has been paying for Allmoxy on top of that. I asked what type of resolution he wanted and he said he doesn't feel like the feedback ever makes it to Brady. He said that he lost millions of dollars because our system failed. Our platform was supposed to make his life easier and it made it ten times harder.

engagements.title: Rocky Mountain Cabinet And Millwork- Contract Renewal (start renewal sequence)
engagements.body: Rocky Mountain Cabinet And Millwork - Renewal is due

( @Mekenzi Falslev 's note but her hubspot is having issues.) JD just gave me a call on his cell and is very unhappy with the service he has been provided through Allmoxy. He is wanting to cancel instead of moving forward with putting his instance on a pause. He stated that his guys always end up teaching our guys how to do things at Allmoxy. That we don't work for our clients. He said he has spoken to multiple customers who feel like Allmoxy doesn't work for them and people at the company don't care about our customers. He said he can think of 4 other ones who feel similarly currently which is a very bad way to operate business. He mentioned he is unsure that there is anything better than Allmoxy but he is going out to explore and find out what is a better software and solution for what they want and need. JD was very adamant that the owners know about this conversation as he feels like they no longer care to be involved or care to make things right when things get escalated. He feels as if they avoid hard conversations and are unwilling to fix the issues within the company and said it only makes sense that they would be made aware of this situation. JD mentioned that they keep paying us a lot of money and we don't pay them anything in return... He also mentioned that we have failed to produce what we said we would produce. I let him know that it is typical that we do a "retention" meeting or an overview call to see where things were dropped, etc. to put our best feet forward when customers choose to cancel. JD said he was open to it then said that if there is integrity left in the company then one of the owners will be on the call. I heard his concerns and validated his frustrations and at the end of the call he said he will not do the cancellation/retention meeting unless one of the owners is on the call. I told him I would talk with Broc about next steps and get back to him by noon tomorrow as he will be on a flight. @Broc Hill tagging you first before adding Beau and/or Brady. @Josh Baker for visibility.

Launched Customer Playbook Does the customer define launched as running and processing orders through Allmoxy? If not what is their definition? Notes: yes When did the customer officially "launch"? 05/02/2022 What has been the most difficult part of this customers onboarding experience? Other Notes: unknown Does the customer want to continue meeting on a regular weekly, biweekly or monthly basis? Notes: as needed What is next for the customer in terms of Allmoxy? If they don't know, what are you going to work towards with them?(Further feature adoption, another product line/services exports, machining data(Mechanlinks), getting their customers to use system, paid dev work, third party/API work, etc) Notes: unknown What is the customers health in your opinion? Green(this customer is in good/great standing and would advocate for Allmoxy - churn would be shocking)

engagements.title: Call with JD Lundblade
engagements.body:
Connected with JD about renewal. He wasn't happy that we turned on his payments for the last two months.


Saying he has two instances and is hoping he isn't being double charged.


Doesn't know what the instance is called and said Beau set it up.


JD Said:


"Yeah, I don't I don't understand that cuz I've seen you guys charged me $250 for the last year and half every single month and we do have two instances and the last person I talked to did not understand cuz we had one instance you guys jacked it all the way up and then he started another instance to try to fix it. And so I was paying on one instance and not the other because it was all messed up."


Wasn't super happy about the pricing. Said he was told by the brothers he would be locked in at $250.


I told him our costs evolve with the company and everyone is moving to a flat rate fee.


Sam looked in Stripe and they have 2 different accounts. Only one being charged for the last 2 months since we turned the payment on. The other one has been collecting $250 payments up until December.


Will talk to Broc/Beau.

Catalog testing 10/14/24 with Michael New sales rep that is ready to hit the ground running. My testing notes Tall: Applied End J Left product not pricing wit certain color optionshttps://share1.allmoxy.com/2NuobXQw Same product but with different finish is insanely pricy (1,600?)  https://share1.allmoxy.com/WnuWmG9N It looks like the Alder select, Alder Rustic, Birch Material on the applied ends doesn't price at all Issues with Applied Ends in general, you get this error message: https://share1.allmoxy.com/jku0Alkj and you can't save a bid due to this. https://share1.allmoxy.com/d5ujgvd0 it auto-fills the width ass 3" but the available width validation shows that it needs to be between 18"- 40". This validation is incorrect for this product (it should around 1-3 inches) Josh & Michael testing notes Tall 1 Fixed 2 Adj 4 Rollouts pricing issue: Should be way less than 11K https://share1.allmoxy.com/L1uEDpGG Floating Miter shelf doesn't have a thickness attribute (additional ask) Applied ends pricing at 0 for certain materials (same as above) https://share1.allmoxy.com/yAueJYjZ These validations don't make sense: https://share1.allmoxy.com/yAueJYP8 Validations off here: https://share1.allmoxy.com/6qu1b278 Fillers and applied ends is where we are seeing the most friction in the catalog. Looks really close though. It seems like most of the catalog is dialed in. Summary Michael and I did some live testing yesterday and found a handful of issues with his catalog. The main area of concern is the Applied Ends and Fillers, there are some that are pricing at $0 with specific finishes. There are also some inaccurate validations for Applied Ends that won't allow you to save a bid even though the auto-filled number is correct. Additionally, a tall cabinet is priced at 11K which is way too expensive. Michael thinks the catalog is mostly good and it's mainly the Applied Ends and Fillers that are causing the most friction. Michael is struggling financially and is requesting a temporary pause and we are working to determine where the disconnect happened with this build. I'm not sure what the time/ cost estimate would be to fix some of these issues, but hopefully, it isn't over 5 hours. We need to figure out how much of this, if any, falls on us to rectify. From a CS/ customer perspective, we certainly could have done more to test his catalog before telling him he was ready to go.

Call with Michael 10/11/24 Issues never fixed, can't order finished end panel. Just became way too much of a hassle. (first we are hearing of this). Still has some issues with some of Juan's work. Can't put together a whole kitchen in Allmoxy.. Doesn't want to bail completely, having financial issues right now. He thought that he would be off to the races three months ago. Pricing isn't accurate either. Formulas aren't working correctly. Walnut cabinet pantry was way too expensive. Finished end panels seemed to be a pain point too He wants to continue having a good relationship with Allmoxy and sees Allmoxy being the future of his company. He has some customers that want to use the platform, but can't as it currently stands He says he is really broke right now, bad two months with the workforce. Let's work together and get everything up and running Certainly doesn't have time or money to meet with Juan. Things aren't working. Frustrated with the services process. Just asking for some grace and for some help. He treats his customers the same way when they are struggling, just looking for some good faith. He's at the point where he needs some help, if we want to have him pay for the rest of the contract and then cancel that is fine, but he really wants this to work and is just asking for a little help. He admits that he shouldn't have ghosted us when we reached out for support but he went into "save my business mode" when Allmoxy wasn't panning out.

@Josh Baker @Madison Theisen @Juan Ridgeway @Broc Hill These guys are requesting to pause their instance for a few months due to not running any orders yet. Can anyone touch on what we are waiting on for them to generate orders and if it's something on our end or on their end?

engagements.title: Call with Michael Ginzel
engagements.body: I've been texting Michael about getting our next onboarding meeting scheduled, no response yet. We are almost ready to launch he just needs to also book with Juan to get things buttoned up in his catalog.

Onboarding 7/17/24 Reviewed triggers? (no) Set up a labor process (need to do) Financial order settings (determine when your customers pay) Default checkout flow (use credit limit or pay before processing) If some customers refuse to do pay before processing, you can change their individual checkout flow Credit limit is extending a certain amount of money to place an order, and set up payment terms (pay when they ship or order is marked complete) You set those terms Default interest rate- if you are doing terms and they haven't paid by the end of the month, it attaches a fee. Daily is every day something is past due, it attaches a fee Sales tax presence Where are you doing business, just Colorado or regional? 5K a year threshold- when you collect 5K in revenue from a state, you need to start collecting sales tax Bank info Stored bank info for internal use, it isn't really actively used but there to keep track of your bank/ banks. Statement editor (credit limit): goes out to your customers at the end of every month if they have an outstanding balance. Overhead management can be tracked here: lease payments, etc. Go to order page, click on the customer to show dashboard If pay before processing, it will just show their order history If there are credit limits, they can log in and pay from here (or you can do an offline payment- customers don't have access) Could be a walk in, pay in cash, or use square. Refund payments (demo) Undo transaction (transaction history report, then refund that payment on your stripe dashboard) Sales tax report (demo) A lot of customers file sales tax quarterly You would select the state and filter the dates- it will show what you owe to the sales tax commission Helpful report for knowing how much you need to pay in taxes Financial integrations (he uses QBO?) We recommend using the export invoices queue function This will get invoice information out of Allmoxy with an export you can use Transaction Pro or Sassant to map the Allmoxy export to QB (which we can help with- typically a one time set up) The more manual route is using the Export Transactions Queue (while having QBO pulled up) and reconciling payments Notes & questions Is there something bad about 3500 Credit Limit and 7500 order limit? They can place an order up to 3500, if they pay it down they can start another order. They can keep placing orders as long as they aren't verified. Wont ship anything until they have paid down their credit limit (if they are maxed out) Does the Status to Export Sales Order matter if it is none? Should he have the default status to charge customers be something other than Shipped if he is doing terms? Make it verified, because if we do it at ordered- there is no. Trigger at verified that says you have to pay 50% of invoice total for us to start the order. Have a trigger set up when payment has been made. Trigger when completed saying remainder of balance is due, or receive interest charge at end of month. (non paid invoices will be charged x amount at the end of the month) Shipping report will also tell him if there is an outstanding balance. For offline payments, why don't I see a credit card number? Is it just the ref #? They do 50% down, and 50% when it is completed (what is the best way to do this with triggers)? How do you ensure the customer pays the 50%. Put in trigger that they will get a finance charge after X amount of days (or at the end of the month) ACH payments (turned on) he wants his customers to be discounted if they want to pay with ACH Stripe processing fee, if they want they can send a physical check or use ACH (TRIGGERS, let them know stripe charges a fee, encourage ACH)- possible? (Michael actually pays the stripe fee from what he gets) 50$ he would get 48$ Don't encourage ACH. If he wants to not pay fees for stripe he can take check or cash payments. Sassant or Transaction Pro  (send info to Michael). He wants a Design category/ product (eventually) Design product fee for him to go to their house and build a design for them.

CS Onboarding Playbook Is this a VIP/Legacy customer?(If you're unsure check with leadership) No Is our Services team being utilized for this build? Yes, Services owning entire build Who is the main POC within the customer? Michael Ginzel Confirm the customers desired Outcome for using Allmoxy Automation Capabilities Notes: Online storefront, Mozaik export When is the customers goal launch date? 07/31/2024 Notes: ASAP, he took a few weeks to get necessary info to Juan What cadence of meeting schedule did you set with the customer? Notes: Weekly or twice a week Ask the customer to define their benchmark for success Online storefront, automation, Mozaik exporting, running orders, ease of customer use What Software(s) does the customer have? How will Allmoxy work with these softwares? Mozaik, Quickbooks Is the customer looking to fit Allmoxy around an existing ordering system or is this their first?(Utilize your Services Rep to more deeply understand how this impacts the catalog build.) Notes: New system Is the customer letting you drive the meeting and follow an agenda or do they want to drive and take charge of meetings? Either can be okay, but dependent on the customers approach to onboarding you will need to pivot your approach to working with them. Notes: He will let me drive, but a mix of both sometimes Any initial concerns with the customer and their ability to launch(personnel, resources available, understanding of product, etc?) Notes: Michael took a while to get services info to Juan, other than that- no.

7/8/24 onboarding meeting with Michael Address the resource allocation, that should be done after launching. Present category/ tagging solution (use verbiage in welcome email trigger that says please give us 24 hours to verify your account and give you access to our products) You will have to select Three Creeks or another company to see this on your end How an order moves through Allmoxy What is your current workflow? Does everything manually, goes onto distributors website, create online orders for materials after he counts them out on Mozaik. Manually types in those orders. Personally hands cabinet manufacturers blue prints. They ship all doors and drawers, he sprays them all. Has all hardware and material shipped here. He builds the cabinets. He hires a moving company or just rents a moving vehicle. I will do a test order There is an expected workflow in Allmoxy Bid --> Ordered --> Verified --> In progress --> completed --> shipped When an order is verified, it is locked (customer can no longer make changes) SHOW TRIGGERS AGAIN Have Michael do a test order Show Production Lines and Work Schedule Report (easy way to see jobs in verified status) He has some production lines set up, make sure the products are associated with those production lines (products, product info, associate with a production line) You can start jobs for each production line --> moving them to in progress (which will drop off from the report) It will show you a start by date and a ship by date (based off lead time) From there, you can mimick what happens on the job floor in Allmoxy by using the scanning station Shipping report Once an order is completed, you're ready to get those out the door. You're looking ahead. It shows you what method is being used, company name, etc. Shipping Methods Notes Hasn't figured out the margin stuff yet Has homework to do. Didn't set up tracking. Standard 2 door base cabinet didn't look right. Vanity order form (vanity sink does not have drawer front, but the image shows).

engagements.title: Michael Ginzel, Three creeks custom cabinets- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations
engagements.body: Michael Ginzel, Three creeks custom cabinets- Allmoxy Scope / Integrations

Onboarding meeting 7/1/24 with Michael Tags These are ways you can group customers together for a certain purpose These can be referenced in triggers and price adjustments Ex. you have a group of customers that are developers, and they get a certain discount. You can give this group of customers a "developer" tag on their company profile that can then be referenced later. You can also put tags on products How do you think you might group certain customers together? Price Adjustments Do you have customers that are priced differently than others? Why? Use price adjustments to achieve this Say you have a customer that is your oldest and most loyal customer, you always give them a 10% discount no matter what. Price adjustments are basically discounts or surcharges that can be applied to customers, groups of customers, or products. They are adjusted PER ITEM, not total order amount Accumulation For when there are multiple price adjustments competing with one another If one discount has a higher priority than the other, the highest priority will override the other discount Ex. if you have a new customer discount and a summer sale discount going on at the same time, you would want the new customer discount to have a higher accumulate than the other. Tip: Use the Always setting, so you know if you are discounting or surcharging every time. You can also discount customers or products with certain tags Triggers How are you currently interacting with customers? Is it manual calls, emails, etc? Triggers can automate that process for you Notify you, and your customers of something happening in Allmoxy Triggers to edit: 1, 6, 12, 16, 18, 19 Does he want to be notified of a bid? Or an order? Homework Edit the triggers, keep them deactivated for now Start thinking about how you want to use price adjustments- write down some ideas Notes Material cost and markup is what makes up our catalog Working on adding cabinet line to catalog that is a standard cheap cabinet. That will be 1 product line he offers directly to one contractor. Doesn't want competitors to see their pricing, use tags to restrict. Wants to see highest price, or just a 24 hour notice. Doesn't even want them to access the catalog until verified. Distribution method, he sent over pricing to Juan.

Onboarding meeting 6/28/24 with Michael Not waiting on anything, Juan has the broad strokes and it needs to be reviewed by Michael- put feedback and tasks in the TO DO LIST spreadsheet --> Juan will meet with him again to go through the to-do list individually Show how to test Covering today Site navigation What he sees vs what his customer will see Creating an account For a customer Individual and Company Adding employees Permissions Creating an order Stripe set up 2.9% fee for all cards, so it is consistent. (paid by the customer) Underwriting process very important Pay before processing? Notes Michael is going to do his research on Stripe vs. Authorize.net He is going to look through his catalog to make notes for Juan Initial glance, every price is half of what it should be. Will follow up with Juan
Services update 6/21/24 Juan emailing him back and forth Did not add waste yield with pricing Juan needs to clarify some things with him We have enough to go off to start
