engagements.body: Title: Canary + Allmoxy Date: Mon Mar 24 2025 12:30:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Participants: dambrogio@canarycc.com, operations@canarycc.com, sarah@allmoxy.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 40 mins Overview In the recent Problem-solving and Technical Support meeting, Dambrogio and Sarah addressed several key challenges related to data export from Allmoxy to QuickBooks, ship date limitations, and reporting visibility. They discussed difficulties with exporting order date information needed for reconciliation, leading Sarah to propose submitting a feature request to enhance the invoice export functionality. The duo explored using the Orders Report for data verification, identifying issues with tracking orders that have been paid but not shipped, and expressed concerns about inventory management where finished products remain unpicked. They concluded with action items, including Sarah's commitment to provide Dambrogio with relevant report links and a screenshot of filters, while Dambrogio will leverage the Orders Report to reconcile current orders. Overall, the meeting focused on establishing clearer reporting processes to improve operational efficiency and customer service. Notes 📊 Export Data and Ship Date Issues Dambrogio discussed challenges with exporting data from Allmoxy to QuickBooks Sarah confirmed ship dates cannot be manually adjusted once marked as shipped Dambrogio identified the need for order date information in the invoice export Sarah explained that charge date and due date are currently visible in exports Dambrogio explained different customer types (prepay, net 30, COD) create reconciliation challenges Sarah offered to submit a feature request for adding order date to exports 🔍 Report Options and Data Verification Sarah showed Dambrogio how to use the Orders Report to cross-reference data Dambrogio attempted to pull an order report from February to March Both explored different filtering options for orders reports Sarah explained the relationship between order dates, charge dates, and due dates They identified challenges when tracking orders that have been paid but not shipped Dambrogio expressed frustration with limited visibility into order history 📋 Inventory Management and Status Tracking Sarah demonstrated filtering reports by order date and status Report showed $82,000 in orders that had been ordered and shipped since January 1 Dambrogio expressed concern about customers using their shop as a warehouse Sarah showed how to identify $116,000 of completed products sitting on the floor They discussed tagging customers by payment terms for better reporting Sarah explained how 'completed' status means product is done but not picked up 🔄 Reconciliation Solutions and Next Steps Dambrogio identified that the Orders Report with order date and status would be ideal Sarah advised against importing unshipped orders into QuickBooks They agreed cross-referencing Orders Report with exports would provide reconciliation Dambrogio explained challenges with customers who place orders over 3-week periods Sarah agreed to email the report URL and screenshot of filters to Dambrogio Sarah committed to submit a feature request for adding order date to exports Dambrogio mentioned a $50,000 order where customer is slowly picking up small portions Action Items Sarah Pierce Submit a feature request to add order date to the invoice export Email Dambrogio the Orders Report URL with screenshots of the filters used Follow up with Dambrogio about the feature request status and possible timeline Dambrogio Use the Orders Report to reconcile current orders and identify completed orders awaiting pickup

Title: Financials Training Date: Thu Feb 13 2025 14:00:00 GMT-0500 (Eastern Standard Time) Participants: dambrogio@canarycc.com, ap@canarycc.com, sarah@allmoxy.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 70 mins Overview The Financial Training meeting focused on integrating financial processes between Almoxy and QuickBooks, addressing technical issues at the start. Key discussions included the functionality of Almoxy, particularly the steps for transferring and exporting data to QuickBooks, with an emphasis on avoiding duplication through optimal settings. Participants reviewed tax adjustments on invoices, the use of TaxJar for tax calculations, and the creation and application of credit memos, while also reconciling payments between Almoxy and QuickBooks. The meeting concluded with an assessment of Stripe's payment processing capabilities and the potential for exploring alternative providers due to concerns about fund holds. Action items were assigned to team members, including further exploration of Almoxy's financial reports and support for invoice exports to QuickBooks. Notes 🔧 Technical Issues and Introduction Meeting started with technical difficulties Sarah and Anna finally connected after audio issues Anna explained she's working on a remote desktop without Bluetooth capability 📊 Financial Setup in Almoxy Discussion about understanding Almoxy's program and its functionality Focus on steps to transfer/export data from Almoxy to QuickBooks Desktop Explanation of exporting invoices queue in Almoxy Process of selecting invoices for export and batching them Recommendation to use 'export and clear' option to avoid duplication 💰 Taxes and Invoice Adjustments Discussion about making tax adjustments on customer invoices Explanation of tax functionality in Almoxy's financial settings Process of setting tax exemptions for customers Ability to manually adjust tax rates on individual orders Almoxy uses TaxJar for tax calculations and updates 🧾 Credit Memos and Payment Processing Explanation of credit memo system in Almoxy Process of creating and applying credit memos Discussion about reconciling payments between Almoxy and QuickBooks Explanation of payment export process from Almoxy Potential issues with duplicate payments when exporting to QuickBooks 💳 Stripe Integration and Payment Processing Discussion about Stripe's payment processing time Concerns about Stripe holding funds for extended periods Comparison with other payment processors like Authorize.net Suggestion to check Stripe account for any restrictions or needed information Possibility of exploring alternative payment processors if issues persist Action Items Anna D'Ambrogio Explore Almoxy's financial reports and familiarize with the system Attempt to export invoices from Almoxy to QuickBooks with Jerry Check Stripe account for any restrictions or required additional information Reconcile AR in Almoxy with AR in QuickBooks after exporting data Sarah Send recording of the meeting to Anna and Jerry Jerry Assist Anna with exporting invoices from Almoxy to QuickBooks

Title: Canary Allmoxy Financials Date: Mon Feb 10 2025 13:00:00 GMT-0500 (Eastern Standard Time) Participants: beau@allmoxy.com, sarah@allmoxy.com, beaulewis1@gmail.com, dambrogio@canarycc.com, 4910812@bcc.hubspot.com, operations@canarycc.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 71 mins Overview The Canary Allmoxy Financials meeting, led by Sarah Pierce, focused on reviewing and addressing challenges in the financial processes related to customer billing and order management. During the session, Darren highlighted issues stemming from multiple bookkeepers, leading to confusion over customer charging statuses and inconsistent aging reports. The discussion included the need for uniform internal controls, the impact of customer terms on revenue recognition, and the complexities of managing shipping statuses, particularly regarding GAAP compliance. Participants debated effective strategies for categorizing customers and improving visibility of completed orders, as well as establishing triggers for customer notifications. Action items included further training on Almoxy's financial settings for new controller Anna, who will familiarize herself with the aging report, while Darren will delve deeper into the system for potential enhancements. Sarah committed to follow up with a recap email and relevant documentation to ensure clarity and alignment among team members. Notes 🏗️ Meeting Setup and Introduction Sarah Pierce introduces the meeting's purpose Darren has had multiple bookkeepers, causing confusion Issues with status to charge customers at different levels Aging reports varying due to inconsistent charging statuses 📊 Financial Status and Challenges Darren explains issues with customer terms and shipping statuses Discussion on changing status to 'completed' globally Bo Lewis emphasizes the need for consistent internal controls Anna (new controller) agrees on the importance of consistent revenue recognition Debate on when to select the status: at completion or shipping 💼 Customer Terms and Payment Processes Darren explains different customer categories: franchises, closet customers, and door dealers Discussion on prepayment options and how they hit the GL Debate on changing order dates to ship dates in exports to QuickBooks Bo suggests moving all invoices to shipped status and focusing on information reports 🔍 Reporting and Visibility Issues Sarah demonstrates the shipping report showing completed but not shipped orders Discussion on the need for better visibility of completed jobs and customer pickup timelines Debate on recognizing revenue at completion vs. shipping Bo emphasizes the importance of GAAP compliance 🚢 Shipping Status and GAAP Compliance Darren expresses frustration with managing orders based on shipping status Discussion on the challenges of managing customer pickup delays Bo and Sarah emphasize the importance of GAAP compliance and consistent revenue recognition Debate on how to manage orders that are completed but not yet picked up by customers 📅 Order Management and Customer Notifications Discussion on setting up triggers for customer notifications upon order completion Debate on how to manage different customer types (COD, net 30, etc.) Sarah explains the aging report and grace period functionality in Almoxy Darren seeks clarification on terms vs. grace periods 🔧 Next Steps and System Understanding Anna to familiarize herself with Almoxy's aging report and financial settings Sarah offers to provide additional training and documentation Darren plans to explore the system and reports over the next few days Agreement to standardize status to charge customers across all accounts Action Items Anna Review Almoxy's aging report and financial settings to ensure understanding and compliance with company standards Reach out to the Almoxy team for additional support if needed after reviewing the system Darren Explore the Almoxy system and reports over the next few days to identify any issues or improvements needed Sarah Pierce Send a recap email with the meeting recording and documentation on financial settings to all participants

Title: Zoom Meeting with Sarah Pierce Date: Thu Aug 22 2024 13:15:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Participants: tony@canarycc.com, sarah@allmoxy.com, dambrogio@canarycc.com, operations@canarycc.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 30 Overview In the Technical Implementation and Training meeting with Sarah Pierce, key discussions revolved around the setup and management of supply imports in the Almoxy system. Tony presented the updated template for importing supplies, emphasizing the need for supplies to be in Almoxy for creating purchase orders, with a long-term goal of linking them to products for automation. Strategies for managing supply and pricing were discussed, including minimum quantity triggers and tracking allocations for orders, while recommending QuickBooks for miscellaneous expenses. The meeting outlined a vendor-by-vendor approach for importing essential supply information and highlighted the system's capability for generating sequential purchase order numbers. Additionally, manual tracking of non-product supplies and job number references were addressed. Action items assigned included Tony testing the import process and data gathering, Sarah coordinating with developers on the supply list, and Stephanie learning the procedure for creating manual purchase orders. Action Items **Tony** Test import process with unique entries in each column (01:41) Start data gathering for supply import (41:55) Wait for clearance from Sarah before importing data (42:08) **Sarah** [+ 4 More] Shorthand Bullet 🔧 **Setting Up Supplies in Almoxy** (00:00 - 09:45) Tony working on importing supplies into Almoxy Template for supply import recently updated Supplies must be in Almoxy to create purchase orders Long-term goal: link supplies to products for automatic population [+ 23 More] Gist The meeting focused on the setup and management of supply imports in the Almoxy system and future automation goals. Bullet Gist 📊 Supply Import Template: Tony presented an updated template critical for importing supplies into Almoxy. 🔗 Automation Goals: The long-term objective is to link supplies to products for automation in the purchasing process. 🛠️ Management Strategies: Strategies discussed include minimum quantity triggers and tracking order allocations. 📑 Vendor Approach: A vendor-by-vendor method was outlined for importing essential supply information. ✅ Action Items: Specific tasks were assigned, including testing the import process and coordinating with developers. Short Summary In the Technical Implementation and Training meeting with Sarah Pierce, discussions centered on optimizing supply imports into the Almoxy system. Tony outlined an updated template necessary for importing supplies, highlighting that supplies must be integrated into Almoxy to facilitate purchase order creation and ultimately link them to products for enhanced automation. Strategies were proposed for managing supply and pricing, including minimum quantity triggers and tracking allocations for orders, along with the recommendation to use QuickBooks for handling miscellaneous expenses. A vendor-by-vendor approach for importing essential supply information was emphasized, along with the system's ability to generate sequential purchase order numbers. The meeting also addressed the manual tracking of non-product supplies and job number references, leading to specific action items: Tony will test the import process, Sarah will coordinate with developers on the supply list, and Stephanie will learn the procedure for creating manual purchase orders.

Retention Meeting October 2024 In attendance: Broc, Kenzi, Chesky Chesky has fallen off the wagon for months with attempting engagements and has been unresponsive. He was very disgruntled in the spring and we turned off his payment.  In the spring he said that he wanted to continue to try to make Allmoxy work and Kenzi reached out several times without him showing up. He randomly booked the retention meeting without reaching out providing the purpose behind the meeting. Goal: to understand where the partnership would be valuable moving forward, create an action plan that makes sense. Chesky is trying not to have customers enter all parts through Allmoxy because they get frustrated. The shop benefits from it but for customers it is a pain the neck. When you have a couple thousand parts to enter it's very time consuming. The way that Allmoxy prices things is very good, you can have a base price, a square footage price but the part he is looking for is the backend office materials, etc. and it comes down to price and what it will cost and take time wise to get setup and see if it's worth it. Essentially wanting a scope for next steps to see where it would take them and what it would look like. Chesky mentioned that he wanted to see how other closet manufacturing shops utilize Allmoxy. Broc feels like there are a lot of variables when it comes to that. And he is unable to show others who are using it. But when he can say is that there are other closet shops that use it for parts and kits (more of a sku'd product) and things like he does and does it successfully. But Chesky does custom closets. They are either having clients do either one of two things. Either having their clients create the order on the front end, or they're allowing almoxy to take client data through an import CSV file and upload it into almoxy. He currently exports out of closet pro and into closet vision. It isn't completely accurate. When they import into closet vision they are able to see it pretty well and tweak it to what it needs to be. Whereas with Allmoxy all he sees is parts. He doesn't know where and what to tweak and it get's confusing. Sarah and Juan would be the best people to ask what solutions there are as they are in thick of it with our customers and can better speak to what that looks like. Without Allmoxy what are the alternatives in your mind? Chesky said build something from scratch but he doesn't want to do that. We don't want them to do that either. What do you see the value of Allmoxy being for dollar wise? The deal we gave him was $899 a month. Our base price is $1099. If Allmoxy is not the main software he isn't sure if he wants to pursue it or not. He is getting in a lot of orders. Inventory, purchasing, etc. are a pain point from him. He wants to make it as simple as possible. What Chesky needs: To understand what is the best way for customers to come into closet pro without customers going through 800 parts. Second, see how much time and money it would take to build the back end of the office functionality side of things such as inventory and pricing etc. We should be able to get this back to him beginning of next week. He could get on Monday or Tuesday. Broc is scheduling a scope call. Chesky said if he doesn't hop on to please call him and remind him to hop on. The catalog isn't laid out super well right now. The way closet pro is laid out is in sections not by parts. Chesky showed us a demo of closet pro/cabinet vision and what it looks like and said he would be willing to show a demo of what it looks like on the scoping call to help us better understand the full picture. Next steps: Get on a no cost scope call with services team with Sarah and Juan since Sarah understands the closet side of things very very well. Broc booked a time for Chesky and team next week. Broc/Kenzi will follow up with Chesky after the scope call to create an action plan moving forward. Fireflies recording: To see what Chesky shared regarding CV and Closet Pro please go to minute 18:21 to view the demo. https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Allmoxy-CS-Sales-Retention-Meeting-uniplank-com::Wk6AZlCNV4itf76f
Met with Chesky at Booth in atlanta - His clients won't buy from him if he uses allmoxy Interested in - API with Closet Pro for pricing Closet Parts for their customers

This customer is an active user of Allmoxy. Review their account, see what functions they are NOT using, then schedule time with the customer to review the following: 

1. Why aren't they using certain functions? Are there pain points we can eliminate? Other software we could integrate better with? 
2. Check in on any must-have features they're requesting.

4. Ask for one or two industry partners/customers/suppliers etc that they'd love to see in Allmoxy.

engagements.body: 6/10/26 - Wurth and Slide-A-Shelf Meeting - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Slide-A-Shelf-Wurth-LAC-Allmoxy-B2B-Meeting::01KT07QD6PTM5VE5K1F2VAQW6M Agenda: Both parties to determine markup and make products available Are we doing any price adjustment? (surcharge of any kind) Turn products to available Discuss next steps of Wurth's current internal processing of B2B orders to align with Slide-a-shelf's operations. Notes: Chris Godinez - joined since Mary couldn't make it due to family issues. Meeting Summary B2B partnership call between Slide-A-Shelf, Wurth LAC, and Allmoxy to align on pricing strategy, order processing workflows, system configuration, and pre-launch requirements Slide-A-Shelf products are built and integrated into Wurth's Allmoxy platform but are currently marked unavailable pending final pricing and markup decisions Meeting covered the full order lifecycle from customer placement through Wurth verification, PO entry, and Slide-A-Shelf production Leadership approval from Wurth's CEO and directors identified as a required step before the partnership goes live System configuration tasks assigned to Carson Ward, with follow-up items distributed across Slide-A-Shelf and Wurth teams Key Takeaways Important Discussions Slide-A-Shelf offers custom pullout shelves as direct-ship products through Wurth's platform, targeting cabinet makers and contractors who prefer not to build their own Wurth will use Allmoxy as the ordering and PO management platform; customers place orders through Wurth, Wurth verifies and enters POs, and Slide-A-Shelf downloads confirmed orders for production Slide-A-Shelf's 30-year brand history and product quality cited as key competitive advantages over brands such as Rev-A-Shelf New side-mount product includes rear sockets as an upgrade; an undermount version is in development and expected to launch first through Wurth Expansion opportunity identified: Slide-A-Shelf products could be promoted to cabinet manufacturers such as Lewis Cabinet Specialties and Timberline Cabinets, though all orders must flow through Wurth's system Orders for Slide-A-Shelf products mixed with other vendors must be split; customers should be directed to place separate orders per vendor to avoid fulfillment issues Technical Issues and Fixes Slide-A-Shelf products are currently visible internally to Wurth's team only and must be toggled to live after markup is finalized Carson confirmed the system supports a flat fee or percentage surcharge applied uniformly across all Slide-A-Shelf products as a placeholder markup Granular per-product pricing adjustments are possible but a standard markup is preferred as the starting point Email notification triggers can be configured to alert Wurth's team when a Slide-A-Shelf product order is placed, enabling order monitoring and split management Chris requested different status bubble colors for verified orders; Carson will investigate expanding color options Carson will set up Chris Codinez and Mary as contacts in the Wurth Slide-A-Shelf Allmoxy account for order verification and PO entry access Shipping charges and product costs are currently combined on invoices; separating them for accounting clarity is under discussion; Carson confirmed system flexibility to support either format Decisions Made Wurth customers will pay shipping separately at checkout; pricing strategy must account for freight costs to remain competitive Jamie Brinker will send Wurth wholesale costs, suggested retail prices, MAP pricing, and shipping cost details; Jeremy Mitchell's pricing manager will use this data to finalize the markup percentage Carson will implement the agreed markup in Wurth's Allmoxy system once confirmed Orders requiring measurement confirmation for six or more shelves will include a phone follow-up from Slide-A-Shelf to reduce costly errors All order price or measurement adjustments must be documented via email approval to maintain invoicing integrity A Slide-A-Shelf-branded letter, installation instructions, and hardware documentation will be included in shipments to Wurth customers; Worth branding adjustments are under review A dedicated support line for Wurth's Slide-A-Shelf customers has been established Carson will maintain a single email thread with all participants to centralize communication Challenges or Concerns Pricing must be set carefully to avoid Wurth's site listing products above Slide-A-Shelf's own retail pricing, which could cause customers to bypass Wurth Shipping costs are a complicating factor; unlike large retailers offering free shipping, Wurth customers will pay freight, which affects perceived competitiveness High product variability (over 15,000 variations) and custom measurement requirements create order accuracy risk; phone verification for larger orders is a current mitigation Brand naming on Wurth's platform requires care to avoid alienating other customers who may sell competing products Final approval from Wurth's CEO and directors is required before going live; Jeremy Mitchell will coordinate this review after pricing and documentation are finalized Action Items Jamie Brinker: Provide Wurth with Slide-A-Shelf wholesale costs, suggested retail prices, MAP pricing, and shipping cost details Jamie Brinker: Share the current thank-you letter draft and installation instructions with Wurth for potential branding adjustments Jamie Brinker: Coordinate internally to manage order adjustments with proper email approvals for price or measurement changes Jamie Brinker: Follow up with Stephanie and the accounting team regarding possible invoice format changes to separate shipping from product costs for Wurth Jeremy Mitchell: Determine the appropriate markup or surcharge to apply on Slide-A-Shelf products in Wurth's system, then communicate pricing strategy and margins to Slide-A-Shelf and the internal pricing team Jeremy Mitchell: Coordinate with CEO and directors for final review and approval of pricing, packaging, and partnership details before going live Jeremy Mitchell: Obtain and share Quality Craft paperwork with the Slide-A-Shelf and Wurth teams to ensure consistent documentation Chris Codinez: Collaborate with Jamie and Carson to finalize paperwork and PO processes, verify orders on Allmoxy, and add POs accordingly Chris Codinez: Provide examples of invoices and packing slips used with current vendors to Slide-A-Shelf for review and replication Chris Codinez: Test and confirm Allmoxy site access and order processing capabilities for Wurth Slide-A-Shelf users Carson Ward: Implement pricing adjustment setup on Wurth's Allmoxy system for Slide-A-Shelf products with ability to apply a uniform surcharge or discount, keeping open the option for granular adjustments if needed Carson Ward: Configure system triggers and notifications to alert Wurth's team of orders containing Slide-A-Shelf products and order status changes Carson Ward: Assist Wurth team contacts (Chris and Mary) with Allmoxy access setup for order verification and purchase order entry Carson Ward: Follow up with Chris and Mary on documentation workflows and integrate required paperwork automation in Allmoxy Carson Ward: Distribute meeting recording and summary notes to all participants and maintain a shared communication thread for updates Next Steps Pricing markup percentage to be confirmed by Jeremy Mitchell's pricing manager after receiving cost data from Jamie Brinker; Carson to implement in Allmoxy once confirmed Wurth products to be toggled live in Allmoxy after markup is finalized and system configuration is complete Paperwork and documentation formats to be aligned between Wurth, Slide-A-Shelf, and Quality Craft to replicate current vendor workflows Order notification triggers to be configured and tested in Allmoxy Wurth contacts (Chris Codinez and Mary) to be onboarded with Allmoxy access in the coming days Jeremy Mitchell to present full pricing, packaging, and partnership details to Wurth's CEO and directors for final approval prior to launch Marketing collateral and customer communication materials (literature, QR code cards, branded inserts) to be prepared by Slide-A-Shelf for inclusion in shipments following leadership approval Additional calls to be scheduled as needed to resolve outstanding questions before go-live

customers.name: Ameridor, Inc
customers.churn_reason: Unresponsive; Timing Not Right; Customer Stakeholder Misalignment

engagements.body: Churn Playbook Please select all of the reasons that apply for why the customer is churning. Add any additional churn context in the note - the more the better here. If there's escalation notes that exist on the account be sure to reference them and include the data of the escalation note in the playbook context. Unresponsive, Timing Not Right, Customer Stakeholder Misalignment Notes: Churned last year because they "went into Allmoxy too fast". We tried to send them some buyout terms, but they completely ghosted and got rid Barb Sublet. Misalignment with sales. Update Pay Status to "Cancelled" Cancelled What's the catalog implementation status? No

Call with Jason Y 1/30/25 Tried to rush the implementation, closing the door on the door program altogether. Paying 7K for a program that he doesn’t use. Are we willing to bank these payments against the day he comes back (potentially)? He is absorbing Ameridor into the cabinet operation. So no longer doing doors (which was the whole project we scoped). They hoped that by end of jan they would have Allmoxy up and running. Jason talked to Beau about cabinet operation, and Beau said it probably wouldn’t be a fit due to the uniqueness of their cabinet program. They did just do a job with a veneered door, wondering if Allmoxy could be good for that. Also has a granite countertop business. Is there potential there? He’s got three businesses going and thought Allmoxy could have been a quicker setup, with Rob being in OK and them being in Colorado, they just decided to terminate their door program and absorb Rob’s shop into their cabinet operation. When they were sold, he was under the impression it would be quicker than it turned out to be. Jason wants to figure out what their options are. Are we going to say "TS" or is there another option?

Call with Rob 1/10/25 I spoke with Rob today and his partner who just bought him out, Jason, needs the weekend to fully think about moving forward with Allmoxy. They are a current paying customer who is almost ready to start the implementation process. Here is what I know. Jason bought Ameridor one year ago for their door program Rob, who owned Ameridor, is responsible for the Allmoxy implementation. Rob is staying on for 1 more year to get Allmoxy set up and train his replacement Jason feels like they may have jumped the gun with Allmoxy and needs the weekend to think/ pray on it. Rob feels that we are the right fit and he thinks Jason will come to the same conclusion He told Jason that he could either have him (Rob) or Allmoxy, and since he is retiring in a year he can't have him Rob feels bad since he has been dragging his feet on getting us a signature on the scoping agreement (which he actually just signed) but wants us to hold off on starting the build until he hears back from Jason I let Rob know that, if Jason still has cold feet, he can meet with Bobby to ease some of his reservations and go over things. Rob is still sending us updated reference material/ pricing so we can get started should this fall in the right direction

Meeting 1/10/25 with Sarah/ Rob/ Barb Action items, Sarah to send some information to Barb. Send Rob Razr gage Who is the Razor Gage rep we could talk to? They are struggling with combining shops and choosing paths Combining a bigger shop or and smaller shop, lot of moving parts right now. Rob signed, Jason doesn't have peace on it- Jason wants to table it. Jason needs to pray on it over the weekend. Rob's training a guy to take over, that guy just quit.

Call with Rob 12/31/24 In the process of moving, that is why he has been so out of pocket Wanted to touch base on validations, they are updating pricing. Wants to meet with Juan to talk on validations. He is headed back to CO on Saturday, will be back on Monday so let's schedule a meeting for Friday. Will have pricing info later in the week.

Notes: Rob Roy: Former owner transitioning out Jason Y: New owner Why did you buy the product? What were they hoping to achieve when they signed the contract and how does Allmoxy fit into that? Replace old ordering system, automation Rob is departing Ameridor in a year, wants to set them up with Allmoxy for new owner AND so he can manage orders when he moves from CO> OK If their answer is too broad, ask them to expand on how they envision their business changing when they purchased Allmoxy. Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) Notes: Replace old system, validations, triggers, price adjustments, exports to machines When does the customer want to "launch" and what would a successful onboarding look like? How do you know when you're there? Ex. Door Product Line is Launched and Cabinet Line is started. Notes: Before Christmas but I let him know that was not realistic.

engagements.title: Rugby & Allmoxy | B2B Connection
engagements.body: Rugby & Allmoxy | B2B Connection

12/11/25 - Onboarding Meeting (Meet with Evolve) https://app.fireflies.ai/view/FW-Allmoxy-Evolve-Cabinet::01KC2EF6N2XAJYWZ7A04G5QGSH Meeting Summary The meeting centered around the integration and setup of Stanley's custom door products within the Allmoxy platform for Rugby to resell. Discussions covered remaining technical issues, product setup steps, pricing configuration, materials management, and shipping logistics. A phased rollout strategy was proposed, including a plan to finalize configurations before the end of the year, with potential go-live in early 2025. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Stanley is waiting for Allmoxy to resolve a pricing issue related to panel thickness before proceeding with catalog sharing. The issue centers on mispricing the quarter-inch center door panel as a three-quarter-inch panel, affecting cost accuracy. A new material line (Tafisa from Rugby) will be added, but only once current pricing issues are resolved. Stanley clarified that Rugby customers should only see materials Rugby stocks, to avoid unsupported orders (e.g., Wilson Art in some markets). Custom product setup for Rugby’s instance will be handled through cloned versions of Stanley’s existing products, with limited material selections shown. Discussion around controlling the user experience: avoiding overwhelming material dropdowns and maintaining clean product presentation. Technical Issues & Fixes Current technical problem: Allmoxy misprices quarter-inch door panels as three-quarter-inch. Solution: Either document changes to send to Aaron for implementation, or set up a direct meeting with Aaron for a collaborative adjustment session. Future product flexibility depends on setting up accurate material depth attributes (e.g., panel thickness) and logic to avoid material bloat in user selections. Custom shipping weight (dim weight) must be manually input due to system limitations. Decisions Made Stanley will hold off on adding Rugby materials until the panel pricing fix is completed. Stanley will enable products for B2B resale once pricing is accurate, and Rugby will then pull them into their instance. Products for Rugby will be cloned and limited to Rugby-approved materials only. A single universal price adjustment will be used for all B2B products (instead of multiple per product). Future customer-specific attributes will be enabled when available to eliminate the need for cloned products. Local delivery will be handled by Rugby for nearby customers, and out-of-region freight will be managed directly by Stanley. Challenges or Concerns Risk of Rugby customers selecting unavailable or unreleased materials (e.g., Tafisa color not in the U.S.). Laminating veneer to match stock colors for quarter-inch panels is unreliable due to color variation. Teams issues on Stanley's end prevented screen sharing for product configuration during the meeting. Shipping complexity due to lack of integrated freight calculation (dim weight must be added manually). Action Items Stanley to: Finalize pricing corrections in Allmoxy for quarter-inch vs. three-quarter-inch panels. Provide list of products to be cloned and prepared for Rugby’s instance. Await Brandon’s list of stocked Tafisa SKUs (both quarter-inch and three-quarter-inch). Work with Carson to schedule a meeting with Aaron next week for pricing logic finalization. Brandon Anderson to: Send list of Tafisa materials stocked at Rugby. Carson Ward to: Coordinate the engineering meeting with Aaron and Stanley. Guide Stanley through enabling B2B product sharing via Allmoxy. Support configuration of cloned products with limited attributes for Rugby. Josh & Rugby Team to: Prepare for eventual integration of Allmoxy instance into the Rugby website (iframe or direct link). Test the ordering process once Stanley's catalog is ready. Next Steps Carson to send Aaron’s availability for a pricing review and configuration session with Stanley. Stanley to complete product setup and confirm pricing accuracy before cloning Rugby-specific versions. Rugby team to test ordering through Stanley’s instance once products and pricing are finalized. Determine shipping strategy per region: local via Rugby; freight handled by Stanley with manual input. Evaluate if temporary direct-link access can be used instead of iframe for faster Rugby launch. Target: Internal testing by January 1, 2025; public launch potentially by March 1, 2025.

Title: Allmoxy Kick Off Call Date: Tue Dec 09 2025 15:15:00 GMT-0500 (Eastern Standard Time) Participants: bobby@allmoxy.com, carson@allmoxy.com, jsilpe@rugbyabp.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 45 mins Overview - Integration with Evolve: Plans for a B2B iframe integration; no native products will be built initially. - Account Creation Control: Customers won't create accounts; access managed internally for pricing and catalog visibility. - Shipping Strategy: Local delivery for nearby customers; drop shipping from Evolve for distant deliveries. - Pricing Management: Pricing controlled internally to avoid exposure to errors; new customer accounts trigger notifications for verification. - Payment Processing: Payments handled through external ERP; merchant gateway on Almoxy will be disabled for financial transactions. - Next Steps: Meeting with Stanley on Thursday to finalize product setup, account management, and pricing permissions. Notes 👋 Introduction and Greetings Participants expressed excitement for collaboration and confirmed partnership with Evolve for B2B product selling. 👤 Role Introduction Carson Ward introduced as customer success/account rep responsible for guiding the connection with Evolve and deploying Almoxy for product integration and customer support. 🔄 Product Integration Focus No native product builds planned; focus on embedding Almoxy via iframe into Rugby’s site for seamless customer ordering directly from Evolve product catalogs. 🛠️ User Setup Demonstration Demonstration of adding users/contacts with permission settings in Almoxy system, highlighting how Brandon, Daryl, and Josh can manage accounts efficiently. 📅 Upcoming Call Preparation Scheduling and preparation for upcoming call with Stanley on Thursday to confirm product catalog availability and account setup essential before deep integration; Carson offered to join to facilitate communication. 🔍 Customer Account Creation Discussion Clarification that new customers creating their own accounts is possible but may not be ideal; discussion on price visibility control and setting proper pricing tiers via tags and account verification to avoid pricing errors or confusion. 📦 Order and Shipping Details Explanation that all order and shipping details flow through Almoxy/Evolve but that pricing and approvals are managed internally to maintain control. ⚙️ Account Setup Confirmation Identified that Rugby does not currently have an Evolve Almoxy account; setup and product readiness need confirmation from Stanley before catalog import. 🏷️ Tagging Mechanism Importance Tags introduced as a critical mechanism to control product visibility, pricing tiers, and customer segmentation; explained the mechanics of tagging on company accounts and product level, highlighting their fragility if deleted. 🚚 Shipping Logistics Discussion Shipping logistics discussed, with options including local delivery by Rugby trucks versus drop shipping directly from Evolve. Default shipping settings and customer restrictions can be configured accordingly. 🔒 Customer Account Control Decision Decision made to disable customer self-account creation on the Almoxy site to ensure Rugby controls customer onboarding, pricing, and access; new accounts created internally, then customers are emailed login info to ensure proper setup. 🔑 Controlled User Access Workflow Workflow clarified that customers cannot see pricing or catalogs without proper account setup and login, fitting Rugby’s controlled user access model. 🔗 Future SSO Implementation Discussion on future plan to implement single sign-on (SSO) so customers logging into Rugby’s site can access Almoxy catalog seamlessly without re-login. ✅ Next Steps Confirmation Confirmation that Thursday’s meeting with Stanley is the critical next step for account and product setup, followed by further implementation of iframe and API integration; Carson requested to be invited to that call. 📄 API Documentation and Support API documentation access available; technical integration will be handled by Rugby’s right technical contact, with Almoxy support routing questions as necessary. 🔚 Meeting Wrap-Up Final wrap-up confirms plan to review product availability with Stanley on Thursday, set up accounts, and proceed with technical integration afterwards. Action Items Josh Silpe Coordinate with Stanley to confirm product availability and enable catalogs for B2B on Thursday call Ensure that customer accounts are created internally before granting access to Almoxy catalog to control pricing visibility and user permissions Work with technical team to explore iframe and API integration for seamless catalog access from Rugby’s site Include Carson Ward on the Thursday call with Stanley for technical and operational support Brandon Anderson Confirm if Rugby has an Almoxy account setup with Evolve and prepare for account creation if not Onboard customer accounts and coordinate pricing/permission assignments before they access Almoxy Support logistics decisions for shipping options and delivery methods Carson Ward Assist in user/contact setup demonstration and support with permissions configuration Join Thursday call with Stanley to facilitate product catalog enablement and account setup Disable customer self-account creation option on Almoxy site per Rugby’s security policy Provide training and guidance on tagging, financial settings, shipping configurations, and permissions management Share API documentation and assist in routing technical questions Send meeting recording and recap following call Stanley Enable and finalize product catalog and pricing for B2B access via Almoxy

@Bobby Lewis Here is the meeting info:  https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Allmoxy-Rugby-Evolve::01K9WR0CA93X1M3F2XBQE0FJJG .  Darryl Miller is the guy to reach out to with follow up on a contract and he is expecting it.
Spoke to Julio on 10/15 about them wanting to cancel both B2B instances. They are terminating their business relationship with both companies. I refunded them for their payment that ran on 10/14.

Title: Allmoxy Demo - : The Job Shop Date: Wed Jul 23 2025 17:45:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Participants: bobby@allmoxy.com, info@thejobshopco.com, dave@thejobshopco.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 45 mins Overview - Bobby Lewis conducted the Allmoxy demo for The Job Shop, a cabinet shop aiming for $500K annual drawer box spending from five key Manhattan clients. - The Job Shop received a referral from a Manhattan client generating $7.5 million annually in residential casework, highlighting potential growth opportunities. - Allmoxy's customer portal allows self-service ordering, tracking, and integrates Stripe for payments at 2.9%, saving 0.6% compared to QuickBooks' 3.5%. - The inventory management system features automated tracking and allocation of materials, ensuring efficient production aligned with order verification. - Bobby's previous experience demonstrated Allmoxy's effectiveness, reducing accounts receivable aging from 62 days to 14 days on 30-day terms. - Project scheduling and Gantt chart features are prioritized for development in 2025 to enhance production planning capabilities. - Full API integration with Zapier is available, allowing connectivity with third-party tools for improved scheduling and task management. - The pricing structure involves a monthly subscription based on annual revenue processed, with an implementation cost of $300-400 for setup. - A hybrid build approach was recommended for The Job Shop, facilitating client learning alongside the implementation engineer for better formula writing. - Ongoing support includes a dedicated customer success representative within the subscription plan to assist with product configuration and troubleshooting. Notes 📋 Initial Context & Introduction Bobby Lewis conducting Allmoxy software demo from Vegas trade show for The Job Shop, a small cabinet shop in Hellertown, Pennsylvania. The Job Shop specializes in custom drawer boxes and received referral from Manhattan client doing $7.5 million in residential casework annually. Client requested they use Allmoxy for billing and payment processing for drawer box orders. The Job Shop targeting five major players in Manhattan market, with potential $500K annual drawer box spending per client. Company uses Mosaic CNC for cabinetry and Kantec dovetail machines for drawer boxes. 💻 Software Overview & Customer Portal Allmoxy creates branded customer portal at company.allmoxy.com with custom logos and optional custom domain. Customer self-service portal allows clients to place orders, see pricing, and track delivery dates Amazon-style. Integrated Stripe payment processing at 2.9% rate vs QuickBooks' 3.5% rate, saving 0.6% on credit card transactions. QuickBooks integration in beta for seamless transaction sync. System includes drawer box templates ready for implementation. Automated triggers send customer notifications for order confirmations, shipping updates, and completion notices. 📦 Inventory Management & Production Features Barcode scanning system for tracking products through production with printable labels for drawer boxes. Supply system tracks inventory automatically, allocating materials when orders verified and comparing against minimum quantities. Work schedule management organizes production by delivery dates with start/stop job tracking. System can generate packing slips, invoices, and shipping reports automatically. Tax Jar integration calculates appropriate tax rates by customer location. Bobby's previous experience: managed $1.6 million monthly production with one secretary using Allmoxy. 🚀 Advanced Features & Development Roadmap Project scheduling and Gantt chart functionality identified as 2025 development priority. Full API integration available with Zapier connectivity for third-party calendar and scheduling systems. System supports customer-specific pricing with granular discounts by company tags or individual products. Cabinet operation scaling available beyond drawer boxes for dealers wanting manufactured parts. Money collection improvement: Bobby's experience showed reduction from 62-day to 14-day average aging on 30-day terms. 💰 Pricing Structure & Implementation Timeline Monthly subscription model based on annual revenue processed through system, locked for 12 months then adjusted based on actual usage. Implementation cost: approximately $300-400 for 2 sessions with implementation engineer for drawer box setup. Customer success representative included in subscription for ongoing support. Setup timeline: estimated 2 hours for simple solid maple dovetail drawer box product configuration. Hybrid build approach recommended where client learns formula writing alongside implementation engineer. Action Items Bobby Lewis Send demo access link with three different user login perspectives (customer, owner, employee) for hands-on exploration Send pricing structure chart showing subscription tiers based on annual revenue Send subscription agreement once pricing tier is determined Provide drawer box product template for immediate implementation Text Dave to join the meeting The Job Shop Team Discuss and determine anticipated annual revenue for first year to set subscription tier Review demo system and explore product formulas, pricing structures, and functionality Respond to Bobby with selected pricing tier and next steps decision

engagements.title: Meeting with Evan Goldstein from
engagements.body: Meeting with Evan Goldstein from

5/14/25 - Catalog working session - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Subtle-Cabinets-Allmoxy-Working-Session::01JV2Q35MHS1ZHRREB3P5BXYG7 Pricing information they shared as of 5/13 is attached. Agenda: Review pricing sheet provided Talk about materials and attribute selections Confirm where Evan and Marques need to enter data for pricing to start calculating. Meeting Summary The meeting involved Carson Ward, Evan, Marques B, and Aaron Dundas reviewing the pricing structure setup in Allmoxy. Evan is transitioning into the primary role of managing Allmoxy-related tasks, while Marques focuses on Cabinet Vision and CNC processes. The goal was to clarify the labor and material pricing logic within Allmoxy, validate current formulas, and empower Evan and Marques to configure and manage pricing independently. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Role Assignments: Evan will manage Allmoxy pricing and configuration going forward; Marques will handle Cabinet Vision and CNC-related tasks. Pricing Formula Structure: Labor and material pricing were discussed in depth, focusing on clarity, customization, and future flexibility. Casework pricing (base, tall, wall, vanity) is calculated using lineal footage multiplied by labor rates. Door pricing is based on square footage, with fallback minimums for small door sizes. Drawer box types (e.g., dovetail, rollout) have differentiated labor costs. Use of Allmoxy Meta Attributes: Meta attributes were introduced to assign custom labor rates based on cabinet type and product characteristics. These attributes allow scalable, global pricing changes and keep labor logic clean and consistent. Meta values must be manually added to each product and defaulted to appropriate values (e.g., “base”, “wall”). Material and Hardware Costing: Pricing tables can include hardware like Blum Tandem glides. Material pricing factors include square footage, waste percentage, and margin settings. Rollout and drawer box labor included separately from material. Technical Issues & Fixes Missing data in the shared pricing sheet (e.g., door per sheet data line) was clarified and corrected. Some final square footage calculations (e.g., for slab doors) didn’t save in the spreadsheet but were resolved in discussion. Proxy formulas were built and tested during the session for pricing logic. Clarification was provided on how Allmoxy handles deleted attributes and their permanent reservation. Decisions Made Use Meta attributes for labor assignment across product categories. Price slab doors at $25/sq ft, painted MDF slab doors at $60/sq ft, and five-piece painted MDF doors at $75/sq ft. Set a fallback labor price of $40/door for doors under 1.5 sq ft. Casework labor rates: Base: $125/lineal ft Tall: $250/lineal ft Drawer box labor: Dovetail: $175 Standard: $20 Rollouts: $120 Glide cost will be included directly in pricing using existing tables since standard hardware is always used. Challenges or Concerns Evan requested further clarification and training on how attributes and pricing formulas function in Allmoxy. Awareness was raised about the irreversible nature of attribute names in Allmoxy and the cascading effects of deleting them. Marques noted uncertainty about integrating laminated five-piece door options, which remain under development. There’s some need for future enhancement around installation pricing visibility and material tracking (e.g., sheet usage). Action Items Carson to send the Zoom recording and transcript for reference. Evan to review the recording and begin hands-on input of materials, attributes, and pricing adjustments in Allmoxy. Marques to provide updated pricing numbers for painted MDF slabs and five-piece doors and help configure new product types. Aaron to finalize proxy formulas and push them to all relevant product types once meta attributes are assigned. Evan & Marques to attach and default meta attributes to all relevant cabinet products in the catalog. Next Steps Evan and Marques to independently input data and test pricing logic in Allmoxy. Once comfortable, they’ll reach out to Carson to schedule a follow-up session. Carson will coordinate availability with Aaron for the next review session, potentially the following week or last week of May depending on progress. Let me know if you'd like this output in a downloadable format or need it copied into a specific HubSpot field.

05/07/25 - Platform Training Session - https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Subtle-Cabinets-Allmoxy-Platform-Intro::01JTGY9WCD4PGVJXGR1V3HZZD7 Agenda: Catalog Check-in - Gathering the data needed? Ready to meet with Aaron for a working session? Platform Training navigation (Home page, order page, companies page, financial settings, order settings) Homework Create a test account and contact Create a test order Setup Stripe Adjust financial settings Complete the data set for what attribute selections and pricing these options should have so we can schedule another working session. Meeting Summary Carson met with Marques and Evan to provide onboarding and guidance on using the Allmoxy platform. The session covered platform navigation, pricing strategy, attribute configuration, and preparation steps for an upcoming technical meeting with Aaron, the implementation engineer. Carson reiterated the importance of structuring the catalog correctly before full operational setup and guided Evan through key areas of the platform to become more self-sufficient. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Meeting Kickoff Context: Recap from the previous session included catalog overview, pricing option planning, and the need to gather data before engaging Aaron. Evan's Role: Evan will be working primarily with Allmoxy. This session served as his functional onboarding. Pricing Model Clarification: Marques and Evan want to price drawer boxes as a flat rate ($100/drawer) rather than by square footage. They have slab door and cabinet frame pricing ready but need guidance on where and how to enter those values. Carson explained the pricing system’s use of proxy variables and emphasized the need for proper engineering input to avoid configuration errors. Technical Issues & Fixes Drawer Box Pricing Confusion: Marques couldn’t locate where to enter drawer pricing independently; Carson explained this needs proxy variable configuration and engineering support. Dev Tool Introduction: Carson introduced the ?dev=1 tool in product pages to test pricing logic and see proxy breakdowns in real time. Test Orders: Carson walked through how to create test orders to validate tax and pricing behavior in the platform. Decisions Made Meeting with Aaron: Scheduled for next Wednesday, 9–11 AM PT, to focus on pricing implementation and deeper catalog configuration. Tax Setup: Chose California as the primary tax nexus state; other nearby states may be added without harm. Default Credit Settings: Credit limit: $0 (users don’t get pre-approved credit by default). Order limit: $500 for now, adjustable per client. Stripe Integration: To be connected via the financial settings page; Stripe collects payments, but Allmoxy handles tax logic and deposits full amounts (including tax) into the user’s account. Challenges or Concerns Marques and Evan are juggling multiple priorities (CNC setup, active jobs, and Allmoxy onboarding) with a lean team. Concern about entering incorrect pricing logic and causing broader platform disruptions. Carson emphasized caution and deferring deeper configuration to Aaron. Question about how tax is managed through Stripe clarified — tax is calculated by Allmoxy but deposited to the user; the business is responsible for remitting it to the state. Action Items Marques and Evan to: Finalize and send their pricing data and catalog setup preferences to Carson before next week’s meeting with Aaron. Begin entering material options (e.g., Birch, Maple) into the attribute tables for box and door materials. Set up a test company with a non-employee email for running simulated orders and testing flows. Carson to: Send meeting recording and recap. Forward pricing questions and context to Aaron. Follow up with internal team about how Stripe presents tax breakdown in reports. Evan to: Continue exploring the product and override pages using the ?dev=1 tool. Avoid modifying proxies or critical formulas until after the session with Aaron. Next Steps Meeting with Aaron confirmed for next Wednesday, 9–11 AM PT, to focus on pricing logic, proxy customization, and catalog structure. Marques and Evan to prepare and send over finalized pricing data. Follow-up session with Carson to revisit platform-side setup will be scheduled after the session with Aaron. Continue light data entry (non-destructive, like materials) and navigation training in the interim.

04/24/25 Kick Off Call  -https://app.fireflies.ai/view/Allmoxy-Kick-Off-Call::01JSF5509THFR228T1N1TVRJVW 🗓️ Kickoff Call Agenda: Subtle Cabinets Implementation Participants: Customer: Marques (Owner), Brother (if applicable) Allmoxy: Carson Ward, Aaron [Engineer], Bobby Lewis Meeting Length: 60 minutes Objective: Establish shared goals, confirm system scope, collect foundational inputs, and begin the implementation plan for Subtle Cabinets' cabinet manufacturing system. 1. Introductions & Call Purpose (5 min) Quick round of intros Overview of the call goals Reinforce the project mission: building an efficient, scalable system for outsourced cabinet manufacturing 2. Business Context Review (5 min) Marques to share brief background: shift from van cabinetry to residential Company vision and short/long-term goals Volume expectations and growth projections 3. Technical Scope Confirmation (10 min) Full Cabinet Template Build: Door styles: likely 2–3 (Shaker, Slab) Drawer box types: 2 expected Construction methods: 1–2 Software Stack Review: Cabinet Vision: fresh setup, confirm export expectations QuickBooks: accounting software confirmed Stripe: confirm usage/setup for billing (if applicable) Inventory Management: clarify if this is planned in phase one Export to Panhandle’s instance for doors/drawer fronts 4. Material & Catalog Inputs (10 min) Review expectations: Profile list Wood list Sheet material selection (confirm vendors/colors) Identify any missing items or vendor dependencies Assign deadlines for remaining inputs 5. Implementation Workflow Overview (10 min) Walkthrough of hybrid implementation process Timeline expectations and phase breakdown Importance of staying prepared for each session Clarify engineer billing (Aaron’s time = billable) 6. System Configuration Alignment (10 min) Pricing formula overview (Base + Overages model) Material Schedules and Catalog structure Inventory setup (if applicable) Naming conventions, instance expectations 7. Q&A & Risk Clarifications (5 min) Address any concerns from the Subtle Cabinets team Confirm understanding and alignment 8. Next Steps & Responsibilities (5 min) Assignments: Customer to upload all required materials to shared folder Bobby to monitor submission and validate completeness Aaron to review templates ahead of next session Carson to coordinate milestone tracking Schedule next build session Notes: Partner wasn't able to make it, struggling with the flu Based just north of San Francisco Introductions - share my role and Aaron's role Goal - establish the scope of the project and understanding the journey to get this live Meeting Summary Purpose: This meeting was held to kick off the Allmoxy onboarding and catalog build process for Marques' cabinet shop, Subtle Cabinets, located north of San Francisco. The session served as an introduction to the team, including Marques' success manager (Carson), implementation engineer (Aaron), and advisor (Bobby), and outlined the scope of the implementation. Context: Marques recently completed a remodel and equipment installation in a 1,000+ sqft shop, acquiring key machines including a 5x10 CNC, a vertical CNC, and an Altendorf F5 edgebander. The team is transitioning to Allmoxy with initial setup focused on building a product catalog and integrating workflows with Cabinet Vision. Urgency: There is moderate urgency due to recent technical setbacks (a computer crash resulting in data loss), and a desire to operationalize the platform swiftly. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Equipment Setup: Shop is operational with full CNC capabilities and a new edgebander. Allmoxy Integration: Initial focus is on catalog setup, attribute configuration, and aligning Allmoxy's product logic with Cabinet Vision exports. Cabinet Vision Data Loss: Marques lost a week of product/pricing data due to BitLocker reset on a new computer—catch-up work is ongoing. Technical Issues & Fixes Data Chain Integrity: Aaron emphasized the importance of attribute linkage and formula integrity in Allmoxy (highlighted by pink boxes for missing references). Cabinet Vision Integration: ORD file generation from Allmoxy will transfer data to Cabinet Vision, but formula/pricing setup must be duplicated in both systems manually. Export Mapping: Precise export names from Cabinet Vision must match Allmoxy’s export fields exactly to avoid breakdowns. Decisions Made Marques will: Begin defining dropdown menus for attributes (materials, drawer types, toe kicks, etc.). Limit door offerings initially to slab doors, delaying MDF and 5-piece options until pricing and CNC setup are finalized. Create construction methods in Cabinet Vision specifically for Allmoxy compatibility. Aaron and Carson will assist with formula creation and platform orientation. Challenges or Concerns Marques needs time to familiarize himself with Allmoxy and finalize product/pricing strategy. Training Evan (partner) and catching up on lost Cabinet Vision work adds pressure. Concerns about system complexity; emphasis on avoiding over-engineering. Action Items Who Task Details Due Date Marques Define catalog dropdowns Focus on most complex cabinet SKU; list attributes, materials, and price-impacting variables Before next check-in Marques Rebuild lost Cabinet Vision data Re-enter pricing and vendor contacts lost due to system crash ASAP Carson Schedule next check-in Confirmed for May 1st, 8 AM Booked Carson Route technical questions Forward formula-specific queries from Marques to Aaron as needed Ongoing Aaron Assist in catalog build Guide attribute setup, formula linking, and ensure export compatibility Next session Marques Add Evan to invite Include Evan (evan@subtlecabinets.com) in future sessions Before May 1st Next Steps May 1st Check-In: Carson and Marques to review catalog prep progress and explore broader Allmoxy platform features. Catalog Construction: Begin formal build with Aaron once dropdowns and key selections are finalized. Ongoing Support: Marques to send questions to Carson for delegation; team to support gradual ramp-up and long-term handoff readiness. Imagery & Visuals: Marques will add product images as needed, including external visuals or screenshots from Cabinet Vision to enhance presentation clarity.

Title: Allmoxy Kick Off Call Date: Thu Apr 24 2025 11:00:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Participants: aaron@allmoxy.com, bobby@allmoxy.com, carson@allmoxy.com, marques@subtlecabinets.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 68 mins Overview The Allmoxy Kick Off Call was an introductory meeting between the Allmoxy team and Marques from Subtle Cabinets, focusing on the implementation of the Allmoxy software following a recent shop remodel. Key topics included an overview of the Allmoxy interface, system functionalities, and the integration with Cabinet Vision, with emphasis on establishing proper naming conventions for data transfer. The discussion highlighted the importance of setting up product attributes and dropdown options, with guidance on starting with slab door configurations. Action items for Marques involved dedicating time to explore the Allmoxy system, identifying pricing structures, and creating a specific construction method in Cabinet Vision. The meeting concluded with scheduling a follow-up for further discussions, underscoring the team's commitment to a collaborative and structured implementation process. Notes 🚀 Introduction and Background Setup Meeting introduced Bobby, Carson (success rep), and Aaron (implementation engineer) to Marques from Subtle Cabinets. Marques has recently completed shop remodel with CNC machine, vertical CNC, and Altendorf F5 Edge Bander. Marques explained his partner couldn't join due to illness. Shop is approximately 1,000 square feet with capability for 1 mil and half mil edge banding. Marques mentioned experiencing a computer crash that resulted in lost Cabinet Vision data. Discussion established that Marques has already imported and tested the template in Cabinet Vision. 📋 System Functionality Overview Aaron demonstrated Allmoxy interface including orders page and settings panel. Explained the 'pink boxes' error indicators that help identify missing data. Covered product page functionality including attributes, pricing, and parts tabs. Discussed formulas and how they work within Allmoxy system. Showed how attributes must be attached to products before they can be referenced in formulas. Explained how proxy variables work to enable global changes across products. Demonstrated testing sections for formulas to verify calculations without affecting total price. 🔧 Cabinet Vision Integration Discussed how Allmoxy exports data to Cabinet Vision through export codes. Clarified that Marques needs to manually enter pricing in Allmoxy, not import from Cabinet Vision. Bobby explained Marques would need to set up construction methods in both Cabinet Vision and Allmoxy. Confirmed that Allmoxy will create an ORD file that can be imported into Cabinet Vision. Established naming conventions between systems must match for proper data transfer. Discussed that Marques should focus on making a specific construction method in Cabinet Vision for Allmoxy. 📊 Product Configuration Focused on setting up dropdown menus for product attributes. Discussed adding different construction types like leg levelers and separate toe kicks. Aaron showed how to make attributes visible or hidden across products. Bobby recommended starting with defining what options should be available in dropdowns. Discussed image configurations for product visualization. Established that Marques wants to start with slab doors before adding more complex door types. 📈 Next Steps and Implementation Approach Carson emphasized the complexity of the system and reassured Marques this is normal. Bobby recommended focusing on defining dropdowns and pricing structure first. Aaron emphasized the system's flexibility and recommended keeping implementation straightforward. Discussed the importance of making the system transferable to other staff in the future. Carson suggested identifying the most complex cabinet type to define maximum required attributes. Bobby suggested pulling data from Cabinet Vision for initial configuration when possible. Marques acknowledged needing to spend dedicated time to understand the system. 🗓️ Wrap-up and Scheduling Carson scheduled a follow-up meeting for next Thursday, August 1st at 8am. Marques requested to add Evan (evan@subtlecabinets.com) to future meetings. Marques mentioned he's training Evan on Cabinet Vision simultaneously. Brief personal conversation about Marques' recent trip to Peru. Team expressed excitement about working together moving forward. Action Items Marques Spend dedicated time (2-3 days) to explore Allmoxy system and prepare detailed questions Identify desired dropdown options and how they should be priced Create a construction method in Cabinet Vision specifically for Allmoxy integration Identify the most complex cabinet offering to define all necessary product attributes Add pricing information to Allmoxy attributes tables Carson Schedule follow-up meeting for Thursday, August 1st at 8am Add Evan (evan@subtlecabinets.com) to future meeting invites Prepare to address platform operation questions in next meeting Aaron Prepare to assist with formula development in future sessions Help customize system based on Marques' defined dropdowns and pricing structure

The meeting encompassed discussions on Don Dodson's business growth by integrating All Moxie with existing software like Mosaic and potential transitions for streamlined operations. Key points included exploring functionalities, order entry processes tailored to project needs, and roles involving team members like Corey and Anthony, targeting different customer segments. Customization options were extensively discussed for efficient cabinet program integration. A system implementation to track job progress using barcode scanners was proposed by Sam Alexander, detailing how stations would scan orders, log employees, and track labor based on station activity. Cost breakdowns were discussed, with pricing structured based on company size tiers. The pricing plan was adjusted for companies approaching three million in revenue, with detailed talks about product catalogs and customization options. Estimates for setup hours ranged from 60 to 100 hours at a $150 per hour rate, with subscriptions billed monthly and integration work billed weekly. Sam's team would handle maintenance tasks and updates beyond the build-out phase as needed. Don expressed cost considerations against possible gains, mentioning further internal discussions, while Sam assured ongoing support and resource availability through effective business model alignment. Overall, the meeting emphasized understanding the best approach for specific needs, considering factors like data transfer limitations and customizations required for seamless operations. Automation prospects were explored concerning invoices processing via Quickbooks integration, and triggers functionality highlighted for automated actions throughout the ordering process. The meetings outlined comprehensive insights into optimizing workflows within the business effectively, ensuring ongoing support and resource availability for future decisions.

Cancellation Note 10/7/25 Meeting Summary The client confirmed their intention to cancel both Allmoxy subscriptions, citing a lack of alignment with their industry needs. The discussion focused on their decision to discontinue use due to poor adoption among their customers, who require visual renderings rather than list-based configurators. The client is currently evaluating alternative platforms better suited for their business model. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Client intends to cancel both Allmoxy accounts. Primary reason: Allmoxy does not fit their industry niche or customer expectations. Customers prefer 3D visualizers, renderings, or drawings instead of list-based workflows provided by Allmoxy. Client was unable to get any users excited or willing to adopt the platform. Technical Issues & Fixes No specific technical issues were reported; the decision was based on feature alignment and customer feedback. Decisions Made Proceed with cancellation of both Allmoxy subscriptions. Cancellation forms will be sent for completion to initiate the process. Challenges or Concerns Lack of customer interest or engagement with Allmoxy. Visual configuration tools are a required feature for their market. Action Items Send cancellation forms for both Allmoxy instances. Process cancellation upon receipt of completed forms. Next Steps Await completed cancellation forms from the client. Client is exploring alternatives, including Odoo and potentially 2020 Insight. Maintain contact for potential future opportunities or collaboration.

Launched Customer Playbook Does the customer define launched as running and processing orders through Allmoxy? If not what is their definition? Notes: Yes When did the customer officially "launch"? 01/13/2025 What has been the most difficult part of this customers onboarding /implementation experience? Other Notes: No real challenges, they had a quick TEK build and a quick onboarding experience. They adopted quickly and wanted to launch quick. Does the customer want to continue meeting on a regular weekly, biweekly or monthly basis? Notes: No, they reach out as needed. Sometimes Michael will reach out with API questions. What is the customers continued benchmark for success? Utilizing the API, potentially doing B2B Notes: Eliminating double-entry What is next for the customer in terms of their business goals and initiatives and how can Allmoxy/CS support these? Notes: Potentially using B2B with a few customers (Eicher being one). Continue to use the API What is the customers health in your opinion? Green(this customer is in good/great standing and would advocate for Allmoxy - churn would be shocking) Could this customer be good to use for marketing Customer Testimonials/Case Studies?(Now or down the line - think personalities, how customer is using system, technical strengths, innovative thinking) Please tag Cheneil & Madison if so. Notes: No

CS Onboarding Playbook Is this a VIP/Legacy customer?(If you're unsure check with leadership) No Is our Services team being utilized for this build? No Notes: TEK build Who is the main POC within the customer? Josh Confirm the customers desired Outcome for using Allmoxy Online Storefront(No more hand to paper ordering) Notes: Cabinet Vision exports and online ordering API When is the customers goal launch date? 01/08/2025 What cadence of meeting schedule did you set with the customer? Notes: twice a week Ask the customer to define their benchmark for success Online storefront, cabinet vision exports, payment processing What Software(s) does the customer have? How will Allmoxy work with these softwares? Cabinet Vision Is the customer looking to fit Allmoxy around an existing ordering system or is this their first?(Utilize your Services Rep to more deeply understand how this impacts the catalog build.) Notes: First ordering system Is the customer letting you drive the meeting and follow an agenda or do they want to drive and take charge of meetings? Either can be okay, but dependent on the customers approach to onboarding you will need to pivot your approach to working with them. Notes: I drove most of the time, they picked up really quickly. Any initial concerns with the customer and their ability to launch(personnel, resources available, understanding of product, etc?) Notes: No

@Josh Baker and @Madison Theisen  Friday Brady, Jake, and I came to the conclusion that when we grant an API access, we want to quickly have them get on with me to scope a quick review and how they plan to use the API.  This will help us get market feedback on what potential needs we will want to convert to our product road map, and also provide assistance in helping them put together a team that will use it properly.    Here is the link https://meetings.hubspot.com/beaulewis/api-access-quick-scope-training?uuid=d0f9f326-faf3-4e89-84d6-811717c8bcd3 .   Coming out of that meeting, I will give jake the green light and grant them keys.  This will allow market feedback to arrive in the appropriate place, me to join together Pro serve clients, and keep work off of Jakes desk that is not filtered.   Any objections to this?   If not, if you don't mind, let's get them on a quick scope call.  ?
