Rigel Cabinetry Onboarding #3/4 with Rigel Team To do Triggers (some are made, they do need to set up a welcome email to customers and new company added to employee) Price adjustments (done) Stripe (done) Tags (done) B2B No drop shipping Amos is assembling and finishing, they send to him RTA Do they want individuals to log in or just businesses? How an order moves through Allmoxy What is your current workflow? I will do a test order There is an expected workflow in Allmoxy Bid --> Ordered --> Verified --> In progress --> completed --> shipped When an order is verified, it is locked (the customer can no longer make changes) Customer's can make changes to Bids and Orders SHOW TRIGGERS AGAIN Test order Production lines/ work schedule report Production lines allow you to mimic what is happening on the floor in Allmoxy. What are the steps in making a slab door for example? Show Production Lines and Work Schedule Report (easy way to see jobs in verified status) We don't need to set up production lines, but it can be useful. We can even just make one category called "in progress". Or we can get more granular. (Show barcode scanning) You can start jobs for each production line --> moving them to in progress (which will drop off from the report) It will show you a start by date and a ship by date (based off lead time) From there, you can mimic what happens on the job floor in Allmoxy by using the scanning station OR you can move orders to in progress manually Shipping Methods How are they doing shipping? LTL Brokerage company They use multiple different companies Based off weight and volume They will add a shipping charge after LTL, then add that to open invoice Financial order settings (determine when your customers pay) Default checkout flow (use credit limit or pay before processing) . You can set this globally or per customer If some customers refuse to do pay before processing, you can change their individual checkout flow Credit limit is extending a certain amount of money to place an order, and set up payment terms (pay when they ship or the order is marked complete) Order limit/ build limit: the amount of product you will build but NOT necessarily ship to your customer. You set those terms Default interest rate- if you are doing terms and they haven't paid by the end of the month, a fee is attached. Daily is every day something is past due, it attaches a fee Sales tax presence Where are you doing business, just MI or regional? 5K a year threshold- when you collect 5K in revenue from a state, you need to start collecting sales tax Statement editor (credit limit): goes out to your customers at the end of every month if they have an outstanding balance. Overhead management can be tracked here: lease payments, etc. Go to order page, click on the customer to show dashboard If pay before processing, it will just show their order history If there are credit limits, they can log in and pay from here (or you can do an offline payment- customers don't have access) Could be a walk in, pay in cash, or use square. Refund payments (demo) Undo transaction (transaction history report, then refund that payment on your stripe dashboard) Accounting Export invoices queue QB or QBO? Notes 50% down, 50% at shipped Internal use products tag

Call with Ivan 1/2/25 Want to set up B2B account for their customer Amos Eicher He gets things flat packed, he finishes and assembles then ships to the customer after putting in his upcharge. They are going to be managing the B2B catalog for him (Amos is Amish), but get him set up with trigger emails so he can see incoming orders.

Onboarding #2 with Rigel Team 12/30/24 Tags These are ways you can group customers together for a certain purpose Cabinet dealers and contractors Pricing based on number of units over a 6 month time period Bronze, silver, gold, platinum (discount off retail price) These can be referenced in triggers and price adjustments Ex. you have a group of customers that are cabinet shops, and they get a certain discount. You can give this group of customers a "cabinet shop" tag on their company profile that can then be referenced later. You can also put tags on products How do you think you might group certain customers together? Price Adjustments Do you have customers that are priced differently than others? Why? Use price adjustments to achieve this Retail vs Wholesale Say you have a customer that is your oldest and most loyal customer, you always give them a 10% discount no matter what. Price adjustments are basically discounts or surcharges that can be applied to customers, groups of customers, or products. They are adjusted PER ITEM, not the total order amount Priority For when there are multiple price adjustments competing with one another If one discount has a higher priority than the other, the highest priority will override the other discount Ex. if you have a new customer discount and a summer sale discount going on at the same time, you would want the new customer discount to have a higher priority Tip: Use the Always setting, so you know if you are discounting or surcharging every time. You can also discount customers or products with certain tags Triggers How are you currently interacting with customers? Everything is manual (phone and emails) Triggers can automate that process for you Notify you, and your customers of something happening in Allmoxy Triggers to edit: 1, 6, 12, 16, 18, 19 Does he want to be notified of a bid? Or an order? Notes TEK needs to make some adjustments with the pricing Testing today and tomorrow CV testing tomorrow Any other dealer they deal with, they would quote them and then they quote the customer In this customer's case (Amos) wants his customers to be able to quote it from Rigel and then Amos gets a cut or incentive out of it They are doing all the selling and quoting for Amos Which it will all go RTA to Amos, however, the customer is paying for them like they would be assembled. They want to set customers up to Bronze pricing He's doing the assembly and finishing Amos's customers pay the bronze discount, and Amos pays the platinum discount and Amos gets the difference. Is there a way to figure out what Amos should be paid? Solution This all needs to be done outside of Allmoxy- until he gets set up B2B and there is an upcharge for his services You'll need to figure out his cut based on the order before sending him the RTA cabinets, maybe include a check for his services with the RTA flat pack.

Onboarding meeting #1 with Ivan & Michael 12/18/24 Notes from previous calls TEK build SKU based catalog Currently lots of double-entry Doesn't want public ordering- they want to verify all customers LTL Shipments with rates negotiated with carriers UPS with smaller packages Sometimes will call but mostly freight shipped Covering today Site navigation What he sees vs what his customer will see Creating an account Does he want their users to log in? Yes- but verify all customers first. Just businesses? For a customer Individual and Company Adding employees Permissions Creating an order What are you imagining the order flow to look like? What is the current order flow? How are you currently or planning to communicate to customers? Has it been manual in the past? How will customers be paying? Pay before processing? Or use a credit limit? They have credit limits, everyone pays as soon as they deliver How do they process payments? Stripe set up 2.9% fee for all cards, so it is consistent. (paid by you) The underwriting process is very important

Call Ivan TEK is almost done, will start training next week. Testing next week. This customer has no access to internet (Amish). He only wants to get involved with payment and delivery. He just finishes and assembles it. For his customers will be slightly higher. His customers can log on, but send money to his bank account. Title: Larkul Date: Wed Jul 23 2025 11:30:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Participants: bobby@allmoxy.com, dambuger@gmail.com, joshb@allmoxy.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 43 mins Overview - Josh Baker introduced himself as the customer success manager for Larkul, highlighting the challenges of conference attendance down to 10,000 compared to the typical 20,000 participants. - Josh's role as a non-billable project manager was clarified, with implementation engineers Aaron and Ray available at $150/hour for backend support. - A hybrid implementation method emerged, wherein the customer manages 95% of the workload, with engineering support provided as necessary. - The current product catalog includes floating shelves, mantles, and kitchen hoods, while display cases have been removed from the scope based on recent discussions. - Pricing for products employs linear foot pricing predominantly, with square foot pricing applied specifically to mantles. - The initial implementation meeting is set for August 22nd at 1:00 PM, after the customer's scheduled bow hunting trip. - Integration with the customer’s Mosaic system necessitates a live meeting for field mapping and export testing to ensure accurate data handling. - QuickBooks integration remains under development, expected to complete in two months, with a Transaction Pro workaround already in place. - User management discussions covered customizable permission levels for employee accounts and clarified public ordering protocols for pricing visibility. - The focus on B2B catalog integration and the potential post-launch exploration of marketplace features was addressed, with a preference for prioritizing pricing and Stripe setup first. Notes 📋 Meeting Introduction & Setup Josh Baker introduces himself as customer success manager for Larkul, with Ayden and Rich participating. Meeting conducted while Josh is at a conference in Vegas with poor hotel WiFi causing audio issues initially. Discussion about conference attendance being lower than usual, around 10,000 vs typical 20,000 attendees. 🔧 Role Definitions & Project Structure Josh explains his role as non-billable project manager for implementation, while Aaron or Ray (implementation engineers) handle backend work at $150/hour. Hybrid implementation approach confirmed where customer does 95% of work with engineering support as needed. Three build types explained: full service, hybrid (customer preferred), and meeting-only approach. 🗂️ Product Catalog Configuration Current product offerings confirmed: floating shelves, mantles, kitchen hoods - display cases removed from scope. Pricing structure uses linear foot pricing for most products, square foot pricing for mantles. Catalog structure already looks solid with proper images and attribute options (quantity, length, height, depth, finish). 📅 Implementation Timeline & Scheduling Initial implementation meeting scheduled for August 22nd at 1:00 PM after customer's bow hunting trip. Customer prefers to handle most catalog build independently, reaching out for questions as needed. Follow-up meetings to be scheduled based on progress and questions that arise. 🔗 Integration Requirements Mosaic integration discussed - export templates can be configured to speak directly to customer's Mosaic system. Integration requires live meeting with engineer to map correct field names and test exports. Customer interested in whether imports create new jobs in Mosaic (confirmed they do). ⚙️ System Configuration & Features QuickBooks integration still in development for next couple months, with Transaction Pro workaround available. Stripe payment processing recommended for online credit card payments. UPS/FedEx shipping integration available based on weight calculations, custom delivery methods already configured. 👥 User Management & Permissions Process explained for adding additional employee accounts with customizable permission levels. Company vs individual contact setup clarified for proper discount application. Public ordering currently enabled - recommendation to require login for pricing visibility discussed. 📈 Advanced Features & Marketplace B2B catalog integration explained for cross-selling with other Almoxy users. Marketplace participation discussed but not highly recommended by Josh. B2B features recommended as post-launch project after pricing and Stripe setup complete. Action Items Josh Baker Send calendar invite for August 22nd implementation meeting at 1:00 PM Send email with knowledge base link and meeting summary Report bug with Almoxy articles not opening Check if Larkul is properly appearing in the marketplace Ayden - Larkul Send any reference materials, pricing methodologies, or catalog information to Josh before August 22nd meeting Create folder with pricing and catalog structure documentation if needed Unassigned Follow up on Google Drive link issue with Bobby for meeting questions/materials

Title: Allmoxy Demo - Date: Tue Jun 03 2025 16:15:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Participants: bobby@allmoxy.com, dambuger@gmail.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 29 mins Overview In the Allmoxy Product Demonstration and Sales Discussion meeting, Ayden from Larkul expressed interest in expanding their product offerings, specifically in kitchen hoods, while currently relying on Allmoxy for managing floating shelves and mantles. The demo highlighted Allmoxy's capabilities to set square foot pricing—a key feature absent in Ayden's current Squarespace setup—along with product categorization, wood variation management, and integration with Mosaic for production efficiencies. Bobby, the presenter, explained the pricing model, starting at $250/month and requiring engineering assistance for setup. export feature for seamless production Customer segmentation capabilities: can separate dealer vs retail customers with different product visibility System allows for company-specific discounts and price adjustments Current business: ~10 jobs per month through Snow Mountain (builder-focused business) 💰 Pricing, Implementation & Next Steps Ayden impressed by Allmoxy's depth of features beyond just plugins QuickBooks integration nearly complete for accounting synchronization Pricing starts at $250/month for new businesses, scales with revenue at annual renewal Implementation requires engineer assistance - engagements.body: Churn Playbook Please select all of the reasons that apply for why the customer is churning. Add any additional churn context in the note - the more the better here. If there's escalation notes that exist on the account be sure to reference them and include the data of the escalation note in the playbook context. Customer Stakeholder Misalignment, Moved to Other Solution Notes: After careful consideration, we have decided that this platform is no longer the right fit for B&L Cabinets, primarily due to the direction our company is heading this year. As we continue to refine our internal workflows and sales process, we are shifting our focus toward a different approach that better aligns with our long-term goals. Additionally, during our initial research and evaluation of Allmoxy, we understood that the platform would offer a more comprehensive design experience for clients,  specifically the ability to place cabinetry within elevations to gain a full visual overview of their space. We have since found that the current configuration-based approach, without that elevation or visual design component, may present challenges for our sales process. From our experience, clients respond more confidently when they can visually understand how cabinetry integrates into their overall layout, rather than selecting individual components in isolation. While Allmoxy offers valuable tools and has strong potential, we’ve come to realize that it does not fully meet our expectations or current needs, particularly when it comes to the route that B&L Cabinets will head this year. Update Pay Status to "Cancelled" Cancelled What's the catalog implementation status? No

Churn Risk Playbook Please share the most up to date health pulse from the CSM's perspective. Red(this customer is not in good standing and is on track to churn) Has this customer asked to pause or cancel their account or is this playbook being utilized preemptively? Requested to cancel Notes: New customer requesting to cancel because they found a different solution. Expected Allmoxy to have a designer it sounds like? They wanted something with a design visual. They are in a contract, but there is no language the contract that suggests penalty for early cancellation. How much is the customers monthly subscription? Please note if the customer has received any reductions in MRR during their lifetime with Allmoxy as well as any significant, recent implementation costs. $350.00 Industry Segment (Data Confirmation) Custom Cabinet Shop Business Size (Data Confirmation) SMB How long have they been a customer of Allmoxy? 09/22/2025 Is this customer launched? No Is this customer working with our implementation team now, if so please elaborate on the nature of the work/project. In talks Notes: They met with IMP one time and then went dark. Is this customer a legacy/strategic account? No Has the customer proposed any solutions that would eliminate or delay their churn risk? This could be a price reduction or delivery on a specific feature. Notes: No, working on getting a meeting set up with them to hear more. Please share any other strategic context that we should consider related to this account. (Ex. Industry influence/connections, personality details, known expansion opportunity, competitor tech stack, etc.) Notes: None Who's the main customer contact associated with this escalation? lisag@perfectioninstall.com Notes: Irick For reporting purposes, please check that this playbook is completed. Yes

Kick off call 11/6/25 B&L Relationship between CS and implementation Build type would be hybrid/ asynch (explain what that is) Who are his end customers going to be? Product offerings will combine cabinets and doors into a single kit, enhancing customer shopping experience; customization options include GOLA systems and LED lights. How are you currently pricing your products? per square foot, hinges, handles, and minimum pricing structure Do you have any reference material that I can share with our engineers? (excel spreadsheet, cutlists, pricing information, product offering, etc)? Notes Allmoxy will just be their "cutting" projects They like our template, but want to add some stuff. He's been adding photos of hardware and materials in the G drive Meeting Summary This kickoff call involved Irick from BNL Cabinets, Bobby Lewis, and Josh Baker from the Allmoxy team to begin implementation and onboarding for BNL's Allmoxy instance. The focus was to align on catalog setup, dropdown logic, customization levels, and next steps toward building the product catalog and pricing logic within Allmoxy. The conversation covered technical setup, internal process shifts at BNL, catalog planning, and upcoming engineering support. Key Takeaways Important Discussions BNL Cabinets is shifting toward prioritizing their own projects while using Allmoxy for external cutting jobs and semi-custom cabinetry. BNL's current manual processes are transitioning to a streamlined, Allmoxy-driven flow, including: Managing and storing materials Standardized product offerings Material and hardware pricing Irick has already begun organizing assets (hardware, material photos) into folders within the shared Google Drive to support catalog development. A “master product” will be built first with all dropdowns and options; it will serve as the foundation for cloning additional products. Technical Issues & Fixes Template-based product setup in Allmoxy was discussed but ultimately deprioritized; team agreed it's faster to build the product catalog from scratch. Reveals (top, bottom, left, right) were added to the interface but presented layout challenges. Irick provided input on preferred dropdown positioning for cleaner UX. Meta tables will be used by engineering to streamline catalog setup and dropdown logic. Decisions Made BNL will: Build a Google Sheet listing dropdowns, options, and preferred order Include pricing logic as it's developed Use that sheet as the blueprint for the Allmoxy engineering team (led by Aaron) to build the catalog from scratch Reveals will be handled at the product level with detailed dropdowns for each direction. Standard products will cover 90% of daily production, with Allmoxy used only for semi-custom work. Fully custom projects will continue to be managed manually. Master product first > Validate > Clone process adopted as implementation strategy. A follow-up meeting is scheduled for next Thursday at 7:45 AM PT to begin work with Aaron. Challenges or Concerns Time constraints for Irick as he balances implementation with running day-to-day operations. BNL’s current pricing model doesn’t yet include hardware or lighting; those are being developed. The need to structure the catalog to be simple enough for clients but detailed enough to avoid ambiguity, especially around reveals and construction methods. Transitioning from a model where customers provide materials to one where BNL handles all sourcing will require internal changes. Action Items Irick (BNL Cabinets): Complete a detailed Google Sheet: Product list Dropdown names and order Option values for each dropdown Initial pricing logic (even if partial) Finalize or update pricing logic in his Excel model to include hardware and materials Coordinate with internal team on final material and hardware selections Josh Baker (Allmoxy): Email Irick contact info and instructions Schedule next meeting with Aaron and Irick for Thursday at 7:45 AM PT Act as point of contact and project manager throughout the build phase Bobby Lewis (Allmoxy): Support handoff to engineering team (Aaron) Assist in any clarification needed for product architecture and dropdown handling Next Steps Irick to deliver the Google Sheet by or before next Wednesday. Kickoff build session with Josh and Aaron scheduled for Thursday at 7:45 AM PT. Weekly cadence of working sessions to be established during next week’s call. Begin catalog build-out based on Irick’s spreadsheet. Continue internal pricing and material sourcing discussions at BNL to align with the Allmoxy rollout plan.

Title: Allmoxy Scope Call - B&L Cabinets Date: Fri Oct 10 2025 12:00:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Participants: bobby@allmoxy.com, robertm@perfectioninstall.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 37 mins Overview - Technical setup confirmed for B&L Cabinets website launch using Amoxy, targeting January 1st to clients; integration of Cabinet Vision and QuickBooks established. - Product offerings will combine cabinets and doors into a single kit, enhancing customer shopping experience; customization options include GOLA systems and LED lights. - Website managed via WordPress on GoDaddy; features like an order online button to link to Amoxy login being implemented. - Emphasis on customizing product attributes in Amoxy, with a focus on rich visual experiences replacing text-based descriptions. - Kickoff process requires quick instance creation post-subscription agreement, with training on catalog management and potential testing phase before Christmas. - Team collaboration noted as a key strength; enthusiasm among participants enhances the project's cultural fit and teamwork. - Agreement reached to expedite contract signing to allow for timely implementation initiation. - Integration with Amoxy platform will allow for independent management of updates by B&L Cabinets team post-training. - Initial catalog customization will align with Amoxy templates, ensuring seamless transition for product offerings. - Ongoing communication about the platform rollout to customers will be led by Robert to keep stakeholders informed. Notes 💻 Technical Setup and Launch Readiness Discussed technical setup and readiness for launch of the new B&L Cabinets website with Amoxy platform, targeting January 1st launch to clients. Confirmed use of Cabinet Vision for cabinet design and QuickBooks for accounting. 🛠️ Product Configuration and Customization Decided on combining cabinet products and doors into one kit for streamlined user experience. Discussed catalog and product configuration flexibility; customers want to add custom components like GOLA systems, lighting track, and hardware. 📦 Website Management and Integration Website currently managed via WordPress through GoDaddy; plan to add an order online button linking to Amoxy login. Executed email exchanges to assign and tie users to the company instance in Amoxy. 📊 Customization and Visual Experience Detailed how product attributes and construction methods can be customized within Amoxy. Emphasized delivering a rich visual experience with images of materials to replace textual descriptions. 🚀 Kickoff Process and Implementation Outlined kickoff process post-subscription agreement signing: fast instance creation, training on catalog management, and engineering support as needed. Confirmed ability to run testing phase before Christmas break to ensure smooth operation. 🤝 Team Collaboration and Next Steps Expressed enthusiasm and cultural connection among participants, strengthening the collaborative spirit. Concluded with agreement to proceed promptly with contract signing to initiate implementation. Action Items Irick Melara Review subscription agreement paperwork and coordinate with accounting team to complete and return as soon as possible Provide email for subscription agreement dispatch and continue communication on implementation progress Robert Assist with subscription agreement review and signing alongside Irick, maintain communications regarding implementation setup Lead company communication to customers about the upcoming platform change and rollout news updates Bobby Lewis Send subscription agreement to Irick and Robert to initiate Amoxy instance creation Provide instructions for DNS setup with GoDaddy for custom URL and facilitate technical onboarding Schedule kickoff call following subscription signing to train B&L Cabinets team on catalog structure, product attributes, and platform usage Provide Cabinet Vision catalogs matching Amoxy template and assist with initial catalog customization Support adding custom product attributes like GOLA system, lighting track options, shelving pin sizes, and hardware selections Ensure training empowers B&L Cabinets to manage updates independently and provide access to engineering resources as necessary

Title: Allmoxy Demo - : B&L Cabinets Date: Tue Sep 30 2025 12:00:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Participants: bobby@allmoxy.com, robertm@perfectioninstall.com, lisag@perfectioninstall.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 83 mins Overview - B&L Cabinets and Perfection Insulation are distinct entities focused on fabrication and installation in Chatsworth, California, emphasizing collaboration for efficiency. - Almoxy's demo showcased a B2B front-end ordering system with full branding and customizable user roles, enhancing user experience. - Integration with QuickBooks Online allows for seamless, real-time account syncing, ensuring billing accuracy and financial tracking. - Automated order validation minimizes errors, streamlining workflows from receipt to manufacturing and shipping to improve operational efficiency. - Transitioning to an owned materials business model enables better control over inventory, aiming for consistent quality and increased profitability. - Bobby Lewis reported a substantial scaling to 400 flat-pack boxes per day with a remarkable 30% annual growth, demonstrating Almoxy's impact. - Variability with client-supplied material thickness presents challenges; sourcing consistent vendor materials is recommended for quality assurance. - Configurable product attributes, such as adjustable shelves, are effectively managed within Almoxy, aligning with Cabinet Vision specifications. - Subscription-based pricing starts at approximately $417/month, tailored to projected annual sales volumes for flexible financial planning. - The implementation timeline projects 8-12 weeks, aiming for a go-live date in the first week of January 2026, promoting urgency in planning. Notes 🔍 Introduction of BNL Cabinets and Perfection Insulation Separate but related entities handling fabrication and installation respectively. Located in Chatsworth, California. 💻 Almoxy Demo Overview Robust B2B frontend ordering system for self-serving cabinet parts. Full branding and customization options with distinct user roles. 🔗 Key Features of Almoxy Integration with QuickBooks Online for real-time account syncing. Flexible shopping cart with price calculation and configurable dropdowns. Cabinet manufacturing via Cabinet Vision CAD software. ⚙️ Order Validation and Automation Minimizes bad orders with order validation rules. Automated workflows from order receipt to manufacturing and shipping. 📈 Business Model Transition Discussion Shift from traditional material-purchased-by-client model to owning materials. Control inventory for consistent quality and profitability. 📦 Bobby Lewis’ Success with Almoxy Scaled to 400 flat-pack boxes/day with 30% annual growth. Automated sales, order management, and inventory processes. ⚠️ Challenges with Material Thickness Variability Issues when clients supply their own materials. Recommendation to source consistent vendor materials. 📏 Configurable Product Attributes Adjustable shelves and built-in microwave cabinet configurations explained. Managed by Almoxy with Cabinet Vision parameters. 💰 Pricing Model Setup Highly customizable formula-driven structure for pricing. Engineering support for hybrid product catalog build. 📅 Subscription-Based Pricing Overview Aligned to projected annual sales volume. Monthly subscription rate starting at approximately $417/month. ⏳ Implementation Timeline Estimated at 8-12 weeks with potential to accelerate. Goal to go live by first week of January 2026. 🌐 Transition to Online Ordering Bobby Lewis shared experience on transitioning fully to online ordering. Consensus on shifting towards a self-served web platform. 📞 Next Steps and Follow-Up Actions Send demo link and detailed pricing documents. Schedule follow-up calls to address questions. Action Items Irick Melara Gather internal team and prepare notes/questions for follow-up discussion with Almoxy team Review demo accounts as owner, customer, and employee to evaluate how well Almoxy meets BNL’s needs Coordinate internally to estimate yearly sales volume for accurate subscription pricing Lead transition efforts and act as primary contact with Almoxy during implementation Bobby Lewis Send demo environment access link and detailed pricing chart to BNL after meeting Provide meeting transcript notes via Fireflies AI summary Offer booking link for follow-up Q&A sessions Support hybrid catalog build and engineer customization for pricing and product configurations Assist with integration planning and timeline management Robert Jr. Participate in discussions about client demographics and ordering habits to help BNL understand market shifts Brian Moura Support discussions on business model shift and potential client retention strategies

Title: Allmoxy Kick Off Call Date: Mon Aug 25 2025 16:45:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Participants: bobby@allmoxy.com, joshb@allmoxy.com, information@craftedcabinet.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 15 mins Overview - Sean Carroll's emergency situation limited his participation; Bobby Lewis introduced Josh Baker as the customer success representative for Allmoxy integration support. - The initial product launch focuses on The Christie vanity in 21-inch and 24-inch widths, both 24 inches deep, with a B2C business model differing from typical B2B clients. - Christie vanity will be available only in walnut, rifts on white oak, and regular white oak finishes—no additional options will be offered. - A shared Google Drive folder has been organized by Sean containing critical product documents, including cut sheets and pricing, which incorporates shipping costs. - Vendor contact information for door ordering and shipping details, including weights and pallet dimensions, will be added to the Google Drive by Sean. - The implementation strategy recommends The Christie as the pilot product for Allmoxy, with plans to replicate the setup for additional products later. - A one-hour training session is set for Sean to learn about product duplication and making minor adjustments post-integration. - Dropdown menus will be used for product configuration to mitigate pricing errors, with a custom size upcharge of 20% for dimensions from 74 to 78 inches. - An empty Allmoxy instance will be initiated for unique product configurations, ensuring clarity in customer ordering and account creation processes. - Future development plans include the readiness of additional products, Giselle and Horizon vanities, awaiting finalization of websites and related options. Notes 📋 Emergency Meeting Context & Introductions Sean Carroll had an emergency come up and could only participate for 10 minutes. Bobby Lewis introduced Josh Baker as Sean's customer success representative who will handle all Allmoxy integration and engineering coordination. Meeting was being recorded as noted by Bobby. 📦 Product Overview & Business Model Sean's company is launching vanities initially, followed by cabinet doors later. Business model is B2C which differs from Allmoxy's typical B2B clients. Main product focus is The Christie vanity available in 21-inch and 24-inch widths, both 24 inches deep. Christie will only be offered in walnut, rifts on white oak, and regular white oak - no other finishes. 📂 Documentation & File Structure Sean organized shared Google Drive folder with product information including photos, cut sheets, elevations, and pricing. Pricing includes shipping to avoid complications during ordering process. Sean plans to add vendor contact information for door ordering and shipping details including weights and pallet dimensions. 🛠️ Implementation Strategy & Next Steps Josh suggested starting with The Christie as the pilot product, then duplicating the setup for other products. Plan includes one-hour training session for Sean to learn product duplication and minor adjustments. Sean mentioned having additional products ready: Giselle and Horizon vanities with pricing built but websites not yet created. Sean's next development phase includes adding stain selection options and related photography. ⚙️ Technical Configuration Decisions Product configuration will use dropdown menus instead of free-text input to prevent pricing errors. Custom sizing option discussed with automatic 20% upcharge for sizes between 74-78 inches. Bobby confirmed starting with empty Allmoxy instance due to unique product differences. Website integration will include direct link to Allmoxy for customer ordering and account creation. Action Items Sean Carroll Complete uploading all Christie vanity documentation to Google Drive folder and notify Josh when ready Organize cut sheets and ordering forms in detailed folder structure for engineering team Provide vendor contact information for door ordering and shipping logistics Add product weights and pallet dimensions for Old Dominion shipping Josh Baker Send contact information email to Sean immediately after meeting Coordinate with engineering team to begin Christie vanity product build once documentation is complete Schedule one-hour training session for Sean on product duplication and configuration adjustments Bobby Lewis Support Josh with Christie product implementation and engineering coordination Guide Sean through additional Allmoxy platform elements after product base is established

Kick off call 8/25 with Sean & Bobby Relationship between CS and implementation Build type would be hybrid/ asynch (explain what that is) Who are his end customers going to be? What products are going to be offered? To start, are we doing the vanity sinks from your website? How are you currently pricing your products? Do you have any reference material that I can share with our engineers? (excel spreadsheet, cutlists, pricing information, product offering, etc)? Meeting Summary The meeting focused on the kickoff and setup for Sean Carroll’s vanity product line in Allmoxy. Due to an emergency, Sean had to leave early, so Bobby and Josh aligned on next steps. Sean is currently preparing assets including pricing, elevations, 3D renderings, and cut sheets for various vanity models—starting with “The Christie.” Josh Baker will be the primary Customer Success Rep coordinating technical integration. The team agreed to start with one product (The Christie) and use it as a template for others. Key Takeaways Important Discussions Sean has: Pricing completed for several vanity models. Started on 3D renderings and is mailing out product samples. Begun populating a shared Google Drive with necessary materials for each product (photos, elevations, cut sheets, and order forms). Primary product to start with is The Christie vanity (24"W x 24"D). Products will be offered in limited finishes: walnut, rift-sawn white oak, and standard white oak. The offering is direct-to-consumer (B2C), differing from Allmoxy’s usual B2B clients, but should be manageable. Technical Issues & Fixes Product listings will use dropdowns for dimensions and options to prevent manual input errors (e.g., no entering "26” when 24” is standard). Sean requested the ability to handle custom size pricing via a percentage add-on (e.g., +20% for custom range). Josh recommended starting with an empty Allmoxy instance due to product uniqueness. Website pricing includes shipping to avoid complications. Decisions Made Start with building “The Christie” in Allmoxy. Engineer will build version 1 of the product, then review with Sean before duplicating to other models. Sean will organize the Google Drive further and populate it with all assets and cut sheets. Engineering team will provide Sean with training on how to duplicate products and make edits in Allmoxy. Challenges or Concerns Need for detailed technical data: pallet weights, dimensions, vendor contact info, and photos. Importance of clearly organizing assets in Drive to minimize back-and-forth or misinterpretation during setup. Sean will be responsible for adding the remaining products (e.g., Giselle, Horizon) once Christie is finalized and system is understood. Action Items Sean Carroll Complete upload of all Christie assets to Google Drive (cut sheets, order forms, pricing, elevations, renderings, etc.). Organize product folders clearly with naming and subfolders by dimension/variant. Confirm product website aligns 1:1 with what will be available in Allmoxy. Provide pallet specs (weight and dimensions) and vendor contact information. Josh Baker Email Sean contact details and initiate Christie project with engineering. Coordinate training on product duplication/editing once Christie is built. Support Sean with implementation and clarification needs throughout the build. Engineering Team (via Josh) Begin setup for The Christie in Allmoxy. Provide walkthrough or documentation/training video once version 1 is complete. Next Steps Sean will give Josh the go-ahead once all Christie materials are uploaded and organized. Josh will notify the engineer to begin setup immediately after. Once The Christie is reviewed and finalized, Sean will move forward with additional products using the same structure. Schedule training session for Sean on how to duplicate/edit products in Allmoxy after initial build is complete.

Title: Allmoxy Scope Call - Crafted Cabinetry and Millwork Date: Wed Aug 20 2025 12:15:00 GMT-0400 (Eastern Daylight Time) Participants: bobby@allmoxy.com, information@craftedcabinet.com Transcript: See full transcript Audio: Listen to audio Duration: 18 mins Overview - Sean Carroll targets $1.8 million in revenue for next year with Bobby Lewis's support to achieve business growth. - Public ordering feature allows customers to configure products without logging in; accounts needed only for saving orders, aligning with Sean's customer interaction needs. - Allmoxy integrates with KCD software for cabinet design, providing end-to-end automation, surpassing basic Shopify order capture capabilities. - Direct vanity linking to Allmoxy configurators from Sean’s B2C website is complicated; alternative to hyperlink to main order page discussed. - Customer workflow established: view options on Sean’s site, click 'Place Order' to access Allmoxy configurator for vanities like Kaden 60-inch double sink. - Subscription agreed at $250/month for initial volume with $100/hour engineering support, aiming $50k-100k in initial revenue. - Crafted Cabinet instance URL is craftedcabinet.allmoxy.com; custom domain available for $20/month, decision made to start with standard URL. - Bobby confirms engineering capacity for immediate project start; contract sent via PandaDoc for quick onboarding. - Kickoff meeting to be scheduled with customer success rep and engineering team to align on implementation strategy. Notes 📈 Initial Discussion & Goal Setting Sean Carroll aiming to reach 1.8 million in revenue next year with Bobby Lewis's assistance Discussion about busy business operations and growth trajectory 🛠️ Public Ordering Configuration Bobby demonstrated public ordering feature allowing customers to view and configure products without logging in initially Customers can see configurator but must create account before saving orders Sean confirmed this approach meets his needs for customer interaction 🔗 Software Integration Discussion Sean uses KCD software for cabinet design, Bobby confirmed Allmoxy integration capability Allmoxy provides end-to-end automation from order capture through production, including printing plans Platform offers significant advantages over basic Shopify accounts which only capture orders 🌐 B2C Website Integration Challenges Sean inquired about direct vanity linking from his website to specific Allmoxy configurators Bobby explained platform is B2B-focused rather than B2C, making direct product linking complex Alternative solution: hyperlink to main order page where customers select specific vanities 📦 Order Process Workflow Agreed on workflow: customers view vanity options on Sean's site, click 'Place Order' to access Allmoxy configurator Customers would select specific models (e.g., Kaden 60-inch double sink) and customize finishes/colors 💰 Pricing and Subscription Agreement $250/month subscription agreed upon for initial volume expectations $100/hour engineering rate for development and support work Sean's initial revenue targets: $50k-100k to start, with scaling expectations 🔧 Technical Setup Details Instance URL set as craftedcabinet.allmoxy.com Custom domain available for $20/month additional Sean opted to start with standard URL, may upgrade later 🗓️ Implementation Timeline & Next Steps Bobby confirmed optimal engineering capacity available for immediate start Contract sent via PandaDoc to information@CraftedCabinet.com Kickoff meeting to be scheduled with customer success rep and engineering team Action Items Bobby Lewis Send subscription agreement via PandaDoc to information@CraftedCabinet.com Create Allmoxy instance for Crafted Cabinet Send login credentials once instance is created Set up kickoff meeting with customer success rep and engineering team Share Google Drive folder for Sean to upload vanity information Sean Carroll Review and sign subscription agreement sent via PandaDoc Compile all vanity product information before kickoff meeting Upload vanity details and renderings to shared Google Drive folder Prepare for engineering consultation meeting to discuss specific requirements
